[So who are those people in Hebrews 3? Now watch it! (This is really intellectual.) The book of Hebrews is written to HEBREWS! It's not written to the saints in Rome or the church in Corinth or to a son in the faith or to a fellow laborer in Christ. ]{.class-0}[It's written to HEBREWS.]{.class-1}

[Remember Matthew 24:13? It was addressed to someone worshipping at a temple (Matt. 24:15), living in Judea (Matt. 24:16), and observing the Sabbath (Matt. 24:20); ]{.class-0}[it wasn't written to any Christian in the Body of Christ.]{.class-1}

[Nearly 80% of the professing Christians in the Church Age thought you could lose your salvation (that's Methodists, Lutherans, Episcopalians, Church of God, Assembly of God, Nazarenes, Catholics, and Greek Orthodox), and the whole trouble was the word ]{.class-0}["end."]{.class-2}[ For the Christian, that ]{.class-0}["end"]{.class-2}[ is the Rapture (1 Cor. 1:8), but for that Jew outside the Church Age, that ]{.class-0}["end"]{.class-2}[ is the Tribulation (Matt. 24:21). Look at Daniel 12.]{.class-0}

"[And at that time shall Michael stand up, the great prince which standeth for the children of thy people ]{.class-2}[\[that's Daniel's ]{.class-0}["people"]{.class-2}[---the ]{.class-0}[Jews,]{.class-1}[ not the Christians\]]{.class-0}[: and there shall be a time of trouble ]{.class-2}[\[Jer. 30:7 says it's ]{.class-0}["the time of Jacob's trouble,"]{.class-2}[ not the Church's\]]{.class-0}[, such as never was since there was a nation even to that same time ]{.class-2}[\[there's Matt. 24:21\]]{.class-0}[: and at that time thy people ]{.class-2}[\[no Christians\]]{.class-0}[ shall be delivered, every one that is written in the book"]{.class-2}[ (Dan. 12:1).]{.class-0}

"[But thou, O Daniel, shut up the words, and seal the book, even to the time of THE END\...."]{.class-2}[ What characterizes that ]{.class-0}["time"]{.class-2}[? ]{.class-0}["\...many shall run to and fro, and knowledge shall be increased"]{.class-2}[ (Dan. 12:4). Incredible advancements in education and speed---communication and transportation. That's where you're at today.]{.class-0}

"[How long shall it be to THE END of these wonders?"]{.class-2}[ (Dan. 12:6). ]{.class-0}["It shall be for a time, times, and an half\...."]{.class-2}[ (Dan. 12:7). That's the last three and a half years of Daniel's Seventieth Week (Dan. 9:27), mentioned five times in Revelation 11--13 as 1,260 days (Rev. 11:3, 12:6), 42 months (Rev. 11:2, 13:5), and three-and-a-half years (Rev. 12:14). ]{.class-0}["\...and when he shall have accomplished to scatter the power of the holy people, all these things shall be finished."]{.class-2}

"[And I heard, but I understood not: then said I, O my Lord, what shall be THE END of these things?"]{.class-2}[ (Dan. 12:8).]{.class-0}

"[And he said, Go thy way, Daniel: for the words are closed up and sealed till the time of THE END"]{.class-2}[ (Dan. 12:9).]{.class-0}

"[But go thou thy way till THE END be: for thou shalt rest, and stand in thy lot at THE END of the days"]{.class-2}[ (Dan. 12:13).]{.class-0}

[That's six times in just one chapter.]{.class-1}[ If you want to see it four more times, see Daniel 8:19, 10:1, 11:27, 35. ]{.class-0}["The end"]{.class-2}[ of the ]{.class-0}["appointed time"]{.class-2}[ is the Second Advent at the end of the Tribulation. Daniel's part of ]{.class-0}["the vision"]{.class-2}[ was sealed (Dan. 12:14) until John unsealed it in Revelation 22:10 (see the comments on that verse in that Commentary).]{.class-0}

[Back to Habakkuk 2:3, ]{.class-0}["But at the end it shall speak, and not lie."]{.class-2}[ John was told, ]{.class-0}["These sayings are faithful and true: and the Lord God of the holy prophets sent his angel to shew unto his servants the things which must shortly be done"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 22:6).]{.class-0}

"[Though it tarry, wait for it; because it will surely come, it will not tarry."]{.class-2}[ Now that is what upsets me about the Lord: I'm always in a hurry and He's not. He says: ]{.class-0}["wait for it."]{.class-2}[ Well, we've been waiting 2,600 years. When the Lord says something is ]{.class-0}["at hand,"]{.class-2}[ it might be twenty years or fifty years or 3,000 years. With God, there is no time because He lives in eternity.]{.class-0}

"[Though it tarry\...it will not tarry."]{.class-2}[ Well, obviously the implication is that ]{.class-0}["the end"]{.class-2}[ will ]{.class-0}["tarry"]{.class-2}[---and it has---but it won't ]{.class-0}["tarry"]{.class-2}[ forever: ]{.class-0}["it will surely come."]{.class-2}[ Christ says in Revelation 22:20, ]{.class-0}["Surely I come quickly."]{.class-2}[ (Well, hurry up, then!)]{.class-0}

4 Behold, his soul which is lifted up is not upright in him: but the just shall live by his faith.

[Now in verse 4, we come to a verse that appears ]{.class-0}[three times]{.class-1}[ in the New Testament, twice to prove salvation by grace through faith. This is the kind of thing that messes up at least 80% of professing Christians. They read Paul using a prophecy like Habakkuk 2:4, and they figure all the Old Testament has been fulfilled: that it's all in ]{.class-0}[the past. ]{.class-1}[That's why you won't hear Schuller or Osteen or any of the TV boys preach on the Old Testament; I don't believe I have ever heard Billy Graham preach a text from it since I started listening to him. That's why a lot of American Christians don't fool with the thing and act like it isn't even there.]{.class-0}

[Brethren, ]{.class-0}[less than a quarter of the prophecies in the Old Testament have been fulfilled.]{.class-1}[ I would say a good 80% of the Old Testament is future; there are at least 500 verses in the Old Testament that await ]{.class-0}[future]{.class-1}[ fulfillment. Every verse about the kingdom being here on earth is a reference to the Millennial kingdom, and it hasn't shown up yet. There are whole chapters on the Antichrist in the Psalms and in Isaiah, and he hasn't shown up yet.]{.class-0}

Recently we had a fellow write the Bookstore complaining about Dr. Ruckman's "futuristic" theology (you know, "the Rapture is a Jesuit doctrine"---that kind of stuff). To prove that every Old Testament prophecy on the earthly Millennial kingdom has already been fulfilled, he pulled out Peter's Pentecostal sermon in Acts 2.

"[Therefore being a prophet, and knowing that God had sworn with an oath to him, that of the fruit of his loins, according to the flesh, he would raise up Christ to sit on his throne; He seeing this before spake of the resurrection of Christ, that his soul was not left in hell, neither his flesh did see corruption. This Jesus hath God raised up, whereof we are all witnesses"]{.class-2}[ (Acts 2:30--32).]{.class-0}

[His argument was since David said Christ was to be raised up ]{.class-0}["to sit on his throne,"]{.class-2}[ and since Peter said that was fulfilled by Christ's resurrection, that every prophecy in the Old Testament about Christ reigning from Jerusalem on the throne of David was ]{.class-0}[fulfilled]{.class-1}[ at Christ's resurrection and ascension. That's the kind of Daffy Duck scholarship these apostates practice.]{.class-0}

[Nothing in Acts 2 about the Tribulation]{.class-1}[ (see the comments on Acts 2:16--21 in that Commentary) or ]{.class-0}[the Millennium was fulfilled at the Resurrection or the Ascension or at Pentecost or anywhere else in the Gospels or the book of Acts. ]{.class-1}[The only thing Peter said was fulfilled was the ]{.class-0}["raised up"]{.class-2}[ part (see Acts 2:32). The ]{.class-0}[purpose]{.class-1}[ for the Resurrection was not fulfilled, and Peter tells you as much in Acts 3:19--21. The problem with these apostates is that they can't (or won't) read ]{.class-0}[sixth-grade English. ]{.class-1}[They mess with the Book, so God messes with their minds.]{.class-0}

[Brethren, that Book is so written that if you don't believe it as it is written, in the context in which it is written, it will mess you up and ]{.class-0}[deceive]{.class-1}[ you. That Old Testament was written to mess up a ]{.class-0}[Gentile]{.class-1}[. That Old Testament talks about a holy land given to one race of people, and the Jew getting the upper hand and ]{.class-0}[ruling over all the nations in the UN from Jerusalem;]{.class-1}[ and that Gentile allegorizes it and applies it to him because he thinks the whole thing is in the ]{.class-0}[past]{.class-1}[. All Moslems, all Catholics, all Greek Orthodox, and all Protestants who aren't Premillennial are ]{.class-0}[deceived.]{.class-1}[ You say, "Deceived by what?" They're deceived by that ]{.class-0}[Old Testament]{.class-1}[ because they won't ]{.class-0}[believe]{.class-1}[ it.]{.class-0}

[In the same way, God wrote that New Testament to mess up a ]{.class-0}[Jew]{.class-1}[. It has messed him up for 2,000 years, and he's messed up right now. If you went to Jerusalem right now, do you know what you would find the Rabbis doing over there? You'd find them teaching their people anything except the prophets. They teach them the ]{.class-0}[Torah]{.class-1}[ (the Law: Genesis--Deuteronomy). They teach them to recite the ]{.class-0}[Shema Yisrael]{.class-1}[: ]{.class-0}["Hear, O Israel: The LORD our God is one LORD"]{.class-2}[ (Deut. 6:4).]{.class-0}

[Oh, shut up! ]{.class-0}[That's not going to do anything for anyone. ]{.class-1}[Any devil knows that: ]{.class-0}["Thou believest that there is one God; thou doest well: the devils also believe, and tremble"]{.class-2}[ (James 2:19). But you see, that's in the New Testament, and the Jew doesn't ]{.class-0}[read]{.class-1}[ the New Testament, so he thinks he's really saying something when he recites the ]{.class-0}[Shema Yisrael.]{.class-1}[ He's saying nothing but what a devil would say.]{.class-0}

[The New Testament says God has a Son and His Son is Deity. ]{.class-0}[No orthodox Jew believes that God had a Son or that Jesus Christ is Deity.]{.class-1}[ Neither does any ]{.class-0}[Moslem]{.class-1}[. Nearly 900 million Moslems and 200 million Jews will be destroyed by that New Testament.]{.class-0}

[Brethren, that's one of the reasons that Book was written---]{.class-0}[to mess up any sinner whose heart isn't right.]{.class-1}[ It is the only Book that the condition for understanding it is the right kind of heart, and an unsaved man doesn't have the right kind of heart. He instinctively resists what's in that Book.]{.class-0}

[By the same token, the Book is written so that if any man ]{.class-0}[wants]{.class-1}[ to do right, he can. If a man ]{.class-0}[wants]{.class-1}[ to find the right way, he can because it's written down in the Book. All he has to do is ]{.class-0}[believe]{.class-1}[ what he reads.]{.class-0}

[All that being said, let's look at Habakkuk 2:4. ]{.class-0}["Behold, his soul which is lifted up is not upright in him: but the just shall live by his faith."]{.class-2}[ That last clause---]{.class-0}["the just shall live by his faith"]{.class-2}[---is quoted three times by the Apostle Paul in the New Testament: Romans 1:17; Galatians 3:11; and Hebrews 10:38. Every single time---in complete defiance of the warnings not to subtract from Scripture (Deut. 4:2 cf. Rev. 22:19)---he leaves out the word ]{.class-0}["his."]{.class-2}[ Of course, he didn't do it on his own; he did it under the direction of the Holy Spirit. That just shows you that God can ]{.class-0}[add]{.class-1}[ or ]{.class-0}[subtract]{.class-1}[ from His own words (e.g., see Jer. 36:32) without doing wrong. ]{.class-0}[You can't]{.class-1}[ but He can, because He knows what He's saying and you don't.]{.class-0}

Now let's take a look at one of those passages, Romans 1:17, and see why Paul and the Holy Spirit did what they did.

"[For therein is the righteousness of God revealed from faith to faith\...."]{.class-2}[ Now to understand how Paul is about to apply the quote from Habakkuk 2:4, you have to understand the difference between ]{.class-0}[Old Testament]{.class-1}[ faith and ]{.class-0}[New Testament]{.class-1}[ faith. There isn't a Ph.D. in 22 commentaries that I have read who understands it, so they can't even guess as to why Paul did what he did in Romans 1:17. The fact of the matter is, in the Old Testament, there's a combination of ]{.class-0}[faith and works]{.class-1}[ involved in salvation.]{.class-0}

[Now that is supposed to be one of "Ruckman's" great ]{.class-0}[heresies]{.class-1}[. The standard teaching of all the Fundamentalists and Conservatives is that salvation is the same in both Testaments. That isn't true ]{.class-0}[before]{.class-1}[ or ]{.class-0}[after]{.class-1}[ the giving of the Law.]{.class-0}

[The argument the Fundamentalists give you is this: "Abraham was saved by grace through faith (Rom. 4), so they must be the same." Izzat so? Let me ask you this: how many of you have been born again? ]{.class-0}[Well, no one in the Old Testament was born again]{.class-1}[ (John 3:5--6 cf. 7:39). How many of you were justified when you received Jesus Christ? Well, ]{.class-0}[Abraham wasn't justified until he offered up his son Isaac]{.class-1}[ (James 2:21). Aren't you justified and don't you receive the imputed righteousness of Christ the moment you get saved? Well, Abraham had righteousness imputed to him ]{.class-0}[years]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-0}[before he was justified ]{.class-1}[(Gen. 15:6 cf. 22:1--10). When Abraham received the imputed righteousness of God by faith, in what was his faith? Was it in the shed blood of Christ? ]{.class-0}[No, it was in having as many kids as the stars in the sky ]{.class-1}[(Gen. 15:5). Did you get justified and receive Christ's imputed righteousness when you believed the Gospel? Well, Abraham didn't believe ]{.class-0}["the gospel"]{.class-2}[ until ]{.class-0}[years after]{.class-1}[ he had righteousness imputed to him and ]{.class-0}[years before]{.class-1}[ he was justified (Gal. 3:8 cf. Gen. 18:18). And your salvation is the ]{.class-0}[same]{.class-1}[ as his, is it?]{.class-0}

[You see, people don't think. They find something in the New Testament they know is so, and they try to jam the rest of the Bible in there to make it fit.]{.class-0}[ It doesn't fit.]{.class-1}

[Do you ]{.class-0}[know for sure]{.class-1}[ where you are going when you die (1 John 5:13)? ]{.class-0}[Why, there is no eternal security in the Old Testament.]{.class-1}[ Samson lost the Holy Spirit and got Him back; Saul lost the Holy Spirit and never got Him back; and David prayed: ]{.class-0}["take not thy holy spirit from me"]{.class-2}[ (Psa. 51:11). It ]{.class-0}[that]{.class-1}[ your salvation? Why, you couldn't lose the Holy Spirit if you tried (Eph. 4:30).]{.class-0}

[Are you adopted as a child of God (Rom. 8:15)? ]{.class-0}[Well, no one in the Old Testament was; ]{.class-1}[Christ had to come and die before anyone could ]{.class-0}["receive the adoption of sons"]{.class-2}[ (Gal. 4:4--5). Abraham wasn't a son of God; Moses wasn't a son of God; David wasn't a son of God. The only ]{.class-0}[individual ]{.class-1}[sons of God in the Old Testament (Israel was a ]{.class-0}[corporate]{.class-1}[ son of God---see Exod. 4:22) were ]{.class-0}[Adam]{.class-1}[ (Luke 3:38) and the ]{.class-0}[angels]{.class-1}[ (Job 38:4--7), and that was by ]{.class-0}[direct creation,]{.class-1}[ not adoption.]{.class-0}

[Are you still not settled on the matter? Well, let me give you something that should straighten things out once and for all. When you accepted Christ as your Saviour, were your sins taken away (John 1:29)? Well, that Bible says, ]{.class-0}["For it is not possible that the blood of bulls and of goats should take away sins"]{.class-2}[ (Heb. 10:4); ]{.class-0}[and that's all Abraham and Abel and Noah and Moses and David and the rest of them ever had.]{.class-1}

You see, "Ruckman's" no heretic. Somebody else is the heretic. I'll let you guess who.

[Back to Romans 1:17---]{.class-0}["\...as it is written, The just shall live by faith."]{.class-2}[ Habakkuk said, ]{.class-0}["his faith,"]{.class-2}[ indicating the faith of the man himself. So whose faith is it in Romans 1:17? Why, it's the ]{.class-0}[Lord's]{.class-1}[ faith (Rom. 3:3, 22; Gal. 2:16, 20; Eph. 3:11--12; Phil. 3:9; James 2:1; Rev. 14:12).]{.class-0}

[Look at Ephesians 2:8, ]{.class-0}["For by grace are ye saved through faith; and that\...." "That" ]{.class-2}[what? That ]{.class-0}[faith.]{.class-1}[ "\...and that ]{.class-2}[\[the antecedent is ]{.class-0}["faith"]{.class-2}[\]]{.class-0}[ NOT OF YOURSELVES . \..."]{.class-2}[ Is that clear? The ]{.class-0}["faith"]{.class-1}[ is not ]{.class-0}[your]{.class-1}[ faith. ]{.class-0}["\...it]{.class-2}[ \[the antecedent is still ]{.class-0}["faith"]{.class-2}[\] ]{.class-0}[is the gift of God."]{.class-2}

[So in Ephesians 2:8, faith is a gift from God. You say, "It's eternal life. Romans 6:23 says,]{.class-0}[ 'the gift of God is eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord.']{.class-2}[" Shut your mouth; you don't know about what you're talking! The antecedent of both ]{.class-0}["that"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["if"]{.class-2}[ in Ephesians 2:8 is ]{.class-0}["faith,"]{.class-2}[ ]{.class-0}[and it is the faith God gave you to be saved. ]{.class-1}[That means that ]{.class-0}[any]{.class-1}[ man can be saved if he exercises the faith God gave him.]{.class-0}

Now if you don't believe that, then get Romans 10 and look at how close that faith is to every unsaved man.

"[But the righteousness which is of faith speaketh on this wise\...."]{.class-2}[ (Rom. 10:16). What does it say? It says, "Get away from space centers and Cape Canaveral and quit trying to get up to the moon and Mars and Jupiter to find it because it ain't there (vs. 6). And stop digging down into the sedimentary rock and the metamorphic rock and the igneous rock and the magma to try to find it because it ain't down there either" (vs. 7).]{.class-0}

[Romans 10:8, ]{.class-0}["The WORD is nigh thee, even in thy mouth, and in thy heart\...."]{.class-2}[ What ]{.class-0}["word"]{.class-2}[ is that? Why, it's ]{.class-0}["the word of FAITH, which we preach."]{.class-2}

[And what is that ]{.class-0}["word of faith"]{.class-2}[ that's being preached? Romans 10:9, ]{.class-0}["That if thou shalt confess with thy mouth]{.class-2}[ \[there's the ]{.class-0}["word"]{.class-2}[\] ]{.class-0}[the Lord Jesus, and shalt believe in thine heart ]{.class-2}[\[and there's the ]{.class-0}["faith"]{.class-2}[\] ]{.class-0}[that God hath raised him from the dead, thou shalt be saved."]{.class-2}

[The ]{.class-0}["word of faith"]{.class-2}[ is the Gospel. Romans 10:16--17 says, ]{.class-0}["But they have not all obeyed the GOSPEL. For Esaias saith, Lord, who hath believed our report? So then faith cometh by hearing, and hearing by the WORD OF GOD."]{.class-2}

[Faith is given to a sinner when the Gospel is given to him]{.class-1}[ through the word of God. And no man---like the "heathen who've never heard"---has any excuse because: ]{.class-0}["The heavens declare the glory of God; and the firmament sheweth his handywork. Day unto day uttereth SPEECH ]{.class-2}[\[as in ]{.class-0}[words]{.class-1}[\]]{.class-0}[, and night unto night sheweth knowledge. There is no speech nor language ]{.class-2}[\[as in ]{.class-0}[words]{.class-1}[\]]{.class-0}[, where their voice]{.class-2}[ \[speaking ]{.class-0}[words]{.class-1}[\] ]{.class-0}[is not heard"]{.class-2}[ (Psa. 19:1--3). God has put enough of His word in ]{.class-0}[nature]{.class-1}[ and in the ]{.class-0}[conscience]{.class-1}[ (Rom. 2:15) that if any man ]{.class-0}[anywhere]{.class-1}[ will respond to the light God has given him, the Lord will get the ]{.class-0}[full message]{.class-1}[ of the Gospel to him (e.g., Cornelius in Acts 10).]{.class-0}

[Now what is the difference between the ]{.class-0}["faith"]{.class-2}[ of Romans 1:17 and Galatians 3:11 and the ]{.class-0}["his faith"]{.class-2}[ of Habakkuk 2:4? Well, the New Testament faith is faith in what Jesus Christ ]{.class-0}[did for you,]{.class-1}[ and the Old Testament faith is a man's faith ]{.class-0}[acting]{.class-1}[ on something God told him to ]{.class-0}[do.]{.class-1}[ You see it illustrated over and over again in Hebrews 11. Abel did this, Noah did that, Abraham did something else. Not a one of them believed on Christ like you do. Noah had faith for what? He had faith to believe that if ]{.class-0}[he built a boat]{.class-1}[ that God would keep him from getting drowned.]{.class-0}

Now if you want the doctrinal import of Habakkuk 2:3--4, then look at Hebrews 10:37--38.

"[For yet a little while, and he that shall come will come, and will not tarry. Now the just shall live by faith: but if any man draw back, my soul shall have no pleasure in him."]{.class-2}

Look at the contrast between that passage and Galatians 3:11--12.

"[But that no man is justified by the law in the sight of God, it is evident: for, The just shall live by faith. And the law is not of faith: but, the man that doeth them shall live in them."]{.class-2}

[Romans and Galatians are faith in the Gospel with ]{.class-0}[no]{.class-1}[ works; Hebrews is faith in Jesus Christ ]{.class-0}[with]{.class-1}[ works (note ]{.class-0}["if any man draw back"]{.class-2}[). If we grant that the same man wrote all three passages (Paul), then we have to recognize that the audience and setting of Hebrews is ]{.class-0}[different]{.class-1}[ from that of Romans and Galatians.]{.class-0}

[We have already discussed the fact that Hebrews was written to ]{.class-0}[Hebrews]{.class-1}[ and not Gentile Christians. But when it comes to the setting, you have to look at the "twist" the writer of Hebrews puts on Habakkuk 2:3---]{.class-0}["For yet a little while"]{.class-2}[ (Heb. 10:37). That ]{.class-0}["little while"]{.class-2}[ places the passage doctrinally in Daniel's Seventieth Week. The expression ]{.class-0}["a little while"]{.class-2}[ is used ]{.class-0}[seven times]{.class-1}[ by Jesus Christ in John 16:16--19, one for each year of the Tribulation, which shows up in John 16:21 (see the comments on John 16:19--21 and Heb. 10:37--38 in those respective Commentaries).]{.class-0}

[Hebrews 10:37 is Christ's return at the Second Advent. Hebrews 10:38 deals with a man in the Tribulation who has to ]{.class-0}["keep the commandments of God, AND the faith of Jesus"]{.class-2}[ to be saved (Rev. 14:12). During that time, no matter how much faith in Christ he has, if he "draws back," he goes to ]{.class-0}["perdition"]{.class-2}[ (Heb. 10:39).]{.class-0}

5 Yea also, because he transgresseth by wine, he is a proud man, neither keepeth at home, who enlargeth his desire as hell, and is as death, and cannot be satisfied, but gathereth unto him all nations, and heapeth unto him all people:

6 Shall not all these take up a parable against him, and a taunting proverb against him, and say, Woe to him that increaseth that which is not his! how long? and to him that ladeth himself with thick clay!

7 Shall they not rise up suddenly that shall bite thee, and awake that shall vex thee, and thou shalt be for booties unto them?

8 Because thou hast spoiled many nations, all the remnant of the people shall spoil thee; because of men's blood, and for the violence of the land, of the city, and of all that dwell therein.

[Now, verse 4 contrasted two men: the just man who believed what God told him to do and did it, and a man whose ]{.class-0}["soul"]{.class-2}[ was not ]{.class-0}["upright"]{.class-2}[ because it was ]{.class-0}["lifted up"]{.class-2}[ in pride (see vs. 5). Verse 5 goes back to the description of the first man in verse 4---the man who was ]{.class-0}["not upright."]{.class-2}

[Now it is not just a description of bad men in general, though you can certainly apply it that way devotionally. It is an individual, specific bad man. Look at the end of verse 5: ]{.class-0}["but gathereth unto him all nations, and heapeth unto him all people."]{.class-2}[ That's not true of all wicked men.]{.class-0}

[Historically, it's undoubtedly a reference to Nebuchadnezzar, but in the prophetic context of verse 3, you are looking at a description of the Antichrist. ]{.class-0}[The Antichrist is the most important person in the Bible outside of Christ Himself.]{.class-1}[ There are 21 types of Christ in the Bible (Adam, Abel, Noah, Shem, Melchizedek, Isaac, Job, Benjamin, Joseph, Moses, Aaron, Joshua, Barak, Gideon, Boaz, David, Solomon, Jonah, Daniel, Mordecai, Stephen): that's 7+7+7---three sevens. There are ]{.class-0}[eighteen]{.class-1}[ types of the Antichrist in the Bible (Cain, Nimrod, Esau, Pharaoh, Balak, Sisera, Abimelech, Saul, Goliath, Absalom, Jeroboam, Ahab, Sennacherib, Nebuchadnezzar, Haman, Caesar, Herod, Judas): that's 6+6+6---three sixes.]{.class-0}

[The Bible is filled with references to the Antichrist, but he's ]{.class-0}[disguised]{.class-1}[. Quite often he's called ]{.class-0}["the wicked"]{.class-2}[ or the ]{.class-0}["wicked man"]{.class-2}[ (see the comments on Job 15:20--35 and 20:4--29 in that Commentary).]{.class-0}

[Verse 5:]{.class-0}[ "Yea also, because he transgresseth by wine."]{.class-2}[ Isaiah 5:11 says, ]{.class-0}["Woe unto them that rise up early in the morning, that they may follow strong drink; that continue until night, till wine inflame them."]{.class-2}

[Proverbs 23:29--31 says, ]{.class-0}["Who hath woe? who hath sorrow? who hath contentions? who hath wounds without cause? who hath redness of eyes? They that tarry long at the wine; they that go to seek mixed wine. Look not thou upon the wine when it is red, when it giveth his colour in the cup, when it moveth itself aright. At the last it biteth like a serpent, and stingeth like an adder."]{.class-2}

[According to that passage, you shouldn't even look at wine? Why? Because if you look at it, you'll ]{.class-0}[smell]{.class-1}[ it; if you smell it, you'll ]{.class-0}[taste]{.class-1}[ it; if you taste it, you'll ]{.class-0}[drink]{.class-1}[ it; if you drink it, you'll ]{.class-0}[lose your inhibitions;]{.class-1}[ if you lose your inhibitions, you'll ]{.class-0}[sin.]{.class-1}[ Continue on with Proverbs 29:33, ]{.class-0}["Thine eyes shall behold strange women, and thine heart shall utter perverse things."]{.class-2}

[Notice how often in the Bible drunkenness is connected with things like ]{.class-0}[sodomy, incest, adultery,]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[fornication.]{.class-1}[ The first time sodomy is alluded to in the Scriptures, it is the first time a man gets drunk (see Gen. 9:20--24 and comments in that Commentary). Lot's two younger daughters got him drunk and committed incest with him (Gen. 19:30--35). After David had committed adultery with Bathsheba, he got her husband Uriah drunk so he would spend time with his wife and cover up David's sin (2 Sam. 11:12--13).]{.class-0}

[Look on down at Habakkuk 2:15. ]{.class-0}["Woe unto him that giveth his neighbour drink, that puttest thy bottle to him, and makest him drunken also, that thou mayest look on their nakedness."]{.class-2}[ That's a reference to someone giving a fellow liquor to get him to commit fornication (cf. Lev. 18:6--18). Just as sure as "God made little green apples," ]{.class-0}[nakedness]{.class-1}[ follows ]{.class-0}[drunkenness]{.class-1}[. When I was growing up, we used to say: "If a girl will smoke, she'll ]{.class-0}[drink,]{.class-1}[ and if she'll ]{.class-0}[drink,]{.class-1}[ she'll ]{.class-0}[do anything."]{.class-1}

[Now the Antichrist is connected with a woman who's called ]{.class-0}["THE MOTHER OF HARLOTS AND ABOMINATIONS OF THE EARTH"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 17:5). She commits fornication with ]{.class-0}["the kings of the earth"]{.class-2}[ and makes them ]{.class-0}["drunk with the wine of her fornication"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 17:2) from ]{.class-0}["a golden cup in her hand full of abominations and filthiness of her fornication"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 17:4). That ]{.class-0}["wine"]{.class-2}[ is associated with ]{.class-0}["blood"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 17:6), and that woman is a city on seven hills connected to a ]{.class-0}[church]{.class-1}[ (Rev. 17:9, 18). Now do you know of any church named after a city like that that claims to drink wine (that is turned into literal blood) out of a golden cup? (I won't tell you what it is; I'll let you figure it out for yourself.)]{.class-0}

[Back to Habakkuk 2:5. ]{.class-0}["He is a proud man."]{.class-2}[ Psalm 10, which is a whole chapter on the Antichrist, says:]{.class-0}[ "The wicked in his pride doth persecute the poor"]{.class-2}[ (Psa. 10:2), and ]{.class-0}["The wicked, through the pride of his countenance, will not seek after God ]{.class-2}[(Psa. 10:4)]{.class-0}[."]{.class-2}

"[Neither keepeth at home\...but gathereth unto him all nations, and heapeth unto him all people."]{.class-2}[ That's a reference to the white horse rider of Revelation 6:2 going ]{.class-0}["forth conquering, and to conquer"]{.class-2}[ and gaining ]{.class-0}["power\...over all kindreds, and tongues, and nations"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 13:7).]{.class-0}

"[Who enlargeth his desire as hell\..."]{.class-2}[; i.e, as Hell enlarges itself (see Isa. 5:14). ]{.class-0}["\...and is as death, and cannot be satisfied."]{.class-2}[ Proverbs 27:20 says, ]{.class-0}["Hell and destruction are never full; so the eyes of man are never satisfied."]{.class-2}[ In Proverbs 30:15--16, ]{.class-0}["the grave"]{.class-2}[ is ]{.class-0}["never satisfied"]{.class-2}[ and never says ]{.class-0}["It is enough."]{.class-2}[ Whether you are talking about a literal graveyard or Hell itself, there is always room to accommodate one more (Ezek. 26:20). In that same way, the Antichrist wants to run the whole world, and he won't be satisfied until he controls everything and everyone.]{.class-0}

[Now when it comes to pride and an imperialistic Church-State, Mohammed and the Pope are "birds of a feather." Mohammed said that whoever obeys ]{.class-0}[Allah's messenger]{.class-1}[ (meaning ]{.class-0}[himself]{.class-1}[), obeys ]{.class-0}[Allah]{.class-1}[ (Sura 4:80). He said the earth was made for ]{.class-0}[him]{.class-1}[ and Allah (see the ]{.class-0}[Holy Hadith,]{.class-1}[ Bukhari, Vol. IV, No. 392), and that Allah made ]{.class-0}[him]{.class-1}[ the best of all men (Dr. Said, ]{.class-0}[Fiqh Al-Sira,]{.class-1}[ 7th ed., pg. 50; Dr. Bint Al-Shati, ]{.class-0}[Daughters of the Prophet,]{.class-1}[ pg. 14). My, what a nice humble fellow he was! Islam is an imperialistic religion spread by ]{.class-0}[fear of the sword]{.class-1}[ (Al-Halabi, ]{.class-0}[The Biography of the Prophet,]{.class-1}[ Vol. 3, pg. 397), not by preaching and evangelism, and claims it will someday rule the whole world (Sura 2:192--193, 9:29).]{.class-0}

[The only other religion that could give Islam any competition in this regard is ]{.class-0}[Roman Catholicism. ]{.class-1}[One should never forget that the Roman Catholic Church is a ]{.class-0}[political]{.class-1}[ entity called the "Vatican State." Its head claims to be the "Vicar of Christ": ]{.class-0}[Vicarius Filii Dei]{.class-1}[---the substitute Son of God. He likes to be called "Holy Father," the title Jesus Christ gave to God the Father (John 17:11). In Boniface VIII's ]{.class-0}[Unum Sanctum---]{.class-1}[a papal bull that has never been altered or rescinded by any Pope in any century since---the Pope declared that every human being on earth must obey him ]{.class-0}[as God]{.class-1}[ and that ]{.class-0}[literal weapons]{.class-1}[ can be used to kill anyone who disagrees with him.]{.class-0}

[Back to verse 5:]{.class-0}[ "But gathereth unto him all nations."]{.class-2}[ Now that verse is talking about what Satan does through the Antichrist: one-world bank, one-world cash, one-world religion, etc., all under the control of the Devil incarnate. He's ]{.class-0}["the god of this world"]{.class-2}[ (2 Cor. 4:4). But come over to Zephaniah 3:8 and look at who's responsible for the Devil doing that and why it is allowed to happen.]{.class-0}

"[Therefore wait ye upon me, saith the LORD, until the day that I rise up to the prey: for MY determination ]{.class-2}[\[notice that the speaker is ]{.class-0}["the LORD"]{.class-2}[\] ]{.class-0}[is to GATHER THE NATIONS]{.class-2}[ \[that's what you just read in Hab. 3:5\]]{.class-0}[, that I may assemble ]{.class-2}[\[the United Nations Assembly---isn't that what it's called? Well, that's a pretty good prediction for ]{.class-0}[2,600 years before it shows up,]{.class-1}[ wouldn't you say? You won't find anything like that in the ]{.class-0}[Koran]{.class-1}[\] ]{.class-0}[the kingdoms, to POUR UPON THEM MINE INDIGNATION, even ALL MY FIERCE ANGER: for all the earth shall be DEVOURED WITH THE FIRE OF MY JEALOUSY."]{.class-2}

[So what you have in Habakkuk 3:5 is a situation like you will find in Job 1--2 and in the life of Joseph. God is allowing the Devil to do something to accomplish the ]{.class-0}[Lord's]{.class-1}[ will (see Gen. 50:20). So while ]{.class-0}["the god of this world"]{.class-2}[ may be the one who actually gathers the nations together in a UN, the author of the United Nations is the Lord Himself. He allows the Devil to do it with one purpose in mind: to kill the whole lot of them in one shot (see Matt. 13:30, 40--42).]{.class-0}

[Verse 6: ]{.class-0}["Shall not these ]{.class-2}[\[that's the ]{.class-0}["nations"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["people"]{.class-2}[ of vs. 5\]]{.class-0}[ take up a parable\...and a taunting proverb against him."]{.class-2}[ That ]{.class-0}["parable"]{.class-2}[ and that ]{.class-0}["proverb"]{.class-2}[ both show us plainly that the passage deals with a character beyond just a nondescript wicked man or Nebuchadnezzar (see Mic. 2:4 and the comments in that Commentary, and Isa. 14:4). What you are reading will be repeated by what's left of the earth's population after the Second Advent (see Isa. 14:16--21; Psa. 52:6).]{.class-0}

"[Woe to him that increaseth that which is not his!"]{.class-2}[ Well, the Devil told Christ in Luke 4:6, ]{.class-0}["All this power ]{.class-2}[\[i.e., ]{.class-0}["all the kingdoms of the world"]{.class-2}[---Luke 4:5\] ]{.class-0}[will I give thee, and the glory of them: for that is DELIVERED UNTO ME; and to whomsoever I will I give it."]{.class-2}[ Of course, Christ was going to wait and get it from His Father after the Resurrection: ]{.class-0}["All power is given unto me in heaven and in earth"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 28:18). Nevertheless, right now, Satan still runs this world by the permissive will of God (2 Cor. 4:4). But the day is coming when the Lord draws the line on him. At the Second Advent, the ]{.class-0}[real owner]{.class-1}[ of the whole shebang shows back up, and the Devil doesn't get it anymore.]{.class-0}

[The Second Coming of Jesus Christ is likened to ]{.class-0}["a thief in the night"]{.class-2}[ (1 Thess. 5:2; Matt. 24:42--44; 2 Pet. 3:10). When Christ comes back, He's going to ]{.class-0}[steal]{.class-1}[ the Devil's property. Of course, it doesn't really belong to Satan, but the old saying is: "Possession is nine-tenths of the law." So when the Lord comes, He comes to take the most valuable thing in the house.]{.class-0}

[Now what's the most ]{.class-0}[valuable thing]{.class-1}[ on this earth right now? Well, it's ]{.class-0}[you,]{.class-1}[ supposedly. Christians are the only thing that keeps this world from rotting to death (Matt. 5:13); you are the only thing that keeps God from blowing it away to a "fare thee well" (Gen. 19:22). When you're gone, out she goes.]{.class-0}

[As far as God is concerned, the most valuable thing on this earth is]{.class-0}[ the saved people in Christ, ]{.class-1}[and He's going to ]{.class-0}[take them out.]{.class-1}[ Now think about that when you try to lead a soul to Christ and you get turned down and become discouraged and think about quitting. Do you think the Lord is happy about you trying to get someone saved? Well, do you think the Devil is happy about that? What if someone came into your home tonight and stole your kids and you never saw them again; would you be kindly disposed toward him? Well, every time you lead a soul to Christ, do you know what you've done? You've stolen once of the Devil's kids (see Eph. 2:2--3). Do you think he's going to pat you on the back and say, ]{.class-0}["Well done, thou good and faithful servant"]{.class-2}[ (cf. Matt. 25:21, 23)?]{.class-0}

"[How long?]{.class-2}[ \[see the comments on Hab. 1:2\]]{.class-0}[ and to him that ladeth himself with thick clay!"]{.class-2}[ Here all the new "Bibles" change ]{.class-0}["thick clay"]{.class-2}[ to ]{.class-0}["pledges"]{.class-1}[ (]{.class-0}[RSV]{.class-1}[) or ]{.class-0}["loans"]{.class-1}[ (]{.class-0}[NASV]{.class-1}[); here the ]{.class-0}["New" King James Version]{.class-1}[ (which is NOT an edition of the ]{.class-0}[KJV]{.class-1}[) reads as the ]{.class-0}[Revised Standard Version]{.class-1}[ of the Liberal National Council of Churches and says ]{.class-0}["pledges."]{.class-1}[ Instead of running to the "original Hebrew," the expositors, translators, revisers, and commentators would have done better to go to the only infallible interpreter of Scripture (see 1 Cor. 2:13) and get His opinion on the matter (see Job 26:17 and the comments in that Commentary, and also Dan. 2:39--43).]{.class-0}

"[Shall they not rise up suddenly that shall bite thee, and awake that shall vex thee\..."]{.class-2}[ (vs. 7). There is somebody who goes up against the Antichrist along with the Lord (see Joel 2; 2 Thess. 1:7; Rev. 19:14). ]{.class-0}["\...and thou shalt be for booties unto them?"]{.class-2}[ Of course the ]{.class-0}["booties"]{.class-2}[ are not baby booties; it's another word for ]{.class-0}[plunder]{.class-1}[ or ]{.class-0}[loot,]{.class-1}[ like a pirate would use the term. When you come back with the Lord at the Second Advent, the whole world belongs to you (see 1 Cor. 3:21--23). That's your Millennial inheritance; you get it by resisting sin (1 Cor. 6:9--10) and suffering with Christ right now (2 Tim. 2:12).]{.class-0}

"[Because thou hast spoiled many nations, all the remnant of the people shall spoil thee; because of men's blood, and for the violence of the land, of the city, and of all that dwell therein"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 8). Now that is what happens at the Second Advent. When Christ returns, He takes the Antichrist and the False Prophet and throws them into ]{.class-0}["a lake of fire"]{.class-2}[ down by the land of Edom, and then He wipes out about]{.class-0}[ 200 million]{.class-1}[ United Nations "peace-keeping forces" (Rev. 19:19--21, 14:20). Any of the Gentile nations that are left are brought before Christ at Jerusalem and judged according to how they treated that Jew during the Tribulation (see Matt. 25:31--46). That's called "The Judgment of the Nations" (see Joel 3:2, 12).]{.class-0}

[Look at the verse here in Habakkuk 2: ]{.class-0}["because of men's blood."]{.class-2}[ The context is the ]{.class-0}[Jew.]{.class-1}[ It's the men of ]{.class-0}["the land"]{.class-2}[---the land of ]{.class-0}[Palestine]{.class-1}[ (Hab. 1:6)---and ]{.class-0}["the city"]{.class-2}[---the city of ]{.class-0}[Jerusalem.]{.class-1}[ That Jew is persecuted during the Tribulation by the Antichrist and is told to flee the land (Matt. 24:15--20).]{.class-0}

[When Christ judges the Gentile nations at the end of the Tribulation, He judges them according to whether they helped that Jew out when he was being persecuted or whether they ignored him. Look at Matthew 25:35--36 and notice that the criteria for the judgment are all ]{.class-0}[works;]{.class-1}[ there's no ]{.class-0}[faith]{.class-1}[ or ]{.class-0}[grace]{.class-1}[ or Book of Life in the entire chapter. This isn't the Great White Throne as the Postmillennialists and Amillennialists teach. This is ]{.class-0}["the throne of his glory"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 25:31): that's the earthly throne of David at Jerusalem (Jer. 14:21; 1 Sam. 2:8; Matt. 19:28).]{.class-0}

[Note further that it's not just any "good works"; it's works ]{.class-0}["done\...unto one of the least of these my brethren"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 25:40). That's a reference to Christ's earthly brethren---the Jews. At that judgment, if a Gentile didn't treat that Jew right and help him out, he's thrown into Hell, but if he did give that Jew help when he needed it, he will ]{.class-0}["inherit the kingdom"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 25:34). He gets the ]{.class-0}["spoil"]{.class-2}[ (Hab. 2:8); i.e., all the stuff the Antichrist and his crew left behind when they got thrown into the lake of fire.]{.class-0}

9 Woe to him that coveteth an evil covetousness to his house, that he may set his nest on high, that he may be delivered from the power of evil!

10 Thou hast consulted shame to thy house by cutting off many people, and hast sinned against thy soul.

11 For the stone shall cry out of the wall, and the beam out of the timber shall answer it.

12 Woe to him that buildeth a town with blood, and stablisheth a city by iniquity!

13 Behold, is it not of the LORD of hosts that the people shall labour in the very fire, and the people shall weary themselves for very vanity?

14 For the earth shall be filled with the knowledge of the glory of the LORD, as the waters cover the sea.

[Verses 9--10 describe a rich fellow "feathering his nest" at the expense of a bunch of poor people so that when the hard times come, he will survive and they won't. That's what Bush and Obama did for the Wall Street boys and the big banks (2007--2010); they gave nearly ]{.class-0}[three]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-0}[trillion dollars of your tax money]{.class-1}[ to them so they could stay afloat while over ]{.class-0}[twenty million Americans]{.class-1}[ lost their jobs and their homes. I've seen statistics that show that if that money had been given to every male over eighteen, every one of them could have gotten 27,000 bucks in cash. That money could have been used to pay off bad debts and mortgages and keep up folks' insurance payments, but instead it was given to the ones that broke the nation. Why? So they could come out good on top while ]{.class-0}[you]{.class-1}[ "get the shaft."]{.class-0}

[Have you ever noticed in the Bible the continual contrast between the rich and the poor? I would say a good 80% of the time, the implication is that the rich are ]{.class-0}[damned]{.class-1}[ and the poor are ]{.class-0}[saved]{.class-1}[ (see the rich man and Lazarus, for example). Now there is nothing particularly righteous about being poor; you will find just as many poor people living like hell as you do rich folks. And there is nothing particularly sinful about being rich. Abraham was rich, Job was rich, and they were both saved men. God was the one who made David and Solomon rich. R. G. LeTourneau was a saved man, and so was J. C. Penny. William Borden and C. T. Studd were both missionaries who came from rich families. But you will see that rich/poor thing all through the Scriptures. Why?]{.class-0}

[Well, the day is coming when the only people ]{.class-0}[who have the money]{.class-1}[ will be the ]{.class-0}[lost.]{.class-1}[ When the Antichrist takes over in the Tribulation, no one can ]{.class-0}["buy or sell"]{.class-2}[ unless he worships Satan, and if he does, he's damned (Rev. 13:15--18, 14:9--11).]{.class-0}

[If you want to see what's going on in Habakkuk 2:9--10, turn over to James and look at chapter 5, verses 1--7. The rich are being judged for hoarding wealth ]{.class-0}["for the LAST DAYS"]{.class-2}[ (James 5:3). James 5:7 says, ]{.class-0}["Be patient therefore, brethren, unto the COMING OF THE LORD."]{.class-2}[ The context is the time just before the Second Advent. How did the rich get rich in that passage? By defrauding the ]{.class-0}["labourers"]{.class-2}[ (James 5:4) and condemning and killing ]{.class-0}["the just"]{.class-2}[ (James 5:6). That's what's happening here in Habakkuk.]{.class-0}

[Notice that the ]{.class-0}["covetousness" ]{.class-2}[of verse 9 is not just wanting the ordinary things warned about in the commandment (see Exod. 20:17); it is wanting something ]{.class-0}["evil."]{.class-2}[ It's like the greed involved in pornography, which is now a multi-]{.class-0}[billion]{.class-1}[ dollar industry. In Pensacola, we see the thing every Memorial Day. The queers congregate at the beach and hold weekend sex orgies. "But just think of the money they bring to our community. And think of all the hotels and motels that will be filled up, and how the money will help us get over the hurricanes. I mean, we need the money to help the local economy, don't we? So let's just make them feel at home while they're here." That's the business.]{.class-0}

[Verse 12 says: ]{.class-0}["Woe to him that\...stablisheth a city in iniquity."]{.class-2}[ A city that does that kind of thing brings the judgment of God down on it. All those businesses downtown and at the beaches invite the sex perverts into town ]{.class-0}[to get their money,]{.class-1}[ and God sends in four hurricanes and just tears the whole place up, and then He lets an oil rig explode out in the Gulf and ruins the fishing industry and the restaurants.]{.class-0}

[Verse 11: ]{.class-0}["For the stone shall cry out of the wall, and the beam out of the timber shall answer it."]{.class-2}[ In other words, the rich men of verses 9--10 have built their houses to protect themselves on the blood of those they've ]{.class-0}[cheated]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[killed]{.class-1}[, and one day, God's going to let the stones and beams of the houses testify against them (cf. Gen. 4:10; Josh. 24:27; Job 31:38; Luke 19:40; James 5:3--4).]{.class-0}

"[Woe to him that buildeth a town with blood, and stablisheth a city by iniquity"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 12). Now the chapter that tells you what the Antichrist and his crowd does when they take over is Job 24.]{.class-0}

"[Some remove the landmarks; they violently take away flocks, and feed thereof. They drive away the ass of the fatherless, they take the widow's ox for a pledge. They turn the needy out of the way: the poor of the earth hide themselves together"]{.class-2}[ (Job 24:2--4). In the Tribulation, they'll have to, or they'll be killed (Rev. 13:15).]{.class-0}

Job 24:5--11 gives the condition of the Jews who won't follow the Antichrist. They are forced to go into the desert to get their food (Job 24:5), and that is where the Lord feeds them just as He fed the children of Israel in the wilderness (see the comments on Rev. 12:14 and Mic. 7:14--15 in those respective Commentaries). They and their children become slave labor for the Antichrist (Job 24:6, 9, 11 cf. James 5:4). They're starved to death (Job 24:10--11 cf. Rev. 6:5--6). They are naked and exposed to the elements (Job 24:7--8).

"[Men groan from out of the city"]{.class-2}[ (Job 24:12). And at last we come to the ]{.class-0}["city"]{.class-2}[ of Habakkuk 2:12. Notice that it is characterized by ]{.class-0}[rebellion]{.class-1}[ (Job 24:13), ]{.class-0}[murder]{.class-1}[ (Job 24:14), ]{.class-0}[robbery ]{.class-1}[(Job 24:14, 16), and ]{.class-0}[adultery]{.class-1}[ (Job 24:15). The first city was set up by a ]{.class-0}[murderer ]{.class-1}[who was a type of the ]{.class-0}[Antichrist]{.class-1}[ (Cain---Gen. 4:17). When Nimrod began his ]{.class-0}[rebellion]{.class-1}[ against God, he did it from a ]{.class-0}[city]{.class-1}[ he built (Gen. 10:9--10, 11:1--9). You haven't gone two more chapters in Genesis before God says that the men of a certain city ]{.class-0}["were wicked and sinners before the LORD exceedingly"]{.class-2}[ (Gen. 13:13). The city in the Tribulation that is built on blood and sin is Babylon the Great (Rev. 17--18).]{.class-0}

[Now if there is any doubt in your mind as to the setting of Job 24, look at verse 17 in that chapter: ]{.class-0}["For the morning."]{.class-2}[ There's the Second Advent exactly as you find it in 2 Peter 1:16--19; Matthew 13:43; and Malachi 4:1--2. In verse 17, you run into ]{.class-0}["the shadow of death,"]{.class-2}[ which is a real shadow that kills anything under it (Job 24:17--19 cf. Isa. 10:33--34, 27:23--29). That shadow sits over the kingdom of the Antichrist (Rev. 16:10) until the Lord comes back and shines forth (Isa. 9:2, 60:1--2 cf. Matt. 16:28--17:2). And in Job 24:20--24, you have the end of the Antichrist (note ]{.class-0}["a little while"]{.class-2}[ in Job 24:24 and then go back and see the comments on Hab. 2:3--4 in regards to Heb. 10:37--38).]{.class-0}

[So in the very first book of the Bible ever written (see the introduction to the book of Job in that Commentary), ]{.class-0}[you have the Antichrist, the Tribulation, and the Second Advent laid out where you can get it. ]{.class-1}[But that's in the Old Testament, and who in America spends any time in the ]{.class-0}[Old Testament]{.class-1}[ any more? If you want information on the Second Coming of Jesus Christ, you will find more in the Old Testament than in the entire book of Revelation.]{.class-0}

"[Behold, is it not of the LORD of hosts that the people shall labour in the very fire, and the people shall weary themselves for very vanity?"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 13). Habakkuk says these people are working in the fire and wearing themselves out over nothing, and he asks, ]{.class-0}["is it not of the LORD\...?"]{.class-2}[ Of course it is. Job recognized that: ]{.class-0}["shall we receive good at the hand of God, and shall we not receive evil?"]{.class-2}[ (Job 2:10). Who do you think brought the Flood---the Devil?]{.class-0}

[People get all upset at the suggestion that God caused the tsunamis in Indonesia and Japan and Hurricane Katrina in New Orleans and the earthquake in Haiti. Well, who do you think caused it? You say, "Mother Nature." Oh, get off it! There is no "Mother Nature," not unless you are a pagan Greek or Roman ("Rhea"---"Mother Nature"---was the wife of "Chronos" \["Saturn"\]---"Father Time"). It is God Almighty, the Creator of heaven and earth, who controls the weather and the seas and the earth. The question is not ]{.class-0}[did]{.class-1}[ God cause it; it is ]{.class-0}[why]{.class-1}[ did God cause it. The answer here in Habakkuk 2 is ]{.class-0}[judgment on man's sin.]{.class-1}

"[For the earth shall be filled with the knowledge of the glory of the LORD, as the waters cover the sea"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 14). The Lord sends all the judgments of the Tribulation to get rid of everyone who rejects His Son (2 Thess. 1:8, 2:11--12) so He can set Him on the throne of David as the Supreme Dictator of the whole earth (Psa. 2; Zech. 14). Verse 14 is a direct reference to the Millennial reign of Christ when the earth experiences a ]{.class-0}["regeneration"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 19:28). Look at Isaiah 11:6--10.]{.class-0}

"[The wolf also shall dwell with the lamb, and the leopard shall lie down with the kid; and the calf and the young lion and the fatling together; and a little child shall lead them. And the cow and the bear shall feed; their young ones shall lie down together: and the lion shall eat straw like the ox. And the sucking child shall play on the hole of the asp, and the weaned child shall put his hand on the cockatrice' den. They shall not hurt nor destroy in all my holy mountain: FOR THE EARTH SHALL BE FULL OF THE KNOWLEDGE OF THE LORD, AS THE WATERS COVER THE SEA. And in that day there shall be a root of Jesse, which shall stand for an ensign of the people; to it shall the Gentiles seek: and his rest shall be glorious."]{.class-2}

[The Second Advent is through that Old Testament from start to finish. That is why the Jewish disciples were expecting Christ to set up His kingdom in Acts 1--4. The only Scriptures they had were the Old Testament; there was no New Testament around yet. The message of the Old Testament is Christ coming to set up His kingdom, and they believed the Old Testament. That's why Peter quotes Joel in Acts 2 and why he talks about ]{.class-0}["the times of refreshing"]{.class-2}[ and the ]{.class-0}["restitution of all things"]{.class-2}[ (Acts 3:19, 21).]{.class-0}

[Well, when Christ told those disciples that it was not for them ]{.class-0}["to know the times or seasons"]{.class-2}[ when the Father would ]{.class-0}["restore again the kingdom to Israel"]{.class-2}[ (Acts 1:6--7), the Catholics say, "See there, those poor, carnal Jews thought it was a ]{.class-0}[literal]{.class-1}[ kingdom, and ]{.class-0}[it's not. ]{.class-1}[God is all through with them, and the kingdom is a ]{.class-0}[spiritual kingdom ]{.class-1}[with ]{.class-0}[us]{.class-1}[ in charge." ]{.class-0}[You can bury them.]{.class-1}

[There are ]{.class-0}[two kingdoms]{.class-1}[ in that Bible (see our work ]{.class-0}[The Sure Word of Prophecy]{.class-1}[), and one of them has been temporarily set aside until the King comes back. The problem is that the UN, the NEA, the ACLU, the Catholic Church, and the "Islamic Nation" want to "bring in the kingdom" ]{.class-0}[without the King.]{.class-1}[ God has a surprise for them: He is setting them all up ]{.class-0}[for the kill,]{.class-1}[ and not a one of them knows what's coming up because they've ]{.class-0}[dumped the Book.]{.class-1}

15 Woe unto him that giveth his neighbour drink, that puttest thy bottle to him, and makest him drunken also, that thou mayest look on their nakedness!

16 Thou art filled with shame for glory: drink thou also, and let thy foreskin be uncovered: the cup of the LORD'S right hand shall be turned unto thee, and shameful spewing shall be on thy glory.

17 For the violence of Lebanon shall cover thee, and the spoil of beasts, which made them afraid, because of men's blood, and for the violence of the land, of the city, and of all that dwell therein.

We have already commented on verse 15 under verse 6.

"[Thou art filled with shame for glory: drink thou also, and let thy foreskin be uncovered: the cup of the LORD'S right hand shall be turned unto thee, and shameful spewing shall be on thy glory"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 16). What a way to talk. He says, "I'm going to shame you by uncovering the part that should be circumcised; I'm going to take all the stuff you glory in and puke all over it." My, what a sweet loving Saviour who just loves all the little children!]{.class-0}

[He told those Laodiceans He would spue them out of His mouth (Rev. 3:16). He told the Jewish priests something worse than that. Do you know what He told those apostate Levites in Malachi 2? He said, ]{.class-0}["Behold, I will corrupt your seed, and spread dung upon your faces]{.class-2}[" (Mal. 2:3). You won't find ]{.class-0}[that]{.class-1}[ "updated" in the modern translations. That took place ]{.class-0}[literally]{.class-1}[ in Sobibor, Auschwitz, Treblinka, Buchenwald, Dachau, etc. The guards took those Jews to the outhouses and stuffed their faces in their own filth and made them eat it. Some of them went mad, and of course, all of them got sick. That went on by the thousands, brother.]{.class-0}

[Whoever wrote that Book knew what was going to happen 2,000 years ]{.class-0}[before]{.class-1}[ it took place and recorded it in a ]{.class-0}[King James ]{.class-1}[Bible 330 years ahead of time. You throw out that Book or ignore it at your own peril.]{.class-0}

[For ]{.class-0}["the cup of the LORD'S right hand,"]{.class-2}[ read Jeremiah 25 and also see the comments on Matthew 26:39 and John 18:11 in those respective Commentaries. That ]{.class-0}["cup"]{.class-2}[ is the cup of God's wrath on sin.]{.class-0}

"[For the violence of Lebanon shall cover thee"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 17). In other words, what went wrong there will go wrong with you (see Zech. 11:1--3). ]{.class-0}["And the spoil of beasts, which made them afraid"]{.class-2}[: all the cattle are taken away. ]{.class-0}["Because of men's blood, and for the violence of the land, of the city, and of all that dwell therein"]{.class-2}[ (see our remarks under vss. 9--12).]{.class-0}

18 What profiteth the graven image that the maker thereof hath graven it; the molten image, and a teacher of lies, that the maker of his work trusteth therein, to make dumb idols?

19 Woe unto him that saith to the wood, Awake; to the dumb stone, Arise, it shall teach! Behold, it is laid over with gold and silver, and there is no breath at all in the midst of it.

20 But the LORD is in his holy temple: let all the earth keep silence before him.

[Verse 18 mentions two kinds of idols: a ]{.class-0}["graven image"]{.class-2}[ and a ]{.class-0}["molten image."]{.class-2}[ Those are defined for you in Exodus 32 when Aaron made the golden calf. He had the children of Israel break off their golden earrings that symbolized their slavery in Egypt, and he melted them down (]{.class-0}["molten"]{.class-2}[). From there, the liquid metal was cooled and formed with an engraving tool (Exod. 32:4). That was the process of idol making.]{.class-0}

"[Idols"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["images"]{.class-2}[ first show up in Genesis 31:19. There are none before the Flood. The teaching of the Liberals and the Modernists is ]{.class-0}[religious evolution:]{.class-1}[ religion progresses from animism to pantheism or polytheism to monotheism; the logical end is Deism, agnosticism, and finally atheism (Marx). That is not the case at all. Before the Flood, everyone was a ]{.class-0}[monotheist]{.class-1}[. After the Flood, people started making statues of some things that were there before the Flood that were called ]{.class-0}["gods"]{.class-2}[ (see the comments on Psa. 82 in Vol. II of that Commentary). The statue represents a god (see Gen. 31:30).]{.class-0}

[That's why God forbade idolatry. He said, ]{.class-0}["Thou shalt have no other gods before me. Thou shalt not make unto thee any graven image\...Thou shalt not bow down thyself to them, nor serve them: for I the LORD thy God am a jealous God"]{.class-2}[ (Exod. 20:3--5). You are not to worship any other gods or the images that represent them. Why? Because God is the only God who is ]{.class-0}[non-created, eternal,]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[self-sustaining.]{.class-1}[ He says, ]{.class-0}["I am the LORD]{.class-2}[ \[that's Jehovah, ]{.class-0}["I AM THAT I AM"]{.class-2}[\]]{.class-0}[: that is my name: and my glory will I not give to another, neither my praise to graven images"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 42:8). God condemns idolatry more than any other sin in the Bible outside of the sins of the tongue.]{.class-0}

[In the Bible, those images are connected with ]{.class-0}[priests]{.class-1}[ called "fathers." Look at Judges 17. In verse 3 of that chapter, there is ]{.class-0}["a graven image and a molten image"]{.class-2}[ just as you find it in Habakkuk 2:18. In verse 10, a guy hires a priest younger than he is and calls him ]{.class-0}["father"]{.class-2}[ and has him minister in a house full of images (Judg. 17:4--5). That's the first time that kind of thing shows up in the Bible. ]{.class-0}[There isn't a Pope in the world who knows that; ]{.class-1}[there isn't a Cardinal, a Bishop, a Monk, or a Nun who ever lived who read enough Bible to be able to tell you that.]{.class-0}

[No Christian has any business going down the ]{.class-0}[via dolorosa]{.class-1}[ ("the way of sorrow") and kneeling before a statue and crossing himself and saying "Hail Mary, full of grapes, blessed be the fruit of the loom" and praying to "Blessed Mary, Blessed Joseph, and Blessed John the Baptist." God condemns a nation that practices that and says He will visit ]{.class-0}["the iniquity of the fathers upon the children unto the third and fourth generation"]{.class-2}[ (Exod. 20:5); that's the problem with Spain, Italy, and every nation in Central and South America.]{.class-0}

[Back to Habakkuk 2: ]{.class-0}["What profiteth the graven image that the maker thereof hath graven it; the molten image, and a teacher of lies, that the maker of his work trusteth therein, to make dumb idols? Woe unto him that saith to the wood, Awake; to the dumb stone, Arise, it shall teach! Behold, it is laid over with gold and silver, and there is no breath at all in the midst of it"]{.class-2}[ (vss. 18--19).]{.class-0}

[The point is what good does the idol do for the fellow that made it, and the answer is ]{.class-0}[absolutely nothing.]{.class-1}[ It's a cold, dead piece of metal or wood. When the children of Israel were worshipping the brazen serpent God had Moses make in the wilderness (see Num. 21:8--9), Hezekiah broke it to pieces and called it ]{.class-0}["Nehushtan"]{.class-2}[---"just a piece of brass" (2 Kings 18:4); there was nothing special about it apart from what God told Moses to do with it.]{.class-0}

[God told Isaiah: ]{.class-0}["They that make a graven image are all of them vanity ]{.class-2}[\[i.e, there's nothing to them\]]{.class-0}[; and their delectable things shall not profit; and they are their own witnesses; they see not, nor know; that they may be ashamed. Who hath formed a god, or molten a graven image ]{.class-2}[\[there's Hab. 2:17\]]{.class-0}[ that is PROFITABLE FOR NOTHING? Behold, all his fellows shall be ashamed: and the workmen\...they shall be ashamed together. The smith with the tongs both worketh in the coals, and fashioneth it with hammers, and worketh it with the strength of his arms: yea, he is hungry, and his strength faileth: he drinketh no water, and is faint]{.class-2}[ \[the guy faints while making the idol because he doesn't have enough money on which to live and get some food; yet he's got to worship the thing he's making even though it can't help him any. You can see that kind of thing all over India\]]{.class-0}[. The carpenter stretcheth out his rule; he marketh it out with a line; he fitteth it with planes, and he marketh it out with the compass, and maketh it after the figure of a man ]{.class-2}[\[like Nebuchadnezzar did in Dan. 3\]]{.class-0}[, according to the beauty of a man; that it may remain in the house. He heweth him down cedars, and taketh the cypress and the oak, which he strengtheneth for himself among the trees of the forest: he planteth an ash, and the rain doth nourish it. Then shall it be for a man to burn: for he will take thereof, and warm himself; yea, he kindleth it, and baketh bread; yea, he maketh a god, and worshippeth it; he maketh it a graven image, and falleth down thereto. He burneth part thereof in the fire; with part thereof he eateth flesh; he roasteth roast, and is satisfied: yea, he warmeth himself, and saith, Aha, I am warm, I have seen the fire: And the residue thereof he maketh a god, even his graven image: he falleth down unto it, and worshippeth it, and prayeth unto it, and saith, Deliver me; for thou art my god"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 44:9--17).]{.class-0}

[Now the modern version of this is found in Tampico or Guadalajara in Mexico, or in Madrid or Barcelona in Spain. The Roman Catholic Priest parades down the street with a big pole in front of him. At the top of that pole is a "UFO": a golden ]{.class-0}[circle]{.class-1}[ with sun rays coming out of it. That's called a "monstrance." In the middle of that thing is a white ]{.class-0}[wafer]{.class-1}[ called "the host." As the Priest marches that thing down the street, all the people fall on their faces. Why? Because that big "cookie" is supposed to be ]{.class-0}[Jesus Christ.]{.class-1}[ The Priest calls it ]{.class-0}["my Lord and my God"]{.class-2}[ (a perversion of Thomas' confession in John 20:28). That's a god you can eat and, I suppose, get rid of when you go to the toilet.]{.class-0}

"[And none considereth in his heart, neither is there knowledge nor understanding to say, I have burned part of it in the fire; yea, also I have baked bread upon the coals thereof; I have roasted flesh, and eaten it: and shall I make the residue thereof an abomination? shall I fall down to the stock of a tree?]{.class-2}[ \[yes, they do it all the time in Brazil, Mexico, Italy, Honduras, Spain, Chile, Guatemala, etc.\]]{.class-0}[ He feedeth on ashes ]{.class-2}[\[he eats his god\]]{.class-0}[: a deceived heart hath turned him aside, that he cannot deliver his soul ]{.class-2}[\[they'll go to Hell with their wafer in their mouths\]]{.class-0}[, nor say, Is there not a lie in my right hand?"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 44:19--20).]{.class-0}

[All the idol is is a hunk of wood or a lump of metal. David wrote: ]{.class-0}["The idols of the heathen are silver and gold, the work of men's hands. They have mouths, but they speak not; eyes have they, but they see not; They have ears, but they hear not; neither is there any breath in their mouths"]{.class-2}[ (Psa. 135:15--17). Habakkuk said they were ]{.class-0}["dumb stone"]{.class-2}[ with ]{.class-0}["no breath"]{.class-2}[---they aren't alive.]{.class-0}

[But that isn't all: ]{.class-0}["They that make them are like unto them: so is every one that trusteth in them"]{.class-2}[ (Psa. 135:18). So everyone that makes an image and everyone that prays to one is just as dumb, stupid, and lifeless spiritually as the image in which they trust: that's everyone from a Pope to a parishioner.]{.class-0}

Now that isn't just an Old Testament thing. Turn over to the New Testament and pick up 1 John 5:20--21.

"[And we know that the Son of God is come, and hath given us an understanding, that we may know him that is true, and we are in him that is true, even in his Son Jesus Christ. This is the true God, and eternal life"]{.class-2}[ (1 John 5:20).]{.class-0}

[Look at that. ]{.class-0}["The true God"]{.class-2}[ in that verse is not a reference to Jehovah or the Father (see John 17:3); it's a reference to ]{.class-0}[Jesus Christ.]{.class-1}[ That's enough to mess up ]{.class-0}[every]{.class-1}[ Jew, Moslem, Hindu, Buddhist, Shinto, Sikh, animist, and ]{.class-0}[atheist]{.class-1}[ in the UN; there are probably more than three billion people on this earth that don't believe that. I dare say you have Baptists, Methodists, Presbyterians, Episcopalians, and Catholics who never got it---despite quoting the Apostles' Creed or some other confession of faith every Sunday.]{.class-0}

[Do you know what's so significant about that verse? Well, Hebrews 1:2--3 says that Jesus Christ, God's Son, is ]{.class-0}["the express image"]{.class-2}[ of God. A true Christian is an]{.class-0}[ image worshipper;]{.class-1}[ he just doesn't worship any ]{.class-0}[image]{.class-1}[ made by ]{.class-0}[man]{.class-1}[. We worship an ]{.class-0}[image]{.class-1}[ made by ]{.class-0}[God]{.class-1}[ Himself, and the ]{.class-0}[image]{.class-1}[ does more than just ]{.class-0}[represent]{.class-1}[ the God we worship---He ]{.class-0}[is]{.class-1}[ the God we worship.]{.class-0}

[Now go back to 1 John 5 and look at verse 21.]{.class-0}[ "Little children, keep yourselves from idols. Amen."]{.class-2}[ So even though a Christian worships an image, he is not an idolater because the image he worships ]{.class-0}["is the true God."]{.class-2}

[So just like in the Old Testament, the Christian is warned about idols. That would be statues (Roman Catholics), pictures (Greek Orthodox), and pagan gods (Hindus, Buddhists, etc.). It also includes ]{.class-0}[spiritual]{.class-1}[ idolatry (see Ezek. 14:4). Colossians 3:5 says, ]{.class-0}["covetousness\...is idolatry."]{.class-2}[ That's where Fundamental, Bible-believing Christians, especially pastors, get in trouble: their ministry becomes their god. Colossians 3:2 says, ]{.class-0}["Set your affection ]{.class-2}[\[not your ]{.class-0}["mind"]{.class-1}[ like it reads in all the corrupt translations. God wants your ]{.class-0}[heart,]{.class-1}[ not your ]{.class-0}[head;]{.class-1}[ He wants what you ]{.class-0}[love]{.class-1}[\] ]{.class-0}[on things above]{.class-2}[ \[the verse before it said ]{.class-0}["where Christ sitteth on the right hand of God"]{.class-2}[\]]{.class-0}[, not on things on the earth."]{.class-2}[ That last part would include your church building, your congregation, your offering, your attendance, your school, your enrollment, and even your ministry (what you do for Christ). Your ]{.class-0}[service for]{.class-1}[ God and your ]{.class-0}[love of]{.class-1}[ God are two separate things, and you need to keep them in the right order; otherwise, you are guilty of idolatry.]{.class-0}

[Martin Luther called the human heart "an idol factory." The only solution to the problem is given in Colossians 3:5---]{.class-0}["Mortify therefore your members which are upon the earth,"]{.class-2}[ and then the ]{.class-0}["members"]{.class-2}[ are listed, the last one being ]{.class-0}["covetousness, which is idolatry."]{.class-2}[ You have to ]{.class-0}["die daily"]{.class-2}[ to self (1 Cor. 15:31) and ]{.class-0}["reckon ye also yourselves to be dead indeed unto sin, but alive unto God through Jesus Christ"]{.class-2}[ (Rom. 6:11).]{.class-0}

[Now when it comes to this business of statues and ]{.class-0}[pictures]{.class-1}[ (see Num. 33:52; Ezek. 8:10), what the Christian needs to keep in mind is this: ]{.class-0}["an idol is nothing in the world"]{.class-2}[ (1 Cor. 8:4). You can have a picture hanging on your wall without it being an idol. You ladies can have figurines on your "knick-knack" shelf without them being idols.]{.class-0}

[Some of the over-zealous brethren complain about me drawing ]{.class-0}[pictures]{.class-1}[ of Jesus Christ in my chalk talks. Well, now, come on, nobody in the congregation is ]{.class-0}[praying]{.class-1}[ to that picture like a Greek Orthodox would do to an ]{.class-0}[icon]{.class-1}[. When my Sunday School teachers put up a flannelgraph picture for the kiddies, none of the boys and girls are bowing down to it like the Catholics would before the "Sacred Heart of Jesus" or a crucifix. Those pictures aren't "aids to worship"; ]{.class-0}[they're sermon or lesson illustrations.]{.class-1}

All the anti-Christmas people won't put up a Christmas tree because it's pagan and Catholic; you know, Jeremiah 10 and all that business. Listen, I know as much about the pagan origins of Christmas as anyone, but let's just face it, no Bible-believing Christian who puts up a Christmas tree (and there are plenty of them that do) is praying to that tree or bowing down to it like a god. It's only a pretty decoration under which to put the presents for the kiddies.

[Brethren, the problem isn't in the statue or the picture; the problem is with ]{.class-0}[what's done]{.class-1}[ with those things and the ]{.class-0}[associations]{.class-1}[ connected with them. The command is: ]{.class-0}["Thou shalt not bow down thyself to them, nor serve them"]{.class-2}[ (Exod. 20:5). The Jews made images of cherubim, palm trees, lilies, etc. in the Temple; but they didn't worship them.]{.class-0}

[The problem comes when the image (whether two-dimensional or three-dimensional) is ]{.class-0}[worshipped]{.class-1}[ or ]{.class-0}[venerated]{.class-1}[ as the "god" it represents. When that happens, a ]{.class-0}[spirit]{.class-1}[ becomes connected to that image, and it's an ]{.class-0}[evil]{.class-1}[ spirit (see 1 Cor. 10:20 and note the context in vss. 14--21 is idolatry in connection with the ]{.class-0}[Lord's Supper,]{.class-1}[ like in the ]{.class-0}[Roman Catholic Mass]{.class-1}[).]{.class-0}

[We had a missionary to Africa here one time at the Bible Baptist Church. On his display table he had some stuff he had brought from the mission field, like nearly all missionaries do. One of those things was a little, old, fat, wooden black statue. It wasn't particularly artistic; in fact, it was an ugly looking thing about eight to ten inches high. I went by there and looked at that thing: I picked it up and looked at it and put it down. There was something that was not right about that ugly little statue. You say, "Ruckman, you're just superstitious." No, I'm telling you there was something ]{.class-0}[wrong]{.class-1}[ with that particular piece of wood.]{.class-0}

[Brethren, if the Devil wants worship (and he does; see Isa. 14:13--14; Luke 4:6--7), the only way he can get it is to get people to ]{.class-0}[bow down to him]{.class-1}[ like they would to God. The way he does that is to get spirits under his authority (Eph. 6:12; Mark 4:4, 15) to become connected with ]{.class-0}[objects]{.class-1}[ to which people ]{.class-0}[bow down]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[pray]{.class-1}[. If you want to see what I mean, look at 1 Samuel 5.]{.class-0}

"[And the Philistines took the ark of God, and brought it from Ebenezer unto Ashdod. When the Philistines took the ark of God, they brought it into the house of Dagon, and set it by Dagon"]{.class-2}[ (1 Sam. 5:1--2).]{.class-0}

The Philistines took the Ark of the Covenant into the temple of Dagon the fish god and set it beside their idol as a trophy of their victory over the Israelites, the idea being that Dagon was mightier than Jehovah. But do you know what they found the next morning when they came in there? The statue of Dagon was flat on his face before the Ark just like he was bowing down to it.

You say, "It was just a coincidence." Really? Look at what happened the next morning after they set Dagon back on his pedestal.

"[And when they arose early on the morrow morning, behold, Dagon was fallen upon his face to the ground before the ark of the LORD; and the head of Dagon and both the palms of his hands were cut off upon the threshold; only the stump of Dagon was left to him"]{.class-2}[ (1 Sam. 5:4).]{.class-0}

The statue was broken off in the middle. The bottom part was laying there before the Ark while the top part was on the threshold of the door like old Dagon was trying to get out of there quick. There was a spirit connected to that idol that couldn't stand to be in the presence of the God of the Ark.

[Now remember, the ]{.class-0}[doctrinal context]{.class-1}[ of Habakkuk 2 has been the Second Advent of Jesus Christ. So what do verses 18--19 have to do with that? Well, in the middle of Daniel's Seventieth Week (Dan. 9:27 cf. Matt. 24:15), the Antichrist comes into the rebuilt Jewish temple and sits down in the Holy of Holies and proclaims himself God (2 Thess. 2:4). Then the False Prophet comes in and sets up an ]{.class-0}[image]{.class-1}[ of the Antichrist and demands the whole world worship it, just like Nebuchadnezzar did with his image in Daniel 3.]{.class-0}

"[And deceiveth them that dwell on the earth by the means of those miracles which he had power to do in the sight of the beast; saying to them that dwell on the earth, that they should make an image to the beast, which had the wound by a sword, and did live. And he had power to give life unto the image of the beast, that the image of the beast should both speak, and cause that as many as would not worship the image of the beast should be killed"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 13:14--15).]{.class-0}

The story of Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego is going to take place again, but this time, those who don't bow will have their heads cut off (Rev. 20:4). After three-and-a-half years of that (Rev. 13:4--7), the One who was with Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego in the midst of the fire (Dan. 3:25---changed in all the new "Bibles" to hide the prophetic application) comes back to bust up the party.

"[How are they brought into desolation, as in a moment! they are utterly consumed with terrors. As a dream when one awaketh; so, O Lord, when thou awakest, thou shalt despise their image"]{.class-2}[ (Psa. 73:19--20).]{.class-0}

[At the Battle of Armageddon, Christ takes the Antichrist and the False Prophet and ]{.class-0}[pitches them into a lake of fire. ]{.class-1}[Then he goes to Jerusalem and enters the temple, just like the Antichrist did three-and-a-half years earlier, and sits down on the ark of the covenant and puts one arm on one cherub and one arm on the other cherub. He looks over at that image that the False Prophet set up and He despises it. He gets up and tears it to pieces. I wouldn't be surprised if the thing tries to run away just like Dagon did.]{.class-0}

[Verse 20:]{.class-0}[ "But the LORD is in his holy temple: let all the earth keep silence before him."]{.class-2}[ At the Episcopal church in Topeka, Kansas where I grew up, the Rector (and that's a good name for him because he sure did make a wreck of things) would stand up every Sunday morning in his black clerical robes and raise his hands over the congregation and drone in a monotone singsong voice: ]{.class-0}["The LORD is in his holy temple: let all the earth keep silence before him."]{.class-2}[ ]{.class-0}[That verse has nothing to do with a call to worship in some church building. ]{.class-1}[That's a literal temple that the Lord's literally in. Right now, that's a reference to the one ]{.class-0}[upstairs]{.class-1}[ (see Isa. 6:1--4 and note the Tribulation context in vss. 11--13), but at the Advent, that's a reference to the Millennial Temple described in Ezekiel 40--46. The last verse of the book of Ezekiel tells you that the name of Jerusalem during the Millennium will be ]{.class-0}[Jehovah-shammah,]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-0}["The LORD is there"]{.class-2}[ (Ezek. 48:35). That's because Jesus Christ is right there in the Temple.]{.class-0}

[Zechariah 2:12--13 says]{.class-0}[, "And the LORD shall inherit Judah his portion in the holy land ]{.class-2}[\[the Millennial inheritance of Jesus Christ as "King of the Jews]{.class-0}["]{.class-2}[\]]{.class-0}[, and shall choose Jerusalem again. Be silent, O all flesh, before the LORD]{.class-2}[ \[like you just read in Hab. 2:20\]]{.class-0}[: for he is raised up out of his holy habitation."]{.class-2}

[]{#section-0_1_ca47dd77fcefdc30_b684.xhtml}

CHAPTER THREE

3:1 A prayer of Habakkuk the prophet upon Shigionoth.

2 O LORD, I have heard thy speech, and was afraid: O LORD, revive thy work in the midst of the years, in the midst of the years make known; in wrath remember mercy.

[Verse 1 matches the openings of some of the Psalms (e.g., see the superscripts to Psa. 86, 90). Look at the end of verse 19: ]{.class-0}["To the chief singer on my stringed instruments"]{.class-2}[; throughout the Psalms you read ]{.class-0}["To the chief Musician."]{.class-2}[ The song here was to be played to harp music and other ]{.class-0}["stringed instruments"]{.class-2}[ (violins, psalteries, dulcimers, etc.). What you are about to get is the same phenomena as you find in the Psalms: a whole chapter of prophecy on the Second Advent couched in the form of a prayer and a song. Because of that, nearly all the expositors miss the import of the passage because they only view it as poetry. If you want to see the whole thing illustrated, read the account recorded in 2 Kings 3:15--19.]{.class-0}

[The ]{.class-0}["Shigionoth"]{.class-2}[ of verse 1 is a variation of the word ]{.class-0}["Shiggaion"]{.class-2}[ (see the superscript to Psa. 7). The word has to do with a musical note or a tune (see our introduction to the Psalms in Vol. I of that Commentary).]{.class-0}

[Verse 2: ]{.class-0}["O LORD, I have heard thy speech, and was afraid."]{.class-2}[ That is a good place to start a prayer. Real prayer should be based on the Scriptures and a proper attitude toward them. Do you want God to look favorably on your prayers? Then ]{.class-0}[tremble]{.class-1}[ at what He says in His Book.]{.class-0}

"[But to this man will I look, even to him that is poor and of a contrite spirit, and trembleth at my word" ]{.class-2}[(Isa. 66:2). ]{.class-0}[In 61 years in the ministry, I have never known one professor or faculty member at any major Christian College, University, or Seminary who trembled at God's words.]{.class-1}

[When Josiah ]{.class-0}["heard the word of the book of the law,"]{.class-2}[ he got scared and ]{.class-0}["rent his clothes"]{.class-2}[ and wept, and because of that, the Lord showed him mercy (2 Kings 22:11, 18). That Book says, ]{.class-0}["The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom"]{.class-2}[ (Prov. 9:10).]{.class-0}
