"[O LORD, revive thy work."]{.class-2}[ Now there is where we get the idea of "revival." Psalm 85:6 says, ]{.class-0}["Wilt thou not revive us again: that thy people may rejoice in thee?"]{.class-2}[ That's where we get the song: "Revive us again; fill each heart with thy love. May each soul be rekindled with fire from above."]{.class-0}

[To ]{.class-0}[revive]{.class-1}[ something, of course, means to bring new ]{.class-0}[life]{.class-1}[ to it (see 2 Kings 13:21; Rom. 14:9). The term is not only used in a physical sense, but also in a spiritual sense. Revival in that latter sense is an Old Testament idea; it is never used that way in the New Testament. It is never used in the New Testament in the sense of backslidden Christians getting right; that's a practical application of the Old Testament term to the New Testament Church.]{.class-0}

[Now there's nothing wrong with that. We talk about a Christian being "backslidden," but that is an Old Testament term applied to Israel (e.g., Jer. 3:8, 11, 8:5; Hos. 6:16). In the New Testament, it's "being out of fellowship with God," but we understand what's being said when we talk about ]{.class-0}[backsliding]{.class-1}[. In the same way, we understand the practical use of the word ]{.class-0}[revival.]{.class-1}[ Paul says, ]{.class-0}["Awake thou that sleepest, and arise from the dead, and Christ shall give thee light"]{.class-2}[ (Eph. 5:14). That wasn't written to a lost person; it was written to the saved. So it is perfectly all right to talk about a Christian having revival.]{.class-0}

In church work today, the word is used to describe a series of special meetings where an evangelist is called in to preach. The reality is that sometimes an evangelist has a revival in those meetings and sometimes he doesn't. In the twelve years I was in evangelistic work, I can't say that I preached more than three meetings that I would call "revivals": that was one every four years.

[Now, Billy Graham holds what is known as "evangelistic crusades"; that is, meetings with the intent of preaching the Gospel to get lost folks saved. Some churches hold "Bible conferences," meetings to give Christians a time of intensive Bible teaching and preaching. But real revivals ]{.class-0}[in the Bible sense]{.class-1}[ were held by men like Sam Jones, Bob Jones Sr., John and Charles Wesley, George Whitefield, Gipsy Smith, and R. A. Torrey: those were the real ]{.class-0}[revivalists]{.class-1}[. Those men would hold big union campaigns, and when certain things took place, then they would call the meeting a revival. I don't recall a "crusade" Billy Graham ever had that had these things in it. Those old time revivalists considered a meeting a revival when the following things took place:]{.class-0}

[1. ]{.class-0}[Apologies are made for unkind and hasty words.]{.class-1}[ If Christians begin to apologize to each other for talking nasty about each other, that's a sign of revival.]{.class-0}

[2. ]{.class-0}[The Holy Spirit dispels lukewarmness and a dead atmosphere.]{.class-1}[ When a church gets on fire instead of being lukewarm and pukey (see Rev. 3:16), there is revival.]{.class-0}

[3. ]{.class-0}[Personal testimonies become warm and natural.]{.class-1}[ That is, you can tell revival is taking place when a fellow gets up to give a word of testimony, and it's from his heart and in his own words.]{.class-0}

[I must admit that this is one of my "pet peeves." I have been to these College banquets where students are brought in and give testimonies to drum up enrollment for the school. The kids who do that have to ]{.class-0}[memorize]{.class-1}[ their testimonies, and it's just sickening, man. I just lose my grace altogether and begin doing foolish stuff just to get them to laugh.]{.class-0}

[One guy got up to give his testimony, and he didn't have the thing memorized, so he was reading it from notecards. Well, he had lost some of his notes, so he couldn't finish his testimony. Brother, if your testimony is so poor and rotten that you have to write the thing down to ]{.class-0}[remember]{.class-1}[ it, you've got problems. That kind of thing is stiff and contrived; a testimony should be natural.]{.class-0}

[4. ]{.class-0}[Christians are willing to face opposition and ridicule.]{.class-1}[ If they're not, they're not revived. Every Christian who ever did anything for God was opposed and ridiculed.]{.class-0}

[5. ]{.class-0}[Fasting and self-discipline start.]{.class-1}[ You discipline yourself and quit over-eating and over-sleeping and a number of other things.]{.class-0}

[6. ]{.class-0}[Christians begin to bring sinners to the meetings.]{.class-1}

Now all of that is considered a "revival." Notice that everything in that list is aimed at saved people, not the lost.

3 God came from Teman, and the Holy One from mount Paran. Selah. His glory covered the heavens, and the earth was full of his praise.

4 And his brightness was as the light; he had horns coming out of his hand: and there was the hiding of his power.

5 Before him went the pestilence, and burning coals went forth at his feet.

6 He stood, and measured the earth: he beheld, and drove asunder the nations; and the everlasting mountains were scattered, the perpetual hills did bow: his ways are everlasting.

7 I saw the tents of Cushan in affliction: and the curtains of the land of Midian did tremble.

[Notice that the whole passage is in the past tense:]{.class-0}[ "came," "covered," "was," "had," "went," "stood,"]{.class-2}[ etc. Because of that, the expositors and commentators put everything you read in chapter 3 in the past in Exodus, Numbers, and Joshua (see vs. 11). ]{.class-0}[But nothing in verse 3 took place in the wilderness wanderings]{.class-1}[. Look at the verse.]{.class-0}

"[God came from Teman, and the Holy One from mount Paran."]{.class-2}[ ]{.class-0}[He never did. ]{.class-1}[That's Esau's territory; the Edomites didn't let the children of Israel pass through their land (Num. 20:14--21). ]{.class-0}["His glory covered the heavens, and the earth was full of his praise."]{.class-2}[ Well, they weren't, but they will be (see comments on 2:14).]{.class-0}

[So what you have here in chapter 3 is future prophecy written in the ]{.class-0}[past tense]{.class-1}[. Look at Isaiah 53 and see that same thing there in regards to the First Coming of Christ.]{.class-0}

"[But he WAS wounded for our transgressions"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 53:5). ]{.class-0}[No He wasn't.]{.class-1}[ He hadn't even been born yet. Why does Isaiah say ]{.class-0}["he made his grave with the wicked"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 53:9) when He hadn't even showed up and wasn't around?]{.class-0}

[Well, it's like I told you in my comments on 2:3 (which see): with God there is no ]{.class-0}[time]{.class-1}[. ]{.class-0}["For thus saith the high and lofty One that inhabiteth eternity"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 57:15). He knows all history from start to finish and gives it to you in detail ]{.class-0}[before]{.class-1}[ it shows up. So when He writes, He doesn't care whether He writes in past, present, or future tense.]{.class-0}

[I get upset with the Lord about that. I hear these hillbillies singing about the Lord coming, and I say sarcastically, "Not today or tomorrow." Well, now, that's kind of a nasty way to talk to the Lord, but He says, ]{.class-0}["in such an hour as ye think not the Son of man cometh"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 24:44). Good, then; ]{.class-0}[surprise me!]{.class-1}[ I figure maybe I can trap Him in His words like that Syrophenician woman did in Mark 7:26--29!]{.class-0}

[Listen, I'm not worried about that. I know my heart's right on the matter. ]{.class-0}[I want to see Him come back.]{.class-1}[ If I've got my head a little screwed up about it, the Lord can take care of that later. Where it gets wrong is where you ]{.class-0}[don't]{.class-1}[ want Him to come back.]{.class-0}

Now what you have here in chapter 3 is all about the Second Advent, and verse 3 gives you part of the route Christ takes when He comes back. So we are going to start here in Habakkuk 3:3 and run the references and give you the stuff Scofield, Bullinger, and Darby (and even Larkin) missed. They missed it because every time a man messes with that Book, he loses something somewhere. Admittedly, Scofield and Larkin don't lose much, but they do lose a little (Scofield more than Larkin) because each of them messes with the Book somewhere and God closes the door on them somewhere.

[Now you know me,]{.class-0}[ I don't mess with it.]{.class-1}[ I don't mess with ]{.class-0}[any]{.class-1}[ of it. If it was completely lost to me and I couldn't get it, I would leave it that way because I have noticed that every time you mess with that Book, you miss something.]{.class-0}

[The reason these guys miss what I'm about to give you is because they pick up one verse and ignore all the other passages that describe what comes before it. They read, ]{.class-0}["this same Jesus, which is taken up from you into heaven, shall so come in like manner as ye have seen him go into heaven"]{.class-2}[ (Acts 1:11), and figure that since He left from the Mount of Olives (Acts 1:12) that He will come back there. Well, that's where He ]{.class-0}[lands]{.class-1}[ (Zech. 14:4), but He does some flying ]{.class-0}[before]{.class-1}[ that. Song of Solomon 2:8 says, ]{.class-0}["The voice of my beloved]{.class-2}[ \[a reference to Christ in type\]]{.class-0}[! behold, he cometh LEAPING UPON THE MOUNTAINS, SKIPPING UPON THE HILLS"]{.class-2}[ (see the comments in that Commentary), and in the Book, the Holy Spirit gives you the ]{.class-0}["mountains"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["hills"]{.class-2}[ by name. As we give the references, we refer you to the map at the end of the chapter for the locations.]{.class-0}

[All right, Deuteronomy 33:2---]{.class-0}["The LORD came from Sinai]{.class-2}[ \[that's in the Sinaitic Peninsula south of Edom\]]{.class-0}[, and rose up from Seir ]{.class-2}[\[that's a mountain in Edom---Deut. 2:1--4\]]{.class-0}[ unto them; he shined forth from mount Paran]{.class-2}[ \[just like you read it in Hab. 3:3\]]{.class-0}[, and he came with ten thousands of saints\...."]{.class-2}

[Did that happen in Exodus or Numbers? Nosiree! ]{.class-0}[Not a one of them showed up.]{.class-1}[ You say, "It was those Jews." ]{.class-0}[No it wasn't.]{.class-1}[ How do you know? Because the Holy Spirit told you in Jude who they were, but who would ]{.class-0}["search the scriptures"]{.class-2}[ (see John 5:39---changed in all the new "Bibles" so you wouldn't do it) and go over to ]{.class-0}[Jude]{.class-1}[ to find out what's in ]{.class-0}[Deuteronomy?]{.class-1}

"[And Enoch also, the seventh from Adam, prophesied of these, saying, Behold, the Lord cometh with ten thousands of his saints, To execute judgment upon all, and to convince all that are ungodly among them of all their ungodly deeds which they have ungodly committed, and of all their hard speeches which ungodly sinners have spoken against him"]{.class-2}[ (Jude 14--15).]{.class-0}

[That's Enoch prophesying of the Second Coming a thousand years before Moses wrote Deuteronomy 33:2.]{.class-1}[ It's in third-grade English, and nobody can get it. Cambridge misses it; Oxford (Scofield) misses it; Thomas Nelson misses it. That's how you know you have a wild Book in your hands. When a Ph.D. picks it up, ]{.class-0}[he can't read it.]{.class-1}

[Now look at that thing. Jude 14 is the direct cross-reference to Deuteronomy 33:2. When Enoch prophesied, ]{.class-0}[there weren't any Jews]{.class-1}[. That was before the Flood, and Abraham hadn't even been born yet. Moreover, nothing Enoch said had ]{.class-0}[anything]{.class-1}[ to do with what took place in Exodus, Leviticus, Numbers, or Deuteronomy.]{.class-0}

[Those ]{.class-0}["ten thousands of saints"]{.class-2}[ show up in Revelation 5:9--11, and they are ]{.class-0}["redeemed by the blood of the Lamb."]{.class-2}[ They come down with Christ in Revelation 19:11--14 and 2 Thessalonians 1:7--10. That's ]{.class-0}[you]{.class-1}[ if you are saved. These dumb Christians don't even know where they're going or how they're going to get there.]{.class-0}

[Now ain't that wild? Sometimes I look at that Book and think to myself, "Boy, if they only knew what is in there." There's enough in that Book to blow the universe away. You say, "Oh, Ruckman\...." Don't "oh, Ruckman" me. ]{.class-0}[I mean that literally.]{.class-1}[ Haven't you ever read 2 Peter 3:10--12? When God says blow, she's gonna ]{.class-0}[blow]{.class-1}[, boy (Dan. 7:9--10 cf. Rev. 20:11).]{.class-0}

[So someday the Lord's going to come back, and when He does, His saints---those who have been saved by grace through faith in this age---are going to come back with Him. When they do, they'll ]{.class-0}[ultimately]{.class-1}[ land on the Mount of Olives, but ]{.class-0}[before]{.class-1}[ they do, they follow the path the Ark of the Covenant took from Mount Sinai in Exodus 40 to the crossing of Jordan in Joshua 3--4. This path is found over and over again in the Scriptures. Look at Judges 5.]{.class-0}

"[LORD, when thou wentest out of Seir, when thou marchedst out of the field of Edom]{.class-2}[ \[that's just what you read in Deut. 33:2 and Hab. 3:3\]]{.class-0}[, the earth trembled, and the heavens dropped, the clouds also dropped water. The mountains melted from before the LORD, even that Sinai]{.class-2}[ \[that's Deut. 33:2 again\]]{.class-0}[ from before the LORD God of Israel"]{.class-2}[ (Judg. 5:4--5).]{.class-0}

[None of that took place in Exodus or Numbers. ]{.class-1}[Sinai didn't ]{.class-0}["melt"]{.class-2}[ in Exodus 19--20; there was no ]{.class-0}[earthquake]{.class-1}[ or ]{.class-0}[deluge]{.class-1}[ in Numbers 21--22. But look at the context of Judges 5. Verse 12 is the resurrection of Christ (cf. Eph. 4:8). Up shows ]{.class-0}["Megiddo"]{.class-2}[ in verse 19: that's Armageddon (Rev. 16:16; Zech. 12:11). Verse 20 says, ]{.class-0}["the stars in their courses fought against Sisera."]{.class-2}[ Well, no ]{.class-0}["stars"]{.class-2}[ fought against ]{.class-0}[anybody]{.class-1}[ in Judges 4. That's a reference to angels (Rev. 1:20; Job 38:7). So at the Second Advent, Christ returns with ]{.class-0}["his mighty angels"]{.class-2}[ to fight against the Antichrist (2 Thess. 1:7).]{.class-0}

[Judges 5 is a song prophesying the Second Advent of Christ just as you find it in Habakkuk 3. In Judges 5:10, ]{.class-1}[you have a bunch of judges riding in on ]{.class-0}["white asses."]{.class-2}[ Those ]{.class-0}["asses"]{.class-2}[ are mules that a King and his sons ride (see 1 Kings 1:3; 2 Sam. 13:19). In the New Testament, those ]{.class-0}["white asses"]{.class-2}[ are changed to ]{.class-0}["white horses."]{.class-2}

"[And I saw heaven opened, and behold a white horse; and he that sat upon him was called Faithful and True, and in righteousness he doth judge and make war\....And the armies which were in heaven followed him upon white horses, clothed in fine linen, white and clean"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 19:11, 14).]{.class-0}

[So you have ]{.class-0}["asses"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["horses"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["armies."]{.class-2}[ Song 6:13 says, ]{.class-0}["What will ye see in the Shulamite? As it were the company of two armies."]{.class-2}[ That's the Bride of Christ in type (see the comments on Song of Sol. 6:10 in that Commentary) returning with her Husband after the Marriage of the Lamb (Rev. 19:9). So at the Second Advent, you have ]{.class-0}["two armies"]{.class-2}[ returning with Christ: Old Testament saints on ]{.class-0}["white asses"]{.class-2}[ to bring back the Jewish kingdom with a Jewish King and New Testament saints on ]{.class-0}["white horses"]{.class-2}[ returning with their Bridegroom.]{.class-0}

[Now let's bring all of this together. At the Second Advent, Christ comes down where God gave the Ten Commandments, Mount Sinai. When He comes down on that mountain, He melts it. Then He "skips" and "leaps" (Song of Sol. 2:8) from mountain to mountain going northward through Edom: that's where ]{.class-0}["Teman," "mount Paran," "Seir," ]{.class-2}[and ]{.class-0}["Bozrah"]{.class-2}[ (see Isa. 63:1) are. ]{.class-0}[He goes through Edom]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-0}["by the king's high way"]{.class-2}[ where the children of Israel tried to travel and the Edomites wouldn't let them (Num. 20:17, 21:22).]{.class-0}

[Now why is that road called ]{.class-0}["the king's high way"]{.class-2}[? You never read about any King in the Bible traveling that way. There's no mention of any Jewish King or ]{.class-0}[any]{.class-1}[ Edomite King going by that route. Mohammed didn't take it, Saladin didn't go there, Richard the Lionhearted never went there. You can't locate ]{.class-0}[any]{.class-1}[ King in history who traveled that thing. So why is it called the King's Highway? ]{.class-0}[Because when the King of Kings comes back, He comes there first before He gets to the Mount of Olives.]{.class-1}

[When Christ goes through Edom, He does two things. First, He rescues a bunch of Jews holed up in Sela-Petra. Look at verses 3, 9, and 13 in Habakkuk 3 and notice that word ]{.class-0}["Selah."]{.class-2}[ According to all the scholars, that's supposed to be a musical rest. But whenever that word shows up in the Psalms, there is a reference to the Second Advent in the immediate context. ]{.class-0}["Selah"]{.class-2}[ is the name of a rock fortress in Edom (2 Kings 14:7) called "Sela-Petra" or just "Petra" today. The Jewish remnant flees there in the Tribulation (cf. Matt. 2416), and the Lord feeds them miraculously (Rev. 12:14) ]{.class-0}[just as He did with Israel in the wilderness]{.class-1}[ (Mic. 7:14).]{.class-0}

[The Lord rescues those Jews in Sela-Petra, and when He does, He sets the place on fire (Obad. 18) to the point ]{.class-0}[where it is still burning in the Millennium. ]{.class-1}[Look at Isaiah 34.]{.class-0}

"[For the sword shall be bathed in heaven: behold it shall come down upon Idumea]{.class-2}[ \[that's another name for Edom\]]{.class-0}[\...for the LORD hath a sacrifice in Bozrah]{.class-2}[ \[that's in Edom; see Isa. 63:1\]]{.class-0}[, and a great slaughter in the land of Idumea\....And the streams thereof shall be turned into pitch, and the dust thereof into brimstone, and the land thereof shall become burning pitch. It shall not be quenched night nor day; the smoke thereof shall go up for ever: from generation to generation it shall lie waste; none shall pass through it for ever and ever"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 34:5--6, 9--10).]{.class-0}

[The Lord comes across that place with a ]{.class-0}[flame thrower]{.class-1}[ and sets up ]{.class-0}[a lake of fire]{.class-1}[ (cf. Rev. 19:20; Isa. 66:23--24) that is ]{.class-0}[a type]{.class-1}[ of the permanent ]{.class-0}["lake of fire"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 20:10, 14--15). In the Millennium, if someone gets out of line and doesn't follow the Sermon on the Mount (Matt. 5:22, 29--30), he is cast alive ]{.class-0}[bodily]{.class-1}[ into that lake of fire down in Edom (Matt. 10:28).]{.class-0}

[So what happens when the Lord comes back is that He comes to Mount Sinai, then heads up the King's Highway like a freight train, burning everything ahead of Him. ]{.class-0}[Then he crosses Jordan right where He was baptized.]{.class-1}[ That's where ]{.class-0}[Joshua]{.class-1}[ crossed when he entered Canaan; that's where ]{.class-0}[David]{.class-1}[ crossed when he was running from Absalom (and where he returned); that's where ]{.class-0}[Elijah]{.class-1}[ crossed when he went up to Heaven by a whirlwind; and that's where ]{.class-0}[Elisha]{.class-1}[ crossed after he saw Elijah go up. On the east side of that spot is where ]{.class-0}[Moses]{.class-1}[ went up into Mount Pisgah and died and was buried there, and on the west side is the road leading from Jericho to Jerusalem. Christ takes that road and comes to the Mount of Olives and lands there. He dismounts His ]{.class-0}[white Arabian stallion]{.class-1}[ (that Mohammed tried to copy in his "Night Journey"), and when His foot hits the ground, it splits the mountain in two (Zech. 14:4).]{.class-0}

[He gets back on His horse and takes the ]{.class-0}[same route]{.class-1}[ He used at His Triumphal Entry, except this time the Eastern Gate of the city is closed. It was cemented shut by the Moslems in A.D. 1187 to stop Christ from fulfilling Ezekiel 44:1--2, and it will remain shut in order to fulfill Ezekiel 44:1--2. Kaiser Wilhelm wanted to enter Jerusalem by that gate, but World War I stopped him. The Grand Mufti of Jerusalem tried blasting the gate open to escape the British Expeditionary Force in 1917 when General Allenby took the city by air and fulfilled Isaiah 31:5. King Hussein of Jordan tried to use the gate to stop Jews from coming to the Wailing Wall, and the Six Day War of 1967 broke out and stopped him. And the last attempt was made in 1977 by the Israelis themselves, and that fell through too.]{.class-0}

[Now do you know what's ]{.class-0}[in front]{.class-1}[ of that gate? ]{.class-0}[It's a Moslem graveyard.]{.class-1}[ Those are the ones rioting over in Europe because somebody drew a cartoon of Mohammed, and the ones bombing your airplanes and trains and then claiming they are a "peaceful religion." Well, the Lord has a sense of humor, did you know that? He will take old Mohammed's white, flying Arabian stallion ]{.class-0}[and stomp all over those dead Moslem bodies]{.class-1}[ (my, what a tender, merciful, sweet, loving Saviour)! When He comes to that wall they cemented shut to keep Him out, He'll go ]{.class-0}[through]{.class-1}[ it without breaking a sweat (see what He did in John 20:19). Listen, people! if He could go through the bowels of the earth and back (Eph. 4:9--10), do you think He's going to be worried about a cement wall? He'll go through there, and it will be "Giddyap, horsey." ]{.class-0}[You'll be right behind Him if you're saved.]{.class-1}

[The Lord will come into Jerusalem and take the Antichrist's army that's attacking the city and smite them with a plague that is exactly like a nuclear bomb going off. ]{.class-0}["Their flesh shall consume away in their mouth"]{.class-2}[ (Zech. 14:12). It'll be just like you saw it in "Raiders of the Lost Ark," boy; except this time it's the ]{.class-0}[real thing]{.class-1}[ and not Hollywood special effects.]{.class-0}

[Now that's one of the routes of the Second Advent. The ]{.class-0}[other one ]{.class-1}[is down from the north through Damascus and Galilee to Armageddon. That's Revelation 19. The trouble is, the Scriptures don't tell you clearly which path the Lord takes ]{.class-0}[first]{.class-1}[. You read a passage like Isaiah 63:1--6 and compare it with Revelation 14:14--20, and it looks like He hits Armageddon first and then goes south to Sinai and comes back up to Jerusalem. But then you read something else like Judges 5 where Sinai comes before Megiddo (Judg. 5:5, 19), and you're not so sure anymore. So all I will say is that there are two routes that will be taken, and one comes before the other, and your guess about which one comes first is as good as mine.]{.class-0}

[Back to Habakkuk 3. ]{.class-0}["And his brightness was as the light"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 4). We touched on this when we cross-referenced you to Job 24:17 in the comments on Habakkuk 2:12. At the end of the Tribulation, God sends darkness on the kingdom of the Antichrist just as He did in the ninth plague on Egypt (Rev. 16:10 cf. Exod. 10:21--23). Joel 2:31 says, ]{.class-0}["The sun shall be turned into darkness, and the moon into blood, before the great and terrible day of the LORD come"]{.class-2}[ (see also Matt. 24:29; Rev. 6:12). That's the condition of the earth when the Lord comes back.]{.class-0}

[When the Lord comes back, He is described as ]{.class-0}["the day star"]{.class-2}[ arising (2 Pet. 1:19); notice that the context of that is the Transfiguration (2 Pet. 1:17--18), which was a picture of Christ coming in His kingdom (Matt. 16:28). When Christ was transfigured, ]{.class-0}["his raiment was white as the light"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 17:2), just like you read in Habakkuk 3:4.]{.class-0}

[Isaiah 60:1--3 says, ]{.class-0}["Arise, shine; for thy light is come, and the glory of the LORD is risen upon thee. For, behold, the darkness shall cover the earth, and gross darkness the people: but the LORD shall arise upon thee, and his glory shall be seen upon thee. And the Gentiles shall come to thy light, and kings to the brightness of thy rising."]{.class-2}

[Poor old Handel put Isaiah 60:1 on the First Advent in his "Messiah." Don't misunderstand me, the "Messiah" is one of the most Christ-exalting pieces of music you will ever hear, but when it came to ]{.class-0}["rightly dividing the word of truth,"]{.class-2}[ Handel didn't know which end was up (see the comments on Hag. 2:7). Now why did Handel do that? He did it because of Isaiah 9:1--2.]{.class-0}

"[Nevertheless the dimness shall not be such as was in her vexation, when at the first he lightly afflicted the land of Zebulun and the land of Naphtali, and afterward did more grievously afflict her by the way of the sea, beyond Jordan, in Galilee of the nations. The people that walked in darkness have seen a great light: they that dwell in the land of the shadow of death, upon them hath the light shined."]{.class-2}

[But don't be too hard on Handel; those two verses mess up ]{.class-0}[every Fundamental, Conservative, saved Bible corrector on the face of the earth. ]{.class-1}[Since ]{.class-0}[part]{.class-1}[ of the passage is quoted in Matthew 4:14--16 in reference to Christ's earthly ministry, the general assumption is that the verses were fulfilled at the First Advent. But 25 words of Isaiah 9:1--2 are ]{.class-0}[omitted]{.class-1}[ in Matthew 4:14--16; only the words used by Matthew were fulfilled at the First Coming, and then only in a ]{.class-0}[spiritual]{.class-1}[ sense. Christ will ]{.class-0}[literally]{.class-1}[ fulfill both Isaiah 9 and Isaiah 60 when ]{.class-0}["the Sun of righteousness"]{.class-2}[ arises ]{.class-0}["with healing in his wings"]{.class-2}[ (Mal. 4:2).]{.class-0}

[Back to Habakkuk 3:4---]{.class-0}["he had horns coming out of his hand: and there was the hiding of his power."]{.class-2}[ Now that part is figurative; God doesn't have literal horns, like a billy goat has, growing out of His hands. In the Bible, horns are a picture of political power. Both Satan and the Antichrist have ]{.class-0}["ten horns"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 12:3, 13:1), and those ]{.class-0}["horns"]{.class-2}[ are said to be ]{.class-0}["ten kings"]{.class-2}[ who ]{.class-0}["receive power as kings one hour with the beast"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 17:12). The fourth beast of Daniel 7 has ]{.class-0}["ten horns,"]{.class-2}[ and a ]{.class-0}["little horn"]{.class-2}[ having ]{.class-0}["eyes like the eyes of a man, and a mouth speaking great things"]{.class-2}[ comes up and supplants three of the horns (Dan. 7:8). That ]{.class-0}["little horn"]{.class-2}[ is the Antichrist (Dan. 7:20--21 cf. Rev. 13:5--7), the last political ruler before Christ returns. The "horns" of Christ's hand are ]{.class-0}["the saints of the most High"]{.class-2}[ who ]{.class-0}["possess the kingdom"]{.class-2}[ when the Lord gets rid of the Antichrist (Dan. 7:22). Christians are ]{.class-0}["kings and priests"]{.class-2}[ who ]{.class-0}["shall reign on the earth"]{.class-2}[ with Christ (Rev. 5:10); the Tribulation martyrs reign with Christ on ]{.class-0}["thrones"]{.class-2}[ and judge as political rulers (Rev. 20:4).]{.class-0}

[Verse 5:]{.class-0}[ "Before him went the pestilence ]{.class-2}[\[you just read about it in Zech. 14:12\]]{.class-0}[, and burning coals went forth at his feet]{.class-2}[ \[see the comments on Edom under vs. 3 and also see 2 Sam. 22:8--15\]]{.class-0}[."]{.class-2}

"[He stood, and measured the earth"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 6). The Lord's not measuring it to get the diameter and circumference and all that. ]{.class-0}[He's dividing it off for judgment.]{.class-1}[ Look at what David did in 2 Samuel 8:2.]{.class-0}

"[And he smote Moab, and measured them with a line, casting them down to the ground, even with two lines measured he to put to death, and with one full line to keep alive."]{.class-2}

[That's what Christ is doing here:]{.class-0}[ "he beheld, and drove asunder the nations."]{.class-2}[ At the Judgment of the Nations, Christ divides the remnant of the nations off into ]{.class-0}["sheep,"]{.class-2}[ which He gives eternal life, and the ]{.class-0}["goats,"]{.class-2}[ which He sends into eternal damnation (Matt. 25:31--46).]{.class-0}

[Again, note the emphasis God puts on ]{.class-0}["the nations."]{.class-2}[ The Old Testament is the account of how God deals with ]{.class-0}[nations, ]{.class-1}[and the New Testament tells you how He deals with ]{.class-0}[individuals.]{.class-1}[ Of course, there are exceptions to that, but that is the general rule. Those ]{.class-0}[nations]{.class-1}[ start out in Genesis 10, and God divided them into three groups. The Devil got them together under Nimrod in Genesis 11, and the Lord split them up again. Deuteronomy 32:8 says He ]{.class-0}["divided"]{.class-2}[ them off and set ]{.class-0}["bounds"]{.class-2}[ between them and numbered them ]{.class-0}["according to the number of the children of Israel"]{.class-2}[ (i.e., into twelve different groups). Paul says He did that so they would ]{.class-0}["seek the Lord"]{.class-2}[ (Acts 17:26--27). So the "United" Nations is a Satanic move to get everybody into one mongrel mass to damn the lot of them to Hell.]{.class-0}

[In Genesis 12, God called out one man and promised to bless ]{.class-0}[the nations]{.class-1}[ through him (Gen. 12:3 cf. 18:18). Those ]{.class-0}[nations]{.class-1}[ start fighting in Genesis 14, and they don't stop fighting until Revelation 20. In Eternity, they are still divided into twelve groups and have to be brought into the New Jerusalem for ]{.class-0}["healing"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 22:2).]{.class-0}

[Back to verse 6:]{.class-0}[ "and the everlasting mountains were scattered, the perpetual hills did bow."]{.class-2}[ And of course that's a reference to what happens at the Second Advent (see the comments on vs. 2). Nahum 1:5 says, ]{.class-0}["The mountains quake at him, and the hills melt, and the earth is burned at his presence, yea, the world, and all that dwell therein."]{.class-2}

[The ]{.class-0}["mountains"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["hills"]{.class-2}[ are ]{.class-0}["everlasting"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["perpetual"]{.class-2}[ insomuch as they have been around as long as anyone can remember (Deut. 33:15; Gen. 49:26). But when the Lord comes back, He not only melts them, He levels them as well. Here's another passage Handel put on the First Advent because the verse before it was applied to John the Baptist (Isa. 40:3 cf. Mark 1:3). He didn't realize that in the Bible, a comma or a semicolon or a period can represent 2,000 years between Advents (Luke 4:17--19 cf. Isa. 61:1--2). Look at Isaiah 40:4--5.]{.class-0}

"[Every valley shall be exalted, and every mountain and hill shall be made low: and the crooked shall be made straight, and the rough places plain: And the glory of the LORD shall be revealed, and all flesh shall see it together: for the mouth of the LORD hath spoken it."]{.class-2}

[Verse 7:]{.class-0}[ "I saw the tents of Cushan in affliction: and the curtains of the land of Midian did tremble." "Cushan"]{.class-2}[ is an abbreviated form of ]{.class-0}["Chushanrishathaim," "the king of Mesopotamia"]{.class-2}[ in Judges 3:8, and the first Gentile to take over Israel in the book of Judges. He is associated here with ]{.class-0}["the land of Midian."]{.class-2}[ Midian was one of Abraham's sons by his concubine Keturah after Sarah died, and was sent ]{.class-0}[out]{.class-1}[ of the Promised Land just as Ishmael was (Gen. 25:1--6). His descendants settled among the Ishmaelites (Gen. 37:25, 28) and the Amalekites (Judg. 6:3). That land is south of the Dead Sea in northern Arabia, but in Judges 6:3, they are also associated with ]{.class-0}["the children of the east."]{.class-2}[ That's Mesopotamia from where Chushanrishathaim was. So ]{.class-0}["the land of Midian"]{.class-2}[ today would include Saudi Arabia, Jordan, and Iraq. ]{.class-0}[Those are all 100% Moslem.]{.class-1}

Of course, the Midianites were Israel's enemies (see Num. 22:6--7, 25:17--18, 32:2--8; Judg. 6:2--6, 7:24--25). Now this is important in regards to the Second Coming because a list of Israel's enemies is given in Psalm 83; they are likened to the Midianites Gideon defeated in Judges 7 and to Sisera in Judge 4-5 (Psa. 83:9--11). Both of those accounts are pictures of the Second Advent (see Zech. 12:4), and Sisera is a type of the Antichrist (see the comments on vss. 2 and 13).

"[For, lo, thine enemies make a tumult: and they that hate thee have lifted up the head. They have taken crafty counsel against thy people and consulted against thy hidden ones. They have said, Come, and let us cut them off from being a nation; that the name of Israel may be no more in remembrance ]{.class-2}[\[genocide---see the documented accounts in chaps. 11--15 of our work ]{.class-0}[Israel: A Deadly Piece of Dirt]{.class-1}[\]]{.class-0}[. For they have consulted together with one consent: they are confederate against thee"]{.class-2}[ (Psa. 83:1--5).]{.class-0}

[Now look at that last verse: ]{.class-0}["they are confederate against thee."]{.class-2}[ Who are those nations that are against Israel in that passage? ]{.class-0}["Edom," "the Ishmaelites," "Moab,"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["the Hagarenes"]{.class-2}[ which are a tribe of Ishmaelites named after Ishmael's mother Hagar (Psa. 83:6); ]{.class-0}["Gebal"]{.class-2}[ (that's in Lebanon), ]{.class-0}["Ammon," "Amalek," "the Philistines,"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["Tyre"]{.class-2}[ (Psa. 83:7); and ]{.class-0}["Assur"]{.class-2}[ (Assyria---Psa. 83:8). The modern day countries represented by those peoples are ]{.class-0}[Transjordan]{.class-1}[ (Edom, Moab, Ammon, Amalek), ]{.class-0}[Saudi Arabia ]{.class-1}[(Ishmaelites and Hagarenes), ]{.class-0}[Lebanon]{.class-1}[ (Gebal and Tyre), the so-called ]{.class-0}["Gaza Strip"]{.class-1}[ (]{.class-0}[the Philistines]{.class-1}[), and ]{.class-0}[Iraq]{.class-1}[ (the Assyrians). In 1945, six Arab States joined together against Israel in the ]{.class-0}[Arab League]{.class-1}[. Those six States were ]{.class-0}[Egypt]{.class-1}[ (Ishmael's mother Hagar was an Egyptian), ]{.class-0}[Iraq, Lebanon, Syria]{.class-1}[ (which would include the Midianites of Judg. 6--7), ]{.class-0}[Saudi Arabia,]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[Transjordan.]{.class-1}[ Since then, all of those nations, along with Moslems in the Gaza Strip, have attacked Israel and said ]{.class-0}[thirteen]{.class-1}[ times that their goal is to drive the nation into the Mediterranean Sea. Asaph, the author of Psalm 83, laid all of that out for you over 3,000 years ahead of time. Whoever "Asaph" was, he had more sense than the President of the United States.]{.class-0}

8 Was the LORD displeased against the rivers? was thine anger against the rivers? was thy wrath against the sea, that thou didst ride upon thine horses and thy chariots of salvation?

9 Thy bow was made quite naked, according to the oaths of the tribes, even thy word. Selah. Thou didst cleave the earth with rivers.

10 The mountains saw thee, and they trembled: the overflowing of the water passed by: the deep uttered his voice, and lifted up his hands on high.

11 The sun and moon stood still in their habitation: at the light of thine arrows they went, and at the shining of thy glittering spear.

[Notice the references to God riding through bodies of water with horses and chariots here in verse 8 and again in verse 15. The picture is Israel passing through ]{.class-0}[the Red Sea]{.class-1}[ under Moses (Isa. 63:11--13) and, by extension, crossing the Jordan River under Joshua (cf. Josh. 1:5), but look at the context of Isaiah 63---]{.class-0}[it is the Second Coming]{.class-1}[ (Isa. 63:1--6).]{.class-0}

[We already know that when the Lord comes back, He comes back with His armies on supernatural horses (Rev. 19:11--14 cf. 2 Kings 2:11--12). But when He does, He passes through a body of water that would make the Atlantic and the Pacific Oceans combined look less than a drop in a bucket. In Genesis 1, the Lord divided the waters that surrounded the earth by a firmament: He says He ]{.class-0}["divided the waters which were under the firmament from the waters which were above the firmament"]{.class-2}[ (Gen. 1:7).]{.class-0}

[That ]{.class-0}["firmament"]{.class-2}[ is more than just the atmosphere between the oceans and the clouds. Genesis 1:14--17 says God set the sun, moon, and stars in that firmament, so Psalm 148:4 talks about ]{.class-0}["waters\...above the HEAVENS"]{.class-2}[---plural. Revelation 4:6 says that before the throne of God in Heaven there is ]{.class-0}["a sea of glass"]{.class-2}[; Job 38:30 says ]{.class-0}["the face of the deep is frozen."]{.class-2}[ So right now, between you and your Saviour, there is a body of water out past the solar system, the galaxies, the star clusters, and the quasars; and that body of water is]{.class-0}[ frozen at the top at absolute zero]{.class-1}[ (see the comments on Song of Sol. 4:8 in that Commentary), where all molecular motion stops. When Christ comes back, He passes through that thing at the Rapture to lead His people out of "Egypt" (the world) like Moses, and He leads them back through it at the Advent to conquer the land like ]{.class-0}[Joshua]{.class-1}[. So if you're a Christian, there are two things you should learn to do before you get to Heaven because you're going to use them up there: you should learn to ]{.class-0}[swim]{.class-1}[, and you should learn to ]{.class-0}[ride a horse!]{.class-1}

[Now this stuff is so far over the heads of the astronomers and astronauts at NASA and Cape Canaveral that they don't even know what's going on: they're just a bunch of dumb bunnies when it comes to this stuff. When Christ went back to Heaven, He passed through that ]{.class-0}["deep"]{.class-2}[ (Job 38:30) ]{.class-0}["BY]{.class-2}[ \[not "with"\] ]{.class-0}[his own blood"]{.class-2}[ (Heb. 9:12) and stained that water into a ]{.class-0}["Red Sea"]{.class-1}[ (not a "Sea of Reeds" like you will find in just about any modern Bible map on the Exodus that you pick up). At the Rapture, Christ calls you out (see John 10:2--3 and comments in that Commentary) in an "exodus" (the word ]{.class-0}[exodus]{.class-1}[ means a "calling out") because the Church (Gk., ]{.class-0}[ekklesia]{.class-1}[) is a "called-out assembly."]{.class-0}

[As you go up through that "Red Sea," you are pursued by "Pharaoh," the King of "Egypt" (a type of the Devil, ]{.class-0}["the god of this world"]{.class-2}[---see Ezek. 32:31 and note the context in vss. 23--30). At the top of that "frozen deep," there is a ]{.class-0}["door"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 4:1; John 10:2--3) through which Christ leads you. When Satan tries to come in behind you, that door slams shut and makes a racket: that's ]{.class-0}["The deep uttereth his voice, and lifted up his hands on high"]{.class-2}[ in verse 10 of Habakkuk 3.]{.class-0}

[How do you know that what I'm saying is right? Look at verse 11: ]{.class-0}["The sun and moon stood still in their habitation."]{.class-2}[ ]{.class-0}[You are dealing with upstairs over your head.]{.class-1}

[Habakkuk 3:9---]{.class-0}["Thy bow was made quite naked,"]{.class-2}[ the idea being that the bow is taken out of its casing or wrapping (like the shield being uncovered in Isa. 22:6). Now normally the word of God is likened to a sword (Heb. 4:12; Eph. 6:17; Rev. 19:15), but here it is likened to a bow shooting out ]{.class-0}[arrows]{.class-1}[ (note ]{.class-0}["even thy word"]{.class-2}[ in the verse). That's because as Christ rides along, He's letting out a war cry, and when He does, lightning comes down before Him and destroys the armies of the Antichrist. Look at 2 Samuel 22:14--15.]{.class-0}

"[The LORD thundereth from heaven, and the most High uttered his voice. And he SENT OUT ARROWS, and scattered them; LIGHTNING, and discomfited them."]{.class-2}

[Verse 9:]{.class-0}[ "according to the oaths of the tribes, even thy word." "The oaths of the tribes"]{.class-2}[ that is not the tribes of Israel making an oath to the Lord; it's the Lord swearing an oath by Himself (see Gal. 3:16--17; Heb. 6:13--16) to the twelve tribes. Note that plural---]{.class-0}["tribes."]{.class-2}[ When God made an oath to Abraham to give him everything from the Nile to the Euphrates (Gen. 15:18--21), that oath was to all twelve tribes (see Psa. 105:6--11; Num. 24:5--9). No "Palestinian," Moslem, Greek Orthodox, Roman Catholic, or "Christian" has ]{.class-0}[any]{.class-1}[ right to ]{.class-0}[any]{.class-1}[ part of the Promised Land.]{.class-0}

"[Thou didst cleave the earth with rivers. The mountains saw thee, and they trembled: the overflowing of the water passed by" ]{.class-2}[(vss. 9--10). A description of what happens in the Tribulation. One of the signs of Christ's coming is ]{.class-0}["earthquakes, in divers places"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 24:7).]{.class-0}

"[And I beheld, when he had opened the sixth seal, and, lo, there was a great earthquake; and the sun became black as sackcloth of hair, and the moon became as blood ]{.class-2}[\[see comments on vs. 4\]]{.class-0}[\...and every mountain and island were moved out of their places. And the kings of the earth, and the great men, and the rich men, and the chief captains, and the mighty men, and every bondman, and every free man, hid themselves in the dens and in the rocks of the mountains. And said to the mountains and rocks, Fall on us and hide us from the face of him that sitteth on the throne, and from the wrath of the Lamb: For the great day of his wrath is come; and who shall be able to stand?"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 6:12--17; see also Rev. 11:13, 16:18--20).]{.class-0}

[Those ]{.class-0}["rivers"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["overflowing of the water"]{.class-2}[ are a flood in the desert where Israel flees to escape the Antichrist (see Rev. 12:14--16; Dan. 9:26; Jer. 47:2).]{.class-0}[ "The deep uttereth his voice, and lifteth up his hands"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 10). Well, ]{.class-0}["the deep"]{.class-2}[ is not a man with real hands and a mouth that can say things. That's a figure of speech known as an anthropomorphism---ascribing the characteristics of a man to a non-human thing. Now one of the rules of "hermeneutics" (interpretation of the Bible) is if a thing ]{.class-0}[can]{.class-1}[ be taken literally, it is ]{.class-0}[always]{.class-1}[ to be taken literally. If it ]{.class-0}[can't be taken literally]{.class-1}[, ]{.class-0}[then]{.class-1}[ it's figurative. The way unbelievers approach the Bible is always to take it ]{.class-0}[figuratively]{.class-1}[ and only occasionally take it ]{.class-0}[literally]{.class-1}[. That ain't the rule. The rule is it's ]{.class-0}[always literal]{.class-1}[ unless it's ]{.class-0}[impossible]{.class-1}[ to believe it literally, and here in verse 10 about ]{.class-0}["the deep,"]{.class-2}[ it's impossible to take it literally.]{.class-0}

[Billy Graham and the Pope don't believe in literal fire in Hell. Well, is it ]{.class-0}[possible]{.class-1}[ that it could be literal fire? Christ said it was (Matt. 5:22, 13:42, 25:41; Mark 9:43--48; Luke 16:23--24); John said it was (Rev. 14:10, 20:10, 14--15, 21:8). Well, if it can be, then it is.]{.class-0}

[Now you take Christ calling Himself a ]{.class-0}[shepherd]{.class-1}[ and you ]{.class-0}[sheep]{.class-1}[ (John 10)---well, now, do ]{.class-0}[you]{.class-1}[ go out in the field and eat grass? Do you have four legs. It's obviously figurative.]{.class-0}

[The thing you have to remember about figures of speech in the Bible is that the Bible defines its own figures. That's known as ]{.class-0}["Sola Scriptura,"]{.class-1}[ the Scriptures alone. It's Scripture with Scripture (1 Cor. 2:13), not the private interpretations of any Pope or church or scholar (2 Pet. 1:20).]{.class-0}

"[The sun and moon stood still in their habitation"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 11). That's obviously a reference to what's known as "Joshua's Long Day."]{.class-0}

"[Then spake Joshua to the LORD in the day when the LORD delivered up the Amorites before the children of Israel, and he said in the sight of Israel, Sun stand thou still upon Gibeon; and thou, Moon, in the valley of Ajalon. And the sun stood still, and the moon stayed\..."]{.class-2}[ (Josh. 10:12--13).]{.class-0}

[That's a great disputed passage today among Bible believers: whether the sun and moon actually stopped or didn't stop; you know, "geocentricity" (earth-centered) or "heliocentricity" (sun-centered). But the fact of the matter is, it doesn't make any difference; everything is from the ]{.class-0}[perspective of the observer.]{.class-1}[ Don't your newspapers still give you sun]{.class-0}[rise]{.class-1}[ and sun]{.class-0}[set]{.class-1}[? I think so.]{.class-0}

"[\...So the sun stood still in the midst of heaven, and hasted not to go down about a whole day]{.class-2}[ \[there's where the term "Long Day" comes from; that's about twelve extra hours of daylight\]]{.class-0}[. And there was no day like that before it or after it ]{.class-2}[\[i.e., when Joshua wrote this\]]{.class-0}[, that the LORD hearkened unto the voice of a man: for ]{.class-2}[\[mark it!\]]{.class-0}[ THE LORD FOUGHT FOR ISRAEL"]{.class-2}[ (Josh. 10:13--14).]{.class-0}

[Now you just read that ]{.class-0}["there was no day like that before it or after it,"]{.class-2}[ but there will be in the future. The Lord is going to do the same thing when the United Nations attacks Jerusalem.]{.class-0}

"[For I will gather all nations against Jerusalem to battle\...Then shall the LORD go forth, and fight against those nations, AS WHEN HE FOUGHT IN THE DAY OF BATTLE"]{.class-2}[ (Zech. 14:2--3).]{.class-0}

[That's a reference to what you just read in Joshua 10:14; the Lord's going to do at the Second Advent what He did in Joshua 10 (see Isa. 28:21). In fact, ]{.class-0}["Joshua"]{.class-2}[ is the Old Testament name for ]{.class-0}[Jesus.]{.class-1}

[If you want to learn about the future, learn about the past.]{.class-0}[ "The thing that hath been, it is that which shall be; and that which is done is that which shall be done: and there is no new thing under the sun"]{.class-2}[ (Eccl. 1:9).]{.class-0}

[The problem is, "the only thing men learn from history is that men never learn from history." If you want to learn the ]{.class-0}[past, present, and future,]{.class-1}[ get in the Book. Now you can learn about history from history books; you can learn about the present from newspapers and magazines; but if you want to learn about the ]{.class-0}[future]{.class-1}[ in relation to the other two, the ]{.class-0}[only place]{.class-1}[ you are going to get it is in the Book.]{.class-0}

"[At the light of thine arrows they went ]{.class-2}[\[see comments on vs. 9\]]{.class-0}[, and at the shining of thy glittering spear."]{.class-2}[ Here's another reference to the word of God as a weapon: a ]{.class-0}[spear.]{.class-1}

[Psalm 35:1--3 says, ]{.class-0}["Plead my cause, O LORD, with them that strive with me: FIGHT AGAINST THEM THAT FIGHT AGAINST ME]{.class-2}[ \[so the context is Josh. 10:14 and Zech. 14:5\]]{.class-0}[. Take hold of shield and buckler, and stand up for mine help. DRAW OUT ALSO THE SPEAR, and stop the way against them that persecute me: say unto my soul, I am thy salvation."]{.class-2}

[That's not ]{.class-0}[spiritual]{.class-1}[ salvation like you find it in the New Testament (see Rom. 10:13, for example). That's the ]{.class-0}[physical]{.class-1}[ salvation of a Jewish remnant as you read about in Joel 2:31--32.]{.class-0}

12 Thou didst march through the land in indignation, thou didst thresh the heathen in anger.

13 Thou wentest forth for the salvation of thy people, even for salvation with thine anointed; thou woundedst the head out of the house of the wicked, by discovering the foundation unto the neck. Selah.

14 Thou didst strike through with his staves the head of his villages: they came out as a whirlwind to scatter me: their rejoicing was as to devour the poor secretly.

15 Thou didst walk through the sea with thine horses, through the heap of great waters.

16 When I heard, my belly trembled; my lips quivered at the voice: rottenness entered into my bones, and I trembled in myself, that I might rest in the day of trouble: when he cometh up unto the people, he will invade them with his troops.

"[Thou didst march through the land in indignation"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 12). There's your "gentle Jesus, meek and mild." Right now, He's a Saviour who's longsuffering and merciful, but when He comes back, it's "Katy, bar the door." As the old expression goes, there's "no quarter given." You'd better not rouse Him up. He may sleep like a lamb, but when He gets up, He's going to be like a ]{.class-0}[lion]{.class-1}[ (see Amos 1:2; Psa. 78:65--66).]{.class-0}

"[Thou didst thresh the heathen in anger."]{.class-2}[ Threshing is part of how grain is separated from chaff to get the kernel; there's ]{.class-0}[threshing]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[winnowing]{.class-1}[ (Ruth 3:2; Isa. 30:24). The grain is taken to the top of flat mountain where the wind's blowing. That grain is spread out there over the threshing floor, and then it is run over with ]{.class-0}["a threshing instrument"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 28:27). That ]{.class-0}["threshing instrument"]{.class-2}[ is a big ]{.class-0}["cart wheel"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 28:27): it's a round sled pulled by oxen. The Law says, ]{.class-0}["Thou shalt not muzzle the ox when he treadeth out the corn"]{.class-2}[ (Deut. 25:4). What's happening there is a fellow is sitting on that round platform driving that ox pulling the sled. As the ox pulls that sled over the grain, it's crushed, and those Jews weren't to muzzle that ox so that if he got tired he could bend down and get a bite to eat because ]{.class-0}["the labourer is worthy of his hire"]{.class-2}[ (Luke 10:7).]{.class-0}

After that grain is crushed, some fellows come along on a windy day and take a "winnowing fan" (a thing like a big pitchfork with closed prongs) and pick up that crushed grain and throw it into the air. The wind blows away all the light chaff, and the heavier kernels fall back to the ground.

[Now the Second Coming of Christ is likened to ]{.class-0}[threshing]{.class-1}[ all through the Prophets. Christ takes the Gentile nations, and He grinds them to powder (Matt. 22:44), and they are ]{.class-0}["like the chaff of the summer threshing floors"]{.class-2}[ (Dan. 2:35; see also Isa. 21:9--10, 28:27--29, 41:15--16; Jer. 51:33; Mic. 4:11--13). That ]{.class-0}["threshing instrument"]{.class-2}[ is a "UFO" described in Ezekiel 1:4--28, 10:1, 8--17; and Revelation 14:14--19. It is a round platform with the Lord sitting on top. That thing is drawn by four cherubim (and one of the faces on those cherubim is ]{.class-0}["an ox"]{.class-2}[---Ezek. 1:10) with wheels between them. That thing goes across the Valley of Megiddo and stomps all over ]{.class-0}[200 million UN troops]{.class-1}[ till the blood comes up to the horses' bridles (Rev. 14:20).]{.class-0}

"[Thou wentest forth for the salvation of thy people]{.class-2}[ \[that would be the ]{.class-0}[Jews,]{.class-1}[ not Christians; see Exod. 19:5--6; Psa. 110:2--3; Joel 2:13; Zech. 12:7--8\]]{.class-0}[, even for salvation with thine anointed"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 13). That word ]{.class-0}["anointed"]{.class-2}[ is the Old Testament word for ]{.class-0}[Messiah]{.class-1}[ (Psa. 2:2; 2 Sam. 23:1; Isa. 61:1 cf. Dan. 9:25--26); the New Testament word is ]{.class-0}[Christ]{.class-1}[ (John 1:41). Before his fall, Satan was a messiah (Ezek. 28:14), so ]{.class-0}["the Lord's Christ"]{.class-2}[ (Luke 2:25; Acts 4:26) is to be distinguished from the Devil's christ (1 John 2:18).]{.class-0}

"[Thou woundest the head out of the house of the wicked, by discovering the foundation unto the neck."]{.class-2}[ And here all the expositors, commentators, and Bible revisers go to pieces again. The ]{.class-0}[New Living Translation]{.class-1}[ (which took the place of Kenneth Taylor's ]{.class-0}[Living Bible]{.class-1}[) translates the verse as ]{.class-0}["You crushed the heads]{.class-1}[ (plural)]{.class-0}[ of the wicked"]{.class-1}[ so you won't associate ]{.class-0}["the wicked"]{.class-1}[ with ]{.class-0}[one, specific man]{.class-1}[ (see comments on 2:4). The "Nutty Idiot's Version" (the ]{.class-0}[NIV]{.class-1}[) is worse than that; it says ]{.class-0}["You crushed the leader of the land of wickedness"]{.class-1}[ so you will ]{.class-0}[lose]{.class-1}[ the cross-reference to Genesis 3:15. If you want "the ]{.class-0}[truth,]{.class-1}[ the ]{.class-0}[whole]{.class-1}[ truth, and ]{.class-0}[nothing but]{.class-1}[ the truth," the only place you are going to get it is in a ]{.class-0}[King James]{.class-1}[ Bible.]{.class-0}

[The very first Messianic prophecy in the Bible (Gen. 3:15) says: ]{.class-0}["And I will put enmity between thee and the woman, and between thy seed and her seed; IT SHALL BRUISE THY HEAD, and thou shalt bruise his heel."]{.class-2}[ As any Bible student knows, that's a prophecy about ]{.class-0}["the Lord's Christ"]{.class-2}[ and the Devil's christ. The Serpent bruising Christ's heel is the Devil having Christ crucified. Because of that, every commentator you pick up will say ]{.class-0}[that Christ bruised the Serpent's head at Calvary. ]{.class-1}[The top fifty Christian Colleges, Universities, and Seminaries are all just ]{.class-0}[as blind as a bat.]{.class-1}

[More than 25 years ]{.class-0}[after]{.class-1}[ Calvary, Paul told you that the wounding of Satan's head was still future.]{.class-0}[ "And the God of peace SHALL bruise Satan under your feet shortly"]{.class-2}[ (Rom. 16:20). Romans was written around A.D. 60, 27 years after the Crucifixion. Of course, Paul said ]{.class-0}["shortly,"]{.class-2}[ and it still hasn't happened yet (see the comments on 2:3).]{.class-0}

[The Catholics, naturally, have the whole thing screwed up: they have ]{.class-0}[Mary]{.class-1}[ doing the bruising. All over Europe you can see statues of Mary standing with the moon under her feet and bright rays like the sun around her head along with twelve stars (see the comments on Rev. 12:1 in that Commentary), and her foot is on the head of the serpent ]{.class-0}[bruising his head.]{.class-1}[ Of course, the verse didn't exactly say that.]{.class-0}

"[And I will put enmity between thee]{.class-2}[ \[the serpent\]]{.class-0}[ and the woman, and between thy SEED ]{.class-2}[\[the serpent's seed\]]{.class-0}[ and her SEED]{.class-2}[ \[the woman's seed\]]{.class-0}[; IT]{.class-2}[ \[the woman's seed; not the woman\]]{.class-0}[ shall bruise thy head, and thou shalt bruise his heel"]{.class-2}[ (Gen. 3:15). It's the ]{.class-0}["SEED"]{.class-2}[ of the woman who bruises Satan's head. It's not Mary; it's Mary's ]{.class-0}[son,]{.class-1}[ Christ.]{.class-0}

[Now that]{.class-0}[ bruising of the head]{.class-1}[ is mentioned over and over again in connection with the Second Advent, and it's not just a ]{.class-0}["leader"]{.class-1}[ like the ]{.class-0}[NIV]{.class-1}[ teaches. It's the ]{.class-0}[literal]{.class-1}[ head of a ]{.class-0}[literal]{.class-1}[ man, who's the seed of the Devil, ]{.class-0}["the son of perdition"]{.class-2}[ (John 6:70--71 cf. John 17:12; 2 Thess. 2:3--9). Look at Psalm 68:21 and note the context in verse 23.]{.class-0}

"[But God shall wound THE HEAD OF HIS ENEMIES]{.class-2}[ \[is He just talking about the Antichrist as Satan's King? No, read on\]]{.class-0}[, and THE HAIRY SCALP]{.class-2}[ \[that's on top of the head where Indians cut it off\]]{.class-0}[ of such an one as goeth on still in his trespasses\....That thy foot may be dipped in the blood of thine enemies]{.class-2}[ (the Second Advent---see Isa. 63:1--6; Rev. 14:19--20, 19:12--15)]{.class-0}[."]{.class-2}

You see, that stuff is in there, but it's "camouflaged." It's "disguised," like in a detective story. Some of you folks who like detective novels ought to spend some time in the Bible trying to figure out some of this stuff.

[The Bible is a deceptive Book.]{.class-1}[ That thing in Psalms is written down poetically so those who don't believe it wouldn't take it to be ]{.class-0}[history]{.class-1}[. That's why all the new Bibles (even some ]{.class-0}[King James]{.class-1}[ Bibles) arrange the Psalms in meter, like poetry, so you wouldn't take it too seriously.]{.class-0}

[For instance, under the comments on verse 7, we referred you to a psalm that asked God to do to the Antichrist what He did to Sisera (Psa. 83:9). Well, what did God do to Sisera back in Judges 4? He let a woman drive a tent peg through his ]{.class-0}[skull]{.class-1}[ (Judg. 4:21--22). In that account, a woman killed a type of the Antichrist (Judg. 5:19--26), but the only reason she did it was because the man who was the type of Christ (Judg. 5:12 cf. Eph. 4:7--10) didn't have enough faith to go to battle without a woman at his side (Judg. 4:8--9). If that had not been the case, Barak would have been the one to kill Sisera.]{.class-0}

[Be that as it may, though, Jael was still not a type of Mary bruising Satan's head like the Catholics teach. Jael was ]{.class-0}["blessed ABOVE women"]{.class-2}[ (Judg. 4:24); Mary was only ]{.class-0}["blessed\...AMONG women"]{.class-2}[ (Luke 1:28, 42). The ones who are ]{.class-0}["blessed"]{.class-2}[ above Mary are ]{.class-0}["they that hear the word of God, and keep it"]{.class-2}[ (Luke 11:27--28). That's a reference to believers in Christ (Matt. 12:46--50). That woman killing the Antichrist is not Mary; it's ]{.class-0}[Christ's Bride]{.class-1}[ coming back with Him ]{.class-0}["as an army with banners"]{.class-2}[ (see comments on Song of Sol. 6:10 in that Commentary).]{.class-0}

[Want to see the same thing again? Here's Abimelech (Judg. 9), another type of the Antichrist. He's killed by a woman casting ]{.class-0}["a piece of a millstone"]{.class-2}[ out of a tower and breaking the guy's ]{.class-0}[skull]{.class-1}[ (Judg. 9:53). Of course, Abimelech had his armorbearer finish him off so it wouldn't be said that a woman killed him, but ]{.class-0}[she]{.class-1}[ got in the death blow.]{.class-0}

[Now in that passage there, the woman's not a type of the Bride of Christ; she's a type of the Old Testament saints who also come down with Christ at the Advent. That's why that woman in Song of Solomon 6 is said to be ]{.class-0}["as it were the company of TWO ARMIES"]{.class-2}[ (see comments on Song of Sol. 6:13 in that Commentary): Old Testament saints on ]{.class-0}[mules]{.class-1}[ and New Testament saints ]{.class-0}[on horses]{.class-1}[, just like we showed you from Judges 5 in the comments on Habakkuk 3:3.]{.class-0}

[Do you see how that thing is all through that Old Testament?]{.class-1}[ Here's an innocent little kids' story for VBS---"David and Goliath." Who would ever think that that's a warning to the United Nations that Satan incarnate is going to take them over, but that a Jew is going to kill him? Not one nation in the UN can get that, but it's in there. There's David, one of the greatest types of Christ in the Old Testament, bringing down a type of the Antichrist, but notice how he does it.]{.class-0}

"[And David put his hand in his bag, and took thence a stone ]{.class-2}[\[Christ is ]{.class-0}["a stone\...cut out without hands"]{.class-2}[ (Dan. 2:34) that grinds the wicked ]{.class-0}["to powder"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 21:44) at His Advent\]]{.class-0}[, and slang it, and smote the Philistine in his forehead ]{.class-2}[\[exactly where Abimelech and Sisera got it: tic, tac, toe; three in a row\]]{.class-0}[, that the stone sunk into his forehead; and he fell upon his face to the earth. So David prevailed over the Philistine with a sling and with a stone, and smote the Philistine, and slew him; but there was no sword in the hand of David ]{.class-2}[\[when Christ comes back, the sword won't be in His hand; it'll be in His ]{.class-0}[mouth]{.class-1}[---Rev. 19:15\]]{.class-0}[. Therefore David ran, and stood upon the Philistine, and took his sword, and drew it out of the sheath thereof, and slew him, and cut off his head therewith"]{.class-2}[ (1 Sam. 17:49--51).]{.class-0}

The thing is all through there. Look at Psalm 110. That's the passage Christ pulled on the Pharisees and Sadducees when they were trying to trip Him up in His words (Psa. 110:1 cf. Matt. 22:44--45). The whole chapter is on the Second Advent. Look at verses 5--6.

"[The Lord at thy right hand]{.class-2}[ \[a reference to Christ according to Matt. 22:42--45\]]{.class-0}[ shall strike through kings in the day of his wrath ]{.class-2}[\[there are ten of them ruling with the Antichrist---Dan. 2; Rev. 17\]]{.class-0}[. He shall judge among the heathen, he shall fill the places with the dead bodies; HE SHALL WOUND THE HEADS OVER MANY COUNTRIES."]{.class-2}

[There it is again. So not only does the Antichrist get ]{.class-0}[his head]{.class-1}[ bruised, but all ten horns on the dragon's head get it too.]{.class-0}

[Verse 14:]{.class-0}[ "Thou ]{.class-2}[\[talking about the Lord, vss. 12--13\]]{.class-0}[ didst strike through with his staves THE HEAD ]{.class-2}[\[and there it is again\]]{.class-0}[ of his villages: they came out as a whirlwind to scatter me: their rejoicing was as to devour the poor secretly."]{.class-2}[ The ]{.class-0}["me"]{.class-2}[ is Habakkuk speaking prophetically for Israel. In the Tribulation, they are ]{.class-0}["the poor"]{.class-2}[ (see the comments on 2:9--10).]{.class-0}

[Now notice that ]{.class-0}["whirlwind"]{.class-2}[ in the verse. When the Lord comes back, He comes back with a ]{.class-0}["whirlwind"]{.class-2}[ like a tornado or a cyclone or a hurricane.]{.class-0}

[Jeremiah 25:32 says, ]{.class-0}["Thus saith the LORD of hosts, Behold, evil shall go forth from nation to nation, and a great whirlwind shall be raised up from the coasts of the earth."]{.class-2}[ The context is the Second Advent (Jer. 25:30--31, 33).]{.class-0}

[Jeremiah 30:23 says, ]{.class-0}["Behold, the whirlwind of the LORD goeth forth with fury, a continuing whirlwind: it shall fall with pain upon the head of the wicked."]{.class-2}[ That's just what you read in Habakkuk 3:14.]{.class-0}

[That ]{.class-0}[whirlwind]{.class-1}[ shows up over and over again in connection with the Second Advent (see Isa. 5:26--28, 17:13, 21:1, 40:23--24, 41:15--16, 66:15; Job 21:17--18; Psa. 58:9--10; Prov. 1:26--27, 10:24--25; Jer. 4:13, 23:19; Hos. 13:3; Nah. 1:3; Zech. 9:14). Ezekiel, who sees a type of Christ returning at the Second Advent, sees God in a ]{.class-0}[whirlwind]{.class-1}[ (Ezek. 1:4); Job, a type of Israel in the Tribulation, sees God in a ]{.class-0}[whirlwind]{.class-1}[ (Job 38:1, 40:6); Elijah, a type of the post-Tribulation rapture, goes up to Heaven in a ]{.class-0}[whirlwind]{.class-1}[ (2 Kings 2:1, 11). So right before Christ comes back, at the post-Tribulation rapture, God sends a ]{.class-0}[hurricane]{.class-1}[ on this earth that goes across the whole globe like the "red eye" on Jupiter. ]{.class-0}[That thing goes around the earth tearing up things until the Advent.]{.class-1}[ All the tornadoes, hurricanes, and typhoons the Lord is sending right now (see the comments on 2:13) are just a ]{.class-0}[warm-up]{.class-1}[ for the real thing that'll make Katrina, Ivan, and Camille look like a summer breeze.]{.class-0}

We have already commented on verse 15 under verses 8 and 10.

"[When I heard, my belly trembled; my lips quivered at the voice: rottenness entered into my bones, and I trembled in myself"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 16). Now ]{.class-0}[that's]{.class-1}[ the Bible: it's a negative Book. The negative things in that Book outnumber the good ]{.class-0}[three to one]{.class-1}[ (see the comments on 1 Cor. 15:3--4 in that Commentary). Americans are to the point where they can't handle anything negative that Book says about them. That's why people like Joel Osteen, Rick Warren, Robert Schuller, and "TBN" (Paul and Jan Crouch) never preach or teach anything negative from the Book---it's bad for business (\$\$\$). It's all "something good is going to happen to you" (Oral Roberts, who had a boy who blew his brains out) and "positive thinking" (Norman Vincent Peale) and "possibility thinking" (Robert Schuller) and "your best life now" (Joel Osteen).]{.class-0}

[Do you know what advice one of these Bible revisers gave one time while trying to get folks to buy and read the new version he helped put out? He said, "To get something out of the Bible, the best thing to do is just do with it like you would eating a banana: take the ]{.class-0}[good]{.class-1}[ part and peel the ]{.class-0}[bad]{.class-1}[ part off and]{.class-0}[ throw it away."]{.class-1}[ That fellow was saying that whatever in the Bible profits ]{.class-0}[you]{.class-1}[ and makes ]{.class-0}[you]{.class-1}[ feel like a big shot and makes ]{.class-0}[you]{.class-1}[ feel good keep, ]{.class-0}[and any word it says against you burn.]{.class-1}[ He was telling you to get rid of all the negative passages.]{.class-0}

[Honest to God, people, I'm no genius, but I've been through that Book a few times. If I gave you a list of all the negative things God says about His own chosen people, you would faint. Why American Christians are dumb enough to think ]{.class-0}[they]{.class-1}[ are exempt, I don't know, but Paul warned you about it in Romans 11:22.]{.class-0}

"[Behold therefore the goodness and severity of God: on them which fell, severity; but toward thee goodness, if thou continue in his goodness: otherwise thou also shalt be cut off."]{.class-2}

[Paul's warning the Gentile nations that God has been good to them in giving them the Gospel and severe to Israel for rejecting it. But you had better watch out and not ]{.class-0}["be wise in your own conceits"]{.class-2}[ (Rom. 11:25) because God can restore Israel since they're the root and they're holy (Rom. 11:16). You're not: you were ]{.class-0}["grafted in"]{.class-2}[ (Rom. 11:17).]{.class-0}

[Now the Book hasn't changed, and God hasn't changed. He's still holy, and He's still ]{.class-0}["a consuming fire"]{.class-2}[ (Heb. 12:29). He wouldn't think anything of killing 20,000,000 of us in one shot---]{.class-0}[including me and my family.]{.class-1}[ I understand exactly what I'm saying, but you know me---I go by the Book. According to that Book, your "worth" isn't even worth writing down: that's why I got saved. I mean, according to that Book, you've got to realize you're a lost sinner, right (Rom. 3:10--23)? And according to that Book, you've got to stop trusting your own goodness, right (Rom. 10:3--4; Eph. 2:8--9; Tit. 3:5)? ]{.class-0}[Well, then, what makes you think you are "worth" anything?]{.class-1}

[Now do you see what a mess you are in in this nation? Who is going to convince the folk who run this country that]{.class-0}[ they aren't worth living?]{.class-1}[ I mean, who's going to convince a bunch of doctors, lawyers, bankers, businessmen, Congressmen, Senators, judges, Governors, Presidents, teachers, etc., of that? You say, "The preachers should." ]{.class-0}[They wouldn't even dare attempt it.]{.class-1}

[Name me one Christian celebrity---like Charles Stanley, Chuck Swindoll, James Dobson, et al.---that has spent ]{.class-0}[any]{.class-1}[ time making the types of folks I just listed feel like they're so dirty and godless that they'll all go to Hell. Why, you can't do it. Why? ]{.class-0}[Because they lost their manhood.]{.class-1}[ How many of them? ]{.class-0}[All of them.]{.class-1}

[Christian people, do you realize that in this country, up until World War I, every orthodox, Fundamental preacher preached on Hell ]{.class-0}[regularly.]{.class-1}[ If a fellow went to a Baptist church, the preacher would preach on Hell until you could smell the smoke. If he went to a Presbyterian church, he found out he was going to Hell under the ]{.class-0}[eternal decree of reprobation]{.class-1}[ unless he was one of the ]{.class-0}[elect]{.class-1}[ and forced by Irresistible Grace to receive Christ. If he went to a Methodist church, he'd hear that even if He received Christ he had to watch out because he might apostatize and ]{.class-0}[lose]{.class-1}[ his salvation and go to Hell anyway. And if he went to a Catholic church, the Priest would tell him that he was headed to Hell if he didn't find the Church Christ founded, join it, be baptized, take the sacraments, and die in a state of grace. The guy couldn't find rest no matter where he went. You can today, even in places like North Carolina where they used to get those kids at five years old and tell them they were going to Hell and burn.]{.class-0}

[Suppose I got up and talked to both houses of Congress, the President, and all nine Supreme Court judges so that when I got through, every one of them would understand in no uncertain terms that ]{.class-0}["every man at his best state is altogether vanity"]{.class-2}[ (Psa. 39:5). What do you think they would do with me? Why, they would throw me out and denounce me as a ]{.class-0}[crackpot fanatic]{.class-1}[ and an ]{.class-0}[extremist]{.class-1}[ and a "Taliban terrorist" and everything else. Not too long ago there was a pastor out in Kansas or Oklahoma (I forget which) who opened the State legislative session in prayer and asked God to forgive them for abortion, sex perversion, Socialism, and a bunch of other stuff; and every one of those State Senators and Congressmen threw a fit over that prayer. They didn't think they needed to be forgiven of anything. That's where you're at in America: you're at the end about to go over the cliff.]{.class-0}

[Back to verse 16:]{.class-0}[ "that I might rest in the day of trouble]{.class-2}[ \[the Tribulation---Jer. 30:7; Dan. 12:1; Zeph. 1:15, etc.\]]{.class-0}[: when he cometh up unto the people, he will invade them with his troops."]{.class-2}[ That invasion and those troops are described in detail in Joel 2:1--11, and we refer you to that passage and our remarks on it in Volume I of the commentary on the Minor Prophets.]{.class-0}

17 Although the fig tree shall not blossom, neither shall fruit be in the vines; the labour of the olive shall fail, and the fields shall yield no meat; the flock shall be cut off from the fold, and there shall be no herd in the stalls:

18 Yet I will rejoice in the LORD, I will joy in the God of my salvation.

19 The LORD God is my strength, and he will make my feet like hinds' feet, and he will make me to walk upon mine high places. To the chief singer on my stringed instruments.

"[The fig tree shall not blossom, neither shall fruit be in the vines; the labour of the olive shall fail\...the fields shall yield no meat; the flock shall be cut off from the fold\...there shall be no herd in the stalls"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 17). As I mentioned in the introduction of this work, all Scripture has three interpretations:]{.class-0}[ historical, doctrinal]{.class-1}[ (usually ]{.class-0}[prophetic]{.class-1}[), and ]{.class-0}[spiritual. Historically,]{.class-1}[ the famine described in this verse is the famine brought on by the invasion of Nebuchadnezzar and the judgment of God on the Jews' land (Deut. 28:15--18, 30:41; Jer. 14:2--8; Joel 1:10--13, 16--18; Amos 4:6--10). ]{.class-0}[Prophetically,]{.class-1}[ this famine is the famine brought on by the two witnesses in the Tribulation (Rev. 11:6, 6:5--6 cf. James 5:7, 17--18). ]{.class-0}[Spiritually,]{.class-1}[ the New Testament commentary on the verse is to be found in Matthew 21:19, 24:32; John 15:1--8; Romans 11:15--24; Matthew 13:38; and Acts 20:28--29. Those passages deal with the fact that ]{.class-0}[right now]{.class-1}[ Israel is in apostasy, not bearing fruit, trusting her own righteousness instead of the righteousness of her Messiah. The nation is in the state found in Luke 13:6--9 and Matthew 21:19.]{.class-0}

[But the day is coming when ]{.class-0}["the Deliverer"]{.class-2}[ comes ]{.class-0}["out of Zion"]{.class-2}[ and turns ]{.class-0}["away ungodliness from Jacob"]{.class-2}[ (Rom. 11:26). When that happens, the remnant of those Jews once more become fruitful to the Lord (Isa. 37:31, 4:2--4; Zech. 8:11--13). That is why Habakkuk says the first positive thing you have read in the book so far. Out of the 56 verses in the book of Habakkuk, only the last two have any positive message.]{.class-0}

"[Yet I will rejoice in the LORD, I will joy in the God of my salvation"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 18). The Christian is commanded: ]{.class-0}["Rejoice in the Lord alway: and again I say, Rejoice"]{.class-2}[ (Phil. 4:4), and ]{.class-0}["Rejoice evermore"]{.class-2}[ (1 Thess. 5:16); he is to ]{.class-0}["rejoice with joy unspeakable and full of glory"]{.class-2}[ (1 Pet. 1:8). For that Jewish remnant, the Second Advent is a time of great rejoicing (Isa. 41:16, 61;10, 65:8--14; Joel 2:18--23; Zeph. 3:11--14; Zech. 2:7--10, 10:6--7).]{.class-0}

"[The LORD God is my strength"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 19). Well, you just read ]{.class-0}["I will joy in the God of my salvation"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 18), so Nehemiah said ]{.class-0}["the joy of the LORD is your strength"]{.class-2}[ (Neh. 8:10 and comments on the verse in that Commentary).]{.class-0}

"[He will make my feet like hinds' feet, and he will make me to walk upon mine high places."]{.class-2}[ The picture is that of a mountain goat. There can be three or four of them out on a ledge, and that ledge can be no wider than their feet, and they keep their footing. You can see them going along the sides of those cliffs with a 100--300 foot drop to the bottom, and they don't fall.]{.class-0}

[A Christian can't "fall from grace" (see our remarks on Gal. 5:4) because he is ]{.class-0}[in Christ]{.class-1}[ as part of His Body (1 Cor. 12:13 cf. Col. 1:27). When Christ comes back, those Jews will be put into a position where they won't ever fall out of favor with the Lord again (Isa. 10:20--22, 30:19--21, 54:8--10, 62:4).]{.class-0}

[]{#section-0_1_fb08d3db102fed19_b685.xhtml}

INTRODUCTION TO

ZEPHANIAH

The book of Zephaniah is one of the dated books of the Minor Prophets. Zephaniah prophesied during the reign of Josiah (1:1), around 630 B.C. This would make him a contemporary of Jeremiah (see Jer. 1:2). The last revival Judah had before everything went to pieces under Jehoahaz, Jehoiakim, Jehoiachin, and Zedekiah took place under Josiah, the one who trembled at God's word (see the comments under Hab. 3:2).

[Unlike Habakkuk, the Bible gives us some information on Zephaniah. Not only do we know exactly when he prophesied, we are given his family lineage back four generations. All the commentators and expositors make him the great, great grandson of King Hezekiah of Judah (see ]{.class-0}["Hizkiah"]{.class-2}[ in 1:1). The name Zephaniah means "Jehovah has hidden" or "Jehovah has treasured."]{.class-0}

[The book has 3 chapters, 53 verses, and 1,616 words. The whole prophecy is about the Second Advent of Jesus Christ (note 1:7--10, 14--16, 2:3, and 3:8). Of course, Zephaniah is mostly]{.class-0}[ negative]{.class-1}[ and contains the most terrible, damning judgment against the United Nations which is to be found in any religious literature put out by anybody since 500 B.C. Chapter 3, verse 8 plainly states that God's purpose for the 191 Gentile nations in the United Nations Assembly is to bind all of its members together to burn them (see Matt. 13:38--42).]{.class-0}

Of course, the main condemnations of the book are aimed at Israel, specifically Judah, in its apostasy, but there are many places, especially in chapter 2, where the Gentiles are included. But as we shall see, the book ends up exactly where the book of Job ends (3:20 cf. Job 42:10), showing you that that book is a picture of the Jew in the Tribulation. The book ends with the restoration of the Jewish people; contrary to the Catholic Church, the Pope, Texe Marrs, the Amillennialists, and all one billion Mohammedans in the "Islamic Nation."

[]{#section-0_1_d995f498c3763279_b686.xhtml}

CHAPTER ONE

1:1 The word of the LORD which came unto Zephaniah the son of Cushi, the son of Gedaliah, the son of Amariah, the son of Hizkiah, in the days of Josiah the son of Amon, king of Judah.

2 I will utterly consume all things from off the land, saith the LORD.

3 I will consume man and beast; I will consume the fowls of the heaven, and the fishes of the sea, and the stumblingblocks with the wicked; and I will cut off man from off the land, saith the LORD.

4 I will also stretch out mine hand upon Judah, and upon all the inhabitants of Jerusalem; and I will cut off the remnant of Baal from this place, and the name of the Chemarims with the priests;

5 And them that worship the host of heaven upon the housetops; and them that worship and that swear by the LORD, and that swear by Malcham;

6 And them that are turned back from the LORD; and those that have not sought the LORD, nor enquired for him.

"[I will utterly consume all things from off the land, saith the LORD"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 2). That's a fine way to start a sermon, isn't it? "Good morning, I'm going to kill everybody." What do you do with something like that? Why, you either have to believe it, or you have to throw it out. ]{.class-0}[America threw it out.]{.class-1}

[When America made its ]{.class-0}[Declaration of Independence,]{.class-1}[ it kicked Jesus Christ out and put in His place some innocuous, unidentified "Creator." When the Constitution was put in as the law of the land, Jesus Christ was kicked out and hidden away in the ]{.class-0}["Anno Domini"]{.class-1}[ ("In the year of our Lord") so no one could identify Him by name. When ]{.class-0}[The Bill of Rights ]{.class-1}[was added to]{.class-0}[ The Constitution ]{.class-1}[to guarantee the freedoms of the individuals and States from the Federal government, Jesus Christ was kicked out again and was nowhere to be found. There is no mention of the name "Jesus Christ" in any of the three main "freedom documents," and when Abraham Lincoln made a fool out of himself at Gettysburg, Jesus Christ got kicked out again and never showed up in the Gettysburg Address either. No wonder the Supreme Court threw prayer and Bible reading out of the public schools!]{.class-0}

[Listen, people, that Book says, ]{.class-0}["This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased" ]{.class-2}[(Matt. 3:17). That's God the Father, the "Creator" of the Declaration of Independence and the Gettysburg Address, speaking about ]{.class-0}[His Son,]{.class-1}[ Jesus Christ. What do you think is going to happen to a country that treats God's Son that way? They're going to get it in the neck---that's what's going to happen.]{.class-0}

[Now notice ]{.class-0}["the land"]{.class-2}[ in verse 2. That's a reference to the Promised Land,]{.class-0}[ the land of Palestine.]{.class-1}[ What you are about to read is God judging the land of Judah through Nebuchadnezzar (see vs. 4), but prophetically, it is a type of what God does in the Tribulation. Look at verse 3. ]{.class-0}["I will consume man and beast\...the fowls of the heaven\...the fishes of the sea\...I will cut off man from off the land."]{.class-2}
