We are not going to get into all of that here; you can study that on your own (one of the best works on the subject is ]{.class-0}[A Chronology of the Old Testament]{.class-1}[ by Floyd Nolen Jones). All you really need to know is that there are ]{.class-0}[three Darisuses]{.class-1}[ mentioned in the Scriptures. Besides the Median King mentioned in the book of Daniel, there is a Persian King of that name given in Ezra 4:24 and another one given in Nehemiah 12:22. The one mentioned here in Haggai is the Persian King of Ezra 4--6 who ordered the work to begin again on the rebuilding of the Temple (Ezra 6:1--3) after Artaxerxes stopped it back in Ezra 4:21--23.]{.class-0}

[Now notice in verse 1 that Zerubbabel is a ]{.class-0}["governor of JUDAH,"]{.class-2}[ ]{.class-0}[not Palestine.]{.class-1}[ There's a State there, and it's ]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ Moslem or "Palestinian." When Abraham entered the land, there were ]{.class-0}[ten different peoples]{.class-1}[ in it that were ]{.class-0}[never]{.class-1}[ united into one State. When Joshua conquered the land, there were ]{.class-0}[31 different Kings]{.class-1}[ over what was known as "City-States"; that is, each city was a separate kingdom. ]{.class-0}[There was no "Palestinian State."]{.class-1}[ For the next 1,000 years, the nation that inhabited that land was the State of ]{.class-0}[Israel.]{.class-1}[ Under the Babylonians, Persians, and Greeks, you have a province known as ]{.class-0}[Judah]{.class-1}[ (as in ]{.class-0}[Jews;]{.class-1}[ not Palestinians). The northern part of that land was called ]{.class-0}[Samaria]{.class-1}[ by the Assyrians, not Palestine.]{.class-0}

[The word ]{.class-0}[Palestine]{.class-1}[ was a Roman word for the country of the Philistines, and the land wasn't called that until ]{.class-0}[after]{.class-1}[ the destruction of Jerusalem. The Roman provinces during the time of Christ were ]{.class-0}["Judaea"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["Galilee"]{.class-2}[ (Luke 3:1), ]{.class-0}["Samaria"]{.class-2}[ (John 4:3--4), ]{.class-0}["Decapolis"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 4:25), ]{.class-0}["Idumaea"]{.class-2}[ (Mark 3:8), and ]{.class-0}[Perea]{.class-1}[ (referred to as ]{.class-0}["beyond Jordan"]{.class-2}[ in John 1:28, 3:26, 10:40). The word ]{.class-0}[Palestina]{.class-1}[ shows up nowhere in the New Testament.]{.class-0}

[There was no "Palestinian State" under the Byzantines]{.class-1}[ (A.D. 629) ]{.class-0}[or any of the Moslems]{.class-1}[ (A.D. 638--939) ]{.class-0}[or the Catholics]{.class-1}[ (A.D. 1099, 1192). From 1517--1831, it was part of the empire of the ]{.class-0}[Ottoman Turks]{.class-1}[ (]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ Palestinians); from 1831--1840, it was taken over by the ]{.class-0}[Egyptians ]{.class-1}[(]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ Palestinians); and from 1840--1917, it became part of the ]{.class-0}[Ottoman Empire]{.class-1}[ again (]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ Palestinians) until it became a ]{.class-0}[British]{.class-1}[ mandate (]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ Palestinian) in 1917 under General Allenby.]{.class-0}

[In his book ]{.class-0}[Innocents Abroad,]{.class-1}[ Mark Twain says that the "Palestinians" had ]{.class-0}[no villages]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[no country;]{.class-1}[ the whole place was occupied by sheep herders and goat herders. Any ]{.class-0}[business]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[government]{.class-1}[ that was transacted there was overseen by ]{.class-0}[French, English, Germans, Russians,]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[Italians]{.class-1}[---NOT PALESTINIANS.]{.class-0}

[When the Jewish delegation brought that up to Jimmy Carter and Secretary of State Cyrus Vance at "Camp David," IT WAS IGNORED! Why? Because the American President and his Secretary of State wanted to take away ]{.class-0}[half]{.class-1}[ of the land of Israel and turn it over to an ]{.class-0}[Egyptian]{.class-1}[ (]{.class-0}[not Palestinian]{.class-1}[) terrorist---]{.class-0}[Yasser Arafat.]{.class-1}[ Ditto both Bushes, Clinton and his wife, and Obama.]{.class-0}

[What the news media calls the "West Bank" is the regions of ]{.class-0}[Judea]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[Samaria.]{.class-1}[ The bank of a river doesn't extend more than a hundred yards on either side. Like evolution, the "West Bank" is a fairy tale invented for news media dupes.]{.class-0}

[Next, notice the reference to the Temple as ]{.class-0}["the LORD'S house"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 2 cf. vs. 9) and ]{.class-0}["the house of the LORD"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 14). That's where Christian pastors got into the bad habit of calling a church building "the house of God."]{.class-0}

[The Catholic practice is to make the sign of the cross every time you go by a Catholic church. They do that because they've got "Jesus" (]{.class-0}[the wafer]{.class-1}[) tucked away in a little box up on the altar, and every Sunday morning the Priest pulls Him out and lets you swallow Him. Then "Jesus" is put back in His cage, and you can go out the door and live as you please.]{.class-0}

[The night I got under conviction, the Holy Spirit got to dealing with me and said, "You'd better find Christ tonight." I was running up and down Palafox Street in downtown Pensacola, in the rain, half drunk, trying to find a church building open. Why was I doing that? Because I had been taught that you could only find Christ in a ]{.class-0}[church]{.class-1}[.]{.class-0}

[I went to the Catholic church, and it was ]{.class-0}[locked]{.class-1}[. I ran down to the Christian Missionary Alliance church, and it was open, but I was afraid to go in. I wound up at the Methodist church on Wright Street. I was looking for Christ in a building because that's what I heard all the preachers say.]{.class-0}

[Brethren, you ought to worship God when you get up to eat your breakfast and when you drive your car down the street. Bob Jones Sr. used to say, "For the Christian, there is no such thing as sacred ground. ]{.class-0}[All ground is holy ground]{.class-1}[ and every bush a burning bush." You shouldn't be any different out in a deer stand or mullet fishing than you are down at the prayer altar.]{.class-0}

[In the New Testament epistles, ]{.class-0}[no building]{.class-1}[ is "the house of God." In the Gospels, the Lord deserted His ]{.class-0}["house"]{.class-2}[ because Israel rejected her Messiah (Luke 13:35; Matt. 23:38), and ]{.class-0}[Jesus Christ Himself]{.class-1}[ was ]{.class-0}["the temple"]{.class-2}[ (John 2:21). After the giving of the Holy Spirit to the Body of Christ, the ]{.class-0}[body of the believer]{.class-1}[ became "God's house" (1 Cor. 3:16, 6:19). In the book of Acts, Christians met in ]{.class-0}[houses,]{.class-1}[ not church buildings (see Acts 12:12; Col. 4:15; Phile. 2). Church buildings don't even show up until A.D. 235.]{.class-0}

[Baptists are really bad about that sort of thing, especially Baptists in the Baptist Bible Fellowship. They're always calling their churches "Baptist Temples" (e.g., "Jacksonville Baptist ]{.class-0}[Temple,"]{.class-1}[ "Detroit Baptist ]{.class-0}[Temple," "Temple]{.class-1}[ Baptist Church"). The fact is that, in the Bible, a temple of the Lord ]{.class-0}[is where God's presence abides]{.class-1}[ (Exod. 40:34--35; 2 Chron. 5:13--14, 7:1--2). In the New Testament, the presence of God is ]{.class-0}[in Jesus Christ ]{.class-1}[(2 Cor. 5:19) and then ]{.class-0}[in the believer]{.class-1}[ (Col. 1:27; Gal. 2:20) through the Holy Spirit (Rom. 8:9--11).]{.class-0}

[Do you know when a church building is a "house of God" or God's "temple"? It is when a bunch of born-again believers are in it; any other time it is ]{.class-0}[just a building.]{.class-1}

[Come on, folks, do you really believe just because a building is used as a "church" that it is the "house of God"? How about St. Peter's Basilica or Notre Dame? Why, the Bible calls those places ]{.class-0}["the habitation of devils"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 18:2).]{.class-0}

[Do you know what the Greek Orthodox cathedral in Constantinople (Istanbul) is today? It is a ]{.class-0}[Moslem mosque.]{.class-1}[ You call that a ]{.class-0}["house of the LORD,"]{.class-2}[ do you?]{.class-0}

[How about St. Paul's Cathedral and Westminster Abbey over in England (Anglican)? When the memorial service for "Princess Di" was held at Westminster Abbey (after stepping out on her husband and running around with a Moslem "prince"), a faggot (Elton John) sang at her funeral. You think that's ]{.class-0}["the LORD'S house"]{.class-2}[?]{.class-0}

[You say, "None of those churches were Baptist." Well, then, how about Riverside ]{.class-0}[Baptist]{.class-1}[ Church in New York, pastored by one of the worst unsaved Liberals America ever produced---Harry Emerson Fosdick? You see, being a Baptist means nothing when it comes to the "house of God." Beauchamp Vick's church in Detroit, Michigan is a Charismatic "House of Prayer" today. There is many a "house of God" that has been turned into a grocery store or a car wash or a restaurant, etc.]{.class-0}

[Now, what's going on in Haggai 1:2--4 is that back in Ezra 4, the Persian King Artaxerxes ordered the rebuilding of the Temple to cease based on the testimony of a bunch of lying "Palestinians" (see the comments on Ezra 4:4 in that Commentary). For nearly fourteen years no work had been done on the Temple. The reason the people were saying ]{.class-0}["The time is not come\...that the LORD'S house should be built"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 2) was because Darius had been reigning for two years already and the law hadn't been changed yet.]{.class-0}

[This is clearly a case of ]{.class-0}["the powers that be"]{.class-2}[ (Rom. 13:1) saying one thing (stop the building) and God saying something else (build the house). Simon Peter told you when that happens: ]{.class-0}["We ought to obey God rather than men"]{.class-2}[ (Acts 5:29). The rule of thumb laid down by Christ is: ]{.class-0}["Render therefore unto Caesar the things which are Caesar's; and unto God the things that are God's"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 22:21). But when the two conflict, you are to obey God and take the consequences from Caesar if necessary.]{.class-0}

[Verse 5 is a great preaching text: ]{.class-0}["Now therefore thus saith the LORD of hosts; Consider your ways."]{.class-2}[ It's so important that He says it twice (vs. 7; see the comments on Gen. 41:32 in that Commentary). That's good preaching for the lost, for the saved, and for a nation.]{.class-0}

[Deuteronomy 32:29 says, ]{.class-0}["O that they were wise, that they understood this, that they would consider their latter end."]{.class-2}

[The problem with folks is that they don't consider how what they are doing is going to ]{.class-0}[end;]{.class-1}[ they just look at the beginning. If a thing looks pretty good ]{.class-0}[at the moment,]{.class-1}[ they do it without considering the consequences of their actions.]{.class-0}

Christ said if a man's going to build a tower, he needs to sit down first and figure out how much it's going to cost him, and if a King is going to go to war against another King, he had better figure out first if he's got the manpower to win (Luke 14:28--33). You'd better consider the cost.

[When a young man takes his first drink of liquor, he's not thinking about turning into a chronic alcoholic and beating his wife or busting up the family. He's not thinking about dying in a drunk driving accident or getting cirrhosis of the liver and wasting away. All he's thinking about is impressing his friends or making time with some girl. But that Bible says: ]{.class-0}["At the LAST]{.class-2}[ \[get it---it's how the thing ends up, not how it starts out\]]{.class-0}[ it biteth like a serpent, and stingeth like an adder"]{.class-2}[ (Prov. 23:32).]{.class-0}

[If a lost sinner would just ]{.class-0}["consider"]{.class-2}[ his ]{.class-0}["latter end,"]{.class-2}[ he would get saved. If he would consider the facts that there's a judgment coming (Heb. 9:27) and that Hell is real, he would repent of his sins and receive Christ. Death doesn't "end it all." Now that's something to ]{.class-0}["consider."]{.class-2}

[You take America---this country hasn't considered the ]{.class-0}["latter end"]{.class-2}[ of anything it's done in the last sixty years. What was the ]{.class-0}["latter end"]{.class-2}[ of repealing Prohibition? Oh, somewhere between 22,000 and 25,000 people killed a year in automobile accidents where liquor was found in at least one of the cars involved; ]{.class-0}[that's more people dead from alcohol than in the entire Prohibition Era with Al Capone and the "Untouchables."]{.class-1}

[What was the ]{.class-0}["latter end"]{.class-2}[ of joining the UN? Oh, over 177 wars since its founding in 1945. You lost the last three you were in (Korea, Vietnam, and Iraq), you're losing the one you're in right ]{.class-0}[now]{.class-1}[ (Afghanistan), and you'll probably lose the one you're about to go into (Iran).]{.class-0}

[What was the ]{.class-0}["latter end"]{.class-2}[ of throwing the Bible and prayer out of the public schools? Oh, ]{.class-0}[a crime rate that has gone up 350% since 1964; ]{.class-1}[over ]{.class-0}[twenty]{.class-1}[ new venereal diseases; one and a ]{.class-0}[half million babies aborted a year;]{.class-1}[ the problems in school going from chewing gum, talking in class, and running in the hallways, to ]{.class-0}[rape, assault, drugs, and murder;]{.class-1}[ teaching our children all the way down to the kindergarten level how to ]{.class-0}[fornicate, commit sodomy, and crossdress;]{.class-1}[ etc. America didn't ]{.class-0}["consider"]{.class-2}[ the ]{.class-0}["latter end"]{.class-2}[ of letting one atheist woman (Madalyn Murray O'Hair) dictate policy for 85% of the American public (over 85% of Americans are professing Christians and more than that profess theism). In South Africa, that was called ]{.class-0}[apartheid:]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-0}[the minority rules the majority.]{.class-1}

[Now in the case here, the Jews didn't ]{.class-0}["consider their latter end."]{.class-2}[ They rejected their Messiah. When Christ was coming to Jerusalem at the Triumphal Entry, He wept over the city. Why? Because He knew what was coming because of their unbelief.]{.class-0}

[Their leaders said ]{.class-0}["We have no king but Caesar"]{.class-2}[ (John 19:15) and ]{.class-0}["His blood be on us, and on our children"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 27:25) without any idea of what that entailed. That meant ]{.class-0}[500 Jews crucified outside Jerusalem]{.class-1}[ and the city being destroyed (A.D. 70). It meant the dispersion of the people for nearly ]{.class-0}[1,900 years. ]{.class-1}[It meant ghettos, yellow stars, confiscation of property, exile, and pogroms. It meant ]{.class-0}[concentration camps, gas chambers,]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[six million dead]{.class-1}[ before the thing was over; and it's still not over yet. An even greater holocaust is still ahead in the future.]{.class-0}

[Christ looked out over that city and said, "If you only knew what was going to happen to you, you'd ]{.class-0}[cry."]{.class-1}

[Look at what happens to a nation that ]{.class-0}[rejects what God says:]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-0}["Ye have sown much, and bring in little; ye eat, but ye have not enough; ye drink, but ye are not filled with drink; ye clothe you, but there is none warm; and he that earneth wages earneth wages to put it into a bag with holes"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 6).]{.class-0}

[Your economy is a wreck. ]{.class-0}[That's a judgment from God.]{.class-1}[ America's three "gods" are money, sex, and education---in that order. So God has judged this nation in the thing it loves the most: ]{.class-0}[money]{.class-1}[ (1 Tim. 6:10). He took your ]{.class-0}[gold]{.class-1}[ away under FDR and your ]{.class-0}[silver]{.class-1}[ away under JFK. You haven't got enough ]{.class-0}[copper]{.class-1}[ in a penny now to sell as scrap.]{.class-0}

[He devalued your dollar until it's not worth ten cents. The American dollar used to be the ]{.class-0}[standard]{.class-1}[ of the world markets, and now they're talking about replacing it with the "Euro" or the Chinese "yuan." Red China owns so much of the American debt now that they could take over this nation without firing a shot or a missile; all they would have to do is "call in their markers."]{.class-0}

[This nation sold out Israel for Arab oil,]{.class-1}[ so what does God do? He blows up an oil well in the Gulf of Mexico and lets it ruin the tourism and seafood business in Florida, Alabama, Mississippi, and Louisiana. He gets you involved in wars in Kuwait, Iraq, Afghanistan, and Iran; and your gasoline goes up to over \$4.00 a gallon. That's God doing that to a nation that booted Him out of its schools, its federal buildings, and now its churches (the Hate Crimes Bill signed by Obama).]{.class-0}

"[Ye looked for much, and, lo, it came to little; and when ye brought it home, I did blow upon it"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 9). That's your "minimum wage" going up, and you're still not able to pay your bills, buy your groceries, and keep a roof over your head. That's ]{.class-0}[God]{.class-1}[ doing that. The spiritual application for a Christian is that if he wants to go broke quickly, just leave God out of the "budget" and spend the money on himself and his family. It will go in one hole and out the other (vs. 6).]{.class-0}

[I learned that lesson the hard way when I attended Bob Jones University. Before I got saved, I used to tithe in the Catholic Church. Well, after I got saved, I was under "grace" and not the Law, you know,]{.class-0}[ so I stopped tithing.]{.class-1}[ I nearly starved to death. The money got down to nothing, and I had to send the wife and baby back to the farm in Alabama. All the food ran out, and I found some old pancake flour back in a cupboard that the former occupant had left there. I mixed it with water and made a blue paste out of it. I was so hungry that it tasted good.]{.class-0}

The next day someone left some groceries outside of my door, and I decided to start tithing again. I put the money in a little cup, even if it was just a few cents. To make a long story short, the Lord blessed, and I got enough money to bring my family back to Greenville. But that wasn't until after I started paying the Lord His tithe again.

"[Therefore the heaven over you is stayed from dew, and the earth is stayed from her fruit. And I called for a drought upon the land"]{.class-2}[ (vss. 10--11). That's God saying ]{.class-0}[He]{.class-1}[ controls the weather, not "Mother Nature." ]{.class-0}[He]{.class-1}[ shuts off the water, and the crops dry up. ]{.class-0}[He]{.class-1}[ sends a frost and destroys the citrus crop. ]{.class-0}[He]{.class-1}[ sends too much rain and drowns the plants. ]{.class-0}[He]{.class-1}[ sends hurricanes and tears everything up. ]{.class-0}[He]{.class-1}[ sends snow storms and brings everything to a halt.]{.class-0}

[Who's doing that? It's God Almighty, the Creator of heaven and earth. It's got nothing to do with the high pressure and low pressure and the jetstream and "El Niño." God may use those things to accomplish what He wants done, but it's ]{.class-0}[God doing it.]{.class-1}

That's why you won't find one farmer out of a thousand who's an atheist. He may not be a Christian, but he won't be an atheist unless he's trying to show off in front of his neighbors and pretend something that isn't true. You can't farm without dealing with the weather, and you can't deal with the weather without doing some praying.

"[I called for a drought."]{.class-2}[ "Oh, God wouldn't do that! 'Jesus loves me, this I know'; 'Jesus loves the little children of the world.' He wouldn't send a drought!" Oh, yes, He would. ]{.class-0}[He's done it,]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[He's done it more than once]{.class-1}[ (read 1 Kings 18:1--5).]{.class-0}

[I was raised in a drought out in Kansas; it was called the "Dust Bowl." It got so dry that all the vegetation dried up and whole farms just blew away; there was nothing left but ]{.class-0}[dust.]{.class-1}[ I don't mean dirt either. The wind blew ]{.class-0}[dry dust]{.class-1}[ across everything.]{.class-0}

[I have a photograph of a farmer and two of his boys going by what's left of a barn half covered with dust. They're headed for an old, broken down "Model T" to drive out west and try to find jobs. Stuck in that dust pile is a sign that says: "You gave us ]{.class-0}[beer;]{.class-1}[ now give us ]{.class-0}[water."]{.class-1}

[That's talking about FDR. He repealed Prohibition and brought back liquor in 1933. Those folks were saying, "All right, you brought back the ]{.class-0}[beer; ]{.class-1}[now give us some ]{.class-0}[water]{.class-1}[, because right now we need that worse than we need the beer." But FDR couldn't give them water. Where do you get water? ]{.class-0}[You get it from the Lord, or you don't get it ]{.class-1}[(Job 5:8--10; Psa. 147:7--8; Matt. 5:45).]{.class-0}

"[Go up to the mountain, and bring wood, and build the house; and I will take pleasure in it, and I will be glorified saith the LORD"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 8). That's a good preaching text for starting a "building program." Of course, ]{.class-0}[historically]{.class-1}[, that's what He's telling those Jews to do in Ezra 5, but prophetically, that's the rebuilding of the Temple before the Second Advent.]{.class-0}

[According to the Book (rejected by 80--90% of all Catholics, Protestants, and Greek Orthodox; and 100% of all Hindus, Buddhists, and Moslems), there will be a Jewish temple with a ]{.class-0}["holy place"]{.class-2}[ in it (2 Thess. 2:3; Matt. 24:15 cf. Heb. 9:1--8) built on Mount Zion, where the Mohammedan "Dome of the Rock" now stands. Right now, there's a Moslem mosque there called the "Mosque of Omar." Supposedly there is a footprint embedded in a rock there of where Mohammed "leapt into Heaven" (before Mohammed was born, the Catholics said the ]{.class-0}[same footprint]{.class-1}[ was where Jesus Christ stood before Pontius Pilate).]{.class-0}

He dreamed he went up to Heaven on a female horse (I guess he had a night-]{.class-0}[mare]{.class-1}[). You'll never guess what her name was; it was ]{.class-0}[Barack.]{.class-1}[ That is who your "first black President" was named after: Mohammed's ]{.class-0}[horse.]{.class-1}

[According to Mohammed (]{.class-0}["Holy" Hadith,]{.class-1}[ Muslim, vol. 1, no. 309), Gabriel came along and kicked him awake---a ]{.class-0}[plagiarization]{.class-1}[ of Acts 12:7---and put him on a white horse---]{.class-0}[plagiarized]{.class-1}[ from Revelation 19:11---with wings---]{.class-0}[plagiarized]{.class-1}[ from Pegasus in Greek mythology.]{.class-0}

[Brethren, there's nothing original in the ]{.class-0}[Koran]{.class-1}[ or Islam. Why does Mohammed allow his followers who are men to have four wives? ]{.class-0}[Because Jacob had four wives.]{.class-1}

Boy, Mohammed, you're really original! Even the "virgins" in Paradise are not original; Mohammed copied that from Zoroastrianism. If that fellow ever had an original thought in his life, it died of loneliness.

[In honor of Mohammed's nightmare, in A.D. 685, Caliph Abd al-Malik commissioned the building of the mosque based on the architecture of a ]{.class-0}[Greek Orthodox church;]{.class-1}[ they even copied ]{.class-0}[that.]{.class-1}[ The problem is that it isn't a church or a mosque at all; it's a ]{.class-0}[shrine.]{.class-1}[ There's some ]{.class-0}[hairs]{.class-1}[ there, supposedly from ]{.class-0}[Mohammed's beard]{.class-1}[ (when he got a shave in Jerusalem, no one knows or can say).]{.class-0}

Now that is what the Jews are up against at present. Their options are:

[5. Try to rebuild the temple as a ]{.class-0}[joint project]{.class-1}[ with the Moslems.]{.class-0}

[All of these options have already been addressed in three passages written more than 2,000 years before the Balfour Declaration in 1917 and ]{.class-0}[recorded in the universal language of the Twenty-first Century more than 300 years before World War II:]{.class-1}[ Zechariah 14:1--17; Ezekiel 40--46; and Acts 15:16--17.]{.class-0}

[The Jews have been trying to get the Temple mount back and get rid of the Mosque of Omar since 1948 when they became a nation again after 1,800 years. The Vatican intervened in 1948 to ]{.class-0}[prevent]{.class-1}[ the Jews getting it back in the "War of Independence," and the ]{.class-0}[UN]{.class-1}[ put it under the Islamic rule of King Abdullah I of Jordan. After Rabbi Shlomo Goren led Jewish prayers within the Temple mount complex (August 15, 1967), the crazy, fanatical Mohammedan nuts went "spastic," so the Chief Rabbinate of Israel posted this notice forbidding all Jewish worship on the Temple mount:

"[Notice and Warning: Entrance to the area of the Temple Mount is forbidden to everyone by Jewish Law owning to the sacredness of the place."]{.class-0}

That did not stop some Jews from trying to get rid of the Dome of the Rock. In 1983, Rabbi Yehoda Getz broke through the Western Wall into a water cistern while looking for the foundations of the old Temple. In 1984, six men attempted to blow up the mosque with thirty pounds of explosives and 22 grenades. In 1986, members of the Knesset tried to investigate whether Moslems were stockpiling weapons beneath the Dome of the Rock, but the Moslems started a riot by telling their people that the Jews were attacking the mosque. In 1969, an Australian got through the Western Wall and tried to burn down the second mosque on the Temple mount, the Al-Aqsa mosque.

[The Jews are fully prepared to rebuild the temple once the mosques are removed. In May 21, 1967, the ]{.class-0}[Washington Post]{.class-1}[ ran an ad that announced that the temple rebuilding project had been started and would be completed "with Divine Guidance and Help." The August 4, 1967 issue of the British magazine]{.class-0}[ The Christian and Christianity Today]{.class-1}[ reported shipments going out from Pier 26 in New York to Jerusalem; these shipments were 500 railcar loads of pre-cut limestone from Bedford, Indiana. Betty Pace, the official secretary of the Limestone Institute of America in Sellersburg, Indiana said, "Thousands of tons have been shipped to New York with no way of determining the ultimate destination of every order. The limestone is similar to ]{.class-0}[Jerusalem limestone."]{.class-1}[ But Jewish masons were brought to Bedford, Indiana to study pneumatic drills for stone cutting.]{.class-0}

In 1986, Rabbi Shlomo Goren said that the Jews were obligated to pray at a sanctuary on the Temple mount, so a synagogue (if not a temple) should be built there on the Eastern Wall. In 1991, Gershon Salomon, the head of a group called the "Temple Mount Faithful," had a four-ton cornerstone prepared for placement; since then, they have tried every six months to lay the cornerstone on the Temple mount.

[In 1979, Dr. Asher Kaufman insisted the true Temple was built a little ]{.class-0}[north]{.class-1}[ of the Mosque of Omar so that the Golden Gate of the Temple went directly into it (see Ezek. 44:2); conversely, the Roman Catholic "Father" Bellarmino Bagatti said the right location would be ]{.class-0}[south]{.class-1}[ of the Dome of the Rock (if you had to choose between the two, the safe bet would be with the Jew---see Rom. 3:1--2). As recently as 2000, the Syrians have taken a definite stand against Bible-believing Christians in the Middle East because the Syrians believe the Christians are involved in a plot to destroy both mosques on the Temple mount in order to rebuild the ]{.class-0}[original]{.class-1}[ structure---the temple. Since 1968, the supreme Moslem council in Jerusalem has said: "The Moslems will ]{.class-0}[never]{.class-1}[ permit ]{.class-0}[any]{.class-1}[ Jew to pray in this area. We are prepared to ]{.class-0}[die]{.class-1}[ for this."]{.class-0}

[They can start digging their ]{.class-0}[graves.]{.class-1}[ God Almighty Himself said, ]{.class-0}["After this I will return, and will build again the tabernacle of David, which is fallen down; and I will build again the ruins thereof, and I will set it up"]{.class-2}[ (Acts 15:16 cf. Amos 9:11).]{.class-0}

[There the Temple is equated with the ]{.class-0}[Tabernacle]{.class-1}[ exactly as it is in 1 Samuel 1:9, 3:3. But ]{.class-0}["the tabernacle of David"]{.class-2}[ was not the Tabernacle Moses built in the wilderness; it was a ]{.class-0}[tent]{.class-1}[ David set up for the Ark of the Covenant when he brought it to Jerusalem (2 Sam. 6:15--17). So depending on how long it takes to set up the temple (see 1 Kings 6:38; Ezra 4:24, 6:15; John 2:20), all the Jews really need is a tent set up with an "Ark" in it. Right now, the "Temple Institute" in Jerusalem has all the furniture and utensils for the temple prepared. They have identified the Levites genetically through DNA testing and are training them in the temple ritual with all the proper clothing. Everything is set up; all they have to do is "move in"; and all they need when they move in is a ]{.class-0}[tent.]{.class-1}

12 Then Zerubbabel the son of Shealtiel, and Joshua the son of Josedech, the high priest, with all the remnant of the people, obeyed the voice of the LORD their God, and the words of Haggai the prophet, as the LORD their God had sent him, and the people did fear before the LORD.

13 Then spake Haggai the LORD'S messenger in the LORD'S message unto the people, saying, I am with you, saith the LORD.

14 And the LORD stirred up the spirit of Zerubbabel the son of Shealtiel, governor of Judah, and the spirit of Joshua the son of Josedech, the high priest, and the spirit of all the remnant of the people; and they came and did work in the house of the LORD of hosts, their God,

15 In the four and twentieth day of the sixth month, in the second year of Darius the king.

"[Zerubbabel the son of Shealtiel, and Joshua the son of Josedech, the high priest"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 12) were the two leaders of the Jews who returned from captivity. Zerubbabel was the ]{.class-0}[political]{.class-1}[ leader, and Joshua was the ]{.class-0}[religious]{.class-1}[ leader.]{.class-0}

[Notice the mixing of Church and State. That's because in the Old Testament, the people of God are a ]{.class-0}[political State]{.class-1}[---]{.class-0}[a theocracy]{.class-1}[. The New Testament knows no such set-up until the return of Jesus Christ as]{.class-0}[ "KING OF KINGS, AND LORD OF LORDS"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 19:16). The Roman Catholic Church is ]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ a New Testament church---]{.class-0}[universally or locally. ]{.class-1}[It is a religious monstrosity (read Rev. 17) trying to counterfeit Old Testament Israel with a "tabernacle" containing the Roman Catholic "God" (the wafer for the Mass), religious vestments, "holy water," candles, a priest class, and a Church-State set-up (the Pope is head of a political State---the Vatican State).]{.class-0}

[The interesting thing about both Zerubbabel and Joshua the high priest is that both of them are connected to the Lord Jesus Christ either in ]{.class-0}[lineage]{.class-1}[ or in ]{.class-0}[type]{.class-1}[. Look at Matthew 1:12; there is Zerubbabel in the line of Christ through Solomon. He's the grandson of Jeconiah, the King of Judah, whose line was cut off from being King (Jer. 22:28--30); so when Zerubbabel shows up, he is merely a Governor in the Persian Empire even though he is the heir to the throne in the line of Solomon. That is also the reason why Joseph could not be the literal father of Jesus Christ; if he were, Christ could not claim the throne of David promised to Him in Luke 1:32 because of the curse placed on Jeconiah. Despite that curse, Zerubbabel is plainly pictured as a type of Christ in Zechariah 4:6--10.]{.class-0}

[Then there is Joshua the high priest. He is obviously a type of Christ both in his name and office. The New Testament word for Joshua is ]{.class-0}[Jesus]{.class-1}[ (see Acts 7:45; Heb. 4:8 in a ]{.class-0}[King James]{.class-1}[ Bible ]{.class-0}[only]{.class-1}[, because the word has been changed in the new versions so you won't make the connection). ]{.class-0}["Christ Jesus"]{.class-2}[ is called the ]{.class-0}["High Priest of our profession"]{.class-2}[ in Hebrews 3:1.]{.class-0}

"[Then Zerubbabel the son of Shealtiel, and Joshua the son of Josedech, the high priest, with all the remnant of the people, obeyed the voice of the LORD their God, and the words of Haggai the prophet"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 12). Up shows the Book! The new translations (like the ]{.class-0}[ASV]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[RSV]{.class-1}[---no difference; same corrupt text) say ]{.class-0}["Every scripture is inspired of God"]{.class-1}[ in 2 Timothy 3:16. The ]{.class-0}[King James]{.class-1}[ is much more ]{.class-0}[accurate]{.class-1}[ in its reading: ]{.class-0}["all scripture is GIVEN BY INSPIRATION of God."]{.class-2}[ The "giving" is described in 2 Peter 1:21---]{.class-0}["holy men of God SPAKE as they were moved by the Holy Ghost."]{.class-2}[ The Scripture was SPOKEN before it was ever written down; that's where the ]{.class-0}["inspiration"]{.class-2}[ occurred.]{.class-0}

[Notice again the emphasis God puts on His ]{.class-0}["WORDS."]{.class-2}[ It's not just the Neo-orthodox "Word" or even the correct Scriptural designation---]{.class-0}["word"]{.class-2}[ (see Psa. 138:2). It's the W-O-R-D-S.]{.class-0}

"[The WORDS of the LORD are pure WORDS\...Thou shalt keep THEM, O LORD, thou shalt preserve THEM from this generation for ever"]{.class-2}[ (Psa. 12:6--7).]{.class-0}

"[The WORDS that I speak unto you, THEY are spirit, and THEY are life"]{.class-2}[ (John 6:63).]{.class-0}

"[He that is of God heareth God's WORDS: ye therefore hear THEM not, because ye are not of God"]{.class-2}[ (John 8:47).]{.class-0}

"[That ye may be mindful of the WORDS which were spoken before by the holy prophets"]{.class-2}[ (2 Pet. 3:2).]{.class-0}

[When you hear these modern apostate Fundamentalists talking about the "preserved Word of God," it's because ]{.class-0}[they're about to mess with the WORDS.]{.class-1}[ There is no "preserved ]{.class-0}[Word]{.class-1}[ of God" (the Neo-orthodox "message") or ]{.class-0}["preserved word]{.class-1}[ of God" without the preserved WORDS of God (cf. vs. 13).]{.class-0}

[Back to verse 12: ]{.class-0}["and the people did fear before the LORD."]{.class-2}[ The Scriptures say: ]{.class-0}["The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom"]{.class-2}[ (Psa. 111: 10; Prov. 9:10). The Lord told Job: ]{.class-0}["Behold, the fear of the Lord, that is wisdom"]{.class-2}[ (Job 28:28). Solomon wrote: ]{.class-0}["The fear of the LORD is the beginning of knowledge"]{.class-2}[ (Prov. 1:7), and ]{.class-0}["The fear of the LORD is a fountain of life"]{.class-2}[ (Prov. 14:27).]{.class-0}

[So the people are getting right with God and having a spiritual revival. Verse 14 says, ]{.class-0}["The LORD stirred up the spirit of Zerubbabel the son of Shealtiel, the governor of Judah, and the spirit of Joshua the son of Josedech, the high priest, and the spirit of all the remnant of the people."]{.class-2}[ That's national revival: the politicians are getting right, the religious leaders are getting right, and the common people are getting right.]{.class-0}

"[Then spake Haggai the LORD'S messenger in the LORD'S message unto the people, saying, I am with you, saith the LORD"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 13). ]{.class-0}["I am with you, saith the LORD."]{.class-2}[ That's what the Bible says about Jesus Christ when He showed up.]{.class-0}

"[Behold, a virgin shall be with child, and shall bring forth a son, and they shall call his name Emmanuel, which being interpreted is, God with us"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 1:23).]{.class-0}

[Before Jesus returned to Heaven, He told His disciples: ]{.class-0}["lo, I am with you alway, even unto the end of the world"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 28:20). That is a reference to Jesus Christ indwelling the body of the born-again believer in the person of the Holy Spirit (Col. 1:27; Rom. 8:9).]{.class-0}

[Now, verse 13 is ]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ talking about the Incarnation or the Holy Spirit. It's talking about God being with a nation to bless its people as they obey Him.]{.class-0}

[Abraham Lincoln was asked one time whether he thought God was on his side in the Civil War. His answer was: "It's not whether God is on my side, but whether I'm on His." Well, that's a good personal way of looking at it, but if ]{.class-0}[a nation's]{.class-1}[ got any sense, it wants God on its side when it goes to war.]{.class-0}

[In World War II, the Germans used to have ]{.class-0}[Gott Mit Uns]{.class-1}[ on their belt buckles; that means "God with us." Of course, those were unsaved Nazis doing that, but they had more sense than the Americans. Americans haven't been concerned with God being with them as a nation for the last sixty years. If they had been, they wouldn't have kicked Him out of their schools and forbidden their "chaplains" to pray in the name of His Son. Don't ask God to bless America after treating Him like that.]{.class-0}

[Verse 13 is God being with the nation He chose after the people have gotten right. He is now going to help them do what He's told them to do. That's what's going to happen at the end of the Tribulation. After being ]{.class-0}[against]{.class-1}[ those Jews for 2,000 years, the Lord's going to fully restore them and make them the ]{.class-0}[rulers]{.class-1}[ of all the other 191 nations in the UN.]{.class-0}

Not too long ago, the Venezuelan dictator Hugo Chavez toured the Middle East and visited Syria. He made a speech there and called the government of the nation of Israel "genocidal"; in other words, the Jewish government is trying to kill off every "Palestinian" in its borders. Chavez said Israel was an "imperialistic nation" that "annihilates" other peoples and that the Israeli people shouldn't support or obey their "genocidal leaders."

[Israel hasn't tried to "annihilate" anyone. They have tried to stop a bunch of Moslems from firing rockets into their cities and killing their people. If Israel didn't give jobs to the Palestinians, they would have no industry or economy. When Bush pressured the Israelis to pull out of the Gaza Strip, the Palestinians suddenly found themselves ]{.class-0}[without jobs, without money, and without food.]{.class-1}

[The Jews have done more for mankind than all the Moslems put together. There's a list floating around of all the Jewish Nobel Prize winners as compared with all the Moslem Nobel Prize winners. I think there are something like ]{.class-0}[129 Jews ]{.class-1}[who have made physical improvements to society, civilization, literature, art, music, science, medicine, etc., and less than ten Moslems. The Moslem Nobel Prize winners are people like Yasser Arafat, who was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize ]{.class-0}[after]{.class-1}[ being ]{.class-0}[the world's number one terrorist.]{.class-1}[ What a great contribution to mankind!]{.class-0}

[Listen, people, the Jews aren't brainwashing their children in ]{.class-0}[military training camps;]{.class-1}[ Moslems do. ]{.class-0}[The Jews don't hijack planes;]{.class-1}[ Moslems do. ]{.class-0}[The Jews don't kill athletes at the Olympics; ]{.class-1}[Moslems do. ]{.class-0}[The Jews don't blow themselves up in restaurants and trains;]{.class-1}[ Moslems do. ]{.class-0}[The Jews don't destroy churches, burn Bibles, and mutilate pastors; ]{.class-1}[Moslems do. ]{.class-0}[The Jews don't traffic in slaves; ]{.class-1}[Moslems do. ]{.class-0}[The Jews don't have leaders calling for "holy war" ]{.class-1}[(jihad) and death to all infidels; Moslems do. If the Moslems put down their weapons today, there would be no more ]{.class-0}[violence; ]{.class-1}[if the Jews put down their weapons today, there would be no more ]{.class-0}[Jews.]{.class-1}

[Right now, the Lord is in the process of restoring the Jews, and the whole world is against them. But the day is coming when God Almighty is going to take the side of that Jew unequivocally, and then He is going to be against the whole world. You say, "]{.class-0}['God is no respecter of persons']{.class-2}[ (Acts 10:34); He wouldn't take sides like that." Just watch Him!]{.class-0}

The chapter ends at Ezra 5:2. Haggai has been preaching for 24 days (vs. 15 cf. vs. 1). The old-time evangelists used to hold three and four-week campaigns. Of course, you can't do that anymore because of television. Bob Jones Sr. told me one time that before television people would come out to hear a man who could speak well, but now people can get that right in their living rooms with television.

[But in those protracted meetings, those old-time evangelists wouldn't give an invitation sometimes until after preaching a week or more. Someone asked Sam Jones one time why he hadn't given an invitation yet in one of his meetings, and he said, "I like to soak my hogs in boiling water before I scrape their bristles off." If I am in a week-long meeting, I generally don't give an invitation until the ]{.class-0}[third]{.class-1}[ or ]{.class-0}[fourth]{.class-1}[ night; I want to give the Holy Spirit time to work.]{.class-0}

[That's especially true in a ]{.class-0}[youth camp]{.class-1}[. I preached in one youth camp, and the first night I didn't give an invitation and sent them on back to their cabins. About 1:00 that night, the camp director got those kids out of bed and back down to the "tabernacle," where he showed them a Christian film and gave an invitation. A few of them got saved, but there was no need to do that. He had those kids "captive" for a whole week; they weren't going anywhere. That was plenty of time for the Holy Spirit to get them under conviction and get them saved before the week was up. You don't want to pick the fruit while it's still green.]{.class-0}

Here in Haggai chapter 1, Haggai preaches 24 days before he gives his "invitation." The invitation is to start the work on the Temple again. The people get "revived" and start the "building program."

[]{#section-0_1_353627393ccd0ee9_b68b.xhtml}

CHAPTER TWO

[Chapter 2 of Haggai consists of three distinct prophecies: 1) verses 1--9 addressed to Zerubbabel, Joshua, and the remnant of the Jews, 2) verses 10--19 addressed to the priesthood and the people, and 3) verses 20--23 addressed to Zerubbabel. Haggai is still prophesying ]{.class-0}["in the second year of Darius the king"]{.class-2}[ (see 1:1, 15). The first prophecy comes on the twenty-first day of ]{.class-0}["the seventh month"]{.class-2}[ (Sept.--Oct.), and the last two prophecies come on the twenty-fourth day of ]{.class-0}["the ninth month"]{.class-2}[ (Nov.--Dec.). For purposes of my comments, I will divide the chapter at the paragraph marks and take each prophecy separately.]{.class-0}

2:1 In the seventh month, in the one and twentieth day of the month, came the word of the LORD by the prophet Haggai, saying,

2 Speak now to Zerubbabel the son of Shealtiel, governor of Judah, and to Joshua the son of Josedech, the high priest, and to the residue of the people, saying,

3 Who is left among you that saw this house in her first glory? and how do ye see it now? is it not in your eyes in comparison of it as nothing?

4 Yet now be strong, O Zerubbabel, saith the LORD; and be strong, O Joshua, son of Josedech, the high priest; and be strong, all ye people of the land, saith the LORD, and work: for I am with you, saith the LORD of hosts:

5 According to the word that I covenanted with you when ye came out of Egypt, so my spirit remaineth among you: fear ye not.

6 For thus saith the LORD of hosts; Yet once, it is a little while, and I will shake the heavens, and the earth, and the sea, and the dry land;

7 And I will shake all nations, and the desire of all nations shall come: and I will fill this house with glory, saith the LORD of hosts.

8 The silver is mine, and the gold is mine, saith the LORD of hosts.

9 The glory of this latter house shall be greater than of the former, saith the LORD of hosts: and in this place will I give peace, saith the LORD of hosts.

[As we stated in the introduction to Haggai's prophecy, ]{.class-0}["the seventh month"]{.class-2}[ (Sept.--Oct.) is the month associated with the Second Advent. The Feast of Trumpets takes place on ]{.class-0}["the first day of the month"]{.class-2}[ (Lev. 23:24), the Day of Atonement takes place on ]{.class-0}["the tenth day of the month"]{.class-2}[ (Lev. 23:27), and the Feast of Tabernacles starts on ]{.class-0}["the fifteenth day of the month"]{.class-2}[ (Lev. 23:34) and runs seven days to the twenty-second day of the month. So Haggai gets his prophecy two days after the Feast of Tabernacles is over.]{.class-0}

[Verse 3: ]{.class-0}["Who is left among you that saw this house in her first glory?"]{.class-2}[ Well, there weren't many left. Seventy years had passed since some of them had seen Solomon's Temple (Jer. 25:11--12, 29:10; Dan. 9:2). Ezra describes them as ]{.class-0}["chief of the fathers, who were ancient men"]{.class-2}[ (Ezra 3:12), and that was sixteen years earlier when the foundation of the Temple was laid. They had to be at least in their eighties then, and if any of them were around when Haggai prophesied, they would have been in their nineties.]{.class-0}

"[And how do ye see it now? is it not in your eyes in comparison of it as nothing?"]{.class-2}[ When Zerubbabel and Joshua laid the foundation of the Temple, those folks who had actually seen Solomon's Temple and remembered what it looked like ]{.class-0}["wept with a loud voice"]{.class-2}[ (Ezra 3:12). They were glad God's house was being rebuilt, but in comparison to Solomon's, it was so pitiful that it made them bawl. Solomon overlaid his Temple with ]{.class-0}["pure gold,"]{.class-2}[ the Bible says (1 Kings 6:21--23). In comparison to that, this new Temple was like ]{.class-0}["Ichabod"]{.class-2}[: ]{.class-0}["The glory is departed"]{.class-2}[ (see 1 Sam. 4:21).]{.class-0}

"[Yet now\..."]{.class-2}[ (vs. 4). The people are getting discouraged in the work. They restarted the building of the Temple back in 1:15 on the twenty-fourth day ]{.class-0}["of the sixth month,"]{.class-2}[ and they've been working for a whole month now (cf. 2:1). They're looking at what's been done and saying, "Why, this is nothing compared to what Solomon's Temple was like. Why are we even bothering to do this?"]{.class-0}

[God is saying, "Even though things seem bad and depressing, keep on building anyway." Ezra 5:2 says, ]{.class-0}["Then rose up Zerubbabel the son of Shealtiel, and Jeshua the son of Jozadak, and began to build the house of God which is at Jerusalem: and with them were THE PROPHETS OF GOD HELPING THEM."]{.class-2}

"[The prophets"]{.class-2}[ in that verse were Haggai and Zechariah (see Ezra 5:1). How were they ]{.class-0}["helping them"]{.class-2}[? They were preaching and prophesying and encouraging the people in the work.]{.class-0}

"[Yet now BE STRONG, O Zerubbabel, saith the LORD; and BE STRONG, O Joshua, son of Josedech, the high priest; and BE STRONG, all ye people of the land, saith the LORD, and work: for I am with you ]{.class-2}[\[see the comments on 1:13)]{.class-0}[, saith the LORD of hosts."]{.class-2}

That's what the Lord tells Joshua, the son of Nun, three times in Joshua 1.

"[BE STRONG and of a good courage: for unto this people shalt thou divide for an inheritance the land, which I sware unto their fathers to give them"]{.class-2}[ (Josh. 1:6).]{.class-0}

"[Only BE THOU STRONG and very courageous, that thou mayest observe to do according to all the law, which Moses my servant commanded thee: turn not from it to the right hand or to the left, that thou mayest prosper withersoever thou goest"]{.class-2}[ (Josh. 1:7).]{.class-0}

"[Have not I commanded thee? BE STRONG, and of a good courage: be not afraid, neither be thou dismayed: for the LORD thy God is with thee whithersoever thou goest"]{.class-2}[ (Josh. 1:9).]{.class-0}

[When Moses gave his charge to Joshua, that was the message he gave him: ]{.class-0}["BE STRONG and of a good courage"]{.class-2}[ (Deut. 31:6--7, 23). That's what a Christian needs if he is going to do anything for God: ]{.class-0}[strength]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[courage]{.class-1}[. He needs the ]{.class-0}[strength]{.class-1}[ to accomplish what God wants him to do, and he needs the ]{.class-0}[courage]{.class-1}[ to do it.]{.class-0}

[The Apostle Paul wrote to his ]{.class-0}["son in the faith"]{.class-2}[ (1 Tim. 1:2): ]{.class-0}["Thou therefore, my son, BE STRONG in the grace that is in Christ Jesus"]{.class-2}[ (2 Tim. 2:1). The Christian is told: ]{.class-0}["Finally, my brethren, BE STRONG in the Lord, and in the power of his might. Put on the whole armour of God, that ye may be able to stand against the wiles of the devil"]{.class-2}[ (Eph. 6:10--11). He is told: ]{.class-0}["Watch ye, stand fast in the faith, quit you like men, BE STRONG"]{.class-2}[ (1 Cor. 16:13).]{.class-0}

[Look at that last reference and notice the last two phrases: ]{.class-0}["quit you like men, be strong."]{.class-2}[ Do you know where that's from? That comes from unsaved, God-defying, dolly-worshipping ]{.class-0}[heathen.]{.class-1}

"[Be strong, and quit yourselves like men, O ye Philistines, that ye be not servants unto the Hebrews, as they have been to you: quit yourselves like men, and fight"]{.class-2}[ (1 Sam. 4:9).]{.class-0}

Now that's just a bunch of Hell-bound sinners talking, but that's good advice. When things look hopeless, don't give up; "act like a man" and fight!

On the wall in my house hangs a placard a prisoner made for me with a wood-burning kit. It has a verse on it that has always been a great encouragement to me in the ministry. It is 2 Samuel 10:12.

"[Be of good courage, and let us play the men for our people, and for the cities of our God: and the LORD do that which seemeth him good."]{.class-2}

That was Joab speaking in that verse. He was surrounded by the Syrians on one side and by the Ammonites on the other. He sets his troops in order and tells them, "Be courageous and ACT LIKE MEN for God and country, and whatever happens, we're in God's hands."

Now that was a self-serving Jew saying that, but if he and a bunch of lost heathen can "ACT LIKE MEN," so can you, Christian! You have a better Commander (the Lord Jesus Christ), you have a better cause (the Gospel), and you have a better empowerment (the Holy Spirit). BE STRONG! BE COURAGEOUS! ACT LIKE A MAN!

[Back to Haggai 2:5---]{.class-0}["According to the word that I covenanted with you when ye came out of Egypt, so my spirit remaineth among you: fear ye not."]{.class-2}

[To understand the verse, you have to go back to the book of Exodus and find out what ]{.class-0}["the word"]{.class-2}[ was that He ]{.class-0}["covenanted"]{.class-2}[ with them. It is found just after the incident with the golden calf when God could have dumped Israel forever for idolatry, but He didn't do it. Moses talked the Lord out of wiping the whole nation out (Exod. 32:9--14, 31--33). (Jack Hyles used to say that if the Lord and Moses had ever gotten into a killing mood at the same time, that there wouldn't be a Jew left alive on this earth.) God made this promise to Moses:]{.class-0}

"[And the LORD said unto Moses, Depart, and go up hence, thou and the people which thou hast brought up out of the land of Egypt, unto the land which I sware unto Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, saying, Unto thy seed will I give it: And I will send an angel ]{.class-2}[\[called ]{.class-0}["mine Angel"]{.class-2}[ in Exod. 32:34, a reference to the Angel of the Lord who is the Spirit of the Lord in Hag. 2:5\]]{.class-0}[ before thee; and I will drive out the Canaanite, the Amorite, and the Hittite, and the Perizzite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite"]{.class-2}[ (Exod. 33:1--2).]{.class-0}

[By the time Moses got through dealing with the Lord, God not only promised to ]{.class-0}[lead]{.class-1}[ them, He promised His ]{.class-0}["presence"]{.class-2}[ would be ]{.class-0}[with]{.class-1}[ them (Exod. 33:14--16).]{.class-0}

[Now that's the Old Testament. The argument of people like Texe Marrs and the Catholic Church is that since the Jews rejected their Messiah, God has rejected them and is done with them as a nation forever (see the reading of 1 Thess. 2:16 in the ]{.class-0}[Amplified Version]{.class-1}[). But the Apostle Paul said, ]{.class-0}["Hath God cast away his people? GOD FORBID\....GOD HATH NOT CAST AWAY HIS PEOPLE which he foreknew"]{.class-2}[ (Rom. 11:1--2).]{.class-0}

[Even though the Jews, ]{.class-0}[as a people]{.class-1}[, are Christ-rejecters and on their way to Hell, they are still ]{.class-0}["beloved for the fathers' sakes"]{.class-2}[ (Rom. 11:28). The Lord will fulfill every promise He made to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob concerning that people; and at His return, He will save the remnant that is left (Rom. 11:25--27).]{.class-0}

[Verse 6:]{.class-0}[ "For thus saith the LORD of hosts; Yet once, it is a little while, and I will shake the heavens, and the earth, and the sea, and the dry land."]{.class-2}[ Because the verse is quoted in Hebrews 12:26, the assumption is made by ]{.class-0}[all]{.class-1}[ the commentators and expositors that the verse has been ]{.class-0}[fulfilled]{.class-1}[ with the First Advent and the preaching of the Gospel. You can take all such comments and deposit them in "File 13."]{.class-0}

[The writer of Hebrews is making a comparison to God coming down on Sinai in the book of Exodus and shaking the earth (Heb. 12:18--21) to the Lord coming back to Sinai at the Second Advent (see the comments on Hab. 3:3) and shaking both the earth ]{.class-0}[and]{.class-1}[ the heavens (both the sky and outerspace---see Matt. 24:29; Mark 13:25). While you certainly can make some ]{.class-0}[spiritual]{.class-1}[ application to the born-again, New Testament Christian in verses 22--24 of Hebrews 12, even that is referring ]{.class-0}[doctrinally]{.class-1}[ to the ]{.class-0}[national]{.class-1}[ application of the new testament to the Jews at the end of the Tribulation (see Heb. 8:8--13 and comments in that Commentary). Note the Millennial ]{.class-0}["kingdom"]{.class-2}[ that follows the Second Advent in Hebrews 12:28, with the consuming fire of God in Hebrews 12:29 just as you find it at the Second Advent (see the comments on Edom under Hab. 3:3). The expression ]{.class-0}["a little while"]{.class-2}[ in Haggai 2:6 sets the verse at the Second Advent (see the comments on Heb. 10:37--38 under Hab. 2:4), and this is the application made by the writer of Hebrews 12.]{.class-0}

"[And I will shake all nations"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 7). All 192 nations in the UN are going to get "shook up"; that includes Israel as well as all the Gentile nations (see Jer. 25).]{.class-0}

"[And the desire of all nations shall come."]{.class-2}[ Johann Sebastian Bach, a great Christian composer, used this verse to write a very profound and beautiful piece of music, a hymn entitled "Jesus, Joy of Man's Desiring." Charles Wesley did the same thing in his Christmas carol "Hark! The Herald Angels Sing"; in the last verse he wrote: "Come, Desire of Nations, come! Fix in us Thy humble home."]{.class-0}

[Now those are beautiful devotional sentiments for a Christian who loves His Saviour. There are Christians in every nation of the world who love their Lord and want to see Him come back, but that's not what the verse said, ]{.class-0}[and it has nothing to do with what the verse is talking about. ]{.class-1}[I am afraid that Bach and Wesley, in their love for Jesus Christ and their naivete, were speaking for ]{.class-0}[themselves]{.class-1}[ from the standpoint of saved, born-again children of God (2 Tim. 4:8; Tit. 2:13); not from the standpoint of a lost world that ]{.class-0}[hates]{.class-1}[ Jesus Christ (John 15:18).]{.class-0}

[The verse here is not dealing with ]{.class-0}["sheep"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["goats"]{.class-2}[ within the nations as you read about them in Matthew 25:32. It is dealing with the nations as corporate entities. Look at John 1:10--12.]{.class-0}

"[He was in the world, and the world was made by him, and the world knew him not"]{.class-2}[ (John 1:10). Well, He ]{.class-0}["loved the world"]{.class-2}[ (John 3:16), and He's ]{.class-0}["the Saviour of the world"]{.class-2}[ (John 4:42), but it's only ]{.class-0}["whosoever believeth in him"]{.class-2}[ out of the world that is saved.]{.class-0}

"[He came unto his own ]{.class-2}[\[the Jews, Israel\]]{.class-0}[, and his own received him not"]{.class-2}[ (John 1:11). That doesn't mean that there weren't individual Jews who did receive Him; that's the point Paul makes in Romans 11:1. But when it comes to the nation as a ]{.class-0}[corporate entity]{.class-1}[, Israel rejected Jesus Christ (see Acts 28:25--28).]{.class-0}

"[But as many as received him, to them gave he power to become the sons of God, even to them that believe on his name"]{.class-2}[ (John 1:12). Do you see the contrast between verse 12 and verses 10--11 in John 1? While the world as a ]{.class-0}[whole]{.class-1}[ rejected Him, and while Israel as a ]{.class-0}[whole]{.class-1}[ rejected Him, it is the ]{.class-0}["as many"]{.class-2}[ from those groups that believe on Him for salvation and receive Him as Saviour who are the ones who are saved and ]{.class-0}["become the sons of God."]{.class-2}

[The word ]{.class-0}["nations"]{.class-2}[ in Haggai 2:7 is used in the sense of John 1:10--11, not John 1:12. There never has been a time in the history of the world that the ]{.class-0}["nations"]{.class-2}[ desired Jesus Christ to ]{.class-0}["come."]{.class-2}[ You may from time to time find a ruler like Queen Victoria who expresses a personal desire for Christ to show up, but not ]{.class-0}[any]{.class-1}[ nation as a whole.]{.class-0}

[When He showed up the first time, Rome and Israel had Him crucified. Right now, Israel isn't looking for Him to come back. They don't even believe their Messiah showed up the first time; they're still looking for Him (and they're ]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ looking for Him to be Jesus Christ---see John 5:43).]{.class-0}

[Even in the United States of America, which had a foundation built on the Bible and Christianity, the name of Jesus Christ never made it into the nation's three major founding documents (he ]{.class-0}[Declaration of Independence, The Constitution,]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[The Bill of Rights]{.class-1}[). Didn't God give Jesus Christ ]{.class-0}["a name which is above every name"]{.class-2}[ (Phil. 2:9)? Then how come a bunch of Christian men and Bible-honoring Deists left that ]{.class-0}["name"]{.class-2}[ out of the founding documents of the nation they set up?]{.class-0}

[Come on, folks; do you really believe a bunch of lost Moslems (Saudi Arabia, Egypt, Libya, Iran, Iraq, Syria, Indonesia, etc.), lost Buddhists (Thailand, Cambodia, Bhutan, etc.), lost Hindus (India, Nepal), and lost atheists (Russia, China, etc.) ]{.class-0}["desire" ]{.class-2}[Jesus Christ? Are you kidding?! They outlaw His Book and kill or imprison His followers.]{.class-0}

[How about the ]{.class-0}[one nation]{.class-1}[ that claims to represent Jesus Christ---the Vatican State? Do you think the Pope wants Jesus Christ to come back? You have never heard or read ]{.class-0}[any]{.class-1}[ public statement by ]{.class-0}[any]{.class-1}[ Pope expressing a ]{.class-0}["desire"]{.class-2}[ for Jesus Christ to come back, ]{.class-0}[and you won't in the future either.]{.class-1}

[What, then, is ]{.class-0}["the desire of all nations"]{.class-2}[? Well, it's not ]{.class-0}["silver"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["gold"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 8), like you will hear J. Vernon McGee teach.]{.class-0}

[If you want to know what ]{.class-0}["all nations"]{.class-2}[ ]{.class-0}["desire," ]{.class-2}[look at what God gives to fulfill that desire. He doesn't give them ]{.class-0}["silver"]{.class-2}[ or ]{.class-0}["gold"]{.class-2}[; He gives them ]{.class-0}["peace."]{.class-2}[ Look down at verse 9.]{.class-0}

"[In this place will I give PEACE, saith the LORD of hosts."]{.class-2}

"[The desire of all nations"]{.class-2}[ is PEACE. When the Antichrist shows up, he comes to power by promising ]{.class-0}[peace]{.class-1}[ (Dan. 8:25, 11:21; 1 Thess. 5:3). Since 1901, every Pope has prayed for "peace on earth," and the Lord has paid no more attention to their prayers than He would those of an atheist.]{.class-0}

[Haggai 2:9 says that when "peace on earth" comes to this earth, it comes ]{.class-0}[first]{.class-1}[ at Jerusalem. In fact, the word ]{.class-0}[Jerusalem ]{.class-1}[means "city of peace." In the infantry that is known as "iron humor." That city was attacked ]{.class-0}[nine times]{.class-1}[ between Judges 1 and Jeremiah 52; it was attacked ]{.class-0}[25 more times]{.class-1}[ between the time of the Maccabees and A.D. 1948; and it has been attacked ]{.class-0}[twice more]{.class-1}[ since then. That's the "city of peace"; that's the Lord pulling a joke on mankind.]{.class-0}

[When Christ was born, the angel said, ]{.class-0}["Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace, good will toward men"]{.class-2}[ (Luke 2:14). Well, did ]{.class-0}["peace"]{.class-2}[ come? ]{.class-0}[No]{.class-1}[. In fact, Christ Himself said, ]{.class-0}["Think not that I am come to send peace on earth: I came not to send peace but a sword"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 10:34).]{.class-0}

[Do you see why you have to]{.class-0}[ rightly divide]{.class-1}[ that Book? If you don't, you'll make the biggest mess of things you ever saw. Jesus Christ ]{.class-0}[will]{.class-1}[ bring "peace on earth," but that's not why He came the ]{.class-0}[first time.]{.class-1}[ The first time He came to make a ]{.class-0}[division]{.class-1}[ between those who would accept Him and those who would reject Him (Matt. 10:35--37). When He comes the second time, He does bring "peace," but He brings it at the point of a ]{.class-0}[sword]{.class-1}[ (Rev. 19:15).]{.class-0}

[Folks, for "peace" to show up on this earth, you first have to give ]{.class-0}["glory to God in the highest."]{.class-2}[ Do you think ]{.class-0}[this nation is giving glory to God ]{.class-1}[when they kick Him out of the schools, forbid you to pray in His name, violate every command in His Book, and stop trusting in Him to the point where they remove "In God We Trust" from the money? ]{.class-0}[There ain't no peace coming; ]{.class-1}[you'd better "lock and load."]{.class-0}

[In that Book, there are ]{.class-0}[three qualifications]{.class-1}[ for peace. The first is ]{.class-0}["glory to God."]{.class-2}[ The next is ]{.class-0}[righteousness;]{.class-1}[ Hebrews 7:2 says that Melchisedec was ]{.class-0}["FIRST\...King of righteousness, and AFTER THAT\...King of peace."]{.class-2}[ The third is ]{.class-0}[purity;]{.class-1}[ James 3:17 says, ]{.class-0}["the wisdom that is from above is FIRST pure, THEN peaceable."]{.class-2}[ The chances of America having peace by giving God the glory, living righteously, and being pure---before the return of the Lord Jesus Christ---are those of ]{.class-0}[a snowball in Hell.]{.class-1}

Amen, amen, and amen! (If you won't amen me, I'll amen myself.) "Amen, Ruckman, give it to them!

[There will be no "peace on earth" until ]{.class-0}["The Prince of Peace"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 9:6) returns in power to the "city of peace" (Jerusalem) to ]{.class-0}["give peace"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 2:2--4; Psa. 46:4--9) by ]{.class-0}[forcibly putting down ]{.class-1}[all His enemies (Psa. 76:3--4, 110:1--6, 2:8--12). Until then, the ]{.class-0}["nations"]{.class-2}[ (all of them---Jew and Gentile) are involved in a ]{.class-0}[conspiracy against]{.class-1}[ Jesus Christ (Psa. 2:1--3); they aren't "desiring" Him.]{.class-0}

[It must be apparent by now that Haggai has left off talking about the Temple that Zerubbabel and Joshua were building, and he has jumped ahead ]{.class-0}[over 2,500 years into the future]{.class-1}[ to the Temple described in Ezekiel 40--46. When the Temple was finished in Ezra 6:15, there is no record of ]{.class-0}["the glory of the LORD"]{.class-2}[ filling it (vss. 7, 9) as you read in Exodus 40:34--35; 1 Kings 8:10--11; or 2 Chronicles 5:13--14, but it does in the ]{.class-0}[Millennial Temple]{.class-1}[ (Ezek. 43:4--5, 44:4). The glorified Christ is right there in the Temple sitting on ]{.class-0}["the throne of the LORD"]{.class-2}[ (Psa. 2:6; Isa. 2:2--3 cf. 1 Chron. 29:23).]{.class-0}

10 In the four and twentieth day of the ninth month, in the second year of Darius, came the word of the LORD by Haggai the prophet, saying,

11 Thus saith the LORD of hosts; Ask now the priests concerning the law, saying,

12 If one bear holy flesh in the skirt of his garment, and with his skirt do touch bread, or pottage, or wine, or oil, or any meat, shall it be holy? And the priests answered and said, No.

13 Then said Haggai, If one that is unclean by a dead body touch any of these, shall it be unclean? And the priests answered and said, It shall be unclean.

14 Then answered Haggai, and said, So is this people, and so is this nation before me, saith the LORD; and so is every work of their hands; and that which they offer there is unclean.

15 And now, I pray you, consider from this day and upward, from before a stone was laid upon a stone in the temple of the LORD:

16 Since those days were, when one came to an heap of twenty measures, there were but ten: when one came to the pressfat for to draw out fifty vessels out of the press, there were but twenty.

17 I smote you with blasting and with mildew and with hail in all the labours of your hands; yet ye turned not to me, saith the LORD.

18 Consider now from this day and upward, from the four and twentieth day of the ninth month, even from the day that the foundation of the LORD'S temple was laid, consider it.

19 Is the seed yet in the barn? yea, as yet the vine, and the fig tree, and the pomegranate, and the olive tree, hath not brought forth: from this day will I bless you.

[This is the second prophecy in the chapter. It is addressed ]{.class-0}["to them that know the law"]{.class-2}[ (Rom. 7:1); in this case, the priests (vs. 11). And just as Paul does in Romans 7:1--4, a spiritual application is then made to the ]{.class-0}["people"]{.class-2}[ (the Jewish remnant---vs. 2) in verses 14--19.]{.class-0}

[Verse 12: ]{.class-0}["If one bear holy flesh in the skirt of his garment]{.class-2}[ \[the priest is carrying around meat that is going to be offered or has already been sacrificed\]]{.class-0}[, and with his skirt do touch bread, or pottage, or wine, or oil, or any meat]{.class-2}[ \[the priest bumps into some kind of food that hasn't been dedicated to God\]]{.class-0}[, shall it be holy?"]{.class-2}

[Now, if we are dealing with a question ]{.class-0}["concerning the law,"]{.class-2}[ then we had better know exactly what ]{.class-0}["the law"]{.class-2}[ says. The specific law under scrutiny here is Leviticus 6:25--27.]{.class-0}

"[Speak unto Aaron and to his sons, saying, This is the law of the sin offering: In the place where the burnt offering is killed shall the sin offering be killed before the LORD: it is most holy. The priest that offereth it for sin shall eat it: in the holy place shall it be eaten, in the court of the tabernacle of the congregation. Whatsoever shall touch the flesh thereof shall be holy."]{.class-2}

[According to ]{.class-0}["the law,"]{.class-2}[ whatever touches ]{.class-0}["holy flesh"]{.class-2}[ is made ]{.class-0}["holy"]{.class-2}[ itself when it touches it. But notice how carefully the question is worded in Haggai 2:12---]{.class-0}["If one bear holy flesh in the skirt of his garment, and WITH HIS SKIRT do touch bread,"]{.class-2}[ etc. The only thing the ]{.class-0}["holy flesh"]{.class-2}[ is touching in that verse is ]{.class-0}["the skirt of his garment."]{.class-2}[ The "holiness" of the meat isn't transferred through the garment to whatever the garment touches, so the priests correctly answer, ]{.class-0}["No."]{.class-2}

[The next question Haggai asks the priests is: ]{.class-0}["If one that is unclean by a dead body touch any of these, shall it be unclean?"]{.class-2}

[The Law says, ]{.class-0}["He that toucheth the dead body of any man shall be unclean seven days\....And whatsoever the unclean person toucheth shall be unclean; and the soul that toucheth it shall be unclean until even"]{.class-2}[ (Num. 19:11, 22).]{.class-0}

[So the priests' answer is: ]{.class-0}["It shall be unclean."]{.class-2}

[That was Samson's problem in the book of Judges. Samson was ]{.class-0}["a Nazarite\...from the womb"]{.class-2}[ (Judg. 13:5), and one of the laws for the Nazarite was that he wasn't to defile his consecration by touching a dead body (Num. 6:6). If he did, he was to cut his hair and make a special offering (Num. 6:9--12). Since Samson was called to ]{.class-0}["begin to deliver Israel out of the hand of the Philistines"]{.class-2}[ (Judg. 13:5), and since the Holy Spirit blessed him with great strength to do that, it was probably God's intent for him to fight one great battle with the Philistines as Othniel, Barak, Gideon, and Jephthah did, and then to shave his head and make the offering, having fulfilled what God called him to do.]{.class-0}

[As it turned out, Samson killed a lion and got honey out of its dead body and ]{.class-0}[didn't get his hair cut ]{.class-1}[and ]{.class-0}[didn't make the offering]{.class-1}[ (Judg. 14:5--9). He killed thirty men with no haircut and ]{.class-0}[no offering]{.class-1}[ (Judg. 14:19). He picked up a ]{.class-0}["jawbone of an ass"]{.class-2}[ and slew a thousand Philistines (Judg. 15:15)---no haircut; ]{.class-0}[no offering]{.class-1}[. That went on until God Himself forced Samson to go to the barbershop (Delilah) and obey the Law. Samson also lost a wife and father-in-law (Judg. 15:6) and his own life ]{.class-0}[to fulfill his offering]{.class-1}[.]{.class-0}

[Now what's the application of the questions Haggai put to the priests? Verse 14: ]{.class-0}["So is this people, and so is this nation before me saith the LORD; and so is every work of their hands; and that which they offer there is unclean."]{.class-2}

[As I have told you before, the Old Testament tells you how God deals with ]{.class-0}[nations,]{.class-1}[ and the New Testament tells you how God deals with ]{.class-0}[individuals.]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-2}[God tells you in this passage how He deals with His own nation.]{.class-0}

Israel had gotten to the place with God where everything they did was unclean, even the things He told them to do. He told them back in Isaiah 1:11--15, "I can't stand your offerings anymore; I won't listen to your prayers anymore; and I can't take anymore of your 'worship services.'"

[So He sent in ]{.class-0}[foreign troops]{.class-1}[ who burned the Temple to the ground and drove the Jews out so that they had no homeland for seventy years. ]{.class-0}[That's how God deals with nations]{.class-1}[. Now, if God treated His own chosen nation like that, do you think He's going to let ]{.class-0}[America]{.class-1}[ get by with the way it's living?]{.class-0}

"[Consider from this day and upward, from before a stone was laid upon a stone in the temple of the LORD"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 15). That's a reference to ]{.class-0}["the day that the foundation of the LORD'S temple was laid"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 18). That took place in Ezra 3:8--10, over sixteen years earlier.]{.class-0}

[Verses 16--17 cover the same material found in 1:6, 11, and you can see the comments on those verses. The passage hearkens back to what God did to Israel ]{.class-0}[before]{.class-1}[ sending them into captivity to the Assyrians (cf. Amos 4:6--11 and note the cross-reference in Amos 4:9 to Hag. 2:17), as well as to the famine God sends in the Tribulation (vs. 16 cf. Rev. 6:5--6).]{.class-0}

Verse 19 refers to the unfruitfulness of Israel as a nation, and for that, we refer you to the comments on Habakkuk 3:17.

[The second prophecy ends with: ]{.class-0}["from this day will I bless you."]{.class-2}[ In other words, although up to this point the nation has been unclean and unfruitful, and God has had to judge it, from this point on, the Lord will restore them and bless them.]{.class-0}

[That is how things are going with Israel right now. They've returned to the land in disbelief and disobedience, just as the Jews did in Ezra 1--3. They are still experiencing the judgment of God and the opposition of the heathen as the Jews did in Ezra 4. But in the Tribulation, things will start to turn around. They will repent and get right with God, and at the Second Advent, the Lord will give them ]{.class-0}[complete restoration]{.class-1}[ and blessing as He promised in Haggai 2:19.]{.class-0}

[Verse 18: ]{.class-0}["Consider now from this day and upward, from the four and twentieth day of the ninth month."]{.class-2}[ Now I have considered it. I have gone over it many times, and I just can't get a lead on it. This is the third time in the book that the number 24 has shown up. It showed up the first time in 1:15 and again in 2:10; it shows up in 2:20 also. I am sure it has something to do with the rebuilding of the Temple at the Second Advent, but it will take somebody who knows more about numbers than I do to figure the thing out.]{.class-0}

20 And again the word of the LORD came unto Haggai in the four and twentieth day of the month, saying,

21 Speak to Zerubbabel, governor of Judah, saying, I will shake the heavens and the earth;

22 And I will overthrow the throne of kingdoms, and I will destroy the strength of the kingdoms of the heathen; and I will overthrow the chariots, and those that ride in them; and the horses and their riders shall come down, every one by the sword of his brother.

23 In that day, saith the LORD of hosts, will I take thee, O Zerubbabel, my servant, the son of Shealtiel, saith the LORD, and will make thee as a signet: for I have chosen thee, saith the LORD of hosts.

We have come to the third and last prophecy in Haggai 2, and it is addressed to Zerubbabel as a type of Christ at the Second Advent. Look at verse 21.

"[I will shake the heavens and the earth."]{.class-2}

[That's what He said back in verse 6. It's a reference to the Second Advent in ]{.class-0}[both places]{.class-1}[ (see the comments on vs. 6).]{.class-0}

[Joel 3:16 says, ]{.class-0}["The LORD also shall roar out of Zion, and utter his voice from Jerusalem; and the heavens and the earth shall shake."]{.class-2}[ What's the context? Zephaniah 3:8 in verse 11; Matthew 25: 31--32 in verse 12; Revelation 14:18--20 in verse 13; Revelation 16:14--16 in verses 9, 10, 14; and Revelation 6:12--13 in verse 15. Second Advent, Second Advent, Second Advent, Second Advent, Second Advent. The "shaking" is described in Revelation 6:14--17.]{.class-0}

"[And the heaven departed as a scroll when it is rolled together; and every mountain and island were moved out of their places. And the kings of the earth, and the great men, and the rich men, and the chief captains, and the mighty men, and every bondman, and every free man hid themselves in the dens and in the rocks of the mountains; And said to the mountains and rocks, Fall on us, and hide us from the face of him that sitteth on the throne, and from the wrath of the Lamb: For the great day of his wrath is come; and who shall be able to stand?"]{.class-2}

And of course, there are all kinds of Scriptures describing the overthrow of the Gentile kingdoms by the Lord Jesus Christ at His return (vs. 22). We will merely list some of the references here for the readers' further study: Psalm 46:6--9, 68:8--30, 76:1--12, 83:1--18, 110:1--6, 149:6--9; Isaiah 24:19--23, 34:1--6, 66:15--18; Jeremiah 25:30--33; Ezekiel 39:17--21; Daniel 2:44; Micah 5:5--15; Zephaniah 3:6--8; Zechariah 12:3--9, 14:3, 12--15; Revelation 16:13--14, 19:17--21.

[While the context of the prophecy is clearly the Second Advent (vss. 21--22), verse 23 actually marks Zerubbabel as a type of Christ (cf. Zech. 4:7--10). Notice the designation ]{.class-0}["my servant"]{.class-2}[; that is what Christ is called in Isaiah 42:1, 43:10. In those passages, He is also called ]{.class-0}["mine elect"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["my servant whom I have chosen,"]{.class-2}[ just like the Lord said of Zerubbabel:]{.class-0}[ "for I have chosen thee."]{.class-2}

[But what stands out in the passage is the phrase: ]{.class-0}["and will make thee as a signet."]{.class-2}[ As we pointed out in the comments on 1:12, Zerubbabel was the grandson of Jeconiah (Matt. 1:12), the King of Judah whose line God cursed (Jer. 22:28--30). The "Je" at the front of Jeconiah's name is an abbreviation for ]{.class-0}[Jehovah.]{.class-1}[ God took His name off the man and said this about him:]{.class-0}

"[As I live, saith the LORD, though Coniah the son of Jehoiakim king of Judah were the SIGNET upon my right hand, yet would I pluck thee thence"]{.class-2}[ (Jer. 22:24).]{.class-0}

[With Zerubbabel, the Lord picks up the ]{.class-0}["signet"]{.class-2}[ He cast away with Coniah. Of course, it is a ]{.class-0}[partial]{.class-1}[ restoration of the genealogical line, for Zerubbabel is only a Governor and not the King. But because of that restoration, Christ claims the ]{.class-0}[legal]{.class-1}[ right to the throne of David through Zerubbabel going back to David's son Solomon and the genealogical right to the throne through Mary back to David's son Nathan (Luke 3:23, 31).]{.class-0}

Haggai's prophecy ends with the Lord addressing His Son (see Psa. 2) through the person of Zerubbabel, exactly as He did with David in the Psalms (see Acts 2:25--35). We will save further comments on Zerubbabel for the following book---Zechariah.

[]{#section-0_1_bb3412c05490f55b_b68c.xhtml}

INTRODUCTION TO

ZECHARIAH

Now as I said in the preface to this work, one of the reasons I put off this second volume on the Minor Prophets for many years was because of the book of Zechariah. There are certain things in the Bible which I have read literally scores of times, and I am no closer to understanding them now than I was when I first laid eyes on them. I have reached my limit of understanding on those passages, and that's as far as I can go.

[Brethren, there are things in that Book that you can't learn except by the ]{.class-0}[revelation of the Holy Spirit;]{.class-1}[ you can't learn them simply by ]{.class-0}[study.]{.class-1}[ Now the Scriptures command me to ]{.class-0}["study"]{.class-2}[ them (2 Tim. 2:15), so I study. I am ]{.class-0}[commanded]{.class-1}[ to read the Scriptures (1 Tim. 4:13), so I read them. I am told to ]{.class-0}[pray for understanding]{.class-1}[ (Psa. 119:169) and wisdom (James 1:5), so I do. But even with all of that, there are still things in the Bible that stump me, and until the Lord shows me some things about them, the only thing I will be able to do is read the verse and say, ]{.class-0}["Ich weiss nicht"]{.class-1}[ (German for "I don't know").]{.class-0}

[Of course, I will give you what I can, and some places in Zechariah are very clear (e.g., chaps. 12--14). But I haven't got a corner on truth. My enemies say, "Ruckman thinks he's right and everyone else is wrong." When I know something to be ]{.class-0}[absolutely so ]{.class-1}[from the Scriptures, you bet your life, brother. The Book is right, and any other opinion is not worthy of consideration. But when it comes to some things in the Book, I don't have the whole thing nailed down, and (up to this point) neither does anyone else.]{.class-0}

[I will tell you something else: ]{.class-0}[education]{.class-1}[ has ]{.class-0}[nothing]{.class-1}[ to do with God showing you something in the Book.
