I have seen God show all kinds of stuff from the Bible to folks who had ]{.class-0}[no education]{.class-1}[ at all. If the Lord wants you to know something from that Book, He will show it to you regardless of your education or lack of it, and if He doesn't, He won't.]{.class-0}

[When it comes to these things, I have absolutely no pride at all. The Lord may show you something in Zechariah that He's left hidden to me. If He does, more power to you, brother. My concern is ]{.class-0}[that Book,]{.class-1}[ not what someone thinks ]{.class-0}[of me.]{.class-1}

Now, Zechariah is Haggai's buddy from Ezra 5:1. Zechariah is the second post-Exilic prophet in the Minor Prophets, and he wrote at the same time Haggai did (520 B.C.). The book of Zechariah has 14 chapters, 211 verses, and 6,443 words.

[The word ]{.class-0}[Zechariah]{.class-1}[ means "Jehovah remembers." In Zechariah 1:1, he is identified as ]{.class-0}["the son of Berechiah."]{.class-2}[ Because of that, he is sometimes equated with ]{.class-0}["Zacharias son of Barachias"]{.class-2}[ in Matthew 23:35. The problem with that is that the Zechariah of Matthew 23:35 was slain ]{.class-0}["between the temple and the altar."]{.class-2}[ Unless the Zechariah of the Minor Prophets died a death which was not recorded anywhere in the Old Testament or tradition (which is highly unlikely given the events recorded in Ezra 6--7), Matthew 23:35 is a reference to the murder of ]{.class-0}["Zechariah the son of Jehoiada the priest"]{.class-2}[ in 2 Chronicles 24:20--21.]{.class-0}

[You see, Matthew 23:35 is one of two definitive statements given by Jesus Christ on the canon of the Old Testament (the other being Luke 24:44; see the comments on Matt. 23:35 in that Commentary). A Hebrew Old Testament is arranged such that it begins with Genesis (]{.class-0}["from the blood of righteous Abel"]{.class-2}[) and ends with 2 Chronicles (]{.class-0}["unto the blood of Zacharias son of Barachias"]{.class-2}[---2 Chron. 24:20--21). That being the case, how can the Zechariah of 2 Chronicles 24:20 be the ]{.class-0}["son of Barachias"]{.class-2}[ when the verse clearly states that his father was ]{.class-0}["Jehoiada the priest"]{.class-2}[?]{.class-0}

[Well, the Jewish Rabbis say this: they say Jehoiada was called "the blessed one," and they have good reason for that if you study what he did in 2 Chronicles 23:12--21, 24:12--16. In Hebrew, "the blessed one" would be ]{.class-0}[Barach-jah;]{.class-1}[ the transliteration of which in Greek would be ]{.class-0}["Barachias,"]{.class-2}[ just as you read in Matthew 23:35. So I take the Zechariah of the book of Zechariah to be different from the one mentioned by Christ in Matthew 23:35.]{.class-0}

Both Zechariah and Haggai prophesied about the rebuilding of the Temple under Zerubbabel the Governor and Joshua the high priest, but as we saw in Haggai, these prophecies often go far beyond any Temple in 520 B.C. or A.D. 4--30 (John 2:20). Once more we find ourselves reading about the Second Advent, with a few references to Christ's First Coming (see Zech. 9:9, 11:12--13, 13:7).

[Like Zephaniah, Zechariah gives the future of the UN "written in the sky" (read chap. 14). That is why the book is ]{.class-0}[ignored]{.class-1}[ by all major politicians and religious leaders. It tells you that God Almighty is going to blow all 191 Gentile nations in the UN to smithereens and then take over as the Supreme Dictator of the world. That's the "meek and lowly" Saviour (Zech. 9:9) who was "wounded for your transgressions" (Zech. 13:6). The "Good Shepherd" (Zech. 13:7) is coming back to ]{.class-0}[clean their plow]{.class-1}[ (Zech. 14:1--3).]{.class-0}

[]{#section-0_1_d156e6236e1958b7_b68d.xhtml}

CHAPTER ONE

1:1 In the eighth month, in the second year of Darius, came the word of the LORD unto Zechariah, the son of Berechiah, the son of Iddo the prophet, saying,

2 The LORD hath been sore displeased with your fathers.

3 Therefore say thou unto them, Thus saith the LORD of hosts; Turn ye unto me, saith the LORD of hosts, and I will turn unto you, saith the LORD of hosts.

4 Be ye not as your fathers, unto whom the former prophets have cried, saying, Thus saith the LORD of hosts; Turn ye now from your evil ways, and from your evil doings: but they did not hear, nor hearken unto me, saith the LORD.

5 Your fathers, where are they? and the prophets, do they live for ever?

6 But my words and my statutes, which I commanded my servants the prophets, did they not take hold of your fathers? and they returned and said, Like as the LORD of hosts thought to do unto us, according to our ways, and according to our doings, so hath he dealt with us.

The commentators usually divide Zechariah's prophecy into two parts: chapters 1--8 consisting of a series of visions and chapters 9--14 comprised of two major "burdens" (9:1, 12:1) \[some of them separate chapters 13--14 off as a third division\]. The first six verses of chapter 1 are usually considered an introduction or preface to the rest of the book.

[Verse 1: ]{.class-0}["In the eighth month, in the second year of Darius, came the word of the LORD unto Zechariah."]{.class-2}[ So Zechariah begins his prophecy between Haggai 2:1--9 and Haggai 2:10--23.]{.class-0}

"[Zechariah, the son of Berechiah, the son of Iddo the prophet."]{.class-2}[ Obviously if you compare Scripture with Scripture, ]{.class-0}["Iddo"]{.class-2}[ is the ]{.class-0}[grandfather]{.class-1}[ of Zechariah (cf. Ezra 5:1). In the Bible, a father can not only be one's literal father but a father-in-law (Luke 3:23; 1 Sam. 24:16), a step-father (Luke 3:23 cf. Matt. 1:16), or any ancestor (Matt. 1:1).]{.class-0}

[Some of the expositors equate ]{.class-0}["Iddo the prophet"]{.class-2}[ in verse 1 with a priest of the same name who also had a son named Zechariah and who returned in the captivity (see Neh. 1:1, 4, 16). If that were the case, then Zechariah was both a prophet ]{.class-0}[and]{.class-1}[ priest like Samuel (1 Sam. 3), Jeremiah (Jer. 1:--2), and Ezekiel (Ezek. 1:2).]{.class-0}

[Verse 2: ]{.class-0}["The LORD hath been sore displeased with your fathers."]{.class-2}[ The word ]{.class-0}["sore"]{.class-2}[ is not used here in the sense of pain (see Gen. 34:25) or a painful area on the skin (like an ulcer or boil---see Lev. 13:42--43). It is used here as an adverb; it means "greatly, "deeply," or "extremely," like you read in Luke 2:9 (]{.class-0}["and they were sore afraid"]{.class-2}[). We would say today "He was ]{.class-0}[very]{.class-1}[ displeased," but it wouldn't carry the depth of meaning that the ]{.class-0}[KJV]{.class-1}[ word has.]{.class-0}

Now you didn't need a knowledge of Greek or Hebrew to figure that out. A good dictionary (like Webster's 1828 Dictionary) could have told you how the word was being used, but you didn't even need that to figure the thing out. Look down at verse 15 in the same chapter.

"[And I am very SORE DISPLEASED with the heathen that are at ease: for I was but A LITTLE DISPLEASED, and they helped forward the affliction."]{.class-2}

[Notice the contrast between ]{.class-0}["sore displeased"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["a little displeased."]{.class-2}[ Now tell me that anyone who could read fifth grade English couldn't figure that out. The problem is that people don't ]{.class-0}[read]{.class-1}[ the Bible and they don't ]{.class-0}[study]{.class-1}[ it.]{.class-0}

"[The LORD hath been sore displeased with your fathers."]{.class-2}[ This was ]{.class-0}["the fierce anger of the LORD"]{.class-2}[ you read about back in Zephaniah 2:2. It was Nebuchadnezzar coming in and tearing everything up, burning everything down, slaughtering a bunch of folk, and carrying the rest into captivity for seventy years. You can see the whole thing described in detail in the book of Lamentations.]{.class-0}

"[Therefore say thou unto them, Thus saith the LORD of hosts; Turn ye unto me\...and I will turn unto you"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 3).]{.class-0}

That's good preaching for any dispensation. You turn to God, and He'll turn to you.

[Isaiah said, ]{.class-0}["Let the wicked forsake his way, and the unrighteous man his thoughts: and let him return unto the LORD, and he will have mercy upon him; and to our God, for he will abundantly pardon"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 55:7).]{.class-0}

[The invitation from the Lord is ]{.class-0}["come"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 11:28; Rev. 22:17), and the promise to all those who respond to that invitation is: ]{.class-0}["him that cometh to me I will in no wise cast out"]{.class-2}[ (John 6:37). I tell folks, "You take Christ, and He'll take you."]{.class-0}

"[Be ye not as your fathers"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 4). He's talking about the bunch to whom Isaiah, Jeremiah, Ezekiel, Hosea, Amos, Micah, Habakkuk, and Zephaniah prophesied (]{.class-0}["unto whom the former prophets have cried"]{.class-2}[). All those prophets preached: "Repent!" "Get right!" "Turn or burn!" "Repent or perish!" The people didn't listen to them; that's why they went into captivity.]{.class-0}

"[They did not hear, nor hearken, unto me, saith the LORD"]{.class-2}[---direct, deliberate refusal to listen to what God says in His Book.]{.class-0}

[Jesus said, ]{.class-0}["He that is of God heareth God's WORDS: ye therefore hear them not, because ye are not of God"]{.class-2}[ (John 8:47).]{.class-0}

[Note, again, that it is the WORDS of God to which you are to listen, not just the "Word" of God (]{.class-0}["my WORDS"]{.class-2}[---vs. 6). The purpose of higher education---secular or Christian---is to talk you out of faith in the Book and convince you ]{.class-0}[that you don't have His WORDS.]{.class-1}

[Verse 5---]{.class-0}["Your fathers, where are they?"]{.class-2}[ They're dead. Nebuchadnezzar came in and slaughtered most of them, and the ones he didn't, died in captivity over in Babylon. ]{.class-0}["And the prophets, do they live for ever?"]{.class-2}[ Well, not down here they don't. The idea is that both the ]{.class-0}["fathers"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["the prophets"]{.class-2}[ were long dead. But note the contrast in verse 6.]{.class-0}

"[But my words and my statutes, which I commanded my servants the prophets"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 6). In other words, even though the prophets were all dead, the ]{.class-0}[words of God]{.class-1}[ which they spoke still lived on in the Scriptures.]{.class-0}

[Jesus said, ]{.class-0}["Heaven and earth shall pass away, but my words shall not pass away"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 24:35).]{.class-0}

[Simon Peter wrote, ]{.class-0}["Being born again, not of corruptible seed, but of incorruptible, by the word of God, which liveth and abideth for ever. For all flesh is as grass, and all the glory of man as the flower of grass. The grass withereth, and the flower thereof falleth away: But the word of the Lord endureth for ever"]{.class-2}[ (1 Pet. 1:23--25).]{.class-0}

"[But my words\...did they not take hold of your fathers?"]{.class-2}[ Zechariah's talking about the words of Scripture as if they're alive and grabbing folks.]{.class-0}

[Hebrews 4:12--13 says, ]{.class-0}["For the word of God is quick]{.class-2}[ \[meaning it's alive---cf. 2 Tim. 4:1; 1 Pet. 4:5)\]]{.class-0}[, and powerful, and sharper than any twoedged sword, piercing even to the dividing asunder of soul and spirit, and of the joints and marrow, and is a discerner of the thoughts and intents of the heart ]{.class-2}[\[that's not God doing that; it's His ]{.class-0}[word.]{.class-1}[ That Book knows all about you and tells it\]]{.class-0}[. Neither is there any creature that is not manifest in his sight: but all things are naked and opened unto the eyes of him with whom we have to do."]{.class-2}

[The word of God there is spoken of as a]{.class-0}[ living person ]{.class-1}[that can see things. Psalm 147:15 says, ]{.class-0}["his word runneth very swiftly."]{.class-2}[ Martin Luther said the Bible had "]{.class-0}[eyes]{.class-1}[ to see with, ]{.class-0}[hands]{.class-1}[ to grip with, and ]{.class-0}[feet]{.class-1}[ to run with." It's ]{.class-0}[alive]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[active.]{.class-1}

"[Like as the LORD of hosts thought to do unto us, according to our ways, and according to our doings, so hath he dealt with us."]{.class-2}[ You ignore that Book to your own peril and hurt.]{.class-0}

[In Proverbs, the Lord said, ]{.class-0}["Because I have called, and ye refused; I have stretched out my hand, and no man regarded; But ye have set at nought all my counsel, and would none of my reproof: I also will laugh at your calamity; I will mock when your fear cometh; When your fear cometh as desolation, and your destruction cometh as a whirlwind; when distress and anguish cometh upon you"]{.class-2}[ (Prov. 1:24--27).]{.class-0}

7 Upon the four and twentieth day of the eleventh month, which is the month Sebat, in the second year of Darius, came the word of the LORD unto Zechariah, the son of Berechiah, the son of Iddo the prophet, saying,

8 I saw by night, and behold a man riding upon a red horse, and he stood among the myrtle trees that were in the bottom; and behind him were there red horses, speckled, and white.

9 Then said I, O my lord, what are these? And the angel that talked with me said unto me, I will shew thee what these be.

10 And the man that stood among the myrtle trees answered and said, These are they whom the LORD hath sent to walk to and fro through the earth.

11 And they answered the angel of the LORD that stood among the myrtle trees, and said, We have walked to and fro through the earth, and, behold, all the earth sitteth still, and is at rest.

"[The month Sebat"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 7; also spelled ]{.class-0}[Shebat]{.class-1}[) is January--February on our calendar; as the verse says, it is ]{.class-0}["the eleventh month"]{.class-2}[ in the Jewish year. So Zechariah's visions begin after Haggai has finished his prophecies in Haggai 2 (note vss. 10 and 20 of that chapter). Now that doesn't mean that Haggai stopped prophesying. Both Haggai's and Zechariah's ministries went on for four more years (see Ezra 6:14--15), but all we have recorded of Haggai's prophecies are those in the second year of Darius' reign.]{.class-0}

[Now this brings up the question of the ]{.class-0}[canon]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[content]{.class-1}[ of the Scriptures. You see, the prophets spoke much more of what God gave them than what is recorded in the Scriptures. If you think the only prophesying Jonah did was what is found in the book he wrote, you are sadly mistaken (see 2 Kings 14:25). So all the apostate scholars are always looking for the "lost books of the Bible" like ]{.class-0}["the book of Jasher"]{.class-2}[ (Josh. 10:13; 2 Sam. 1:18) and ]{.class-0}["the epistle from Laodicea"]{.class-2}[ (Col. 4:16) and the so-called ]{.class-0}["Book of Enoch"]{.class-1}[ (Jude 14).]{.class-0}

[The fact of the matter is that God intentionally ]{.class-0}[left things out]{.class-1}[ of the Scriptures that He said, just like He ]{.class-0}[added]{.class-1}[ things to the Scriptures (Jer. 36:32). If you were able to get Jeremiah's "original manuscript" (Jer. 36:4), you would ]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ have what God wanted you to have in the book of Jeremiah. If someone did find Paul's epistle to the Laodiceans, they would be ]{.class-0}[cursed by God]{.class-1}[ if they put it into the canon of Scripture (Prov. 30:1; Rev. 22:18), because the Holy Spirit ]{.class-0}[purposefully left it out.]{.class-1}[ The mad dash to find some "lost book" of the Bible is just one more Satanic attempt to destroy the authority of the ]{.class-0}[Scriptures God gave you.]{.class-1}

[Brethren, the authority is ]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ in God's ]{.class-0}[spoken]{.class-1}[ words. Look at what Peter wrote in 2 Peter 1:17--20.]{.class-0}

"[For he received from God the Father honour and glory, when there came such a voice to him from the excellent glory, This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased. And this voice which came from heaven we heard\...We have also a MORE SURE WORD OF PROPHECY"]{.class-2}[ (2 Pet. 1:17--19).]{.class-0}

"[More sure"]{.class-2}[ than what? ]{.class-0}["More sure"]{.class-2}[ than the ]{.class-0}[audible voice of Almighty God.]{.class-1}[ What is that ]{.class-0}["sure word of prophecy"]{.class-2}[? It is the ]{.class-0}["prophecy of the SCRIPTURE"]{.class-2}[ (2 Pet. 1:20). My final authority is ]{.class-0}[the Book I hold in my hand.]{.class-1}[ It is not some lost pile of papers (the "original manuscripts") that no one has ever seen, and it sure ain't any Neo-orthodox "message" that's not comprised of ]{.class-0}[words.]{.class-1}

[Zechariah 1:8---]{.class-0}["I saw by night."]{.class-2}[ Now that's a prophetic reference to the Church Age or the Tribulation (see the comments on Hab. 2:1).]{.class-0}

"[And behold, a man riding upon a red horse, and he stood among the myrtle trees that were in the bottom; and behind him were there red horses, speckled, and white."]{.class-2}[ And there, I'm ]{.class-0}[out,]{.class-1}[ man; I don't know what on earth that is. I can identify some things in the verse, and I can give you a few cross-references, but when it comes to any kind of intelligible exposition of the passage, the only thing I have to say is: ]{.class-0}["Ich weiss nicht."]{.class-1}

[Now, the ]{.class-0}["man"]{.class-2}[ in the passage is identified in verses 9--11 as ]{.class-0}["the angel of the LORD."]{.class-2}[ In 3:1--2, that is Jesus Christ in His pre-incarnate state. I know where another ]{.class-0}["red horse"]{.class-2}[ is. It's the second horse of the "four horsemen of the Apocalypse" in Revelation 6, and it represents ]{.class-0}[war.]{.class-1}

"[And there went out another horse that was red: and power was given to him that sat thereon to take peace from the earth, and that they should kill one another: and there was given unto him a great sword"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 6:4).]{.class-0}

[The problem is, ]{.class-0}[Jesus Christ ]{.class-1}[isn't the rider of ]{.class-0}[any]{.class-1}[ of the horses in Revelation 6. Now if you went to any major Seminary of any mainline denomination in the country (Presbyterian, Methodist, Southern Baptist, etc.), you would be taught that the white horse rider of Revelation 6:2 is Jesus Christ because of Revelation 19:11--13. In Revelation 6, He's supposed to be spiritually "bringing in the kingdom" to conquer the world. After that you can write ]{.class-0}[rubbish.]{.class-1}

[Now we have gone into great detail on this mysterious "Mr. X" in the ]{.class-0}[Bible Believer's Commentary on Revelation]{.class-1}[ (chap. 6) and the work on ]{.class-0}[The Mark of the Beast,]{.class-1}[ so we will just give a short synopsis here. The white horse rider of Revelation 6 is ]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ Jesus Christ, although he is such a good ]{.class-0}[imitation]{.class-1}[ that he can fool all the Amillennial and Postmillennial expositors and commentators. The rider of Revelation 6:2 has "a crown," while Jesus Christ has ]{.class-0}["many crowns"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 19:12). The weapon of the white horse rider of Revelation 6 is a ]{.class-0}["bow"]{.class-2}[; Jesus Christ has ]{.class-0}["a sharp sword"]{.class-2}[ that goes ]{.class-0}["out of his mouth"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 19:15). Since the bow of the rider in Revelation 6 has no arrows, he conquers ]{.class-0}[peacefully]{.class-1}[ (Dan. 11:21, 24, 8:25); Jesus Christ comes back to take ]{.class-0}["the kingdoms of this world"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 11:15) by a ]{.class-0}[blood bath]{.class-1}[ (Rev. 14:20, 19:13--21; Isa. 63:1--4). ]{.class-0}[The white horse rider of Revelation 6:2 ]{.class-1}[is ]{.class-0}[obviously the Antichrist.]{.class-1}

[Back to verse 8:]{.class-0}[ "and he]{.class-2}[ \[i.e., the Angel of the Lord\]]{.class-0}[ stood among the myrtle trees that were in the bottom."]{.class-2}[ Well, that ]{.class-0}["bottom"]{.class-2}[ is the rich soil in the bottom of a valley. Folks in North Carolina and Tennessee like that bottom land because it's good land for growing crops; those hills have so many rocks in them that you can hardly grow anything there. But what those ]{.class-0}["myrtle trees\...in the bottom"]{.class-2}[ have to do with the Lord Jesus Christ riding the ]{.class-0}["red horse"]{.class-2}[ of war in the Church Age, I have no idea, man. The myrtle tree is connected with the restoration of Israel in the Millennium (Isa. 41:19, 55:13) and the Feast of Tabernacles that will be celebrated then (Neh. 8:15). What ]{.class-0}[that]{.class-1}[ has to do with the text here, I don't know.]{.class-0}

"[And behind him]{.class-2}[ \[the Angel of the Lord again\]]{.class-0}[ were there red horses, speckled, and white."]{.class-2}[ Now I've got the red horse and the white horse identified over there in Revelation 6: they're ]{.class-0}[war]{.class-1}[ and the ]{.class-0}[Antichrist.]{.class-1}[ But what those ]{.class-0}["speckled"]{.class-2}[ horses are beats the fire out of me. The other two horses in Revelation 6 are the black horse of famine (Rev. 6:5--6) and the pale horse of ]{.class-0}["Death, and Hell"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 6:8); there ain't no ]{.class-0}["speckled"]{.class-2}[ ones there (and changing ]{.class-0}["speckled"]{.class-2}[ to "dapple" or "sorrel" does nothing to help you understand the text).]{.class-0}

[Verse 9:]{.class-0}[ "Then said I, O my lord, what are these?"]{.class-2}[ Zechariah's asking the Angel of the Lord about that bunch of red and white speckled horses behind that red horse.]{.class-0}

"[And the angel that talked with me said unto me, I will shew thee what these be."]{.class-2}[ ]{.class-0}[No, you won't; quit your lying!]{.class-1}[ (Just kidding, folks. That's just my old Adamic nature rising up after I've read through the text more than 150 times and studied with 22 commentaries and five Hebrew lexicons, and I STILL AIN'T GOT THE THING YET!)]{.class-0}

"[These are they whom the LORD hath sent to walk to and fro through the earth"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 10). Then I know this for sure about those horses: they aren't physical horses.]{.class-0}

[Over in Job, the Lord asks Satan, "Where've you been?" Satan answers, ]{.class-0}["From going to and fro in the earth, and from walking up and down in it"]{.class-2}[ (Job 1:7, 2:2). That's a ]{.class-0}[spiritual]{.class-1}[ being going through the earth ]{.class-0}["seeking whom he may devour"]{.class-2}[ (1 Pet. 5:8).]{.class-0}

[The horses here in Zechariah 1:10 are ]{.class-0}[spiritual]{.class-1}[ creatures made of fire (2 Kings 2:11), that can become visible or invisible at will (2 Kings 6:17), and can pass through solid objects if necessary (Joel 2:9). That's why the verse says they ]{.class-0}["walk to and fro THROUGH the earth."]{.class-2}

[Verse 11: ]{.class-0}["And they answered."]{.class-2}[ Who's the ]{.class-0}["they"]{.class-2}[? Well, in the context, it's the ]{.class-0}["horses"]{.class-2}[ of verse 8. Of course, if that were the case, it wouldn't be the first time that God caused something like a horse to talk (Num. 22:28; 2 Pet. 2:16). But it's probably a reference to the riders on the horses (cf. Joel 2:4).]{.class-0}

"[We have walked to and fro through the earth, and, behold, all the earth sitteth still, and it is at rest."]{.class-2}[ Now, the only time ]{.class-0}["the earth\...is at rest"]{.class-2}[ is in the Millennium. When Gog and Magog get ready to attack Jerusalem at the end of the Millennium (Rev. 20:7--9), they say, ]{.class-0}["I will go up to the land of unwalled villages; I will go to them that are at rest, that dwell safely, all of them dwelling without walls, and having neither bars nor gates"]{.class-2}[ (Ezek. 38:11).]{.class-0}

Now, the application the Lord's going to put on the vision in verses 12--17 is the restoration of Israel in Ezra and Nehemiah, which is a picture of Israel from 1917 to the beginning of the Millennium. So generally speaking, the vision has something to do with the Second Advent, but I'm at a loss as to the details.

12 Then the angel of the LORD answered and said, O LORD of hosts, how long wilt thou not have mercy on Jerusalem and on the cities of Judah, against which thou hast had indignation these threescore and ten years?

13 And the LORD answered the angel that talked with me with good words and comfortable words.

14 So the angel that communed with me said unto me, Cry thou, saying, Thus saith the LORD of hosts; I am jealous for Jerusalem and for Zion with a great jealousy.

15 And I am very sore displeased with the heathen that are at ease: for I was but a little displeased, and they helped forward the affliction.

16 Therefore thus saith the LORD; I am returned to Jerusalem with mercies: my house shall be built in it, saith the LORD of hosts, and a line shall be stretched forth upon Jerusalem.

17 Cry yet, saying, Thus saith the LORD of hosts; My cities through prosperity shall yet be spread abroad; and the LORD shall yet comfort Zion, and shall yet choose Jerusalem.

[The ]{.class-0}["threescore and ten years"]{.class-2}[ is the seventy-year Babylonian Captivity prophesied by Jeremiah in Jeremiah 25:11--12, 29:10. The promise in those passages was that Nebuchadnezzar would destroy Judah and Jerusalem and take the people into captivity in Babylon, and that they would remain there for seventy years. At the end of that time, God would send in another nation to destroy the Chaldeans and send the Jews back to the land of Israel. That's the stuff about which you read in the book of Daniel.]{.class-0}

[Now, all that was fulfilled in Ezra 1, but that was nearly seventeen years before what you are reading here. In those seventeen years, the Jews started to build their Temple again, but they were stopped by the "Palestinians" (identified with the ]{.class-0}[Arabians]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[Jordanians]{.class-1}[ in Neh. 2:19, 4:17) lying about them to the King of Persia (Ezra 4). In light of all that, the Angel of the Lord (Jesus Christ in 3:1--2) asks Jehovah, "I thought your judgment against this people was supposed to end after seventy years. How much longer are you going to be angry with them and take it out on them?"]{.class-0}

[That's what I keep asking the Lord: "Haven't you whipped your chosen people enough?" Evidently not. ]{.class-0}[He's whipped them for nineteen centuries,]{.class-1}[ and He's not done yet. He had six million of them killed in the last century, and ]{.class-0}[He's going to kill about twice that many before it's all over. ]{.class-1}[According to the Apostle Paul, that's to be a lesson to you as a ]{.class-0}[Gentile nation ]{.class-1}[(Rom. 11:22), ]{.class-0}[but it has been ignored by every President since Eisenhower ]{.class-1}[(with the possible exception of Reagan).]{.class-0}

"[And the LORD answered the angel that talked with me with good words and comfortable words"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 13). Well, prophetically speaking, He will (Rom. 11:25--27), but that doesn't take place until after the Tribulation.]{.class-0}

[Verse 14:]{.class-0}[ "I am jealous for Jerusalem and for Zion with a great jealousy."]{.class-2}[ Jealousy is one of the attributes of God (Exod. 20:4--5, 34:19). That's what Liberals don't like---]{.class-0}[a jealous God.]{.class-1}[ That's because they're stepping out on the Lord (James 4:4).]{.class-0}

[Jealousy is ]{.class-0}[normal]{.class-1}[ in a love relationship. Lady, if your husband wouldn't mind another guy messing around with you, he doesn't care much about you; and sister, if you don't mind your husband shacking up with another woman, then you don't think much of him. If you love your spouse, you are going to have some jealousy.]{.class-0}

[Now, the Lord's jealousy is a ]{.class-0}[perfect]{.class-1}[ jealousy (2 Cor. 11:2). It's not a paranoid or covetous or envious kind of thing. The Lord created you and provides for you, and He rightly expects something from the relationship.]{.class-0}

[Over in the Song of Solomon you read: ]{.class-0}["jealousy is cruel as the grave: the coals thereof are coals of FIRE which hath a most vehement FLAME"]{.class-2}[ (Song of Sol. 6:8).]{.class-0}

[The reason a man ends up in Hell is because of ]{.class-0}[the jealousy of God.]{.class-1}[ What do I mean by that? Well, the Lord loved you enough to send His Son to die in your place and take the judgment ]{.class-0}[you deserved]{.class-1}[ for your sin in ]{.class-0}[Himself]{.class-1}[. He sent His Holy Spirit to convict you of your sin and woo you to Jesus Christ. He gave you His word containing ]{.class-0}["exceeding great and precious promises"]{.class-2}[ of salvation (2 Pet. 1:4). And with all that, you turn Him down. ]{.class-0}[How do you think the Lord is going to react to that?]{.class-1}

[Proverbs 6:34--35 says, ]{.class-0}["For jealousy is the rage of a man\...."]{.class-2}[ If a man can get jealous enough to shoot a guy for messing with his daughter or wife, can you imagine what the jealousy of a Being of perfect righteousness and perfect holiness must be like?]{.class-0}

"[\...therefore he will not spare in the day of vengeance\...."]{.class-2}[ Why, that's a reference to ]{.class-0}[Jesus Christ ]{.class-1}[at the Second Advent in Isaiah 61:1--2 (cf. Luke 4:17--21). In that passage, the Gospel of love is preached first, and after it is rejected comes ]{.class-0}["the day of vengeance of our God"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 61:2).]{.class-0}

"[\...He will not regard any ransom; neither will he rest content, though thou givest many gifts."]{.class-2}[ You aren't going to "make up" with God for rejecting His Son by your giving or church attendance or "turning over a new leaf" or anything else. You will either accept God's ]{.class-0}["free gift"]{.class-2}[ (Rom. 5:15--18), or you will get His ]{.class-0}[jealousy]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[vengeance]{.class-1}[ (Rom. 6:23; 2 Thess. 1:8). When you turn down Jesus Christ, you are playing with ]{.class-0}[fire]{.class-1}[ (Heb. 12:29)---and I don't mean just "separation from God" like you hear from Billy Graham.]{.class-0}

[Back to Zechariah 1:14---]{.class-0}["I am jealous for Jerusalem and for Zion with a great jealousy."]{.class-2}[ That's God setting His affections on ]{.class-0}[one city]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[one place]{.class-1}[ in that city (see Psa. 2:6). You reckon Jimmy Carter feels that way about it? How about Bush (either one of them)? How about Clinton or Obama? When ]{.class-0}[Clinton]{.class-1}[ was President, the Congress passed a law to move our embassy in Israel from Tel Aviv to Jerusalem; ]{.class-0}[it hasn't been moved yet.]{.class-1}

"[I am very sore displeased with the heathen that are at ease"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 15). These are ]{.class-0}["the heathen"]{.class-2}[ you find at Walmart, K-Mart, Target, etc. They're not the ones in Africa, Southeast Asia, the Amazon, and back in the bush.]{.class-0}

"[For I was but a little displeased."]{.class-2}[ Now that's the Lord God talking there. If you want to see what He means by ]{.class-0}["a little displeased,"]{.class-2}[ read Deuteronomy 28. That's the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob telling you exactly what He's going to do to His ]{.class-0}[chosen people]{.class-1}[ for messing up.]{.class-0}[ "A little displeased"]{.class-2}[?! You talk about an understatement, boy; that's it. Nineteen centuries of persecution---that's what the Lord calls ]{.class-0}["a little displeased."]{.class-2}

"[And they helped forward the affliction."]{.class-2}[ In other words, God used those heathen to judge His people, but they were too eager to push the thing forward.]{.class-0}

[Now that's what you have to look out for. Christ said, ]{.class-0}["Woe unto the world because of offences! for it must needs be that offences come; but woe to that man by whom the offence cometh"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 18:7). The offence may come, but don't let it come ]{.class-0}[by you.]{.class-1}[ You don't have to be anti-Semitic; you can be a friend to the Jewish people (Rom. 11:28).]{.class-0}

[Verses 16--17 are more promises of the restoration of Israel as a nation with Jerusalem as its capital. Now that has been ]{.class-0}[partially]{.class-1}[ fulfilled, but ]{.class-0}[not fully.]{.class-1}[ Look at verse 16.]{.class-0}

"[I am returned to Jerusalem with mercies."]{.class-2}[ ]{.class-0}[Not yet,]{.class-1}[ no. He's giving them a fit, and it's getting worse (see Rev. 11).]{.class-0}

"[My house shall be built in it."]{.class-2}[ ]{.class-0}[Not yet, ]{.class-1}[it hasn't (see the comments on Hag. 1:8).]{.class-0}

"[A line shall be stretched forth upon Jerusalem."]{.class-2}[ That's a surveyor's ]{.class-0}["line"]{.class-2}[ used for measuring distances. We will have more to say about this when we get to chapter 2, verses 1--4.]{.class-0}

[Verse 16:]{.class-0}[ "Thus saith the LORD of hosts; MY cities\...."]{.class-2}[ Did you get that? That's Jehovah speaking. He says, ]{.class-0}["MY cities."]{.class-2}[ The land of Palestine belongs to ]{.class-0}[the Lord;]{.class-1}[ not the UN, not the ]{.class-0}[Catholics]{.class-1}[, and not the ]{.class-0}[Moslems]{.class-1}[.]{.class-0}

"[My cities through prosperity shall yet be spread abroad; and the LORD shall yet comfort Zion, and shall yet choose Jerusalem"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 17). Those promises have a double application: a ]{.class-0}[temporary, partial]{.class-1}[ fulfillment in Ezra and Nehemiah, and a ]{.class-0}[permanent, complete]{.class-1}[ fulfillment in the Millennium.]{.class-0}

[The ]{.class-0}["prosperity"]{.class-2}[ has to do with the fulfillment of the Abrahamic land grant given in Genesis 15:18. There's a famous passage in Isaiah 35:1--2 that all the prophecy boys go to when they want to talk about "Bible prophecy in the news," and that sort of thing. It says, ]{.class-0}["the desert shall rejoice, and blossom as the rose. It shall blossom abundantly."]{.class-2}

That has nothing to do with the farmers in Israel recovering the land through irrigation. That's God taking the desert in the Sinaitic Peninsula, and between the Jordan and the Euphrates Rivers, and causing them to bloom and grow like the Garden of Eden. Look at Isaiah 51:3.

"[For the LORD shall comfort Zion: he will comfort all her waste places; and he will make her wilderness like Eden, and her desert like the garden of the LORD."]{.class-2}

[That's not happening right now, not even in Palestine. ]{.class-0}[That's a Millennial promise.]{.class-1}

[Another way you know that the reference is to the Millennium is that the ]{.class-0}["comfort"]{.class-2}[ here also deals with the cessation of war. Isaiah 40:1--2 says, ]{.class-0}["Comfort ye, comfort ye my people, saith your God. Speak ye comfortably to Jerusalem, and cry unto her, that her warfare is accomplished."]{.class-2}

That doesn't take place until Christ chooses Jerusalem as the capital of the earth and fulfills Isaiah 2:4 by wiping out the UN (Zech. 14). The whole earth will be run by a Judean Jew sitting on a Jewish throne on the Jewish Temple mount in Jerusalem (Luke 1:31--33 cf. Psa. 2:6).

18 Then lifted I up mine eyes, and saw, and behold four horns.

19 And I said unto the angel that talked with me, What be these? And he answered me, These are the horns which have scattered Judah, Israel, and Jerusalem.

20 And the LORD shewed me four carpenters.

21 Then said I, What come these to do? And he spake, saying, These are the horns which have scattered Judah, so that no man did lift up his head: but these are come to fray them, to cast out the horns of the Gentiles, which lifted up their horn over the land of Judah to scatter it.

[The ]{.class-0}["four horns"]{.class-2}[ of verse 18 are not horns like a trumpet, trombone, etc.: they are the horns of an ]{.class-0}[animal]{.class-1}[. In the Bible, horns are used as symbols of power and authority. The Beast of Revelation 13 and 17 has ]{.class-0}["ten horns"]{.class-2}[ with ]{.class-0}["ten crowns"]{.class-2}[ on them (Rev. 13:1), representing ]{.class-0}["ten kings"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 17:12).]{.class-0}

[In Daniel 8, you have a ram with two horns fighting a genuine, sure-enough ]{.class-0}[unicorn]{.class-1}[---a ]{.class-0}["he goat"]{.class-2}[ with one great, big, old horn sitting there right between his eyeballs (Dan. 8:5). When the angel interpreted the dream for Daniel, he told him that the ]{.class-0}["two horns are the KINGS of Media and Persia"]{.class-2}[ (Dan. 8:20) and the big horn on the goat was Alexander the Great (Dan. 8:21).]{.class-0}

The Vikings understood the idea, and so did the American Indians. The Vikings had helmets with horns sticking out of both sides of it symbolizing power in battle, and the shamans among the American Indians would wear a buffalo headdress with horns on it to show their position of authority.

So whatever these horns are, they represent some kind of power or kingdom. Look at the interpretation in verse 19.

"[These are the horns which have scattered Judah, Israel, and Jerusalem."]{.class-2}

[So you are dealing here with ]{.class-0}[Gentile Kings]{.class-1}[ or kingdoms (see vs. 21) which were involved in the destruction of the northern kingdom of Israel, the southern kingdom of Judah, and the capital city of Jerusalem. The problem is that, up to this point, there were only ]{.class-0}[two]{.class-1}[ kingdoms that ]{.class-0}["scattered"]{.class-2}[ those Jews: ]{.class-0}[Assyria]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-16}[and ]{.class-0}[Babylon.]{.class-1}

[Jeremiah 50:17 says, ]{.class-0}["Israel is a scattered sheep; the lions have driven him away: first the king of Assyria ]{.class-2}[\[that would be ]{.class-0}[Shalmaneser]{.class-1}[---2 Kings 17:1--6\]]{.class-0}[ hath devoured him; and last this Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon hath broken his bones."]{.class-2}

[What you have here is prophecy given in the past tense, as you find it in Isaiah 53 (see the comments on Hab. 3:3). It not only included the two past kingdoms that dispersed those Jews, ]{.class-0}[but two future kingdoms that would do it as well.]{.class-1}

[Now, Scofield tries to make the ]{.class-0}["four horns" ]{.class-2}[here match the Gentile world powers of Daniel 2 and 7. The problem with that, of course, is that the ]{.class-0}[five]{.class-1}[ kingdoms of Daniel 2 don't match exactly the ]{.class-0}[four]{.class-1}[ kingdoms of Daniel 7, because the kingdoms of Daniel 7 arose ]{.class-0}[after]{.class-1}[ Babylon (Dan. 7:17 cf. 7:1, 2:37--38). Besides that, ]{.class-0}[Assyria]{.class-1}[ is not one of the nations in either Daniel 2 or 7.]{.class-0}

[The nations represented in Nebuchadnezzar's image were: Babylon, Medo-Persia, Greece, Rome, and the kingdom of the Antichrist. Of those, neither Persia nor Greece ]{.class-0}["scattered"]{.class-2}[ Israel, Judah, or Jerusalem; in fact, Cyrus the Persian restored the Jews to their homeland and Jerusalem. Of the Gentile world powers in the visions in the book of Daniel, the ones that scatter those Jews would be: Babylon, Rome (under Titus in A.D. 70), and Mystery Babylon under the Antichrist (Rev. 11:1--2; Zech. 14:1--2). Along with Assyria, those would make up the ]{.class-0}["four horns."]{.class-2}

[Now look at what happens next: ]{.class-0}["And the LORD shewed me four carpenters"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 20). The ]{.class-0}["carpenters"]{.class-2}[ come along ]{.class-0}["to fray"]{.class-2}[ (i.e., to rub off or to wear off) and ]{.class-0}["cast out"]{.class-2}[ the Gentile powers that scattered those Jews. That could be a reference to the kingdoms God used to wipe out Assyria, Babylon, Rome, and the Antichrist. Look at Jeremiah 50:18--19.]{.class-0}

"[Therefore thus saith the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel; Behold, I will punish the king of Babylon and his land, as I have punished the king of Assyria. And I will bring Israel again to his habitation."]{.class-2}

[Now if the ]{.class-0}["carpenters"]{.class-2}[ are the Kings or kingdoms that overthrew the nations that scattered the Jews, that is still not without its problems. Assyria was overthrown by ]{.class-0}[Babylon]{.class-1}[, which would make Babylon both a "horn" and a "carpenter." Cyrus the Persian conquered Babylon and restored Israel to the land, but when ]{.class-0}[imperial Rome]{.class-1}[ fell to the barbarian tribes of Germany, it was simply replaced by ]{.class-0}[papal Rome ]{.class-1}[which went right on with the scattering and persecuting of the Jews without skipping a beat. Israel was restored to the land in 1917 by England and in 1948 by America, but papal Rome was never "frayed" or ]{.class-0}["cast out,"]{.class-2}[ and she won't be until Revelation 17--18.]{.class-0}

[The context of Jeremiah 50:17--19 obviously jumps to the restoration of Israel at the Second Advent (see Jer. 50:19--20). The image in Nebuchadnezzar's dream wasn't destroyed at the Second Advent, though, by ]{.class-0}["four carpenters"]{.class-2}[; it was destroyed by ]{.class-0}[just one]{.class-1}[ (Dan. 2:44--45 cf. Matt. 21:42--44; Mark 6:3). As to the exact identity of the other three "carpenters," I have only one comment to make: ]{.class-0}["Ich weiss nicht."]{.class-1}

[]{#section-0_1_dd271d1f114aa787_b68e.xhtml}

CHAPTER TWO

2:1 I lifted up mine eyes again, and looked, and behold a man with a measuring line in his hand.

2 Then said I, Whither goest thou? And he said unto me, To measure Jerusalem, to see what is the breadth thereof, and what is the length thereof.

3 And, behold, the angel that talked with me went forth, and another angel went out to meet him,

4 And said unto him, Run, speak to this young man, saying, Jerusalem shall be inhabited as towns without walls for the multitude of men and cattle therein:

5 For I, saith the LORD, will be unto her a wall of fire round about, and will be the glory in the midst of her.

[The ]{.class-0}["measuring line"]{.class-2}[ of verse one is connected with the rebuilding of Jerusalem in the Millennium (see 1:16).]{.class-0}

"[Behold, the days come, saith the LORD, that the city shall be built to the LORD from the tower of Hananeel unto the gate of the corner. And the measuring line shall yet go forth over against it unto the hill Gareb, and shall compass about to Goath"]{.class-2}[ (Jer. 31:38--39).]{.class-0}

Those measurements are given in Ezekiel 48:35.

"[It was round about eighteen thousand measures: and the name of the city from that day shall be, The LORD is there."]{.class-2}

[Jeremiah said ]{.class-0}["the city shall be built to the LORD,"]{.class-2}[ and in Ezekiel, the city is called ]{.class-0}["Jehovah-shammah"]{.class-1}[ because Jesus Christ, God in the flesh, is right there sitting on the throne in the Temple of Ezekiel 40--46. The "Dome of the Rock" won't be there, the "Mosque of Omar" won't be there, "Allah" won't be there, Mohammed won't be there, and "Mary" won't be there. ]{.class-0}["The LORD"]{.class-2}[ ]{.class-0}[will be present there.]{.class-1}

[Now notice that the measurements of the city in the Millennium are ]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ the measurements of the New Jerusalem. In Ezekiel, Jerusalem is a little over five miles around. The New Jerusalem is ]{.class-0}["twelve thousand furlongs"]{.class-2}[ on each side: that would be 1,500 miles long and 1,500 miles wide. That would be the distance between Miami, Florida and Montreal, Canada, and from Washington, D.C. to Denver, Colorado. If you set that thing down in the Middle East, it would cover the ground from Egypt to India, and from Turkey to Ethiopia; it would completely cover the land of Palestine. When people like Texe Marrs say that God is all finished with the earthly Jerusalem, and that the only Jerusalem from which Jesus Christ will rule is the New Jerusalem, ]{.class-0}[they don't know about what they're talking.]{.class-1}

[So the city that is being measured in verse 2 is the Millennial Jerusalem. Look at how it is described in verse 4:]{.class-0}[ "Jerusalem shall be inhabited as towns without walls."]{.class-2}

[Then it's not the New Jerusalem, because the New Jerusalem has ]{.class-0}["a wall great and high"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 21:12). That's not all; it's not the Jerusalem of Ezra and Nehemiah, because it was a ]{.class-0}["reproach"]{.class-2}[ for ]{.class-0}["the walls of Jerusalem"]{.class-2}[ to be ]{.class-0}["broken down"]{.class-2}[ (Neh. 1:3); that's why they were rebuilt. But in the Millennium, Israel is ]{.class-0}["the land of unwalled villages"]{.class-2}[ (Ezek. 38:11).]{.class-0}

"[\...for the multitude of men and cattle therein"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 4). Jeremiah 31:27 says that in the Millennium, the Lord is going to ]{.class-0}["sow"]{.class-2}[ that land ]{.class-0}["with the seed of men, and with the seed of beast."]{.class-2}

[Verse 5:]{.class-0}[ "For I, saith the LORD, will be unto her a wall of fire around about, and will be the glory in the midst of her."]{.class-2}[ ]{.class-0}[Well, not now, no.]{.class-1}[ Jesus Christ left Jerusalem in Matthew 24:3 and took ]{.class-0}["the glory"]{.class-2}[ with Him (Matt. 23:38--39). Titus came into that city and brought his own ]{.class-0}[fire]{.class-1}[ with him and burned the place to the ground. When the Antichrist attacks the city in Zechariah 14:1--2, the Lord doesn't protect the city at all; He lets the Antichrist have his way, and ]{.class-0}[then]{.class-1}[ the Lord comes back to deliver those Jews (Zech. 14:3). But in the Millennium, when ]{.class-0}["The LORD is there,"]{.class-2}[ you'll have a situation just like Israel had in the wilderness with ]{.class-0}["a pillar of cloud" "by day"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["a pillar of fire" "by night"]{.class-2}[ (Exod. 13:21). Look at Isaiah 4:5.]{.class-0}

"[And the LORD will create upon every dwelling place of mount Zion, and upon her assemblies, a cloud and smoke by day, and the shining of a flaming fire by night: for upon all the glory shall be a defence."]{.class-2}

6 Ho, ho, come forth, and flee from the land of the north, saith the LORD: for I have spread you abroad as the four winds of the heaven, saith the LORD.

7 Deliver thyself, O Zion, that dwellest with the daughter of Babylon.

8 For thus saith the LORD of hosts; After the glory hath he sent me unto the nations which spoiled you: for he that toucheth you toucheth the apple of his eye.

9 For, behold, I will shake mine hand upon them, and they shall be a spoil to their servants: and ye shall know that the LORD of hosts hath sent me.

"[Ho, ho, come forth, and flee from the land of the north"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 6). Well, hello, Santa! What are you doing here?]{.class-0}

[You say, "Ah, Ruckman\...." "Ah, Ruckman," nothing. ]{.class-0}[Look at the verse.]{.class-1}[ There's someone coming out of the north saying ]{.class-0}["Ho, ho."]{.class-2}[ You just expand it to "Ho! Ho! Ho!" so you end up with a ]{.class-0}[trinity]{.class-1}[ (you know what I mean, jellybean---cf. Isa. 6:3).]{.class-0}

[You never ran into a greater impersonator of Jesus Christ (outside of the Antichrist himself) than Santa Claus.]{.class-1}[ He dresses in ]{.class-0}[red]{.class-1}[ (Isa. 63:1; Rev. 19:13); he has ]{.class-0}[white hair]{.class-1}[ and a ]{.class-0}[beard]{.class-1}[ (Dan. 7:9; Rev. 1:14); he's a ]{.class-0}[carpenter]{.class-1}[ (Mark 6:3); he comes from the ]{.class-0}[north]{.class-1}[ (Psa. 48:1--2; Ezek. 1:4); he comes down the chimney and lands in the ]{.class-0}[fireplace]{.class-1}[ (Rev. 1:15, 10:1); ]{.class-0}[little children come and sit in his lap]{.class-1}[ (Mark 10:13--16). He has ]{.class-0}[books]{.class-1}[ with which to ]{.class-0}[judge]{.class-1}[ (Dan. 7:10; Rev. 10:12 cf. "Santa Claus is Coming to Town"); he's ]{.class-0}[omniscient]{.class-1}[ ("Santa Claus is Coming to Town"); he's ]{.class-0}[eternal]{.class-1}[ ("Yes, Virginia, There is a Santa Claus"); he requires ]{.class-0}[faith]{.class-1}[ in himself ("Miracle on 34th Street"); he has ]{.class-0}[churches]{.class-1}[ named after him ("Saint Nicholas"). He's called "Kris Kringle," which means "little Christ child"; but at the same time, he's called "St. Nick," and "Old Nick" is a British name for the Devil.]{.class-0}

[Now, actually, verse 6 has to do with the restoration of Israel to the land. Look at the last part of the verse: ]{.class-0}["for I have spread you abroad as the four winds of the heaven, saith the LORD."]{.class-2}[ Those Jews were dispersed to every corner of the earth.]{.class-0}

[Isaiah 43:5--6 says, ]{.class-0}["Fear not: for I am with thee: I will bring thy seed from the east, and gather thee from the west; I will say to the north, Give up; and to the south, Keep not back: bring my sons from far, and my daughters from the ends of the earth."]{.class-2}

[Isaiah 49:12--13 says, ]{.class-0}["Behold, these shall come from far: and, lo, these from the north and from the west; and those from the land of Sinim. Sing, O heavens; and be joyful, O earth; and break forth into singing, O mountains: for the LORD hath comforted his people, and will have mercy upon his afflicted."]{.class-2}

[This "fleeing from the north" began with the break-up of the Soviet Union in the 1990s. For the first time Jews were allowed to immigrate to Israel. Today you have a bunch of poor Russian Jews in Israel who "fled the north." So like many other prophecies throughout the Scripture, you have a ]{.class-0}[double application:]{.class-1}[ a ]{.class-0}[partial]{.class-1}[ fulfillment now and a ]{.class-0}[complete]{.class-1}[ fulfillment at the Second Advent.]{.class-0}

[Verse 7:]{.class-0}[ "Deliver thyself, O Zion, that dwellest with the daughter of Babylon."]{.class-2}[ There's Babylon in the Old Testament, and her daughter shows up in Revelation 17:5---]{.class-0}["MYSTERY, BABYLON THE GREAT, THE MOTHER OF HARLOTS AND ABOMINATIONS OF THE EARTH."]{.class-2}[ In Revelation 18:4, the Jews are commanded: ]{.class-0}["Come out of her, my people, that ye be not partakers of her sins, and that ye receive not of her plagues."]{.class-2}

"[The glory"]{.class-2}[ of verse 8 is ]{.class-0}["the glory"]{.class-2}[ of verse 5 that defends Israel in the Millennium. That ]{.class-0}["glory"]{.class-2}[ shows up at the Second Advent (Isa. 40:5) after the UN has attacked Jerusalem and the Jews (Zech. 14:1--3).]{.class-0}

"[The apple of his eye"]{.class-2}[ is what is known in translating as "dynamic equivalence." In translating, there is ]{.class-0}[formal correspondence,]{.class-1}[ which is a ]{.class-0}[literal]{.class-1}[ translation, and ]{.class-0}[dynamic equivalence,]{.class-1}[ which gives the ]{.class-0}[meaning]{.class-1}[ of the text. For the most part, the ]{.class-0}[Authorized Version]{.class-1}[ is formal correspondence, but now and then, you find a translation that gives an English ]{.class-0}[idiom,]{.class-1}[ like you have here.]{.class-0}

[The literal translation of the Hebrew phrase here would be "the little man of the eye." It is a reference to the pupil. The English idiom ]{.class-0}["apple of his eye"]{.class-2}[ means "the best thing you've got," the idea behind it being that the pupil is the best part of the eye because that is where the light enters.]{.class-0}

[In the verse here, ]{.class-0}["the apple"]{.class-2}[ of God's eye is ]{.class-0}[Israel]{.class-1}[ (see Deut. 32:10; Psa. 17:8; Lam. 2:18). So the Lord is saying, "You 'touch' my chosen people, and I'll 'touch' you."]{.class-0}

[The promise to Abraham was: ]{.class-0}["I will bless them that bless thee, and curse him that curseth thee"]{.class-2}[ (Gen. 12:3). That promise ]{.class-0}[wasn't given to any "Christian"]{.class-1}[ (Catholic, Greek Orthodox, or any other denomination) or ]{.class-0}[Moslem]{.class-1}[ (from Ishmael and Esau); it was given to the ]{.class-0}[twelve tribes of Israel]{.class-1}[ (Num. 24:9 cf. 24:2). That being the case, America is now under the curse of God for siding with Israel's enemies to take away the land God Almighty gave to that nation (see Bush's "Road Map" or Obama's and Hillary's "foreign policy").]{.class-0}

"[For, behold, I will shake mine hand upon them ]{.class-2}[\[cf. Isa. 19:16\]]{.class-0}[, and they shall be a spoil to their servants"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 9). Those Jews have been serving the Gentile nations for nearly 2,600 years now, but the Lord's going to turn that thing around in the Millennium. ]{.class-0}[All 191 Gentile nations in the UN are going to serve that Jew, and if they don't, they're going to get it in the neck.]{.class-1}

"[And the sons of strangers shall build up thy walls, and their kings shall minister unto thee: for in my wrath I smote thee, but in my favour have I had mercy on thee\....For the nation and kingdom that will not serve thee shall perish; yea, those nations shall be utterly wasted"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 60:10, 12).]{.class-0}

10 Sing and rejoice, O daughter of Zion: for, lo, I come, and I will dwell in the midst of thee, saith the LORD.

11 And many nations shall be joined to the LORD in that day, and shall be my people: and I will dwell in the midst of thee, and thou shalt know that the LORD of hosts hath sent me unto thee.

12 And the LORD shall inherit Judah his portion in the holy land, and shall choose Jerusalem again.

13 Be silent, O all flesh, before the LORD: for he is raised up out of his holy habitation.

"[Sing and rejoice, O daughter of Zion"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 10). For the Scriptural commentary, read Isaiah 12.]{.class-0}

"[I will dwell in the midst of thee"]{.class-2}[---He says it twice: once in verse 10 and once in verse 11. This is the same thing we ran into in Zephaniah 3:15, 17, and you can see the comments on those verses.]{.class-0}

"[And many nations shall be joined to the LORD in that day, and shall be my people"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 11). ]{.class-0}[The verse has nothing to do with New Testament salvation for Gentiles at the First Advent at all. ]{.class-1}[This is a]{.class-0}[ second]{.class-1}[ calling out of the Gentiles that takes place during the Millennium. That's what you read about in Isaiah 2:2--4.]{.class-0}

[That shows up over and over again in the book of Isaiah. Isaiah says, ]{.class-0}["In that day there shall be a root of Jesse]{.class-2}[ \[a reference to Jesus Christ in the Millennium---see Isa. 11:6--9\]]{.class-0}[, which shall stand for an ensign of the people; to it shall the Gentiles seek\...And he shall set up an ensign for the nations"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 11:10, 12).]{.class-0}

There's an interesting passage over in Isaiah 19 on Egypt in the Millennium. Now I don't have all the answers on the passage, but it ends with this:

"[Whom the LORD of hosts shall bless, saying, Blessed be Egypt my people, and Assyria the work of my hands, and Israel mine inheritance"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 19:25).]{.class-0}

That's the Lord calling two Gentile nations that held the Jews in bondage (Egypt) and captivity (Assyria) by terms given to the nation of Israel (see Isa. 51:16, 65:19, 22; Ezek. 39:7, 44:23; Isa. 29:23, 45:11, 60:21, 64:8). That's what happens to the Gentile peoples who make it through the Judgment of the Nations in Matthew 25.

[Back to Zechariah 2: ]{.class-0}["And the LORD shall inherit Judah his portion in the holy land, and shall choose Jerusalem again"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 12). That's God choosing ]{.class-0}[one]{.class-1}[ city out of ]{.class-0}[one]{.class-1}[ nation to rule the earth (see the comments on 1:17). It has nothing to do with Mecca, Rome, New York, Geneva, London, Paris, Washington, D.C., Beijing, or Moscow. Out of all the countries on earth, the Lord chose the land of ]{.class-0}[Israel]{.class-1}[ as His inheritance (Deut. 32:9; Jer. 10:16, 51:19). Out of the twelve tribes in Israel, He chose ]{.class-0}[Judah]{.class-1}[ to bring forth His King (Gen. 49:10). Out of all the cities in Judah, He chose ]{.class-0}[Jerusalem]{.class-1}[ as His capital (Matt. 5:35). Out of all the mountains of Jerusalem; He chose ]{.class-0}[Zion]{.class-1}[ (not Calvary) for His throne (Psa. 2:6). That's how particular the Lord is.]{.class-0}

[Notice that in the Millennium, Israel is called ]{.class-0}["the holy land"]{.class-2}[; that is not its state ]{.class-0}[right now.]{.class-1}[ Right now, Jerusalem is ]{.class-0}["Sodom and Egypt"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 11:8), but after Christ takes away the sin of His people (Isa. 4:3--4, 40:2; Mic. 7:18--20), it becomes ]{.class-0}["the holy city"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 52:1) and ]{.class-0}["the beloved city"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 20:9).]{.class-0}

[In the Scriptures, that land is referred to over and over again as God's land (see Hos. 9:3; Isa. 14:25; Deut. 32:43; 1 Kings 8:36; etc.). God promised that land to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, and their descendants (Gen. 12:7; Heb. 11:9); and no Catholic, Hindu, Buddhist, or Moslem gets a foot of it. It's a Jewish piece of land destined to be ruled over by a Jewish King who is ]{.class-0}[God in the flesh.]{.class-1}

We have already commented on verse 13 under Habakkuk 2:20 (which see).

[]{#section-0_1_27d2cd4057c6b4aa_b68f.xhtml}

CHAPTER THREE

3:1 And he shewed me Joshua the high priest standing before the angel of the LORD, and Satan standing at his right hand to resist him.

2 And the LORD said unto Satan, The LORD rebuke thee, O Satan; even the LORD that hath chosen Jerusalem rebuke thee: is not this a brand plucked out of the fire?

This passage contains a startling revelation that nobody discusses or preaches. Nobody says anything about it, so it's bound to be important.

The scene of verses 1--2 is like that of Job 1:6--12, 2:1--7. Here the Holy Spirit deals with Joshua the high priest, one of the two leaders of the Jews who returned from the captivity (see the comments on Hag. 1:1). He stands before the Angel of the Lord, with Satan to his right.

[In the Old Testament, the Angel of the Lord is a pre-incarnate appearance of Christ in angelic form; you will sometimes hear it referred to as a "theophany" or a "Christophany." The Apostle Paul said, ]{.class-0}["For there stood by me this night the angel of God, whose I am, and whom I serve"]{.class-2}[ (Acts 27:23). Well, he didn't worship an angel; he's referring to Jesus Christ (Rom. 14:8; 2 Cor. 10:7; Rom. 1:1; Gal. 1:10). Over in Galatians 4:14, Paul writes, ]{.class-0}["but received me as an angel of God, EVEN AS CHRIST JESUS."]{.class-2}

[This is the thing that messes everybody up, ]{.class-0}[especially the Mohammedans.]{.class-1}[ God is so powerful that He can manifest Himself in a number of different ways. In Revelation 5:6--7, you have God on the throne in Heaven. Before Him there's ]{.class-0}["a Lamb" "slain from the foundation of the world"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 5:6 cf. 13:8). Who's that? Why, that's ]{.class-0}["the Lamb of God, which taketh away the sin of the world"]{.class-2}[ (John 1:29); that's ]{.class-0}["the Word"]{.class-2}[ who ]{.class-0}["was made flesh"]{.class-2}[ (John 1:14): it's Jesus Christ. The Lamb gets up and turns into a man (or an angel) and goes up to God on the throne to take the book out of His hand. ]{.class-0}[Well, which one is God?]{.class-1}[ The Lamb, the man (or angel), or the One on the throne? The answer is ]{.class-0}[all of them.]{.class-1}[ That's ]{.class-0}[one]{.class-1}[ God manifested in ]{.class-0}[different]{.class-1}[ ways.]{.class-0}

[Of course, that can be stretched too far. The tribal peoples of the world practice a thing called "animism"; the Hindus are ]{.class-0}[pantheists.]{.class-1}[ Animism says the Spirit of God is ]{.class-0}[in]{.class-1}[ nature so you should worship it; pantheism says God ]{.class-0}[is]{.class-1}[ nature so you should worship it. That's taking the manifestation of God in His creation (Psa. 19:1--6) and twisting it to where you're worshipping and serving ]{.class-0}["the creature more than the Creator"]{.class-2}[ (Rom. 1:25).]{.class-0}

[That's the position of the modern "green movement" and ]{.class-0}[tree huggers]{.class-1}[ that are so concerned about "protecting the environment" for the spotted owl and the white pussy cat, and "global warming" and "climate change." They believe the earth is a ]{.class-0}[goddess]{.class-1}[ (Gaia), and when you hurt the earth, you are hurting your ]{.class-0}[mother]{.class-1}[ ("Mother Nature"). I tell them: "Nuke the unborn baby whales" and "Be kind to animals: kiss a shark."]{.class-0}

[So in verse 1, you have the Jewish high priest standing before the Second Person of the Trinity who is in the form of the Angel of the Lord. At Joshua's right hand is Satan. If Joshua is facing the Angel, that would place Satan on the Lord's ]{.class-0}[left]{.class-1}[ hand. Now we are not going into the matter here, but in the Scriptures, the ]{.class-0}[left hand]{.class-1}[ is a bad thing because it is connected with Satan and the Antichrist (see the comments on 11:17). The right hand is a good thing because it is connected with Jesus Christ (see Psa. 110:1).]{.class-0}

[The word ]{.class-0}[Satan]{.class-1}[ means "adversary" (1 Pet. 5:8). That's what he's doing here: he's resisting Joshua. The Bible says, ]{.class-0}["Resist the devil, and he will flee from you"]{.class-2}[ (James 1:7), but you can't do it in your own strength. You have to first ]{.class-0}["submit yourselves therefore to God"]{.class-2}[ (James 1:7) and ]{.class-0}["be strong in the Lord, and in the power of his might"]{.class-2}[ (Eph. 6:10). Then you have to don the equipment God gave you to fight the Devil (Eph. 6:11--18).]{.class-0}

[Here in verse 2, Satan appears as he did in Job, as ]{.class-0}["the accuser of our brethren"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 12:19). The picture is the Devil coming before God as a prosecuting attorney to accuse you of sin so the Lord will judge you. If you are saved, you have a defense attorney who speaks up for you when that happens.]{.class-0}

"[My little children, these things write I unto you, that ye sin not. And if any man sin, we have an advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous"]{.class-2}[ (1 John 2:1).]{.class-0}

[When the Devil comes before God and says, "Lord, Pete Ruckman did this thing over here, and he did that over there, and he did this thing right here; your holiness demands the death penalty (Rom. 6:23)," the Lord says, "Sorry, you've got the wrong defendant." The Devil says, "What do you mean I've got the wrong defendant?! Who do you think that is standing before you?" The Lord replies, ]{.class-0}["That's my son; ]{.class-1}[as far as I'm concerned, he's never done anything wrong."]{.class-0}

[That Bible says, ]{.class-0}["Herein is our love made perfect, that we may have boldness in the day of judgment: because AS HE IS]{.class-2}[ \[i.e., Jesus Christ\]]{.class-0}[, SO ARE WE in the world"]{.class-2}[ (1 John 4:17).]{.class-0}

[Of course, you'll be chastised for any wrong doing as a son (Heb. 12:5--7), you'll reap what you sow in the flesh (Gal. 6:7--8), you'll ]{.class-0}["suffer loss"]{.class-2}[ at the Judgment Seat of Christ (1 Cor. 3:13--15); but when it comes to the Law, it has been taken ]{.class-0}["out of the way"]{.class-2}[ at Calvary (Col. 2:14).]{.class-0}

[When the Devil presses the issue with the Lord and says, "What do you mean he's done nothing wrong? ]{.class-0}[He's guilty,"]{.class-1}[ my Advocate steps up to the bench. "Your Honor, my client has already been tried for this offense and served the sentence for his conviction. ]{.class-0}[You can't try a man twice for the same offense." ]{.class-1}[The Devil objects and says, "When was this man tried?" My Attorney says, "At Calvary," and presents Exhibit A, Exhibit B, Exhibit C, Exhibit D, and Exhibit E: the nailprints in His hands and feet, and the spearthrust in His side (John 20:27). The Judge brings His gavel down and says, ]{.class-0}["Case dismissed!"]{.class-1}

[Back to Zechariah 3---]{.class-0}["And the LORD said unto Satan"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 2). Who's ]{.class-0}["the LORD"]{.class-2}[ there? That's ]{.class-0}["the angel of the LORD"]{.class-2}[ back in verse 1. He's called ]{.class-0}["the LORD"]{.class-2}[ there (i.e., ]{.class-0}[Jehovah]{.class-1}[) just as He was over in Hosea 12:4--5.]{.class-0}

"[Yea, he had power over the angel, and prevailed ]{.class-2}[\[talking about Jacob wrestling with the Angel of the Lord in Gen. 32:24--30\]]{.class-0}[: he wept, and made supplication unto him: he found him in Bethel\...."]{.class-2}[ Then suddenly the ]{.class-0}["him"]{.class-2}[ in reference to the Angel of the Lord changes to ]{.class-0}["us"]{.class-2}[: ]{.class-0}["\...and there he spake with US."]{.class-2}[ Who's the ]{.class-0}["us"]{.class-2}[? Verse 5:]{.class-0}[ "Even the LORD God of hosts."]{.class-2}

The Jehovah's Witness teaching that Jesus Christ is another "God" apart from God the Father is blasphemous nonsense. In the Old Testament, Jesus Christ is called "Jehovah" (see Zech. 12:10 and note the speaker in vs. 4).

"[And the LORD said unto Satan, The LORD rebuke thee, O Satan."]{.class-2}[ Now there's a wild thing. That's one member of the Trinity referring the Devil to ]{.class-0}[another member of the Trinity]{.class-1}[ exactly as the ]{.class-0}[archangel]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-0}[Michael]{.class-1}[ did in Jude 9.]{.class-0}

"[Yet Michael the archangel, when contending with the devil he disputed about the body of Moses, durst not bring against him a railing accusation, but said, The Lord rebuke thee."]{.class-2}[ ]{.class-0}

[That is what caused the J.W.s to identify Michael as Jesus Christ. Never mind the fact that in Daniel 10, the Angel of the Lord and Michael are ]{.class-0}[two separate characters]{.class-1}[ (note Dan. 10:13, 21).]{.class-0}

[Now the question is, why did the Lord have to do this? The reason is that ]{.class-0}[before]{.class-1}[ Christ's incarnation, the Devil contended that the Lord knew nothing by ]{.class-0}[experience ]{.class-1}[about man's temptations to sin]{.class-0}[.]{.class-1}[ What did ]{.class-0}[God]{.class-1}[ know about losing everything like Job did? What did ]{.class-0}[God]{.class-1}[ know about having to prove your love by sacrificing your son like He asked Abraham to do? Was ]{.class-0}[God]{.class-1}[ ever poor? Then what did He know what it was like to have to steal in order to feed your family?]{.class-0}

[When Richard Wurmbrand was in a Communist prison camp, he got to where he preached a series of sermons to God on His "unjust laws." He said, "God, you said ]{.class-0}['thou shalt not bear false witness.' ]{.class-2}[Well, what do you know about having to lie to prevent your wife and children from being taken and tortured?" That kind of stuff, you know. ]{.class-0}[He wasn't being blasphemous;]{.class-1}[ he was just "blowing off steam" from being tortured and beaten and starved to death by the Communists.]{.class-0}

[That's the kind of accusations Satan was making against God back in the Old Testament, except he meant it. This is a being who is not afraid to take on God Almighty and to keep it up for 6,000 years. ]{.class-0}[He's not afraid to curse God to His face]{.class-1}[ (see Rev. 13:5--6).]{.class-0}

[Now no one is going to force God into doing anything, but God is ]{.class-0}[just]{.class-1}[. He wouldn't do wrong to save your soul, but He would make arrangements to satisfy His holiness in regards to your sin.]{.class-0}

"[Giving the Devil his due," the Lord comes down to earth as ]{.class-0}[a man]{.class-1}[ and submits himself to temptation and hardships and death ]{.class-0}[as a man]{.class-1}[ (Phil. 2:5--8). When the Devil tempts Him in Matthew 4 and Luke 4, Jesus doesn't say ]{.class-0}["The LORD rebuke thee, O Satan"]{.class-2}[; He tells him to ]{.class-0}[get out]{.class-1}[: ]{.class-0}["Get thee hence, Satan"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 4:10). Why could He do that? ]{.class-0}[Because He went through what a man would go through, and He beat the Devil at it.]{.class-1}

[I guarantee you, Satan didn't appreciate that a bit, because he no longer had an argument left against the Lord. ]{.class-0}[The Lord]{.class-1}[ now knew what it was like to be tired and hungry and have His family turn against Him. The Bible says, He was ]{.class-0}["a man of sorrows, and acquainted with grief"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 53:3). ]{.class-0}[He]{.class-1}[ knew what it was like to be slapped and spit on and mocked and beaten and hung up naked before a crowd. The Devil didn't have a leg to stand on.]{.class-0}

[Verse 2: ]{.class-0}["is not this a brand plucked out of the fire."]{.class-2}[ John Wesley's parents called him "a brand plucked from the burning" because when he was a young child, he was saved from a fire that burned his family's house down.]{.class-0}

The picture here is that of a fellow who has been saved "by the seat of his britches"; you can still smell the proverbial smoke where he almost got burned. A lot of new converts are like that; I was like that when I got saved. When I got saved, I cussed all the way through the prayer. You talk about still being able to smell the smoke, boy; I was it!

3 Now Joshua was clothed with filthy garments, and stood before the angel.

4 And he answered and spake unto those that stood before him, saying, Take away the filthy garments from him. And unto him he said, Behold, I have caused thine iniquity to pass from thee, and I will clothe thee with change of raiment.

5 And I said, Let them set a fair mitre upon his head. So they set a fair mitre upon his head, and clothed him with garments. And the angel of the LORD stood by.

[Here you have a wonderful illustration of a sinner receiving God's]{.class-0}[ imputed righteousness]{.class-1}[. Such passages throw the Hyperdispensationalists "for a loop," for they insist that the Body of Christ and New Testament salvation by grace through faith is a Pauline revelation found nowhere in the Law or the prophets. The fact is that there are all kinds of pictures and types of both throughout the Old Testament, and Paul didn't hesitate to accommodate Old Testament passages aimed ]{.class-0}[doctrinally]{.class-1}[ at someone else and apply them ]{.class-0}[spiritually ]{.class-1}[to teach New Testament doctrine (see the comments on Gal. 4:24 in that Commentary).]{.class-0}

[In the passage before us, you have a picture of a religious sinner clothed in the ]{.class-0}["filthy rags"]{.class-2}[ of his own self-righteousness (Isa. 64:6). The type is even defined right in the verse: ]{.class-0}["Take away the filthy garments\...Behold, I have caused thine iniquity to pass from thee, and I will clothe thee with change of raiment"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 4). The New Testament application is found in Colossians 3:9--10.]{.class-0}

"[Ye have put off the old man with his deeds ]{.class-2}[\[i.e., the old Adamic nature\]]{.class-0}[; And have put on the new man ]{.class-2}[\[the ]{.class-0}["new creature" "in Christ"]{.class-2}[---2 Cor. 5:17)]{.class-0}[, which is renewed in knowledge after the image of him that created him."]{.class-2}

[The ]{.class-0}["fair mitre"]{.class-2}[ of verse 5 is the ]{.class-0}["mitre"]{.class-2}[ worn by the high priest in the Tabernacle (Exod. 28:4). It had a gold plate tied to it reading: ]{.class-0}["HOLINESS TO THE LORD" ]{.class-2}[(Exod. 28:36--37); in Exodus 29:6, it is called ]{.class-0}["the holy crown"]{.class-2}[ (Exod. 29:6). So every Christian is a priest (1 Pet. 2:5, 9) who can earn crowns at the Judgment Seat of Christ (see 1 Cor. 9:25; 1 Thess. 2:19; 2 Tim. 4:8; James 1:12; 1 Pet. 5:4; Rev. 2:10).]{.class-0}

6 And the angel of the LORD protested unto Joshua, saying,

7 Thus saith the LORD of hosts; If thou wilt walk in my ways, and if thou wilt keep my charge, then thou shalt also judge my house, and shalt also keep my courts, and I will give thee places to walk among these that stand by.

8 Hear now, O Joshua the high priest, thou, and thy fellows that sit before thee: for they are men wondered at: for, behold, I will bring forth my servant the BRANCH.

9 For behold the stone that I have laid before Joshua; upon one stone shall be seven eyes: behold, I will engrave the graving thereof, saith the LORD of hosts, and I will remove the iniquity of that land in one day.

10 In that day, saith the LORD of hosts, shall ye call every man his neighbour under the vine and under the fig tree.

[Verse 7 is a charge given to Joshua that promises the reward of ]{.class-0}[position]{.class-1}[ for faithful service: ]{.class-0}["if thou wilt walk in my ways, and if thou wilt keep my charge, then thou shalt judge my house, and shalt also keep my courts, and I will give thee places to walk among those that stand by."]{.class-2}

[Christ told the twelve apostles: ]{.class-0}["ye also shall sit upon twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 19:28). That's a reward for leaving everything to follow Christ. Paul wrote Timothy: ]{.class-0}["If we suffer, we shall also reign with him"]{.class-2}[ (2 Tim. 2:12).]{.class-0}

"[The BRANCH"]{.class-2}[ of verse 8 is a Messianic title given to Jesus Christ (Isa. 11:1; Jer. 33:15). Outside of those two references, the term occurs four more times, each one matching the themes of one of the four Gospels (Matthew, Mark, Luke, and John).]{.class-0}

[The theme of Matthew is Jesus Christ as the ]{.class-0}["King of the Jews"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 2:2), so in Jeremiah 23:5 you have the ]{.class-0}["Branch"]{.class-2}[ as the ]{.class-0}["King."]{.class-2}

"[Behold, the days come, saith the LORD, that I will raise unto David a righteous Branch, and a King shall reign and prosper, and shall execute judgment and justice in the earth."]{.class-2}

[The theme of Mark is Jesus Christ as the ]{.class-0}[Servant of God.]{.class-1}[ You just read here in Zechariah 3:8---]{.class-0}["my servant the Branch."]{.class-2}

[The theme of Luke is Jesus Christ as ]{.class-0}["the Son of man."]{.class-2}[ Zechariah 6:12 says, ]{.class-0}["Behold the man whose name is The BRANCH."]{.class-2}

[Finally, the theme of John is Jesus Christ as ]{.class-0}["the Son of God."]{.class-2}[ Isaiah 4:2 calls him ]{.class-0}["the branch of the LORD."]{.class-2}

[Now look at that thing. Did you know there are only ]{.class-0}[two Gospels]{.class-1}[ that give Christ's genealogy: Matthew and Luke? Matthew 1:1 runs Christ's lineage back to the first Jewish King of His line (David) and the father of the Jews (Abraham), so in Jeremiah 23:5--6, ]{.class-0}["David," "Judah," ]{.class-2}[and ]{.class-0}["Israel"]{.class-2}[ are mentioned in connection to the ]{.class-0}["Branch."]{.class-2}[ Luke 3:38 runs Christ's lineage back to the first man---]{.class-0}["Adam,"]{.class-2}[ so Zechariah 6:12 calls Christ ]{.class-0}["the man."]{.class-2}[ A servant has no genealogy, so Zechariah 3:8 identifies Christ as merely a ]{.class-0}["servant."]{.class-2}[ John runs Christ all the way back before Genesis 1:1---]{.class-0}["In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God"]{.class-2}[ (John 1:1). So in Isaiah 4:2, up shows ]{.class-0}["the LORD."]{.class-2}

[Quite a "coinky-dinky," don't you think? That's ]{.class-0}[three men]{.class-1}[ writing anywhere from 100--200 years apart, and they just "happen" to match ]{.class-0}[four other men]{.class-1}[ writing over 400 years later with ]{.class-0}[no collusion between any of them. ]{.class-1}[You couldn't do that with a computer no matter what you did. There's a hand involved in that Book, and it's not a human hand. A man may be pushing the pen, but that's about all he's doing.]{.class-0}

[By the way, the word ]{.class-0}[Nazarene]{.class-1}[ means "a branch" (from the Hebrew word ]{.class-0}[netser]{.class-1}[). The New Testament says about Christ: ]{.class-0}["He shall be called a Nazarene"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 2:23). That's a ]{.class-0}[Nazarene,]{.class-1}[ not a Nazarite, who was a fellow like Samson with a vow on him (see Num. 6).]{.class-0}

[Verse 9: ]{.class-0}["For behold the stone that I have laid before Joshua."]{.class-2}[ That's another picture of Christ.]{.class-0}

"[Jesus saith unto them, Did ye never read in the scriptures, The stone which the builders rejected, the same is become the head of the corner"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 21:42). The only structure which has a ]{.class-0}["head"]{.class-2}[ that is a ]{.class-0}["corner"]{.class-2}[ is a pyramid.]{.class-0}

[Now look at what Zechariah says about this stone: ]{.class-0}["upon one stone shall be seven eyes."]{.class-2}[ So on the back of your dollar bill, you have a pyramid where the headstone is separate from the rest of that structure, and right in the middle of that stone is an all-seeing eye.]{.class-0}

[You say, "That's an Illuminati symbol." Be that as it may, the image is taken straight from a ]{.class-0}[King James]{.class-1}[ Bible. You won't get away from that Book. It runs the world whether anyone realizes it or not.]{.class-0}

[Now those ]{.class-0}["seven eyes"]{.class-2}[ on that ]{.class-0}["stone"]{.class-2}[ are found on Jesus Christ as the Lamb up in Heaven, and they are defined.]{.class-0}

"[And I beheld, and, lo, in the midst of the throne and of the four beasts, and in the midst of the elders, stood a Lamb as it had been slain, having seven horns and seven eyes, which are the seven Spirits of God sent forth into all the earth"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 5:6).]{.class-0}

Of course, there is only one Holy Spirit (Eph. 4:4), but He is manifested seven ways, just as Jesus Christ can be manifested as a man, an angel, a rock, a lamb, etc. In Isaiah 11:2, the Holy Spirit is called:

[1) ]{.class-0}["the spirit of the LORD"]{.class-2}

[2) ]{.class-0}["the spirit of wisdom"]{.class-2}

[3) ]{.class-0}["and understanding"]{.class-2}

[4) ]{.class-0}["the spirit of counsel"]{.class-2}

[5) ]{.class-0}["and might"]{.class-2}

[6) ]{.class-0}["the spirit of knowledge"]{.class-2}

[7) ]{.class-0}["and of the fear of the LORD"]{.class-2}

"[Behold, I will engrave the graving thereof, saith the LORD of hosts."]{.class-2}[ Now what that engraving is, I have no idea. The high priest wore two stones on his shoulders that were engraved ]{.class-0}["with the names of the children of Israel"]{.class-2}[ (Exod. 28:9--12), but there is only ]{.class-0}["one stone"]{.class-2}[ here.]{.class-0}

"[And I will remove the iniquity of that land in one day."]{.class-2}[ That ]{.class-0}["day"]{.class-2}[ is ]{.class-0}["the day of the LORD," ]{.class-2}[the Second Advent. Turn on over to Zechariah 12:8--10.]{.class-0}

"[In that day ]{.class-2}[\[Second Advent\]]{.class-0}[ shall the LORD defend the inhabitants of Jerusalem; and he that is feeble among them at that day shall be as David; and the house of David ]{.class-2}[\[watch it!\]]{.class-0}[ shall be as GOD, as the ANGEL OF THE LORD before them ]{.class-2}[\[he's talking about the Second Coming of Jesus Christ---look at vs. 10\]]{.class-0}[\....And I will pour upon the house of David, and upon the inhabitants of Jerusalem, the spirit of grace and of supplications: AND THEY SHALL LOOK UPON ME WHOM THEY HAVE PIERCED]{.class-2}[ \[why, that's a reference to the return of Christ in Rev. 1:7\]]{.class-0}[, and they shall mourn for him, as one mourneth for his only son, and shall be in bitterness for him, as one that is in bitterness for his firstborn."]{.class-2}

When Jesus Christ returns in power, those Jews see the nail prints in His hands and feet, and that spear thrust in His side, and they realize who their Messiah is. They repent and get right, and the nation is converted in a day.

[Verse 10: ]{.class-0}["In that day]{.class-2}[ \[Second Advent\]]{.class-0}[, saith the LORD of hosts, shall ye call every man his neighbour under the vine and under the fig tree."]{.class-2}[ Those Jews are back in the land where they should be, and they are all at peace. That's the way it was during the reign of Solomon.]{.class-0}

"[And Judah and Israel dwelt safely, every man under his vine and under his fig tree, from Dan even to Beersheba, all the days of Solomon"]{.class-2}[ (1 Kings 4:25).]{.class-0}
