[Solomon's name comes from the word ]{.class-0}[shalom,]{.class-1}[ meaning "peace," as does Salem: Jerusalem---"city of peace." Solomon's reign is a picture of Christ's Millennial kingdom. Look at Micah 4:1--4.]{.class-0}

"[But in the last days it shall come to pass, that the mountain of the house of the LORD shall be established in the top of the mountains, and it shall be exalted above the hills, and people shall flow unto it. And many nations shall come, and say, Come, and let us go up to the mountain of the LORD, and to the house of the God of Jacob; and he will teach us of his ways, and we will walk in his paths: for the law shall go forth of Zion, and the word of the LORD from Jerusalem. And he shall judge among many people, and rebuke strong nations afar off; and they shall beat their swords into plowshares, and their spears into pruninghooks: nation shall not lift up a sword against nation, neither shall they learn war any more. But they shall sit every man under his vine and under his fig tree; and none shall make them afraid: for the mouth of the LORD of hosts hath spoken it."]{.class-2}

In the Millennium, Israel is back to an agrarian society with optimal conditions, just like there were in the Garden of Eden (Ezek. 36:35).

"[Behold, the days come, saith the LORD, that the plowman shall overtake the reaper, and the treader of grapes him that soweth seed ]{.class-2}[\[they haven't finished getting a crop out of the ground before they are plowing and planting the next one\]]{.class-0}[; and the mountains shall drop sweet wine, and all the hills shall melt. And I will bring again the captivity of my people of Israel, and they shall build the waste cities, and inhabit them; and they shall plant vineyards, and drink the wine thereof; they shall also make gardens, and eat the fruit of them. And I will plant them upon their land, and they shall no more be pulled up out of their land which I have given them, saith the LORD thy God"]{.class-2}[ (Amos 9:13--15).]{.class-0}

"[But ye, O mountains of Israel, ye shall shoot forth your branches, and yield your fruit to my people of Israel; for they are at hand to come. For, behold, I am for you, and I will turn unto you, and ye shall be tilled and sown: And I will multiply men upon you, all the house of Israel, even all of it: and the cities shall be inhabited, and the wastes shall be builded: And I will multiply upon you man and beast; and they shall increase and bring fruit: and I will settle you after your old estates, and will do better unto you than at your beginnings: and ye shall know that I am the LORD"]{.class-2}[ (Ezek. 36:9--11).]{.class-0}

[Those are promises to the Jews of ]{.class-0}[permanent restoration and prosperity. ]{.class-1}[The fact that they rejected their Messiah, and are dying and going to Hell by the millions means nothing to God in regards to ]{.class-0}[those promises]{.class-1}[ at all (Rom. 11:29). The Lord's going to let things go on until the Gentiles nearly wipe them out, and then He's coming back to rescue them and destroy their enemies. At that point, He's going to convert every one of them left alive and ]{.class-0}[fulfill every promise He made to them in the Old Testament.]{.class-1}

"[For I will take you from among the heathen, and gather you out of all countries, and will bring you into your own land. Then will I sprinkle clean water upon you, and ye shall be clean: from all your filthiness, and from all your idols, will I cleanse you. A new heart also will I give you, and a new spirit will I put within you ]{.class-2}[\[that's not an individual's New Birth; that's a national promise to a nation\]]{.class-0}[: and I will take away the stony heart out of your flesh, and I will give you an heart of flesh ]{.class-2}[\[obviously figurative\]]{.class-0}[. And I will put my spirit within you, and cause you to walk in my statutes, and ye shall keep my judgments, and do them. And ye shall dwell in the land that I gave to your fathers; and ye shall be my people, and I will be your God. I will also save you from all your uncleannesses; and I will call for the corn, and will increase it, and lay no famine upon you. And I will multiply the fruit of the tree, and the increase of the field, that ye shall receive no more reproach of famine among the heathen"]{.class-2}[ (Ezek. 36:24--30).]{.class-0}

[National deliverance, national salvation, national restoration, and national prosperity to ]{.class-0}[one nation]{.class-1}[---Israel. ]{.class-0}[No Catholic, no Moslem, no union of nations, no President, etc., is going to stop it.]{.class-1}[ The Lord God Almighty is going to bring every word right through on the money, and He won't think anything about wiping out over four billion Gentiles to do it (Rev. 9:15--18).]{.class-0}

[]{#section-0_1_baffbabd80546a9a_b690.xhtml}

CHAPTER FOUR

4:1 And the angel that talked with me came again, and waked me, as a man that is wakened out of his sleep,

2 And said unto me, What seest thou? And I said, I have looked, and behold a candlestick all of gold, with a bowl upon the top of it, and his seven lamps thereon, and seven pipes to the seven lamps, which are upon the top thereof:

3 And two olive trees by it, one upon the right side of the bowl, and the other upon the left side thereof.

4 So I answered and spake to the angel that talked with me, saying, What are these, my lord?

5 Then the angel that talked with me answered and said unto me, Knowest thou not what these be? And I said, No, my lord.

"[The angel"]{.class-2}[ of verse 1 is the Angel of the Lord that spoke to Zechariah in 1:9--13 and 2:3.]{.class-0}

[Now the ]{.class-0}["candlestick"]{.class-2}[ of verse 2 is called a "Menorah," and it is the national symbol of Israel (1948). The "original" was the Golden Candlestick in the Tabernacle (and later in the Temple) that provided light to the Holy Place (Exod. 35:14).]{.class-0}

In the Bible, the Menorah is a picture of several things. It is, first of all, a picture of the Scriptures themselves.

"[Thy word is a lamp unto my feet, and a light unto my path"]{.class-2}[ (Psa. 119:105).]{.class-0}

It is a picture of the Holy Spirit.

"[And there were seven lamps of fire burning before the throne, which are the seven Spirits of God ]{.class-2}[\[see the comments on 3:9\]]{.class-0}["]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 4:5).]{.class-0}

Just as the Golden Candlestick shed light on the Table of Showbread in the Tabernacle, so the Holy Spirit sheds light on the Scripture (cf. Luke 4:4).

The Menorah is also a picture of the Church.

"[And I turned to see the voice that spake with me. And being turned, I saw seven golden candlesticks\...the seven candlesticks which thou sawest are the seven churches"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 1:12, 20).]{.class-0}

[Notice in Revelation 1:13 that John sees Jesus ]{.class-0}["in the midst of the seven candlesticks."]{.class-2}[ The Golden Candlestick was the likeness of a blossoming almond tree (Exod. 25:31--33), like Aaron's rod that ]{.class-0}[budded]{.class-1}[ (]{.class-0}["knops"]{.class-2}[), ]{.class-0}[blossomed ]{.class-1}[(]{.class-0}["flowers"]{.class-2}[), and ]{.class-0}[bore]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-0}["almonds"]{.class-2}[ (see Num. 17:8). That blossoming rod was a picture of Jesus Christ coming up from the dead to be our ]{.class-0}["High Priest"]{.class-2}[ (Heb. 3:1). So on the ]{.class-0}[middle branch]{.class-1}[ (see the comments on 3:8) of the Golden Candlestick, there are ]{.class-0}[four]{.class-1}[ sets of ]{.class-0}["knops," "flowers,"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["almonds"]{.class-2}[ instead of three as there are on the six side branches.]{.class-0}

[Jesus Christ is the taller, more important branch ]{.class-0}["in the midst"]{.class-2}[ of the other branches. He is the ]{.class-0}[fourth branch]{.class-1}[, so He shows up in the manger at Bethlehem ]{.class-0}[four thousand years]{.class-1}[ after Creation (cf. John 3:19--21, 1:4--13). That is why God created the ]{.class-0}[sun]{.class-1}[ (cf. Mal. 4:2)---]{.class-0}["the greater light"]{.class-2}[---on the ]{.class-0}[fourth day]{.class-1}[ (see the comments on Gen. 1:14--19 in that Commentary).]{.class-0}

[When Jesus Christ showed up on earth, He was called ]{.class-0}["the true Light"]{.class-2}[ (John 1:9). He was born witness to by John the Baptist, whom He called ]{.class-0}["a burning and a shining light"]{.class-2}[ (John 5:35). In the Gospels, you will find the witness of John the Baptist to Jesus Christ ]{.class-0}[seven times]{.class-1}[---one for each branch of the Menorah (Matt. 3:11--12; Mark 1:7--8; Luke 3:16--17; John 1:15, 26--27, 29, 36). And just as the Golden Candlestick gave light on the Table of Showbread, John gave light on the Bread of Life (John 6:48) and shows you that Jesus was ]{.class-0}["the Christ"]{.class-2}[ (John 1:20--31), the ]{.class-0}["Son"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 3:17; Mark 1:11; Luke 3:22), and ]{.class-0}["the Lamb"]{.class-2}[ (John 1:29, 36).]{.class-0}

Now what you have here is a seven-pronged candlestick of gold with a bowl sitting on the top of the middle branch. Each of the branches are hollow and the side branches are attached to the middle branch and opening into it. The oil is poured in the bowl of the middle branch and goes down through it into the side branches until all are filled to the tops where the lamps are (this is what is known as an "oil candle" where the wick floats on top of the oil).

On either side of the candlestick (the right and left sides) are two olive trees. Each tree has a golden pipe bringing the oil from the tree to the center bowl of the lamp (see vs. 12).

[Now Zechariah sees all of this, and he asks the Angel of the Lord, ]{.class-0}["What are these, my lord?"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 3). The answer that the Lord gives Zechariah is one that I find very annoying. He basically says, "Don't you know?" (]{.class-0}["Knowest thou not what these things be?"]{.class-2}[) Well, no (vs. 5). If he had, he wouldn't have asked you now, would he?]{.class-0}

[That's why I say the Bible is the most deceitful, treacherous, misleading Book you've ever read in your life. The things in that Book are disguised so you can't get them unless you've got the key. It is the one Book in the world that requires you to know the Author before you can understand it (1 Cor. 2:13--14). After that, you have to ]{.class-0}[read]{.class-1}[ it (Josh. 1:8), ]{.class-0}[believe]{.class-1}[ it (John 8:47), ]{.class-0}[search ]{.class-1}[it (John 5:39; Acts 17:11), ]{.class-0}[study]{.class-1}[ it (2 Tim. 2:15), and even then there are things in it you won't get unless the Author reveals them to you.]{.class-0}

6 Then he answered and spake unto me, saying, This is the word of the LORD unto Zerubbabel, saying, Not by might, nor by power, but by my spirit, saith the LORD of hosts.

7 Who art thou, O great mountain? before Zerubbabel thou shalt become a plain: and he shall bring forth the headstone thereof with shoutings, crying, Grace, grace unto it.

8 Moreover the word of the LORD came unto me, saying,

9 The hands of Zerubbabel have laid the foundation of this house; his hands shall also finish it; and thou shalt know that the LORD of hosts hath sent me unto you.

10 For who hath despised the day of small things? for they shall rejoice, and shall see the plummet in the hand of Zerubbabel with those seven; they are the eyes of the LORD, which run to and fro through the whole earth.

"[This is the word of the LORD unto Zerubbabel"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 6). The ]{.class-0}["this"]{.class-2}[ of verse 6 has nothing to do with the ]{.class-0}["these"]{.class-2}[ of verse 5. The Lord doesn't tell Zechariah what the ]{.class-0}["olive trees"]{.class-2}[ are until you get to verse 11. The ]{.class-0}["this"]{.class-2}[ refers to what the Lord is about to say to Zerubbabel.]{.class-0}

[This is a manner of speaking that Yankees have picked up from the Bible. A fellow will say something to someone, and the other person will say, "This is true." What does he mean "this"? Shouldn't he say ]{.class-0}["that's]{.class-1}[ true?" But that way of speaking comes from the Bible.]{.class-0}

[Come on over to Acts 2 and see the same thing. The Holy Spirit comes down on the Church at Pentecost, and the apostles begin to preach in other languages. The crowd to whom they're preaching is wondering what's going on when Peter gets up and says, ]{.class-0}["this is that which was spoken by the prophet Joel"]{.class-2}[ (Acts 2:16).]{.class-0}

That's what messed up every tongues-blabbering Charismatic from Bethel College in Topeka, Kansas and Azusa Street Mission in Los Angeles to TBN and the Brownsville Revival. They teach that Acts 2 was the "early rain" of the Holy Spirit and the modern Charismatic movement is the "latter rain" of the Holy Spirit. You talk about a bunch of screwed-up nonsense!

[In Acts 2:17--21, Peter quotes Joel 2:28--32. ]{.class-0}[There's nothing in Joel 2 about the First Advent or the giving of the Spirit at Pentecost at all;]{.class-1}[ the whole chapter---]{.class-0}[all 32 verses]{.class-1}[---is Second Advent from start to finish. The Holy Spirit wasn't poured out on ]{.class-0}["all flesh,"]{.class-2}[ not even all ]{.class-0}[Jewish]{.class-1}[ flesh as you find it in Zechariah 12:10. ]{.class-0}[There are no tongues mentioned in Joel 2.]{.class-1}[ The only people "prophesying" in Acts 2 are twelve apostles, ]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-0}["sons"]{.class-2}[ or ]{.class-0}["daughters"]{.class-2}[; and nobody is seeing ]{.class-0}["visions"]{.class-2}[ or dreaming ]{.class-0}["dreams."]{.class-2}

[There are no ]{.class-0}["wonders in heaven"]{.class-2}[ or ]{.class-0}["signs in the earth"]{.class-2}[: no ]{.class-0}["blood," ]{.class-2}[no ]{.class-0}["fire,"]{.class-2}[ no]{.class-0}[ "smoke,"]{.class-2}[ and no dark sun or bloody moon. In fact, there is not ]{.class-0}[one thing]{.class-1}[ that Joel says that takes place in Acts 2, not even the part about ]{.class-0}["whosoever shall call on the name of the Lord shall be saved"]{.class-2}[ (Acts 2:21), which in Joel 2 is a reference to ]{.class-0}[physical deliverance]{.class-1}[ of a Jewish remnant at the Second Advent (read Joel 2:32).]{.class-0}

[All Peter is doing in Acts 2 is saying, ]{.class-0}["This is what Joel said]{.class-1}[ would take place at the last days," and then telling you what Joel ]{.class-0}[said.]{.class-1}[ It has nothing to do with anything before it. That is what you have here in Zechariah 4:6---"Here's what God is saying to Zerubbabel."]{.class-0}

[Now here's what the Lord says to Zerubbabel: ]{.class-0}["Not by might, nor by power, but by my spirit, saith the LORD of hosts"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 6). In other words, if Zerubbabel is going to accomplish what the Lord has called him to do, then it won't be by physical or military might, or by political or material power. If the thing is going to be done, and done ]{.class-0}[right]{.class-1}[, it will be by the Spirit of God. The olive oil and the candlestick are both types of the Holy Spirit.]{.class-0}

[Verse 7: ]{.class-0}["Who art thou, O great mountain?"]{.class-2}[ What's that ]{.class-0}["mountain"]{.class-2}[? Beats the fire out of me; there was no ]{.class-0}["mountain"]{.class-2}[ in chapters 1, 2, 3, or in the first six verses here in chapter 4. Keep on reading.]{.class-0}

"[Before Zerubbabel thou shalt become a plain."]{.class-2}[ Isaiah 40:4 says, ]{.class-0}["Every valley shall be exalted, and every mountain and hill shall be made low: and the crooked shall be made straight, and the rough places plain."]{.class-2}[ That's a reference to Christ's Second Coming (see the context in Isa. 40:1--5). So the picture here in Zechariah 4:7 is Zerubbabel as a type of Christ at the Second Advent. Look at the next part of the verse.]{.class-0}

"[And he shall bring forth the headstone thereof with shoutings, crying, Grace, grace unto it."]{.class-2}[ Why, that ]{.class-0}["headstone"]{.class-2}[ is the top cornerstone of a ]{.class-0}[pyramid]{.class-1}[ which we discussed in the comments on 3:9; notice the ]{.class-0}["seven eyes"]{.class-2}[ in it in verse 10 of this chapter. It's a reference to Jesus Christ. Matthew 21:42 says, ]{.class-0}["The STONE which the builders rejected, the same is become the HEAD of the corner."]{.class-2}

[Notice that that ]{.class-0}["headstone"]{.class-2}[ crushes whomever it falls on: ]{.class-0}["And whosoever shall fall on this stone shall be broken: but on whomsoever it shall fall, it will grind him to powder"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 21:46).]{.class-0}

That's the smiting stone of Daniel 2 that destroys the kingdom of the Antichrist.

"[And in the days of these kings shall the God of heaven set up a kingdom, which shall never be destroyed: and the kingdom shall not be left to other people, but it shall break in pieces and consume all these kingdoms, and it shall stand for ever. Forasmuch as thou sawest that the stone was cut out of the MOUNTAIN without hands, and that it broke in pieces the iron, the brass, the clay, the silver, and the gold"]{.class-2}[ (Dan. 2:44--45).]{.class-0}

[So there is a transfer of ]{.class-0}[kingdoms]{.class-1}[ going on here. That ]{.class-0}["stone"]{.class-2}[ is cut out of one mountain and levels the image representing Gentile kingdoms; it then becomes ]{.class-0}["a great mountain"]{.class-2}[ of its own, which is the kingdom God sets up (Dan. 2:35, 44).]{.class-0}

[Ezekiel 21:25--27 says, ]{.class-0}["And thou, profane wicked prince of Israel ]{.class-2}[\[a prophetic reference to the Antichrist\]]{.class-0}[, whose day is come, when iniquity shall have an end]{.class-2}[ \[the end of the Tribulation---see the comments on Hab. 2:3\]]{.class-0}[. Thus saith the Lord GOD; Remove the diadem, and take off the crown: this shall not be the same: exalt him that is low ]{.class-2}[\[Christ\]]{.class-0}[, and abase him that is high ]{.class-2}[\[the Antichrist\]]{.class-0}[. I will overturn, overturn, overturn it: and it shall be no more, until he come]{.class-2}[ \[the Second Coming of Christ\]]{.class-0}[ whose right it is; and I will give it him ]{.class-2}[\[just like He promised in Luke 1:32--33\]]{.class-0}[."]{.class-2}

[Back to Zechariah 4: ]{.class-0}["The hands of Zerubbabel have laid the foundation of this house; his hands shall also finish it"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 9). Now we are back to the rebuilding of the Temple in the book of Ezra. This was the theme of Haggai 1;]{.class-0}[ it was fulfilled in Ezra 6:14--15.]{.class-1}

[Verse 10:]{.class-0}[ "For who hath despised the day of small things?"]{.class-2}[ It is very common to overlook ]{.class-0}["small things."]{.class-2}[ Men are masters at that; they're always concerned with the big things and forget the small stuff.]{.class-0}

[When I was in College, one of my teachers told me, "Ruckman, you've got to learn how to pay attention ]{.class-0}[to the small details."]{.class-1}[ That was something I didn't want to do, but I finally learned what he meant by having to write all the time. I found that I had to proofread something ]{.class-0}[five times]{.class-1}[ before I got the thing straight, and then, I would still be missing something.]{.class-0}

"[Who hath despised the day of small things?"]{.class-2}[ It is the ]{.class-0}[little things]{.class-1}[ that will make or break you. Like memorizing your Greek vocabulary words and your English parts of speech, student. Like remembering anniversaries and birthdays, and picking up after yourself (husband). Like cleaning the house and washing the dishes (wife).]{.class-0}

[Over in Song of Solomon 2:15, it says, ]{.class-0}["the little foxes spoil the vines."]{.class-2}[ It's not the ]{.class-0}[big]{.class-1}[ sins---like adultery, murder, kidnapping, grand theft, etc.---that are going to bring you down, Christian. It will be the ]{.class-0}[little ones]{.class-1}[ like laziness, gluttony, carelessness, conceit, and the like.]{.class-0}

"[Who hath despised the day of small things?"]{.class-2}[ There is an old poem that goes: "For want of a nail the shoe was lost; for want of a shoe the horse was lost\...." That thing goes on until ]{.class-0}[a King loses a war]{.class-1}[ just because someone didn't have a little thing like a horseshoe nail.]{.class-0}

[One time during the Civil War, a kid picked up a cigar wrapper that had been tossed on the ground. It just happened to have Lee's attack plans drawn out on it for the Battle of Gettysburg. That boy turned it over to the Yankee Generals. The Battle of Gettysburg was a turning point for the worse for the South in the Civil War, and all because of a small thing like a kid picking up a piece of trash. The Apostle Paul's life was saved one time over a boy (his nephew) overhearing a conversation and reporting it to the Romans (Acts 23:16--22); that was just a ]{.class-0}[small thing.]{.class-1}

"[Who hath despised the day of small things?"]{.class-2}[ Most of the real Bible-believing Christian leaders of this country---the ones who have really done a job for the Lord---have come from ]{.class-0}[small]{.class-1}[ places, not big cities. Bob Jones Sr. was born in Alabama. Was he born in Mobile? No. Birmingham? No. Montgomery? No. He was born in ]{.class-0}[Skipperville,]{.class-1}[ Alabama. Where's that? Well, it's near Dothan, but it isn't on a map. He came from ]{.class-0}[Brannon Stand]{.class-1}[ in Skipperville---some little place where a pioneer made a stand against the Indians and got killed.]{.class-0}

[When he was alive, Jack Hyles had the largest Sunday School in America. Hyles was born in ]{.class-0}[Italy,]{.class-1}[ Texas. Does anyone know where Italy, Texas is without looking it up?]{.class-0}

[Gipsy Smith was from ]{.class-0}[Wanstead ]{.class-1}[(]{.class-0}[Epping Forest]{.class-1}[), England. Lee Roberson was born in ]{.class-0}[English,]{.class-1}[ Indiana. John R. Rice was born in ]{.class-0}[Gainesville, ]{.class-1}[Texas. Beauchamp Vick was born in ]{.class-0}[Russellville,]{.class-1}[ Kentucky.]{.class-0}

[Mel Trotter was born in ]{.class-0}[Orangeville,]{.class-1}[ Illinois---not Chicago. Lester Roloff didn't come from Dallas or Austin or San Antonio; he came from ]{.class-0}[Dawson,]{.class-1}[ Texas. Adoniram Judson wasn't from Boston; he was born in ]{.class-0}[Malden,]{.class-1}[ Massachusetts.]{.class-0}

[W. B. Riley was born in ]{.class-0}[Green County,]{.class-1}[ Indiana. George Truett was born in ]{.class-0}[Hayesville,]{.class-1}[ North Carolina. Peter Cartwright was born in ]{.class-0}[Amherst County,]{.class-1}[ Virginia. Sam Jones came from ]{.class-0}[Oak Bowery,]{.class-1}[ Alabama; and J. Frank Norris was from ]{.class-0}[Dadeville,]{.class-1}[ Alabama.]{.class-0}

[David Livingstone, the missionary who opened the interior of Africa to the Gospel, was born in ]{.class-0}[Blantyre,]{.class-1}[ Scotland. And the guy who led Billy Graham to the Lord---Mordecai Ham---was born in ]{.class-0}[Scottsville,]{.class-1}[ Kentucky.]{.class-0}

All those fellows came from little towns that hardly anybody has ever heard of.

"[Who hath despised the day of small things?"]{.class-2}[ You can't afford to overlook them. Mother, that baby of yours is just a tiny little thing, but you had better not treat the responsibility of raising that child lightly. Adolf Hitler's mother had a baby; did she despise it? Genghis Khan's mother had a baby; did she despise it? Charlemagne's mother had a baby; did she despise it?]{.class-0}

[I don't know whether you ever noticed it or not, but throughout the books of 1 & 2 Kings, when a King begins his reign, the Bible will often tell you who his ]{.class-0}[mother]{.class-1}[ was. Then you are told whether that King was a good King or a bad King. You can infer from that that the mother is given the credit for raising a good son or she gets the blame when that son is wicked.]{.class-0}

"[The plummet"]{.class-2}[ of verse 18 is the "bob" at the end of a ]{.class-0}["plumbline,"]{.class-2}[ and you can see the comments on that under Amos 7:8 in Volume I of this Commentary.]{.class-0}

[We have already identified the ]{.class-0}["seven eyes"]{.class-2}[ of verse 10 in the comments on 3:9. The verse here says, ]{.class-0}["the eyes of the LORD\...run to and fro through the whole earth."]{.class-2}[ Well, obviously that's figurative. God's eyeballs aren't running around somewhere looking for something. That's what is known as an ]{.class-0}[anthropomorphism]{.class-1}[---ascribing human bodily features to God or human features to a non-human thing.]{.class-0}

[Of course, the Lord came down to earth as a man, but the essence of God is that of ]{.class-0}["a Spirit."]{.class-2}

"[God is a Spirit: and they that worship him must worship him in spirit and in truth"]{.class-2}[ (John 4:24).]{.class-0}

[Verse 10 is talking about the omnipresence and omniscience of God. Proverbs 15:3 says, ]{.class-0}["The eyes of the LORD are in every place, beholding the evil and the good."]{.class-2}

11 Then answered I, and said unto him, What are these two olive trees upon the right side of the candlestick and upon the left side thereof?

12 And I answered again, and said unto him, What be these two olive branches which through the two golden pipes empty the golden oil out of themselves?

13 And he answered me and said, Knowest thou not what these be? And I said, No, my lord.

14 Then said he, These are the two anointed ones, that stand by the Lord of the whole earth.

[Now Zechariah's back on the subject of the Menorah and the olive trees. This is obviously not the Golden Candlestick in the Tabernacle and the Temple. There were no olive trees in the Holy Place. The olive trees here are feeding the Menorah through ]{.class-0}["golden pipes"]{.class-2}[ to keep it burning continually. In the Tabernacle, the priests were to keep the Golden Candlestick burning all the time.]{.class-0}

"[Command the children of Israel, that they bring unto thee pure oil beaten for the light, to cause the lamps to burn continually. Without the vail of the testimony, in the tabernacle of the congregation, shall Aaron order it from the evening unto the morning before the LORD continually: it shall be a statute for ever in your generations. He shall order the lamps upon the pure candlestick before the LORD continually"]{.class-2}[ (Lev. 24:2--4).]{.class-0}

That Golden Candlestick was to be kept burning morning and night. That's where the Catholics get the "eternal flame" they put on JFK's grave in Arlington Cemetery. The thing hadn't been burning 24 hours before someone blew it out and it had to be relit.

When Israel got backslidden in the days of the judges, Eli and his sons let that lamp go out at night (1 Sam. 3:3). That was a picture of the Jews not getting any revelation from God through His word (1 Sam. 3:1).

[Now look at what Zechariah's seeing. There's a ]{.class-0}[golden]{.class-1}[ candlestick (vs. 2) with ]{.class-0}["GOLDEN pipes"]{.class-2}[ feeding ]{.class-0}["GOLDEN oil"]{.class-2}[ into it. You're looking at a scene from a ]{.class-0}[golden city]{.class-1}[ (Rev. 21:21). Zechariah is having a vision of something in Heaven. Look at verse 14.]{.class-0}

"[These are the two anointed ones, that stand by the Lord of the whole earth."]{.class-2}

[So the olive trees are figurative, and the figure stands for two ]{.class-0}[people.]{.class-1}[ Revelation 11:3--4 defines those people as the ]{.class-0}["two witnesses"]{.class-2}[ who show up during the last half of Daniel's Seventieth Week. The Mormons and the Christian Scientists come out with the ridiculous private interpretation that these two witnesses are ]{.class-0}[two books]{.class-1}[ (the Mormons say it is the Bible and the ]{.class-0}[Book of Mormon,]{.class-1}[ and the Christian Scientists say it is the Bible and Mary Baker Eddy's ]{.class-0}[Key to the Scriptures]{.class-1}[). Such heretical nonsense comes from a ]{.class-0}[total disregard for the Scriptures.]{.class-1}

[Zechariah told you ]{.class-0}["the two anointed ones" ]{.class-2}[were around in 519 B.C. (notice the present tense verb ]{.class-0}["are"]{.class-2}[ in vs. 14), so there isn't the least chance that either one of them could be the ]{.class-0}[Book of Mormon]{.class-1}[ or ]{.class-0}[Key to the Scriptures.]{.class-1}[ Moreover, the ]{.class-0}["two witnesses"]{.class-2}[ were ]{.class-0}["two prophets"]{.class-2}[ with ]{.class-0}[mouths]{.class-1}[ (Rev. 11:5), ]{.class-0}["feet"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 11:11), and ]{.class-0}["bodies"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 11:8) that could be ]{.class-0}["hurt"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 11:5), ]{.class-0}[killed]{.class-1}[ (Rev. 11:7), ]{.class-0}["put in graves"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 11:9), and ]{.class-0}[resurrected]{.class-1}[ (Rev. 11:11).]{.class-0}

[The Fundamental and Conservative scholars recognize the ]{.class-0}["two witnesses"]{.class-2}[ of Revelation 11 are ]{.class-0}[people,]{.class-1}[ but they identify them as Enoch and Elijah because they were the only two Old Testament saints that were caught up bodily into Heaven (see Heb. 11:5; 2 Kings 2:11). They forget (or ignore) the fact that ]{.class-0}[Moses']{.class-1}[ body was taken up to Heaven after his death (Jude 9).]{.class-0}

[Now why couldn't one of the witnesses be Enoch? Well, although Enoch was indeed a prophet (Jude 14--15), the Bible never speaks of him as standing before the Lord (Gen. 5:24). The two people who ]{.class-0}["stand by the Lord"]{.class-2}[ on the Mount of Transfiguration were Moses and Elijah (Matt. 17:3).]{.class-0}

Want to see it again? Look at 1 Kings 17:1 and Exodus 33:21.

"[And Elijah the Tishbite\...said unto Ahab, As the LORD God of Israel liveth, BEFORE WHOM I STAND"]{.class-2}[ (1 Kings 17:1).]{.class-0}

"[And the LORD said, Behold, there is a place BY ME, and thou shalt STAND upon a rock"]{.class-2}[ (Exod. 33:21).]{.class-0}

[Furthermore, the ]{.class-0}[Transfiguration]{.class-1}[ was a picture of the Second Advent (2 Pet. 1:16--18). When the Old Testament closes, do you know what two names show up in connection with Christ's Advent? Malachi 4:4--5, ]{.class-0}["Remember ye the law of MOSES my servant, which I commanded unto him in Horeb for all Israel, with the statutes and judgments. Behold, I will send you ELIJAH the prophet before the COMING OF THE GREAT AND DREADFUL DAY OF THE LORD."]{.class-2}

[Enoch doesn't enter the picture at all. The ]{.class-0}["two witnesses"]{.class-2}[ of Revelation 11 ]{.class-0}[die.]{.class-1}[ If Enoch were one of them, there would be no picture of New Testament saints who ]{.class-0}[never die ]{.class-1}[and ]{.class-0}[never will die]{.class-1}[ (1 Cor. 15:53--54; 1 Thess. 4:17). You see, Enoch was a ]{.class-0}[Gentile]{.class-1}[, not a Jew. He was raptured before the judgment of God fell on the whole earth at the Flood. He represents Gentile Christians in the Body of Christ who are caught up to Heaven in a ]{.class-0}[pre]{.class-1}[-Tribulation rapture. Moses and Elijah are ]{.class-0}[Jews]{.class-1}[ who represent ]{.class-0}[Jews]{.class-1}[ (both living \[Elijah\] and dead \[Moses\]) caught up in a ]{.class-0}[post]{.class-1}[-Tribulation rapture (Rev. 14), but who die in the Millennium (Isa. 65:20).]{.class-0}

[Now if there is any doubt in your mind about the identity of these ]{.class-0}["two witnesses,"]{.class-2}[ just look at the description of them in Revelation 11:5--6.]{.class-0}

"[And if any man will hurt them, fire proceedeth out of their mouth, and devoureth their enemies\..."]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 11:5). Moses told Korah, Dathan, and Abiram: ]{.class-0}["But if the LORD make a new thing, and the earth open her mouth, and swallow them up, with all that appertain unto them, and they go down quick into the pit; then ye shall understand that these men have provoked the LORD"]{.class-2}[ (Num. 16:30). When he says that, the earth opens up, and they go down alive into ]{.class-0}[the fire]{.class-1}[, and ]{.class-0}[fire]{.class-1}[ comes out of the Tabernacle and does its work on the priests who stand with them (Num. 16:22, 35). Over in 2 Kings 1, Ahaziah sends his soldiers to bring in Elijah, and twice Elijah says: ]{.class-0}["If I be a man of God, then let fire come down from heaven, and consume thee and thy fifty,"]{.class-2}[ and that's exactly what happened (2 Kings 1:10--12).]{.class-0}

"[These have power to shut heaven, that it rain not in the days of their prophecy\..."]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 11:6). ]{.class-0}["The days of their prophecy"]{.class-2}[ are ]{.class-0}[1,260 days]{.class-1}[ (Rev. 11:3): that's 42 months (Rev. 13:5), three-and-a-half years (Rev. 12:14). That's what Elijah did, and he did it for the exact same amount of time.]{.class-0}

"[Elias was a man subject to like passions as we are, and he prayed earnestly that it might not rain: and it RAINED NOT on the earth by the space of THREE YEARS AND SIX MONTHS"]{.class-2}[ (James 5:17).]{.class-0}

"[And have power over waters to turn them to blood, and to smite the earth with all plagues, as often as they will"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 11:6). That's Moses in Exodus 7--12; ]{.class-0}[that's not Enoch]{.class-1}[. Enoch doesn't factor into the entire discussion.]{.class-0}

For further study, we refer you to the comments on Revelation 11:3--6 in that Commentary.

[]{#section-0_1_66a27acfdd14f135_b691.xhtml}

CHAPTER FIVE

[We come to a chapter here that deals with ]{.class-0}[UFOs.]{.class-1}[ Now they're not]{.class-0}[ flying saucers]{.class-1}[, but they are UFOs. People have gotten so used to calling UFOs "flying saucers" that they have forgotten that the acronym UFO doesn't stand for "Flying Saucer" at all. A UFO is an Unidentified Flying Object. There have been UFOs in ]{.class-0}[cigar]{.class-1}[ shapes, ]{.class-0}[football]{.class-1}[ shapes, ]{.class-0}[tear drop]{.class-1}[ shapes, and ]{.class-0}[ball]{.class-1}[ shapes, as well as ]{.class-0}[saucer]{.class-1}[ shapes (see chap. 3 of ]{.class-0}[Black is Beautiful]{.class-1}[).]{.class-0}

[In the passage before us, there are ]{.class-0}[three]{.class-1}[ Unidentified Flying Objects. The first one is in verses 1--4, and the second and third ones are in verses 5--11. There are two more in the Scriptures: one is found in Ezekiel 1, and there's another one in 2 Samuel 5:24. You say, "What are all those things flying around there?" Nobody knows for sure. That's why they're called ]{.class-0}[Unidentified]{.class-1}[ Flying Objects.]{.class-0}

5:1 Then I turned, and lifted up mine eyes, and looked, and behold a flying roll.

2 And he said unto me, What seest thou? And I answered, I see a flying roll; the length thereof is twenty cubits, and the breadth thereof ten cubits.

3 Then said he unto me, This is the curse that goeth forth over the face of the whole earth: for every one that stealeth shall be cut off as on this side according to it; and every one that sweareth shall be cut off as on that side according to it.

4 I will bring it forth, saith the LORD of hosts, and it shall enter into the house of the thief, and into the house of him that sweareth falsely by my name: and it shall remain in the midst of his house, and shall consume it with the timber thereof and the stones thereof.

[The first UFO is ]{.class-0}["a flying roll."]{.class-2}[ Of course, that's not a roll like a biscuit or a bun. That's a ]{.class-0}[scroll,]{.class-1}[ which is papyrus or parchment with writing on it that is rolled up.]{.class-0}

[This ]{.class-0}["flying roll"]{.class-2}[ is ]{.class-0}["twenty cubits"]{.class-2}[ long and ]{.class-0}["ten cubits"]{.class-2}[ wide. A cubit is about eighteen inches, so the thing measures approximately thirty feet by fifteen feet. You've never seen a scroll that big, I don't reckon. Obviously, whatever this thing is, it's not an ordinary writing scroll like the one on which Baruch wrote the book of Jeremiah (Jer. 36:2).]{.class-0}

[Now, whatever this thing is, it's connected to the Second Advent. Look at verse 3: ]{.class-0}["This is the curse that goeth forth over the face of the whole earth."]{.class-2}[ That's how your Old Testament ends.]{.class-0}

"[Behold, I will send you Elijah the prophet before the coming of the great and dreadful day of the LORD ]{.class-2}[\[Second Advent\]]{.class-0}[: And he shall turn the heart of the fathers to the children, and the heart of the children to their fathers, lest I come and SMITE THE EARTH WITH A CURSE" ]{.class-2}[(Mal. 4:5--6).]{.class-0}

[So in Zechariah 4:14, up shows Elijah, and within three verses, up shows that ]{.class-0}["curse."]{.class-2}

[Now what is this ]{.class-0}["curse"]{.class-2}[? Verse 3: ]{.class-0}["for every one that stealeth shall be cut off as on this side according to it; and every one that sweareth shall be cut off as on that side according to it."]{.class-2}

[There are all kinds of things that take place during the Tribulation that could be considered a ]{.class-0}["curse,"]{.class-2}[ but this thing is directly on people. And whatever this curse is, it's ]{.class-0}[selective:]{.class-1}[ it singles out ]{.class-0}[thieves]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[blasphemers.]{.class-1}[ Those two sins (stealing and blasphemy) characterize the Antichrist. He's an ]{.class-0}["idol shepherd"]{.class-2}[ (see 11:17 and comments on that verse) whom the Good Shepherd calls ]{.class-0}["a thief and a robber"]{.class-2}[ (John 10:1), and in Revelation 13:5, he has ]{.class-0}["a mouth speaking great\...blasphemies."]{.class-2}[ ]{.class-0}[So this curse affects the Antichrist and his followers.]{.class-1}

[But it does more than just that. Verse 5:]{.class-0}[ "it shall enter into the HOUSE of the thief, and into the HOUSE of him that sweareth falsely by my name: and it shall remain in the midst of the HOUSE, and shall consume it with the timber thereof and the stones thereof."]{.class-2}

[So this curse not only affects those who follow the Antichrist, it goes into houses and rots the houses and everything in them. There is only one disease in the Bible that affects ]{.class-0}[people]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[things:]{.class-1}[ it's leprosy. Look at Leviticus 14:34, ]{.class-0}["When ye be come into the land of Canaan, which I give to you for a possession, and I put the plague of leprosy in a house."]{.class-2}

[Leviticus 14:44--45, ]{.class-0}["Then the priest shall come and look, and, behold, if the plague be spread in the house, it is a fretting leprosy in the house: it is unclean. And he shall break down the house, the stones of it, and the timber thereof, and all the mortar of the house; and he shall carry them forth out of the city into an unclean place."]{.class-2}

[Leprosy can get into ]{.class-0}[people]{.class-1}[ (Lev. 13:1--46), it can get into ]{.class-0}[houses]{.class-1}[ (Lev. 14:33--45), and it can get into ]{.class-0}[clothes:]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-0}["the garment also that the plague of leprosy is in"]{.class-2}[ (Lev. 13:47). That leprosy in people shows up as a ]{.class-0}["bright spot"]{.class-2}[ (Lev. 13:2) or a ]{.class-0}["white reddish sore"]{.class-2}[ (Lev. 13:42--43).]{.class-0}

[In the Tribulation, the Lord sends ]{.class-0}["a noisome and grievous sore upon the men which had the mark of the beast, and upon them which worshipped his image"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 16:2). The Beast is ]{.class-0}["a leopard"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 13:2---watch out for that thirteen!)---a ]{.class-0}[spotted]{.class-1}[ cat (Jer. 13:23---watch out for that ]{.class-0}[thirteen!]{.class-1}[). So if you have the mark of a leopard, you have ]{.class-0}[spots.]{.class-1}

"[But these, as natural brute BEASTS, made to be taken and DESTROYED\...SPOTS they are and blemishes"]{.class-2}[ (2 Pet. 2:13---watch out for that thirteen!).]{.class-0}

[When the people in the Tribulation take the Mark of the Beast, they take a ]{.class-0}[spot]{.class-1}[ called a rosetta, which looks like a ]{.class-0}[kiss]{.class-1}[ from two huge ]{.class-0}[black lips]{.class-1}[ (cf. Luke 22:47--48). Now, we are not going to get into all the material on the Mark of the Beast here; for a thorough study of the subject, get the work entitled ]{.class-0}[The Mark of the Beast]{.class-1}[ and read chapter 4. What is pertinent for our study here is that when the Lord pours out that first vial in Revelation 16, that spot turns into a ]{.class-0}["grievous sore"]{.class-2}[ that turns out to be ]{.class-0}[leprosy]{.class-1}[. That leprosy gets into the ]{.class-0}[garments]{.class-1}[ (Jude 23) and the houses (Zech. 5:3--4) and ]{.class-0}[rots]{.class-1}[ everything it touches. ]{.class-0}[In the Old Testament, the only way a Gentile gets rid of that leprosy is to go down under the water publicly seven times ]{.class-1}[(2 Kings 5:10--14).]{.class-0}

5 Then the angel that talked with me went forth, and said unto me, Lift up now thine eyes, and see what is this that goeth forth.

6 And I said, What is it? And he said, This is an ephah that goeth forth. He said moreover, This is their resemblance through all the earth.

7 And, behold, there was lifted up a talent of lead: and this is a woman that sitteth in the midst of the ephah.

8 And he said, This is wickedness. And he cast it into the midst of the ephah; and he cast the weight of lead upon the mouth thereof.

9 Then lifted I up mine eyes, and looked, and, behold, there came out two women, and the wind was in their wings; for they had wings like the wings of a stork: and they lifted up the ephah between the earth and the heaven.

10 Then said I to the angel that talked with me, Whither do these bear the ephah?

11 And he said unto me, To build it an house in the land of Shinar: and it shall be established, and set there upon her own base.

[The second UFO shows up in verse 5. Zechariah looked at the thing, and he still didn't know what it was. The thing is described by the Angel of the Lord as ]{.class-0}["an ephah"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 6). An ephah is like a bushel basket. You can see some things that look like it in chapter 4 of ]{.class-0}[Black is Beautiful.]{.class-1}[ One was seen in China in 1960, and another one was seen in Russia in 1965.]{.class-0}

[Notice the peculiar switch in verse 6 from singular (]{.class-0}["it"]{.class-2}[) to plural (]{.class-0}["their"]{.class-2}[). Now why that is, I don't know, unless it's referring to the ephah ]{.class-0}[and]{.class-1}[ what's in it.]{.class-0}

[There's ]{.class-0}["a talent of lead"]{.class-2}[ along with that ephah (vs. 7), and the text says, ]{.class-0}["this is a woman that sitteth in the midst of the ephah."]{.class-2}[ Well, is the ]{.class-0}["talent of lead"]{.class-2}[ a ]{.class-0}["woman"]{.class-2}[? Is it an image of a woman made out of lead? I don't know.]{.class-0}

[The way I usually read it is that the woman is sitting in that ephah (kind of like a pilot in a cockpit), and that the ]{.class-0}["talent of lead"]{.class-2}[ is something like a manhole cover floating over her head. An angel comes along and takes that lead manhole cover and slams it down on the top of that ephah, sealing the woman in (kind of like a hatch on an aircraft).]{.class-0}

[Now that woman is defined in verse 8 as ]{.class-0}["wickedness,"]{.class-2}[ so what you have here is an evil spirit that is ]{.class-0}[female.]{.class-1}[ Then she couldn't be a "fallen angel." Nearly all the Bible scholars say that demons are fallen angels,]{.class-0}[ but not one angel in that Bible is a woman.]{.class-1}[ Every angel in the Bible is ]{.class-0}[male]{.class-1}[ (see Gen. 19:1, 5; Judg. 13:3--6; Acts 1:10; Rev. 21:17). Brethren, Satan's kingdom is composed of more than just "demons"; there are ]{.class-0}["principalities," "powers," "the rulers of darkness of this world," "spiritual wickedness in high places"]{.class-2}[ (Eph. 6:12), as well as ]{.class-0}["thrones"]{.class-2}[ and]{.class-0}[ "dominions"]{.class-2}[ (Col. 1:16).]{.class-0}

[What you have here is female ]{.class-0}["wickedness."]{.class-2}[ In Jeremiah 44:17--19, 25, she is called ]{.class-0}["the queen of heaven."]{.class-2}[ In the Old Testament, she was worshipped under the name of ]{.class-0}[Ashtoreth]{.class-1}[, the consort of Baal (Judg. 2:13; she was also known as]{.class-0}[ "Ishtar"]{.class-1}[ or ]{.class-0}["Istarte,"]{.class-1}[ from whence comes the word ]{.class-0}[Easter]{.class-1}[); the Roman Catholic Church calls her ]{.class-0}["Mary." ]{.class-1}[In Revelation 17, she is called ]{.class-0}["MYSTERY, BABYLON THE GREAT, THE MOTHER OF HARLOTS AND ABOMINATIONS OF THE EARTH"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 17:5).]{.class-0}

[After this ]{.class-0}[female demon]{.class-1}[ has been sealed in the ephah with the lead weight, out of the basket come ]{.class-0}["two women"]{.class-2}[ who have ]{.class-0}["wings"]{.class-2}[ like those ]{.class-0}["of a stork"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 9). So from this passage come three "old wives' tales" that are just as phony as any ]{.class-0}["fable"]{.class-2}[ Aesop ever concocted \[cf. 1 Tim. 4:7\]: 1) Storks bring babies, 2) angels are women, 3) angels have wings. You can file all three right along with ]{.class-0}[The Three Billy Goats Gruff, Rumplestiltskin,]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[Jack and the Beanstalk.]{.class-1}

[Now, these stork-winged women are obviously connected with the woman in the ephah. Since that woman is ]{.class-0}["THE MOTHER OF HARLOTS,"]{.class-2}[ I take these to be two of her children. They are ]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ angels, for not only are there no female angels (see comments on vss. 7--8), ]{.class-0}[there are no angels with wings.]{.class-1}

[Hebrews 13:2 says, ]{.class-0}["Be not forgetful to entertain strangers: for thereby some have entertained angels unawares."]{.class-2}[ Well, don't you think someone would notice if a visitor had a pair of wings with a ten to fifteen foot span? When those angels show up in Genesis 18--19, no one gives them a second glance; they appear as ]{.class-0}[normal men with no wings.]{.class-1}[ When these matters are brought up, someone inevitably points to the ]{.class-0}["angel flying through the midst of heaven"]{.class-2}[ in Revelation 8:13 as proof that angels have to have wings to be able to fly, but the comic books are filled with both men and women flying around with no wings at all (Superman, Wonder Woman, Captain Marvel, and that bunch).]{.class-0}

[Now, the Devil has wings, but ]{.class-0}[he never was an angel.]{.class-1}[ When Billy Graham talks about the Devil being a fallen angel, he hasn't any idea about what he's saying than the man in the moon. The Bible never calls the Devil ]{.class-0}[an angel; ]{.class-1}[it says he ]{.class-0}["is transformed into an angel of light"]{.class-2}[ exactly as his ]{.class-0}["workers"]{.class-2}[ transform ]{.class-0}["themselves into the apostles of Christ"]{.class-2}[ (2 Cor. 11:13--14). ]{.class-0}[Neither one of them is the genuine article.]{.class-1}

[Before his fall, Satan was a ]{.class-0}[cherub]{.class-1}[ (Ezek. 28:14--16), and ]{.class-0}[cherubs have wings]{.class-1}[ (Ezek. 1, 10). The spirits connected with Satan are pictured as ]{.class-0}[unclean birds.]{.class-1}[ In Christ's Parable of the Sower, the birds (plural---Mark 4:4) are called ]{.class-0}["Satan"]{.class-2}[ (singular---Mark 4:15).]{.class-0}

[The Fundamentalist teaching that the ]{.class-0}[KJV]{.class-1}[ translation of ]{.class-0}[daimonion]{.class-1}[ (]{.class-0}["devils"]{.class-2}[) is wrong because "there is only one Devil but many demons" is just so much theological ]{.class-0}[baloney.]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-16}[Jesus Christ is THE Son of God, but He has a Body made up of ]{.class-0}[sons]{.class-1}[ of God. In the same way, Satan is THE Devil who has a spiritual kingdom made up of ]{.class-0}[devils.]{.class-1}[ These unclean spirits are pictured as ]{.class-0}[winged creatures.]{.class-1}

[Revelation 18:2 says, ]{.class-0}["Babylon the great is fallen, is fallen, and is become the habitation of DEVILS, and the hold of every FOUL SPIRIT, and a cage of every UNCLEAN AND HATEFUL BIRD."]{.class-2}

[When Jesus was baptized, the Holy Spirit came down on Him in the form of a ]{.class-0}[dove]{.class-1}[ (Matt. 3:16). What color is a dove? ]{.class-0}[It's white. ]{.class-1}[When Noah wanted to find out if the waters of the Flood were gone from the earth, he sent out a ]{.class-0}[white]{.class-1}[ bird (a dove---Gen. 8:8--11) and a ]{.class-0}[black]{.class-1}[ bird (a raven---Gen. 8:7). Well, if that ]{.class-0}[white]{.class-1}[ bird represents the Holy Spirit, what do you think that ]{.class-0}[black]{.class-1}[ bird represents?]{.class-0}

[At this point I can hear the screams of ]{.class-0}["Racist!" "Arayan nation!" "White supremacist!"]{.class-1}[ Shut your mouth! That's a racist attitude that would ]{.class-0}[change the Bible]{.class-1}[ in order to cover up for ]{.class-0}[one race]{.class-1}[ (see the comments on Song of Sol. 1:5, 5:10 in that Commentary) and create a mass of ]{.class-0}[mongrelized automatons ]{.class-1}[that can be controlled by the government (see the comments on Gen. 11:1--4 in that Commentary). In the Bible, that ]{.class-0}[white]{.class-1}[ dove is a ]{.class-0}[clean]{.class-1}[ bird that pictures a ]{.class-0}[clean]{.class-1}[ Spirit (the Holy Spirit), and that ]{.class-0}[black]{.class-1}[ raven is an ]{.class-0}[unclean]{.class-1}[ bird (Lev. 11:13--15) that represents an ]{.class-0}[unclean]{.class-1}[ spirit (Satan).]{.class-0}

[In Matthew 12:24, Satan is called ]{.class-0}["Beelzebub the prince of the devils."]{.class-2}[ Beelzebub was the god of ]{.class-0}[filth.]{.class-1}[ The name means "lord of the flies." Flies are ]{.class-0}[small, unclean, winged]{.class-1}[ creatures. This is another way that you know "demons" are ]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ fallen angels. Mary Magdalene had ]{.class-0}["seven devils"]{.class-2}[ in her (Luke 8:2); the maniac of Gadara had a thousand (Mark 5:12--13 cf. Matt. 8:28). You aren't going to fit a thousand or even seven creatures the size of a full-grown man into a person's body, but you could if the beings were the size of birds or flies.]{.class-0}

[Now the women here in Zechariah 5:9 have ]{.class-0}[storks']{.class-1}[ wings. ]{.class-0}[The stork is listed as an unclean bird in Leviticus 11:19 and Deuteronomy 14:18, ]{.class-1}[so these two women are connected to ]{.class-0}[Satan,]{.class-1}[ not God. In Greek mythology, those winged women are known as "harpies" (see chap. 3 of ]{.class-0}[Black is Beautiful]{.class-1}[).]{.class-0}

[According to Alfred Edersheim (]{.class-0}[History of the Jewish Nation,]{.class-1}[ Vol. 2, pg. 272), when Catholics in the Middle Ages painted angelic women with wings, they were actually depicting a Jewish myth called ]{.class-0}[Lilith]{.class-1}[. The legend of Lilith was created by the apostate Rabbis who put together the ]{.class-0}[Babylonian Talmud]{.class-1}[---AFTER Zechariah wrote his prophecy. Lilith was supposed to be Adam's first wife (taken from a ridiculous ]{.class-0}["private interpretation"]{.class-2}[ of Gen. 1:27) who wouldn't submit to his authority, so she ran off and married Satan and gave birth to creatures called ]{.class-0}[succubi]{.class-1}[. Lilith and the succubi were ]{.class-0}[night demons]{.class-1}[ who seduced men in their sleep. They were pictured as ]{.class-0}[winged female spirits.]{.class-1}

[Storks]{.class-1}[ were sacred to Venus (the Roman name for Ishtar \[Ashtoreth\], the ]{.class-0}["queen of heaven"]{.class-2}[). Venus was the goddess of fertility, so if two storks built a nest on your roof (usually the chimney), it meant your family was going to receive a "blessing" from Venus (a baby); hence, the stork bringing babies down the chimney. The Catholics connected the stork to the Annunciation and Easter because of the bird's associations with Spring (the season of fertility). The Scandinavians said a stork flew around the cross at the Crucifixion crying, "Strengthen! Strengthen!"]{.class-0}

[(I've got a joke in my Bible here about a family of storks. One night the mother is gone, and the baby asks the father, "Where's Momma?" "She's off bringing babies to people and making them happy," says the father. The next night the father stork is gone, and the baby asks the mother, "Where's Daddy?" The mother stork tells the baby stork, "He's out delivering babies and bringing joy to new mommies and daddies." Well, the third night the baby stork is missing, and next morning when he comes back, his parents ask, "Where have you been? We've been worried sick about you all night." The baby stork replies, ]{.class-0}["I've just been flying around a convent, scaring the daylights out of the nuns."]{.class-1}[ That's funny, folks; you can laugh. The Pope can't stop you yet.)]{.class-0}

[Now, these stork-winged women haul that basket with that woman in it off to ]{.class-0}["the land of Shinar"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 11). Where's that? Why, that's where the Tower of Babel was built (Gen. 11:1--9), and where the first kingdom in the Bible was established by a type of the Antichrist (Gen. 10:9--10). So this woman known as ]{.class-0}["wickedness"]{.class-2}[ is associated with a city that's the ]{.class-0}[Bride of Satan]{.class-1}[ (Rev. 17), just as Christ has a city that's His Bride (Rev. 21:9--10).]{.class-0}

[Clarence Larkin taught that there were two Babylons in the book of Revelation: ]{.class-0}[religious]{.class-1}[ Babylon (Rev. 17) and ]{.class-0}[commercial]{.class-1}[ Babylon (Rev. 18). Religious Babylon was Babylon in its mystery form (Rev. 17:5) as represented by the Roman Catholic Church (Rev. 17:9, 18), but he also taught that literal Babylon would be ]{.class-0}[rebuilt]{.class-1}[ someday. That began in the 1980s with Saddam Hussein in Iraq. Larkin lays the whole thing out for you in his book on ]{.class-0}[Dispensational Truth]{.class-1}[ (pp. 140--144).]{.class-0}

[When those stork-winged women reach Babylon, they set that woman down on a pedestal like a statue (]{.class-0}["set there upon her base"]{.class-2}[). So this woman is associated with the ]{.class-0}[image]{.class-1}[ of the Antichrist (Rev. 13:14--15).]{.class-0}

In the Old Testament, Baal worship (the male god) was always accompanied by Ashtoreth worship (the female goddess---see Judg. 2:13, 10:6; 1 Sam. 7:3--4, 12:10), and just as sure as two and two make four, this resulted in fornication as part of the worship service in imitation of the gods (Num. 25:1--3). In Revelation 2:20, you have a false prophetess pushing fornication and idolatry in the Church.

"[Notwithstanding I have a few things against thee, because thou sufferest that woman Jezebel, which calleth herself a prophetess, to teach and to seduce my servants to commit fornication, and to eat things sacrificed unto idols.]{.class-2}

[What is going to happen in the near future is one, worldwide, ecumenical church, ]{.class-0}[controlled from Rome]{.class-1}[ (Rev. 17:9), practicing not only idolatry ("venerating images"), but also having cannibalistic ]{.class-0}[sex orgies]{.class-1}[ right in the church service (Isa. 6:13; Psa. 16:4; Deut. 32:31--33; Acts 15:29). You can see the whole thing illustrated in ]{.class-0}[Ruckman's Apocalypse]{.class-1}[ (pg. 233). That's what's behind what you're reading here in Zechariah 5.]{.class-0}

[]{#section-0_1_edced6a27c55fe13_b692.xhtml}

CHAPTER SIX

6:1 And I turned, and lifted up mine eyes, and looked, and, behold, there came four chariots out from between two mountains; and the mountains were mountains of brass.

2 In the first chariot were red horses; and in the second chariot black horses;

3 And in the third chariot white horses; and in the fourth chariot grisled and bay horses.

4 Then I answered and said unto the angel that talked with me, What are these, my lord?

5 And the angel answered and said unto me, These are the four spirits of the heavens, which go forth from standing before the Lord of all the earth.

6 The black horses which are therein go forth into the north country; and the white go forth after them; and the grisled go forth toward the south country.

7 And the bay went forth, and sought to go that they might walk to and fro through the earth: and he said, Get you hence, walk to and fro through the earth. So they walked to and fro through the earth.

8 Then cried he upon me, and spake unto me, saying, Behold, these that go toward the north country have quieted my spirit in the north country.

As I told you in the introduction to Zechariah, there are certain places in Zechariah (especially in the first eight chapters) for which I have no explanation whatsoever. This passage here is just as enigmatic to me as Song of Solomon 8 (see the remarks in that Commentary). "The Hebrew" has nothing to offer at all; the expositors and commentators are a perfect blank. Of course, I'll give you what I can, but I'm afraid it won't be much.

[Take, for instance, those ]{.class-0}["mountains of brass"]{.class-2}[ in verse 1, I couldn't tell you what they are at all. Matthew Henry tells you they are "the immovable counsels and decrees of God; and therefore reckon it as much our folly to quarrel with them, as it is our duty to submit to them." ]{.class-0}[Scripture?]{.class-1}[ I said, "Scripture"?! ]{.class-0}[Matthew Henry had absolutely no Scriptural backing for what he said.]{.class-1}[ He didn't get that interpretation from one verse in the Bible---]{.class-0}[any]{.class-1}[ Bible. He got it from a ]{.class-0}[note]{.class-1}[ in the ]{.class-0}[Geneva]{.class-1}[ Bible.]{.class-0}

"[By the mountains he means the external counsel and providence of God, by which he has from before all eternity declared what will come to pass, and that which neither Satan nor all the world can alter."]{.class-0}

[That's the kind of Five-point Calvinistic BALONEY to which some of the Reformed, Presbyterian, and "TR" brethren want to return you. It's that kind of theological nonsense that was the reason for the Holy Spirit leading King James I to dump the ]{.class-0}[Geneva]{.class-1}[ Bible. We appreciate the place of the ]{.class-0}[Geneva]{.class-1}[ in the preservation and purification of the Scriptures in English, but we will not return to something which the Holy Spirit has ]{.class-0}[laid aside;]{.class-1}[ especially when He has given us His perfect words in English in the ]{.class-0}[King James Authorized Version.]{.class-1}

[The ]{.class-0}["mountains of brass"]{.class-2}[ have nothing whatsoever to do with any "eternal decree of God" made before Genesis 1:1. ]{.class-0}["Brass,"]{.class-2}[ in the Bible, is a picture of the ]{.class-0}[judgment of God on sin]{.class-1}[ (see the comments on the Brazen Altar and the Brazen Laver in our sermon booklet on ]{.class-0}[The Tabernacle]{.class-1}[). The context here has to do with God's judgment on the earth during the Tribulation, but why it is pictured as ]{.class-0}["two mountains,"]{.class-2}[ ]{.class-0}[Ich weiss nicht.]{.class-1}

[Now the ]{.class-0}["four chariots"]{.class-2}[ in the passage nearly match the "Four Horsemen of the Apocalypse" in Revelation 6:1--8, but not quite (see the comments on 1:8). The commentators say that the chariots are not "real" chariots---]{.class-0}[even though Elisha said they were]{.class-1}[ (2 Kings 2:11, 6:17)---but "the spirits run around with chariot speed." They then proceed to tell you that the black horses represent "awful desolations," the white horses represent "cruelty and guilt"\..., but why go on? That is nothing but ]{.class-0}[ignorant guesswork]{.class-1}[ by men who ]{.class-0}[do not believe the Bible.]{.class-1}

[The horses are defined in verse 5 as ]{.class-0}["the four spirits of the heavens."]{.class-2}[ The Bible is constantly likening spirits to ]{.class-0}[animals]{.class-1}[ (see the comments on 5:9). Scofield identifies the horses with the ]{.class-0}["four angels standing on the four corners of the earth"]{.class-2}[ in Revelation 7:1, but if the horses here in Zechariah 6 have anything to do with the horses of Revelation 6, that couldn't be the case at all. The horsemen of Revelation 6 are the ]{.class-0}[Antichrist]{.class-1}[ (the white horse rider), ]{.class-0}[War]{.class-1}[ (the red horse rider), ]{.class-0}[Famine]{.class-1}[ (the black horse rider), and ]{.class-0}[Death]{.class-1}[ (the pale horse rider). What do those have to do with the ]{.class-0}["four angels"]{.class-2}[ in Revelation 7:1--3 or the ]{.class-0}["four angels"]{.class-2}[ in Revelation 9:14 or any other "four angels" in the book of Revelation?]{.class-0}

[Verse 6: ]{.class-0}["The black horses\...go forth into the north country; and the white go after them."]{.class-2}[ So already you have a problem with making these chariots match the horsemen in Revelation 6. In Revelation, the Antichrist goes out first, and Famine is the third rider. In this passage, the Antichrist would follow Famine.]{.class-0}

"[The north country"]{.class-2}[ would be all the land north of Israel. It would include Lebanon, Turkey (Asia Minor), Europe, and Russia. This would match the white horse rider, for in Daniel 11, the Antichrist is called ]{.class-0}["the king of the north"]{.class-2}[ (Dan. 11:28--45).]{.class-0}

"[The grisled ]{.class-2}[\[that's a gray color, matching the ]{.class-0}["pale horse"]{.class-2}[ of Rev. 6:8\]]{.class-0}[ go forth toward the south country ]{.class-2}[\[that would be Arabia and Africa\]]{.class-0}[. And the bay went forth" ]{.class-2}[(vss. 6--7). Now in verse 3, the ]{.class-0}["grisled"]{.class-2}[ horses were ]{.class-0}[both]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-0}["grisled and bay"]{.class-2}[; they weren't two separate color of horses like you find in verses 6--7. In verses 6--7, the color that's missing from verses 2--3 is the ]{.class-0}["red horses"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 2). Well, ]{.class-0}["bay"]{.class-2}[ is a reddish-brown color, so ]{.class-0}["the bay"]{.class-2}[ in verse 7 must be a reference to the red horses of verse 2.]{.class-0}

"[And the bay went forth, and sought to go that they might walk to and fro through the earth"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 7; see the comments on 1:10--11).]{.class-0}

[Verse 8: ]{.class-0}["Behold, these that go toward the north country ]{.class-2}[\[that would be the black and white horses\]]{.class-0}[ have quieted my spirit in the north country."]{.class-2}[ What ]{.class-0}[that]{.class-1}[ means I haven't got any idea in the world. If these chariots have something to do with God's judgment on the earth in the Tribulation (see the comments on vs. 1), then it means that whatever these chariots were sent forth to do satiated the Lord's anger at those people up north.]{.class-0}

"[Thus shall mine anger be accomplished, and I will cause my fury to rest upon them, and I will be comforted"]{.class-2}[ (Ezek. 5:13).]{.class-0}

"[So will I make my fury toward thee to rest, and my jealousy shall depart from thee, and I will be quiet, and will be no more angry"]{.class-2}[ (Ezek. 16:42).]{.class-0}

"[That thou mayest remember, and be confounded, and never open thy mouth any more because of thy shame, when I am pacified toward thee for all that thou hast done, saith the Lord GOD"]{.class-2}[ (Ezek. 16:63).]{.class-0}

[So that's the general idea behind ]{.class-0}["quieted my spirit,"]{.class-2}[ but how or when they do that, I haven't gotten figured out yet.]{.class-0}

[From a practical standpoint, Ecclesiastes 10:4 says, ]{.class-0}["If the spirit of the ruler rise up against thee, leave not thy place; for yielding pacifieth great offenses."]{.class-2}[ In other words, if you know the Lord's got something against you, the best thing is to yield and repent instead of fighting and resisting; it will save you from getting the fire beat out of you.]{.class-0}

9 And the word of the LORD came unto me, saying,

10 Take of them of the captivity, even of Heldai, of Tobijah, and of Jedaiah, which are come from Babylon, and come thou the same day, and go into the house of Josiah the son of Zephaniah;

11 Then take silver and gold, and make crowns, and set them upon the head of Joshua the son of Josedech, the high priest;

12 And speak unto him, saying, Thus speaketh the LORD of hosts, saying, Behold the man whose name is The BRANCH; and he shall grow up out of his place, and he shall build the temple of the LORD:

13 Even he shall build the temple of the LORD; and he shall bear the glory, and shall sit and rule upon his throne; and he shall be a priest upon his throne: and the counsel of peace shall be between them both.

14 And the crowns shall be to Helem, and to Tobijah, and to Jedaiah, and to Hen the son of Zephaniah, for a memorial in the temple of the LORD.

15 And they that are far off shall come and build in the temple of the LORD, and ye shall know that the LORD of hosts hath sent me unto you. And this shall come to pass, if ye will diligently obey the voice of the LORD your God.

"[Heldai,"]{.class-2}[ (called ]{.class-0}["Helem"]{.class-2}[ in vs. 14),]{.class-0}[ "Tobijah,"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["Jedaiah"]{.class-2}[ in verse 10 are all priests who returned from captivity. The only one you can locate in Scripture is Jedaiah (see Ezra 2:36). ]{.class-0}["Zephaniah,"]{.class-2}[ in this case, is probably a priest who was carried into captivity; he is called ]{.class-0}["the second priest"]{.class-2}[ in 2 Kings 25:18 and Jeremiah 52:24, meaning he was a secondary high priest serving with the chief high priest. It is a very common thing in the Bible to find two high priests serving at one time (e.g., ]{.class-0}["Zadok and Abiathar"]{.class-2}[---2 Sam. 15:35, 17:15, 20:25), which explains why Jesus appeared before both Caiaphas and his father-in-law Annas (Luke 3:2; John 18:13, 24; Acts 4:6). In the case before us in Zechariah 6, ]{.class-0}["Joshua the son of Josedech"]{.class-2}[ would be the chief high priest and ]{.class-0}["Josiah the son of Zephaniah"]{.class-2}[ (called ]{.class-0}["Hen"]{.class-2}[ in vs. 14) would be ]{.class-0}["the second priest"]{.class-2}[ serving with Joshua.]{.class-0}

[Now what you have in this passage is Joshua the high priest as a ]{.class-0}[type]{.class-1}[ of Jesus Christ the ]{.class-0}["High Priest"]{.class-2}[ (Heb. 3:1). The name ]{.class-0}["Joshua"]{.class-2}[ is the Old Testament word for ]{.class-0}["Jesus"]{.class-2}[ (cf. Acts 7:45; Heb. 4:8). Notice Joshua's connection to ]{.class-0}["The BRANCH"]{.class-2}[ in verse 12 (see the comments on 3:8).]{.class-0}

[Two crowns are made for Joshua: ]{.class-0}["silver"]{.class-2}[ for ]{.class-0}[redemption]{.class-1}[ (symbolizing a ]{.class-0}[priest]{.class-1}[) and ]{.class-0}["gold"]{.class-2}[ for ]{.class-0}[Deity]{.class-1}[ (symbolizing a ]{.class-0}[King]{.class-1}[) \[vs. 11\]. Look at verse 13: ]{.class-0}["he shall be a priest upon his throne."]{.class-2}[ Then it has nothing to do with Joshua in 519 B.C. No high priest in the Old Testament sat on a throne. ]{.class-0}["The BRANCH"]{.class-2}[ is a Priest (vs. 13) and ]{.class-0}["a King"]{.class-2}[ (Jer. 23:5), like Melchisedec (Heb. 7:1--3).]{.class-0}

[Christ is ]{.class-0}["a Prophet"]{.class-2}[ (Deut. 18:18 cf. Acts 3:22--26), a Priest (Heb. 3:1), and a King (1 Tim. 6:15). He was ]{.class-0}["a Prophet"]{.class-2}[ when He showed up on earth at His First Advent (John 1:25, 6:14); He ]{.class-0}[will be]{.class-1}[ a King when He returns at His Second Advent (Rev. 19:16). Right now in the Church Age, He is not ruling as a King; He is ]{.class-0}["the Apostle and High Priest of our profession"]{.class-2}[ (Heb. 3:1). he is the Chief Priest, and if you are saved, you are a priest with Him (1 Pet. 2:5, 9).]{.class-0}

[One of the great doctrines of the Reformation was "The Priesthood of the Believer." Americans care nothing for the truth anymore, so you don't hear much about it. The Priesthood of the Believer teaches that there is only ]{.class-0}[one]{.class-1}[ High Priest, and He's not some drunken wino over in Rome; He's not one of Hitler's buddies (like Pius XII). The doctrine teaches that]{.class-0}[ every believer in Christ is a priest]{.class-1}[, and not just a bunch of child-molesting bachelors. The only "sacrifices" those priests offer are ]{.class-0}["spiritual sacrifices"]{.class-2}[ (1 Pet. 2:5), NOT a starchy cookie and a jug of hootch.]{.class-0}

"[For the kingdom of God is NOT meat ]{.class-2}[\[the wafer\]]{.class-0}[ and drink]{.class-2}[ \[the wine\]]{.class-0}[; but righteousness, and peace, and joy in the Holy Ghost"]{.class-2}[ (Rom. 14:17).]{.class-0}

[Verse 12: ]{.class-0}["Behold the man ]{.class-2}[\[cf. John 19:5\]]{.class-0}[ whose name is The BRANCH; and he shall grow up out of his place\...."]{.class-2}[ Isaiah 53:2 says of Christ: ]{.class-0}["For he shall grow up before him as a tender plant, and as a root out of a dry ground." "\...and he shall build the temple of the LORD ]{.class-2}[\[the Millennial temple of Ezek. 40--46, not the Temple of Ezra 6\]]{.class-0}[."]{.class-2}

[Verse 13: ]{.class-0}["he shall bear the glory."]{.class-2}[ Isaiah 22 says, ]{.class-0}["And the key of the house of David will I lay upon his shoulder]{.class-2}[ \[Christ---Isa. 9:6\]]{.class-0}[; so he shall open, and none shall shut; and he shall shut, and none shall open]{.class-2}[ \[Christ---Rev. 3:7\]]{.class-0}[. And I will fasten him as a nail in a sure place; and he shall be for a glorious throne to his father's house. And they shall hang upon him all the glory of his father's house"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 22:22--24).]{.class-0}

[The ]{.class-0}["throne"]{.class-2}[ of Zechariah 6:13 and Isaiah 22:23 is called ]{.class-0}["the throne of his glory"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 19:28, 25:31). It is the throne of the Millennial Davidic kingdom (Jer. 14:21; 1 Sam. 2:8 cf. Luke 1:32; Isa. 9:7), called ]{.class-0}["the throne of the LORD"]{.class-2}[ (1 Chron. 29:23; Jer. 3:17). It is a literal, physical, visible throne of a literal, physical, visible kingdom with Jerusalem as its capital.]{.class-0}

"[The counsel of peace shall be between them both]{.class-2}[ \[the priest and the throne\]]{.class-0}[."]{.class-2}[ Jesus Christ is called ]{.class-0}["Counsellor"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["The Prince of Peace"]{.class-2}[ in His kingdom (Isa. 9:6).]{.class-0}

[Verse 14: ]{.class-0}["And the crowns shall be\...for a memorial in the temple of the LORD."]{.class-2}[ The silver and gold crowns made for Joshua were to remind Joshua and Josiah, and all the priests who followed them, of the coming Priest-King.]{.class-0}

"[And they that are far off shall come and build in the temple of the LORD"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 15). That's a reference to the second gathering of the Gentiles in the Millennium.]{.class-0}

[Isaiah 60 says, ]{.class-0}["And the Gentiles shall come to thy light, and kings to the brightness of thy rising ]{.class-2}[\[Second Advent---Isa. 40:1--2\]]{.class-0}[\....they shall bring gold and incense; and they shall shew forth the praises of the LORD\....Surely the isles shall wait for me, and the ships of Tarshish first, to bring thy sons from far, their silver and their gold with them, unto the name of the LORD thy God, and to the Holy One of Israel, because he hath glorified thee. And the sons of strangers shall build up thy walls, and their kings shall minister unto thee"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 60:3--10).]{.class-0}

[That's called "Zionism";]{.class-1}[ it's considered to be racism by the United Nations. It's the teaching that Jerusalem becomes the capital of the whole world, and all 191 Gentile nations in the UN become subservient to the Jews. Every Gentile in the world will eventually]{.class-0}[ worship the God of Israel.]{.class-1}

"[For from the rising of the sun even unto the going down of the same my name shall be great among the Gentiles; and in every place incense shall be offered unto my name, and a pure offering: for my name shall be great among the heathen, saith the LORD of hosts"]{.class-2}[ (Mal. 1:11).]{.class-0}

[The Gentiles are worshipping Jehovah God in the flesh (Psa. 45:6, 110:1). That's the same Jehovah who sent His messenger to ]{.class-0}["prepare the way"]{.class-2}[ before Him (Mal. 3:1 cf. Mark 1:1--2) at His First Coming. They bring the materials to Jerusalem to build the Millennial Temple.]{.class-0}

"[In that day ]{.class-2}[\[Second Advent\]]{.class-0}[ will I raise up the tabernacle of David that is fallen, and close up the breaches thereof; and I will raise up his ruins, and I will build it as in the days of old: That they may possess the remnant of Edom, and of ALL THE HEATHEN, which are called by my name ]{.class-2}[(see the comments on Zeph. 3:9)]{.class-0}[, saith the LORD that doeth this"]{.class-2}[ (Amos 9:11--12).]{.class-0}

[Two callings out of the Gentiles:]{.class-1}[ one now in the Church Age and one in the future during the Millennium (Acts 15:14--17).]{.class-0}

[Back to Zechariah 6:15---]{.class-0}["And this shall come to pass, if ye will diligently obey the voice of the LORD your God."]{.class-2}[ So the rebuilding of the Temple in the book of Ezra is a type of the building of the Millennial Temple (see the comments on Hag. 1:8, 2:9). That's what those Jews were looking for when Christ showed up the first time (]{.class-0}["and ye shall know that the LORD of hosts ]{.class-2}[\[Jehovah\]]{.class-0}[ hath sent me]{.class-2}[ \[]{.class-0}["The BRANCH"]{.class-2}[ of verse 12---Jesus Christ\]]{.class-0}[ unto you ]{.class-2}[\[the Jews\]]{.class-0}[)."]{.class-2}[ The problem was they failed to distinguish the ]{.class-0}[suffering Servant ]{.class-1}[from the ]{.class-0}[conquering King]{.class-1}[. The first time He came, it was to raise up ]{.class-0}["the temple of his body"]{.class-2}[ (John 2:19--21); the second time He comes, it will be to ]{.class-0}["raise up the tabernacle of David that is fallen"]{.class-2}[ (Amos 9:11).]{.class-0}

[]{#section-0_1_5ca9fa5398641aaa_b693.xhtml}

CHAPTER SEVEN

7:1 And it came to pass in the fourth year of king Darius, that the word of the LORD came unto Zechariah in the fourth day of the ninth month, even in Chisleu;

2 When they had sent unto the house of God Sherezer and Regemmelech, and their men, to pray before the LORD,

3 And to speak unto the priests which were in the house of the LORD of hosts, and to the prophets, saying, Should I weep in the fifth month, separating myself, as I have done these so many years?

4 Then came the word of the LORD of hosts unto me, saying,

5 Speak unto all the people of the land, and to the priests, saying, When ye fasted and mourned in the fifth and seventh month, even those seventy years, did ye at all fast unto me, even to me?

6 And when ye did eat, and when ye did drink, did not ye eat for yourselves, and drink for yourselves?

7 Should ye not hear the words which the LORD hath cried by the former prophets, when Jerusalem was inhabited and in prosperity, and the cities thereof round about her, when men inhabited the south and the plain?

"[Chisleu,"]{.class-2}[ as verse 1 states, is ]{.class-0}["the ninth month"]{.class-2}[ in the Jewish calendar, and it falls in November-December of our calendar. The Jews spell it ]{.class-0}["Chislev"]{.class-1}[ or ]{.class-0}["Kislev."]{.class-1}[ The prophecy of chapter 7 falls roughly in the middle of Zechariah's ministry as given in Scripture (Zech. 1:1 cf. Ezra 6:14--15).]{.class-0}

[What's going on in verses 2--3 is that the Jews send a delegation of men to the Temple (see the comments on ]{.class-0}["the house of God"]{.class-2}[ under Hag. 1:2) to ask the priests (Joshua and Josiah---see the comments on 6:10) and the prophets (Haggai and Zechariah) this question: ]{.class-0}["Should I weep in the fifth month, separating myself, as I have done these so many years?"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 3).]{.class-0}

[What the Jews of the captivity had done was set aside certain months as months of fasting and repentance. Verse 6 lists ]{.class-0}["the fifth and seventh"]{.class-2}[ months, but if you look over in 8:19, they also included the fourth and tenth months. The seventh month was the month where the Day of Atonement (]{.class-0}[Yom Kippur]{.class-1}[) was observed (see the comments in the introduction to Haggai and under Hag. 2:1). The Day of Atonement was a fast instituted by the Lord for the people to repent and get right each year. During the seventy-year captivity, the Jews had added three more days like it.]{.class-0}

[Now what the Lord has Zechariah tell those fellows is this: ]{.class-0}["When ye fasted and mourned\...did ye at all fast unto me\...?"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 5). The Lord is saying, "When you were fasting and crying, were you doing it for my sake?" The answer you are to infer from that is: ]{.class-0}["No, they weren't."]{.class-1}[ How do you know that's so? Because the other eight months of the year they were living for themselves. Look at verse 6.]{.class-0}

"[And when ye did eat, and when ye did drink, did not ye eat for yourselves, and drink for yourselves?"]{.class-2}

They were doing like a lot of Catholics and Baptists and Methodists and Presbyterians, etc., do: they go to church for one to two hours on Sunday morning, and the rest of the week they live like the Devil.

Bob Jones Sr. used to say, "There is no difference between the secular and the sacred. To the Christian, all ground is 'holy ground' and every bush a 'burning bush.'" You shouldn't live any different at home, on the ball field, at work, in the deer stand, on the fishing boat, etc., than you do in church.

[That Bible says, ]{.class-0}["And whatsoever ye do in word or deed, do all in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God and the Father by him"]{.class-2}[ (Col. 3:17).]{.class-0}

[It says, ]{.class-0}["Whether therefore ye eat, or drink, or whatsoever ye do, do all to the glory of God"]{.class-2}[ (1 Cor. 10:31).]{.class-0}

[The problem wasn't the fasting and the mourning; ]{.class-0}[it was the motivation behind it.]{.class-1}[ One black preacher said, "The Lawd don't care what you do; what He wants to know is ]{.class-0}[why you does it."]{.class-1}[ The Jews had fallen into the trap they fell into over and over again: performing religious rituals with no change of heart (see Isa. 1:11--17; Matt. 23:23).]{.class-0}

[What did God want from them? Verse 7: ]{.class-0}["Should ye not hear the words which the LORD hath cried by the former prophets\...?"]{.class-2}[ That is what is more important than fasting and crying. While ]{.class-0}["Martha was cumbered about much serving,"]{.class-2}[ her sister Mary ]{.class-0}["sat at Jesus' feet, and heard his word"]{.class-2}[ (Luke 10:39--40); Jesus said, ]{.class-0}["Mary hath chosen that good part"]{.class-2}[ (Luke 10:42).]{.class-0}

[Samuel told Saul, ]{.class-0}["Hath the LORD as great delight in burnt offerings and sacrifices, as in obeying the voice of the LORD? Behold, to obey is better than sacrifice, and to hearken than the fat of rams"]{.class-2}[ (1 Sam. 15:22).]{.class-0}

[That's the problem the Jews of Christ's day had: they had stopped listening to God's ]{.class-0}[words. ]{.class-1}[Jesus told them: ]{.class-0}["Why do ye not understand my speech? even because ye cannot hear my word\....He that is of God heareth God's words: ye therefore hear them not, because ye are not of God"]{.class-2}[ (John 8:43, 47).]{.class-0}

8 And the word of the LORD came unto Zechariah, saying,

9 Thus speaketh the LORD of hosts, saying, Execute true judgment, and shew mercy and compassions every man to his brother:

10 And oppress not the widow, nor the fatherless, the stranger, nor the poor; and let none of you imagine evil against his brother in your heart.

11 But they refused to hearken, and pulled away the shoulder, and stopped their ears, that they should not hear.

12 Yea, they made their hearts as an adamant stone, lest they should hear the law, and the words which the LORD of hosts hath sent in his spirit by the former prophets: therefore came a great wrath from the LORD of hosts.
