13 Therefore it is come to pass, that as he cried, and they would not hear; so they cried, and I would not hear, saith the LORD of hosts:

14 But I scattered them with a whirlwind among all the nations whom they knew not. Thus the land was desolate after them, that no man passed through nor returned: for they laid the pleasant land desolate.

[Now the point the Lord is making in verses 9--10 is that the Jews should have done more than just fast and cry out to God. They should have]{.class-0}[ listened to what God said]{.class-1}[ in Isaiah, Jeremiah, Hosea, Amos, Micah, and the rest of the prophets who spoke before Nebuchadnezzar came in and tore the whole place up, and ]{.class-0}[followed up on it, ]{.class-1}[executing]{.class-0}[ ]{.class-1}["true judgments,"]{.class-2}[ showing mercy and compassion, taking care of those in need, and not imagining ]{.class-0}["evil"]{.class-2}[ against their fellow Jews.]{.class-0}

[Isaiah told them, ]{.class-0}["Wash you, make you clean; put away the evil of your doings from before mine eyes; cease to do evil; Learn to do well; seek judgment, relieve the oppressed, judge the fatherless, plead for the widow"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 1:16--17).]{.class-0}

[What God wanted from that Jew was more than sacrifices and fastings and sacraments; He wanted them ]{.class-0}["to do justly, and to love mercy, and to walk humbly with thy God"]{.class-2}[ (Mic. 6:8). Jesus told the Pharisees that ]{.class-0}["the weightier matters of the law"]{.class-2}[ are ]{.class-0}["judgment, mercy, and faith"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 23:23).]{.class-0}

[The goal of repentance is not just sorrow and tears. John the Baptist told his congregation, ]{.class-0}["Bring forth therefore fruits worthy of repentance"]{.class-2}[ (Luke 3:8). That's not just an Old Testament, pre-Crucifixion thing either; when the Apostle Paul defined the "Pauline ministry," he said he told the Gentiles to whom he preached ]{.class-0}["that they should repent and turn to God, and do works meet for repentance"]{.class-2}[ (Acts 26:20; see also the comments on Acts 20:21 in that Commentary).]{.class-0}

[Verses 11--12 should be compared with Nehemiah 9:29 and Jeremiah 17:23. Notice that the Jews ]{.class-0}["stopped their ears"]{.class-2}[ to the ]{.class-0}[words]{.class-1}[ of the Lord through the prophets; that's exactly the same reaction Stephen got in Acts 7:57. Moreover, the Jews of the First Century got the same thing from God for doing that as the Jews in 750--586 B.C. Those Jews back there in Isaiah and Jeremiah ]{.class-0}["stopped their ears,"]{.class-2}[ and God sent Nebuchadnezzar in there to get them. The Jews in the book of Acts ]{.class-0}["stopped their ears,"]{.class-2}[ and the Lord sent Titus in there in A.D. 70 to do exactly what Nebuchadnezzar did back in 586 B.C. ]{.class-0}["Therefore came a great wrath from the LORD of hosts"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 12).]{.class-0}

[You see those ]{.class-0}["hearts as an adamant stone"]{.class-2}[ there in verse 12? That's the present state of the nation of Israel. As a people, they have hardened their hearts to the Gospel and Jesus Christ, but at the Second Advent, the Lord's going to change that.]{.class-0}

"[And I will give them one heart, and I will put a new spirit within you; and I will take the stony heart out of their flesh, and will give them an heart of flesh: That they may walk in my statutes, and keep mine ordinances, and do them: and they shall be my people, and I will be their God"]{.class-2}[ (Ezek. 11:19--20).]{.class-0}

[Verse 13: ]{.class-0}["Therefore it is come to pass, that as he cried\...."]{.class-2}[ Who's the ]{.class-0}["he"]{.class-2}[ there? It's ]{.class-0}["the LORD of hosts"]{.class-2}[ (end of vs. 12). The crying there is speaking out loudly like you find it in Proverbs 1:20--21.]{.class-0}

"[Wisdom crieth without; she uttereth her voice in the streets: She crieth in the chief place of concourse, in the openings of the gates: in the city she uttereth her words."]{.class-2}

[That's what you do when you street preach. People here in Pensacola complain about our young men yelling at them out on the street corners on a Saturday. Well, they yell to be heard above the traffic and the closed windows and the air conditioners running in the cars. The city council passed some kind of noise ordinance making it illegal to use a ]{.class-0}[loudspeaker;]{.class-1}[ if our street preachers could use a loudspeaker,]{.class-0}[ they wouldn't have to yell.]{.class-1}

[Here in Zechariah 7:13, the Lord cried out to those Jews through the prophets, and the]{.class-0}[ people ]{.class-1}[wouldn't listen, so when Nebuchadnezzar came in there,]{.class-0}[ the Lord]{.class-1}[ wouldn't listen to those Jews praying to Him for help. That's what the Lord warns you about in Proverbs 1.]{.class-0}

"[Because I have called, and ye refused; I have stretched out my hand, and no man regarded\...Then shall they call upon me, but I will not answer; they shall seek me early, but they shall not find me" ]{.class-2}[(Prov. 1:24, 28).]{.class-0}

[When God calls on you and requires something of you, He expects you ]{.class-0}[to listen]{.class-1}[ and take heed to what He says. If you don't, He may not slap you down right away. He may do something much worse than that. He may let you go along until you get in a mess and need His help, and ]{.class-0}[there won't be any help coming.]{.class-1}

[You won't hear ]{.class-0}[that]{.class-1}[ preached on Christian FM stations. You won't get that from Dobson's "family hour." You won't get it today from 90% of the pulpits in this country. ]{.class-0}[That's a part of the Book modern Christian leaders pretend is not even there. ]{.class-1}[They'll tell you God is just waiting with bated breath to forgive you. I teach the Lord won't turn you away if you come to Christ (John 6:37), but He will let you reap what you sow in the flesh (Gal. 6:7--8).]{.class-0}

[Back to Zechariah 7:14---]{.class-0}["But I scattered them with a whirlwind among all the nations whom they knew not."]{.class-2}[ That's the state of ]{.class-0}[the Jews ]{.class-1}[in the Church Age (see James 1:1). And that's the handwriting on the wall for the UN, the EU, and the ]{.class-0}[USA.]{.class-1}[ In Jeremiah 30:11, the Lord told you He would ]{.class-0}["make a full end of all nations"]{.class-2}[ where He scattered those Jews. Well, did He scatter some of those Jews here in the United States? Then "that's all she wrote." ]{.class-0}[The Lord's going to wipe out America, and there's nothing you can do to stop it.]{.class-1}

[While it is true that the Lord has blessed America ]{.class-0}[in the past]{.class-1}[ for blessing that Jew (Gen. 12:3; Num. 24:9), you are living in the day when the Lord is putting a hook in the jaw of the United States to bring it to the point of fulfilling what He said in Jeremiah 30:11 (cf. Isa. 30:28; Ezek. 38:4). Like I've told you over and over and over again: ]{.class-0}["No country can live like Americans have been living and get away with it."]{.class-1}

[]{#section-0_1_6691ada899d310de_b694.xhtml}

CHAPTER EIGHT

8:1 Again the word of the LORD of hosts came to me, saying,

2 Thus saith the LORD of hosts; I was jealous for Zion with great jealousy, and I was jealous for her with great fury.

3 Thus saith the LORD; I am returned unto Zion, and will dwell in the midst of Jerusalem: and Jerusalem shall be called a city of truth; and the mountain of the LORD of hosts the holy mountain.

4 Thus saith the LORD of hosts; There shall yet old men and old women dwell in the streets of Jerusalem, and every man with his staff in his hand for very age.

5 And the streets of the city shall be full of boys and girls playing in the streets thereof.

6 Thus saith the LORD of hosts; If it be marvellous in the eyes of the remnant of this people in these days, should it also be marvellous in mine eyes? saith the LORD of hosts.

7 Thus saith the LORD of hosts; Behold, I will save my people from the east country, and from the west country;

8 And I will bring them, and they shall dwell in the midst of Jerusalem: and they shall be my people, and I will be their God, in truth and in righteousness.

[We have already commented on the jealousy of God (vs. 2) under 1:14 and Zephaniah 1:18 (which see). That ]{.class-0}["great fury"]{.class-2}[ in verse 2 is ]{.class-0}["the great day of his wrath"]{.class-2}[ at the Second Advent (Rev. 6:17).]{.class-0}

[Now, the tendency of the scholars is to relegate the fulfillment of the whole chapter to the ]{.class-0}[past.]{.class-1}[ Some of the commentators (e.g., Dummelow) will take verse 6 and run you over to Psalm 118:23 to prove this because Christ quoted that passage in Mark 12:11 and Matthew 21:42. The fact is that although there might be a ]{.class-0}[temporary, partial]{.class-1}[ fulfillment of verses 4--5 in Ezra and Nehemiah, none of the prophecies in the chapter have been fulfilled ]{.class-0}[literally or completely]{.class-1}[ in the Old Testament or the Gospels.]{.class-0}[ The whole chapter is on the restoration of Israel at the Second Advent and the Millennium.]{.class-1}

[Compare verse 2 with Isaiah 34:2, 63:1--6, and 66:15. Compare verse 3 with Psalm 2:6 and Isaiah 2:2--3. Compare verses 7--8 with Isaiah 11:11--12 and Jeremiah 23:8, 31:7--8. Compare verse 8 with Hosea 1:10--11 and Jeremiah 30:18--22, 31:31--33. Compare verses 21--23 with Isaiah 2:2--3, 60:1--2; Zechariah 14:16--21; and Malachi 1:11. It's Second Advent all the way through: Second Advent and Millennium. ]{.class-0}[None of those prophecies have been fulfilled from the time Zechariah wrote the chapter ]{.class-1}[(518 B.C.) ]{.class-0}[until today.]{.class-1}

[We have commented extensively on the material in verse 3 under Zephaniah 3:15 (which see). Jerusalem will be changed from ]{.class-0}["Sodom and Egypt"]{.class-2}[ in the Tribulation (Rev. 11:8) to ]{.class-0}["the holy city"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 52:1), ]{.class-0}["the beloved city"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 20:9), and ]{.class-0}["a city of truth"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 3).]{.class-0}

[Verses 4--5 are one of the passages on which the song "The Holy City" is based. The prophecy obviously goes beyond anything that takes place in Ezra and Nehemiah because ]{.class-0}["it is marvellous in the eyes of the remnant of this people"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 6). They are reacting to what Zechariah said in the same way the disciples reacted to Jesus' words about a camel going ]{.class-0}["through the eye of a needle"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 19:24--25; Mark 10:24--26).]{.class-0}

[Isaiah 65:20 says, ]{.class-0}["There shall be no more thence an infant of days, nor an old man that hath not filled his days: for the child shall die an hundred years old; but the sinner being an hundred years old shall be accursed."]{.class-2}

[Jeremiah 30:19--20 says, ]{.class-0}["And I will multiply them, and they shall not be few; I will also glorify them, and they shall not be small. Their children also shall be as aforetime, and their congregation shall be established before me."]{.class-2}

[Both of those are Millennial passages that tell you that during Christ's reign, those Jews are going to multiply just as the Lord promised Abraham in Genesis 15:5 (also see the comments on Hos. 1:10 in Vol. I of this Commentary). At that time, they will live to the kinds of ages which men experienced ]{.class-0}[before]{.class-1}[ the Flood.]{.class-0}

[Verse 7 is another prophecy of the regathering of Israel to the land God promised them (see the comments under Zeph. 3:18--20). Zechariah mentions ]{.class-0}["east"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["west,"]{.class-2}[ but the regathering is from all four cardinal directions of the globe (Psa. 107:2--3; Isa. 43:5--6, 49:12, 66:19--20; Jer. 31:8--9). ]{.class-0}["The east country]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["the west country"]{.class-2}[ are defined in Scriptures (Isa. 11:11--12, 27:12--13; Hos. 11:10--11) as ]{.class-0}["Assyria]{.class-2}[ (the ]{.class-0}[east,]{.class-1}[ including Babylon and Persia) and ]{.class-0}["Egypt"]{.class-2}[ (the ]{.class-0}[west]{.class-1}[), but the directions of east and west also include the circuit of the sun (Psa. 50:1, 18:3), meaning that those Jews will be gathered from anywhere the sun rises or sets.]{.class-0}

We have already commented on the last half of verse 8 in Volume I of this Commentary under Hosea 1:10--11 and 2:23 (which see). Other cross-references on the passage would be Leviticus 26:12; Jeremiah 30:22, 31:1, 33, 32:37--38; Ezekiel 11:19--20, 36:26--28, 37:26--27; and Zechariah 13:9.

9 Thus saith the LORD of hosts; Let your hands be strong, ye that hear in these days these words by the mouth of the prophets, which were in the day that the foundation of the house of the LORD of hosts was laid, that the temple might be built.

10 For before these days there was no hire for man, nor any hire for beast; neither was there any peace to him that went out or came in because of the affliction: for I set all men every one against his neighbour.

11 But now I will not be unto the residue of this people as in the former days, saith the LORD of hosts.

12 For the seed shall be prosperous; the vine shall give her fruit, and the ground shall give her increase, and the heavens shall give their dew; and I will cause the remnant of this people to possess all these things.

13 And it shall come to pass, that as ye were a curse among the heathen, O house of Judah, and house of Israel; so will I save you, and ye shall be a blessing: fear not, but let your hands be strong.

14 For thus saith the LORD of hosts; As I thought to punish you, when your fathers provoked me to wrath, saith the LORD of hosts, and I repented not:

15 So again have I thought in these days to do well unto Jerusalem and to the house of Judah: fear ye not.

[Now once more we have a ]{.class-0}[partial fulfillment ]{.class-1}[of the passage in the books of Ezra and Nehemiah, and that is obvious from verses 9--10, but you can't relegate it just to that. Look at verse 12.]{.class-0}

"[For the seed shall be prosperous\...."]{.class-2}[ It wasn't in Nehemiah 1:3; ]{.class-0}["the remnant"]{.class-2}[ there was ]{.class-0}["in great affliction and reproach."]{.class-2}

"[\...the vine shall give her fruit, and the ground shall give her increase, and the heavens shall give their dew\...."]{.class-2}[ These are signs of the Second Advent. After eighteen centuries of desolation (see the comments under Hag. 1:1), the land of Israel became productive again when the nation was reborn (Isa. 27:6, 66:8). The "Palestinians" never did anything with the land, but when those Jews returned, they irrigated it, and it started producing fruits, vegetables, and other crops (Isa. 58:11--12). The rainfall in Israel, between 1931 and 1960, increased 2.1 inches annually; from 1960 to 1980 it increased ]{.class-0}[21.1 inches]{.class-1}[ annually. There are trees growing in ]{.class-0}[forests]{.class-1}[ once more (Isa. 41:19--20).]{.class-0}

"[\...and I will cause the remnant of this people to possess all these things."]{.class-2}[ Hitler destroyed 42% of the Jewish population in the Roman Catholic Holocaust. As of 2005, there were a little over ]{.class-0}[five million Jews]{.class-1}[ living in Israel. They have begun buying land again (the kibbutzes), just as God promised Jeremiah (Jer. 32:44). They have also begun rebuilding the cities of ancient Israel (Ezek. 36:10, 22, 25, 38). These things are all a prelude of what God's going to do for those Jews at Christ's return.]{.class-0}

"[Behold, the days come, saith the LORD, that the plowman shall overtake the reaper, and the treader of grapes him that soweth seed; and the mountains shall drop sweet wine, and all the hills shall melt. And I will bring again the captivity of my people of Israel, and they shall build the waste cities, and inhabit them; and they shall plant vineyards, and drink the wine thereof; they shall also make gardens, and eat the fruit of them. And I will plant them upon their land, and they shall no more be pulled up out of their land which I have given them, saith the LORD thy God"]{.class-2}[ (Amos 9:13--15).]{.class-0}

[Verse 13: ]{.class-0}["And it shall come to pass, that as ye were a curse among the heathen ]{.class-2}[\[]{.class-0}[God]{.class-1}[ did that to them---Jer. 24:9, 25:18, 26:6, 29:18, 42:18, 44:12, 22\]]{.class-0}[, O house of Judah, and house of Israel; so will I save you."]{.class-2}[ Now that has nothing to do with "spiritual Jews" (Rom. 2:28--29) or ]{.class-0}["the Israel of God"]{.class-2}[ (Gal. 6:16). The ]{.class-0}["house of Judah"]{.class-2}[ and the ]{.class-0}["house of Israel"]{.class-2}[ are ]{.class-0}[political]{.class-1}[ entities composed of ]{.class-0}[physical Jews.]{.class-1}[ The reference here is to the future application of the new testament to the Jews in the Millennium, and you can read a full discussion of the matter in the ]{.class-0}[Bible Believer's Commentary on Hebrews]{.class-1}[ under Hebrews 8:8--13.]{.class-0}

"[And ye shall be a blessing."]{.class-2}[ That's the promise God gave to Abraham in Genesis 12:2--3. Both Peter and Paul apply that to the First Coming of Jesus Christ (see Acts 3:25--26; Gal. 3:8, 14), and that's true. But the context ]{.class-0}[here]{.class-1}[ is the Second Advent and the Millennium. When Christ returns and those Jews finally get right, they are a blessing to the whole earth because the nations finally have access to the Lord through Israel (see the comments on vss. 20--23).]{.class-0}

[Verses 14--15 should be compared with Jeremiah 31:27--28, which see. The commandment in Psalm 122:6 is: ]{.class-0}["Pray for the peace of Jerusalem: they shall prosper that love thee."]{.class-2}[ When you do that, you're actually praying for the return of the Lord Jesus Christ.]{.class-0}

16 These are the things that ye shall do; Speak ye every man the truth to his neighbour; execute the judgment of truth and peace in your gates:

17 And let none of you imagine evil in your hearts against his neighbour; and love no false oath: for all these are things that I hate, saith the LORD.

18 And the word of the LORD of hosts came unto me, saying,

19 Thus saith the LORD of hosts; The fast of the fourth month, and the fast of the fifth, and the fast of the seventh, and the fast of the tenth, shall be to the house of Judah joy and gladness, and cheerful feasts; therefore love the truth and peace.

[Now we have already covered the material in these verses in the comments on 7:4--10 (which see). Note that the Apostle Paul pulls out verse 16 and applies it to the Body of Christ in Ephesians 4:25. The Scripture is not only given for ]{.class-0}["doctrine,"]{.class-2}[ it's also ]{.class-0}["for reproof, for correction, for instruction in righteousness"]{.class-2}[ (2 Tim. 3:16).]{.class-0}

[Notice in both verses 16 and 19 that ]{.class-0}["truth"]{.class-2}[ comes ]{.class-0}[before]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-0}["peace."]{.class-2}[ It's ]{.class-0}[truth]{.class-1}[ before peace (vss. 16, 19), ]{.class-0}[righteousness]{.class-1}[ before peace (Heb. 7:2), and ]{.class-0}[purity]{.class-1}[ before peace (James 3:17). So no truth, no peace; no righteousness, no peace; no purity, no peace. I'll tell you something else: you won't get any ]{.class-0}["peace"]{.class-2}[ without ]{.class-0}["glory to God"]{.class-2}[ FIRST (Luke 2:14; see the comments on Hag. 2:7, 9).]{.class-0}

[Harry S. Truman said, "The UN is our last, best hope for peace." Well, lock the guy up; he's a mental case. ]{.class-0}[There have been over 177 wars since the UN was founded in 1945. ]{.class-1}[That's your "last, best hope for peace," is it? It is if you're psycho!]{.class-0}

[The only hope for peace this world has is the return of ]{.class-0}["the Prince of Peace"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 9:6). Until then, it's ]{.class-0}["wars and rumors of wars"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 24:6), with three world wars coming up (Rev. 6, 19, 20).]{.class-0}

[The Bible says that ]{.class-0}["in the last days"]{.class-2}[ men would be ]{.class-0}["ever learning, and never able to come to the knowledge of the truth"]{.class-2}[ (2 Tim. 4:1, 7), that they would ]{.class-0}["turn away their ears from the truth"]{.class-2}[ (2 Tim. 4:4), and that they would ]{.class-0}[not believe the truth]{.class-1}[ (2 Thess. 2:12). What's the truth? It's Jesus Christ (John 14:6); it's the Scriptures (John 17:17) as revealed by the Holy Spirit (John 16:13; 1 John 5:6). ]{.class-0}[No truth, no peace.]{.class-1}

20 Thus saith the LORD of hosts; It shall yet come to pass, that there shall come people, and the inhabitants of many cities:

21 And the inhabitants of one city shall go to another, saying, Let us go speedily to pray before the LORD, and to seek the LORD of hosts: I will go also.

22 Yea, many people and strong nations shall come to seek the LORD of hosts in Jerusalem, and to pray before the LORD.

23 Thus saith the LORD of hosts; In those days it shall come to pass, that ten men shall take hold out of all languages of the nations, even shall take hold of the skirt of him that is a Jew, saying, We will go with you: for we have heard that God is with you.

[Now look at that; that's a bunch of people going to Jerusalem to pray. Isaiah 56:6--7 says, ]{.class-0}["Also the sons of the stranger ]{.class-2}[\[those are Gentiles\]]{.class-0}[, that join themselves to the LORD]{.class-2}[ \[they're proselytes to Judaism; they're ]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ converts to Christianity\]]{.class-0}[, to serve him, and to love the name of the LORD, to be his servants, every one that keepeth the sabbath from polluting it, and taketh hold of my covenant ]{.class-2}[\[that's not you; see Col. 2:14--17\]]{.class-0}[; Even them will I bring to my holy mountain]{.class-2}[ \[Mt. Zion in Jerusalem---Psa. 2:6; Isa. 2:2--3\]]{.class-0}[, and make them joyful in my house of prayer\...for mine house shall be called an house of prayer for all people"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 56:6--7).]{.class-0}

[It wasn't in the Gospels; Christ called it ]{.class-0}["a den of thieves"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 21:13). When a woman asked Him where the proper place of worship was, He said, ]{.class-0}["the hour cometh, when ye shall neither in this mountain, nor yet at Jerusalem, worship the Father"]{.class-2}[ (John 4:21). If you are a Christian, you don't have to go to Jerusalem to find a house of God ]{.class-0}["to pray before the LORD,"]{.class-2}[ because ]{.class-0}["your body is the temple of the Holy Ghost"]{.class-2}[ (1 Cor. 6:19).]{.class-0}

[But in the Millennium things are different. God's physical presence is in the Temple in Jerusalem in the person of Jesus Christ ]{.class-1}[(Ezek. 48:35). That Gentile is required to come to ]{.class-0}[Jerusalem]{.class-1}[ to get God's word (Mic. 4:1--2), and if he doesn't, the Lord shuts off the rain (Zech. 14:16--19).]{.class-0}

[All of this has to do with two things: the reinstating of the Levitical priesthood and Temple sacrifices (Ezek. 40--46) and the ]{.class-0}[second gathering of the Gentiles]{.class-1}[ (Acts 15:14--17). We have already commented on both of these, so we refer you to our remarks under Zephaniah 3:9.]{.class-0}

Before leaving Zechariah chapter 8, however, I want you to see some things in verse 23. What is behind this passage is the epitome of what the United Nations calls racism---"Zionism."

"[Thus saith the LORD of hosts; In those days it shall come to pass, that TEN MEN shall take hold out of all languages of the nations."]{.class-2}[ Notice the number ]{.class-0}["ten"]{.class-2}[ in connection with the Gentile ]{.class-0}["nations."]{.class-2}[ If you pick up any Conservative or Fundamental expositor, he will tell you that ten is the "number of completion." ]{.class-0}[It is nothing of the sort.]{.class-1}

[The Gentile nations are given in Genesis ]{.class-0}[10.]{.class-1}[ Noah is the ]{.class-0}[tenth]{.class-1}[ from Adam, and he is the Father of the Gentiles. The first Gentile kingdom is found in Genesis ]{.class-0}[10:10;]{.class-1}[ it is Babel. The last Gentile kingdom on this earth is ]{.class-0}["MYSTERY BABYLON"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 17), and it is represented by the ]{.class-0}[ten]{.class-1}[ toes on Daniel's image (Dan. 2) and ]{.class-0}["the ten horns"]{.class-2}[ on the Beast (Rev. 17:12). When Abraham sends his servant to get a Gentile bride for his son Isaac, Eliezer (see Gen. 15:2) takes ]{.class-0}["ten camels"]{.class-2}[ with him (Gen. 24:]{.class-0}[10]{.class-1}[) and is asked by Rebekah's Gentile family to wait ]{.class-0}[ten]{.class-1}[ days before taking her (Gen. 24:55). When Peter uses ]{.class-0}["the keys of the kingdom of heaven"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 16:19) to open the door of the Gospel to the Gentiles, he does it in Acts ]{.class-0}[10.]{.class-1}[ The missionary call to the Gentiles is in Romans ]{.class-0}[10.]{.class-1}[ Ten is the ]{.class-0}[Gentile number.]{.class-1}

[That's why you count by tens. You count one to ten, and then you start over. God counts one to seven and then starts over: it's seven days, and then a new week begins; seven weeks and then Pentecost begins something new; the Jews begin their secular calendar with ]{.class-0}[Rosh Hashanah]{.class-1}[ in the seventh month; Jubilee is in the seventh year, and then the count starts over (Lev. 23, 25). Ten is not the number of completion in God's mathematics; seven is.]{.class-0}

"[Even shall take hold of the skirt of him that is a Jew, saying, We will go with you: for we have heard that God is with you." "God is with you"]{.class-2}[; it's a variation of the word ]{.class-0}["Immanuel"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 7:14), which means ]{.class-0}["God with us"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 1:23). God is right there with those Jews in the Millennium (see the comments under Zeph. 3:15). That being the case, look at Isaiah 45:14.]{.class-0}

"[Thus saith the LORD, The labour of Egypt, and merchandise of Ethiopia and of the Sabeans, men of stature, shall come over unto thee ]{.class-2}[\[the Jews\]]{.class-0}[, AND THEY SHALL BE THINE]{.class-2}[ \[what does He mean by that? Read on\]]{.class-0}[: they shall come after thee: IN CHAINS THEY SHALL COME OVER]{.class-2}[ \[they're slaves\]]{.class-0}[, and they shall fall down unto thee ]{.class-2}[\[the Gentiles are bowing down to those Jews\]]{.class-0}[, they shall make supplication unto thee ]{.class-2}[\[just like they do in Zech. 8:23\]]{.class-0}[, saying, Surely GOD IS IN THEE; and there is none else, there is no God."]{.class-2}

[In the Millennium, the Gentile nations will be ]{.class-0}[slaves]{.class-1}[ to those Jews, and ]{.class-0}[if they don't serve them, they'll be destroyed]{.class-1}[ (see the remarks under Zeph. 3:19--20). The Gentile bows down to the Jew and ]{.class-0}[licks his feet]{.class-1}[ just as he does to Jesus Christ (Isa. 49:23; Mic. 7:17 cf. Psa. 72:9--11). How's that for "racism"! That's ]{.class-0}[God Almighty]{.class-1}[ taking that Jew and raising him up above every other people on earth to a position just under His own Son (see Psa. 2:10--12).]{.class-0}

[Hey deah! ]{.class-0}[Have a nice day! ]{.class-1}[Smile, God loves you and has a wonderful plan for your life! What's "the plan"? "Down, boy!" "Speak!" "Roll over!" "Play dead!" ]{.class-0}[That's the plan for every member of the UN, the EU, NATO, and the USA.]{.class-1}

[]{#section-0_1_4ac57050909bb5a7_b695.xhtml}

CHAPTER NINE

9:1 The burden of the word of the LORD in the land of Hadrach, and Damascus shall be the rest thereof: when the eyes of man, as of all the tribes of Israel, shall be toward the LORD.

2 And Hamath also shall border thereby; Tyrus, and Zidon, though it be very wise.

3 And Tyrus did build herself a strong hold, and heaped up silver as the dust, and fine gold as the mire of the streets.

4 Behold, the Lord will cast her out, and he will smite her power in the sea; and she shall be devoured with fire.

5 Ashkelon shall see it, and fear; Gaza also shall see it, and be very sorrowful, and Ekron; for her expectation shall be ashamed; and the king shall perish from Gaza, and Ashkelon shall not be inhabited.

6 And a bastard shall dwell in Ashdod, and I will cut off the pride of the Philistines.

7 And I will take away his blood out of his mouth, and his abominations from between his teeth: but he that remaineth, even he, shall be for our God, and he shall be as a governor in Judah, and Ekron as a Jebusite.

8 And I will encamp about mine house because of the army, because of him that passeth by, and because of him that returneth: and no oppressor shall pass through them any more: for now have I seen with mine eyes.

"[The burden of the word of the LORD" ]{.class-2}[(vs. 1). We have commented on the word ]{.class-0}["burden"]{.class-2}[ under Habakkuk 1:1 (which see). Notice that here, though, ]{.class-0}["the burden"]{.class-2}[ is not laid on the one doing the preaching (as in the case of Habakkuk); it's laid on Israel's enemies. The Lord is going to tell the recipients of the message something that's going to be a load on them that's hard to carry. The real problem, however, comes when someone puts a load on you that ]{.class-0}[God]{.class-1}[ never said. Look at Jeremiah 23.]{.class-0}

"[I have not sent these prophets, yet they ran: I have not spoken to them, yet they prophesied\....I have heard what the prophets said, that prophesy lies in my name, saying, I have dreamed, I have dreamed ]{.class-2}[\[there's Martin Luther King Jr.'s "I have a dream"\]]{.class-0}[\....Therefore, behold, I am against the prophets, saith the LORD, that steal my words every one from his neighbor ]{.class-2}[\[like the translators of the ]{.class-0}[NIV]{.class-1}[ that stole over 64,000 of them right out of the text\]]{.class-0}[\....Behold, I am against them that prophesy false dreams, saith the LORD, and do tell them, and cause my people to err by their lies, and by their lightness; yet I sent them not, nor commanded them: therefore they shall not profit this people at all, saith the LORD ]{.class-2}[\[that's a bunch of preachers who are absolutely worthless; they aren't even worth shootin'\]]{.class-0}[. And when this people, or the prophet, or a priest, shall ask thee, saying, What is the burden of the LORD? ]{.class-2}[\[this lying bunch is coming to Jeremiah to find out what God actually did say after lying about what He said and getting rid of what He said\]]{.class-0}[ thou shalt then say unto them, What burden?"]{.class-2}[ \[The Lord says, "What burden are you talking about? I haven't heard of any burden."\] (Jer. 23:21, 25, 32--35).]{.class-0}

[Now what's going on there? Why does the Lord tell Nahum, Habakkuk, and Zechariah to preach about "the burden of the Lord" and then tell Jeremiah to tell the ones who come to him, "]{.class-0}['What burden?']{.class-2}[ I ain't seen no burden around here"?]{.class-0}

[Well, Christ said about those Pharisees: ]{.class-0}["they bind heavy burdens and grievous to be borne, and lay them on men's shoulders; but they themselves will not move them with one of their fingers"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 23:4).]{.class-0}

[Why did the Lord say that? Because those Pharisees were ]{.class-0}["teaching for doctrines the commandments of men"]{.class-2}[ (Mark 7:7) and ]{.class-0}["making the word of God of none effect through your tradition"]{.class-2}[ (Mark 7:13). They were laying a lot of legalistic stuff on the people that God ]{.class-0}[never said]{.class-1}[ in the Old Testament, and they would do nothing to help the folks with what they required them to do.]{.class-0}

[Now Jesus Christ laid a burden on His followers too, but He said, ]{.class-0}["my yoke is easy, and my burden is light."]{.class-2}[ That ]{.class-0}["yoke"]{.class-2}[ means that the ]{.class-0}[Lord's taking the weight off you and putting it on Himself. ]{.class-1}[When the Lord lays a burden on a sinner, the goal is repentance and restoration. It's supposed to be a blessing, not ]{.class-0}[a load.]{.class-1}[ A lot of people won't get saved because they think they have to do this and do that, and give up this and stop doing that; what's supposed to be a blessing becomes a burden.]{.class-0}

[In ]{.class-0}[Pilgrim's Progress,]{.class-1}[ John Bunyan has his pilgrim get a burden on his back the more he reads the Scriptures. That was a picture of the Holy Spirit convicting the sinner of the guilt and penalty of his sins. The purpose of that was to drive him to Calvary where Christ could ]{.class-0}[remove]{.class-1}[ the burden from him. That's what happened when I got saved. I had stolen a Bible, but all I could find in it was death, damnation, and judgment. I couldn't find anything about the Gospel or salvation at all, but the Holy Spirit was setting me up, see, so that when Hugh Pyle walked into that radio station where I worked, I was ready to receive Christ when he showed me how.]{.class-0}

Go back to Jeremiah 23.

"[And when this people, or the prophet, or a priest, shall ask thee, saying, What is the burden of the LORD? thou shalt then say unto them, What burden? I will even forsake you, saith the LORD. And as for the prophet, and the priest, and the people, that shall say, The burden of the LORD, I will even punish that man and his house\....And the burden of the LORD shall ye mention no more: for every man's word shall be his burden: FOR YE HAVE PERVERTED THE WORDS OF THE LIVING GOD, of the LORD of hosts our God"]{.class-2}[ (Jer. 23:33--34, 36).]{.class-0}

[The difference between Nahum, Habakkuk, and Zechariah and the crowd to which Jeremiah was preaching is that Nahum, Habakkuk, and Zechariah were preaching ]{.class-0}[what God said]{.class-1}[ and the other bunch were giving their congregations the ]{.class-0}[ASV, RSV, NIV, CEV, NEV, ESV, NKJV,]{.class-1}[ Moffat, Weymouth, Goodspeed, and the other 223 perversions of Scripture put out since 1880.]{.class-0}

Brethren, the question is not: "What are the 'fundamentals'?" or "What does the Bible teach?" The question is: "What did God SAY?"

[Jeremiah 23:37, ]{.class-0}["Thus shalt thou say to the prophet\...What hath the LORD SPOKEN?"]{.class-2}

[I'm not interested in what God ]{.class-0}[meant;]{.class-1}[ you can make Him "mean" anything. I want to know what He ]{.class-0}[said.]{.class-1}

[I open my radio programs with: "Dr. Ruckman will tell you what the Bible ]{.class-0}[says]{.class-1}[ about the lesson and not merely what it is ]{.class-0}[presumed to teach."]{.class-1}[ The reason that is said is because you can make the Bible "teach" ]{.class-0}[anything,]{.class-1}[ but you can't make it SAY anything except what it SAYS, unless you ]{.class-0}[change]{.class-1}[ it.]{.class-0}

[So since 1880, 233 corrupt versions have shown up to do just that: they've ]{.class-0}[changed what God said ]{.class-1}[so they can make God say ]{.class-0}[what they want Him to say. ]{.class-1}[Not one of those corrupt pieces of trash is what they call "reliable"; you can take the whole lot and throw them in the dumpster.]{.class-0}

[When you preach what God told you to preach in His Book, you can say]{.class-0}[ "the burden of the LORD,"]{.class-2}[ and when you ]{.class-0}[don't,]{.class-1}[ you ]{.class-0}[can't]{.class-1}[ (Jer. 23:38). When you pervert what God says, that's not ]{.class-0}["the burden of the LORD"]{.class-2}[; that's ]{.class-0}[your]{.class-1}[ burden (Jer. 23:36). You either have a Bible that is ]{.class-0}[God's]{.class-1}[ words, or you have a "Bible" where ]{.class-0}[men]{.class-1}[ have ]{.class-0}[corrupted]{.class-1}[ what God said (2 Cor. 2:17). When you have an ]{.class-0}[AV,]{.class-1}[ you have the ]{.class-0}[Scriptures in English]{.class-1}[ (and ]{.class-0}["ALL scripture is given by inspiration of God,"]{.class-2}[ 2 Tim. 3:16); you have ]{.class-0}[God's very words]{.class-1}[ in your language; and you have the only Bible that is "]{.class-0}[the truth,]{.class-1}[ the ]{.class-0}[whole]{.class-1}[ truth, and ]{.class-0}[nothing but]{.class-1}[ the truth."]{.class-0}

"[Hadrach," "Damascus,"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["Hamath"]{.class-2}[ (vss. 1--2) are all Syrian cities. ]{.class-0}["Tyrus, and Zidon"]{.class-2}[ (vss. 2--3) are Phoenician cities along the coast of Lebanon. ]{.class-0}["Ashkelon," "Gaza," "Ekron,"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["Ashdod"]{.class-2}[ (vss. 5--7) are all Philistine cities (see the comments on Zeph. 2:4). All those places are Israel's enemies to this day, and they all belong to ]{.class-0}[one religion.]{.class-1}

[Now, verse 1 says that what you are reading is future prophecy that has not been fulfilled yet: ]{.class-0}["shall be the rest thereof: when the eyes of man, as of all the tribes of Israel, shall be toward the LORD."]{.class-2}[ That's saying that Israel is going to be at ]{.class-0}["rest" ]{.class-2}[in]{.class-1}[ the land of her enemies; that is, she's going to ]{.class-0}[own]{.class-1}[ Syria, Lebanon, and the Gaza Strip.]{.class-0}

[That's the fulfillment of the Abrahamic land grant (Gen. 15:18) in the Millennium; it's ]{.class-0}[not now.]{.class-1}[ Israel hasn't been at ]{.class-0}["rest"]{.class-2}[ with any of those nations since Zechariah 9, and she's not right now. Moreover, Israel's ]{.class-0}["eyes"]{.class-2}[ are ]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-0}["toward the LORD"]{.class-2}[ right now, and they won't be till the Second Advent (Zech. 12:10).]{.class-0}

[The burdens laid on the places here in Zechariah 9 are given in other places in the Scriptures. You can read ]{.class-0}["the burden of Damascus"]{.class-2}[ in Isaiah 17 and ]{.class-0}["the burden of Tyre"]{.class-2}[ in Isaiah 23. Ezekiel 28 makes it clear that the wisdom and materialism of Tyre were Satanically inspired and controlled. The destruction of Tyre under Nebuchadnezzar and Alexander the Great was a picture of the destruction of Babylon the Great and the Son of Perdition ]{.class-0}[that will take place at the Second Advent]{.class-1}[ (Ezek. 26--28 cf. Rev. 17--19).]{.class-0}

[For verse 5, we refer you to Amos 1:6--8 and the comments on that passage in Volume I of this Commentary. Verse 6, though, is absolutely unexplainable; all the commentators are perfect blanks. Outside of the fact that the prophecy concerns modern "Palestinians," the only sure exegesis I can make is ]{.class-0}["Ich weiss nicht."]{.class-1}[ There is the possibility that the ]{.class-0}["bastard"]{.class-2}[ of verse 6 could be a reference to the Son of Perdition (who would have a counterfeit "virgin birth," cf. John 8:41).]{.class-0}

[Now, I say that because of what's in verses 7--8: ]{.class-0}["And I]{.class-2}[ \[that's the Lord speaking, cf. vss. 4, 6\]]{.class-0}[ will take away his ]{.class-2}[\[the ]{.class-0}["bastard"]{.class-2}[ of vs. 6\]]{.class-0}[ blood out of his mouth, and his ]{.class-2}[\[the]{.class-0}[ "bastard"]{.class-2}[ of vs. 6\]]{.class-0}[ abominations from between his teeth\...." ]{.class-2}[During the Tribulation, the Antichrist is sacrificing those Jews on the altar (Psa. 44:22 cf. Rev. 6:9) and eating their flesh (Isa. 6:13) and drinking their blood (Psa. 16:4) in the Mass (Rev. 17:4--6). Christ stops that at His return.]{.class-0}

"[\...but he that remaineth ]{.class-2}[\[who's that? I don't know; keep on reading\]]{.class-0}[, even he, shall be for our God\..."]{.class-2}[ Well, the one "remaining" after the Lord gets rid of the Antichrist would be Jesus Christ Himself. He's the "last man standing," and He would ]{.class-0}["be for our God"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["as a governor in Judah"]{.class-2}[ (Mic. 5:2 cf. Matt. 2:6), but I'm not too sure about that interpretation.]{.class-0}

[If that were the case, though, then ]{.class-0}["Ekron"]{.class-2}[ would be ]{.class-0}["as a Jebusite"]{.class-2}[ inasmuch as the Jebusites first owned Jerusalem until David (a type of Christ) came along and kicked them out (2 Sam. 5:6--10). In the passage before us, the Antichrist is in charge of Ekron until the Lord Jesus Christ comes back at the Second Advent. (By the way, in the same chapter where David takes Jerusalem, he defeats the Philistines in a battle that is a picture of the Second Advent (2 Sam. 5:17--25 and especially compare vs. 24 in that passage with the remarks on UFOs in Zech. 5\].)]{.class-0}

[Zechariah 9:8---]{.class-0}["And I will encamp about mine house because of the army."]{.class-2}[ That's an army around the Temple. Well, I know where ]{.class-0}[that]{.class-1}[ is. That is Revelation 11 and Zechariah 14.]{.class-0}

"[And there was given me a reed like unto a rod: and the angel stood, saying, Rise, and measure the TEMPLE of GOD ]{.class-2}[\[there's ]{.class-0}["mine house"]{.class-2}[ in Zech. 9:8\]]{.class-0}[, and the altar, and them that worship therein. But the court which is without the temple leave out, and measure it not; for it is given unto the Gentiles; and the holy city shall they tread under foot forty and two months ]{.class-2}[\[the last half of Daniel's Seventieth Week\]]{.class-0}[" ]{.class-2}[(Rev. 11:1--2).]{.class-0}

"[Behold, the day of the LORD cometh ]{.class-2}[\[Second Advent\]]{.class-0}[, and thy spoil shall be divided in the midst of thee. For I will gather all nations against Jerusalem to battle ]{.class-2}[\[there's ]{.class-0}["the army"]{.class-2}[ surrounding the Temple\]]{.class-0}[; and the city shall be taken, and the houses rifled, and the women ravished; and half of the city shall go forth into captivity, and the residue of the people shall not be cut off from the city. Then shall the LORD go forth, and fight against those nations, as when he fought in the day of battle ]{.class-2}[\[and there's the Lord ]{.class-0}["encamped about mine house"]{.class-2}[\]]{.class-0}[" ]{.class-2}[(Zech. 14:1--3).]{.class-0}

Want another one? Look at Zechariah 12:3, 8--9.

"[And in that day will I make Jerusalem a burdensome stone for all people: all that burden themselves with it shall be cut in pieces, though all the people of the earth be gathered together against it\....In that day shall the LORD defend the inhabitants of Jerusalem\...And it shall come to pass in that day, that I will seek to destroy all the nations that come against Jerusalem."]{.class-2}

[Back to the end of Zechariah 9:8---]{.class-0}["for now have I seen with mine eyes."]{.class-2}[ That's not an "anthropomorphism" as you find it in Psalm 34:15 or Proverbs 15:3; it's not a "spiritualization" as you find it in 2 Chronicles 16:9 or Proverbs 22:12. Those are ]{.class-0}[real]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-0}["eyes"]{.class-2}[ on a ]{.class-0}[real]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-0}["man"]{.class-2}[ (1 Tim. 2:5) who has ]{.class-0}[real]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-0}["feet"]{.class-2}[ (Zech. 14:4). The return of Jesus Christ is a ]{.class-0}[bodily]{.class-1}[ return (Acts 1:11) of a ]{.class-0}[physical]{.class-1}[ body (Luke 24:39) that could eat (Luke 24:41--43) and be touched (John 20:27).]{.class-0}

9 Rejoice greatly, O daughter of Zion; shout, O daughter of Jerusalem: behold, thy King cometh unto thee: he is just, and having salvation; lowly, and riding upon an ass, and upon a colt the foal of an ass.

10 And I will cut off the chariot from Ephraim, and the horse from Jerusalem, and the battle bow shall be cut off: and he shall speak peace unto the heathen: and his dominion shall be from sea even to sea, and from the river even to the ends of the earth.

11 As for thee also, by the blood of thy covenant I have sent forth thy prisoners out of the pit wherein is no water.

[The passage is devastating to all Amillennialists and Postmillennialists,]{.class-1}[ for just as sure as the world, verse 9 is First Advent and verse 11 is First Advent. But sandwiched right between two verses on the First Coming of Christ is a verse on the Second Coming that had ]{.class-0}[no fulfillment whatsoever]{.class-1}[ during the ]{.class-0}[earthly ministry]{.class-1}[ of Jesus Christ, and ]{.class-0}[won't]{.class-1}[ until His return in power (see Hos. 2:18). Two advents side by side; and it messes up all the Catholics, Lutherans, Presbyterians, Methodists, Episcopalians, and Baptists who reject the Premillennial approach to prophecy.]{.class-0}

That happens over and over again in Scripture. Look at Genesis 49.

"[The sceptre shall not depart from Judah, nor a lawgiver from between his feet, until Shiloh come; and unto him shall the gathering of the people be ]{.class-2}[\[Second Advent\]]{.class-0}[. Binding his foal unto the vine, and his ass's colt unto the choice vine]{.class-2}[ \[the Triumphal Entry at the First Advent---Matt. 21:1--5\]]{.class-0}[; he washed his garments in wine, and his clothes in the blood of grapes ]{.class-2}[\[Second Advent---Isa. 61:1--4\]]{.class-0}["]{.class-2}[ (Gen. 49:10--11).]{.class-0}

"[But his bow abode in strength, and the arms of his hands were made strong by the hands of the mighty God of Jacob; (from thence is the shepherd]{.class-2}[ \[First Advent---John 10:14\]]{.class-0}[, the stone of Israel ]{.class-2}[\[Second Advent---Dan. 2:44--45\]]{.class-0}["]{.class-2}[ (Gen. 49:24).]{.class-0}

[The classic example of this is in Luke 4:18--19 and Isaiah 61:1--2, which provides the textbook case of ]{.class-0}["rightly dividing the word of truth"]{.class-2}[ (2 Tim. 2:15). In Luke 4, Christ reads Isaiah 61:1--2 and ]{.class-0}[ends the passage at the comma in verse 2 because everything up to that point deals with His First Advent ]{.class-1}[(Luke 4:21). After the comma in Isaiah 61:2, you have ]{.class-0}["the day of vengeance of our God"]{.class-2}[---the ]{.class-0}[Second Advent; the comma in the verse represents 2,000 years of history. ]{.class-1}[The First and Second Advents are]{.class-0}[ right together]{.class-1}[ in that passage, separated only by a ]{.class-0}[comma]{.class-1}[.]{.class-0}

[The apostate Amillennialists and Postmillennialists try to spiritualize Zechariah 9:10 so that it takes place in A.D. 33. ]{.class-0}[Nothing]{.class-1}[ in the verse happened then---spiritually or physically.]{.class-0}

[Of course, Zechariah 9:9 is a prophecy of Jesus' "Triumphal Entry" on Palm Sunday (see Matt. 21:4--5; John 12:14--15). It is a First Advent prophecy. That being the case, such corrupt perversions of the Scriptures (see Jer. 23:36) as those put out by James Moffat and the ]{.class-0}[RSV]{.class-1}[ of the Communist National Council of Churches changed ]{.class-0}["he is just, and having salvation"]{.class-2}[ to ]{.class-0}["he is triumphant and victorious."]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-16}[Why, Christ wasn't ]{.class-0}["triumphant"]{.class-1}[ or ]{.class-0}["victorious"]{.class-1}[ the first time He came; that's His ]{.class-0}[Second Coming.]{.class-1}[ What is the scholarship of men like Moffat and the translators of the ]{.class-0}[RSV]{.class-1}[ worth? ]{.class-0}[Not fifteen cents!]{.class-1}[ If you paid to sit under men like that, you got ]{.class-0}[defrauded.]{.class-1}

[Verse 10: ]{.class-0}["And I will cut off the chariot from Ephraim, and the horse from Jerusalem ]{.class-2}[\[Second Advent---Mic. 5:9--11, 15\]]{.class-0}[, and the battle bow shall be cut off ]{.class-2}[\[Second Advent---Psa. 76:1--3\]]{.class-0}[: and he shall speak peace unto the heathen ]{.class-2}[\[Second Advent---see the comments on Hag. 2:7, 9\]]{.class-0}[, and his dominion shall be from sea even to sea, and from the river even to the ends of the earth ]{.class-2}[\[Second Advent---Psa. 72:8; Isa. 49:6\]]{.class-0}[."]{.class-2}[ The verse is Second Advent from start to finish; there's nothing in it that was fulfilled at Jesus Christ's First Coming.]{.class-0}

[Verse 11, on the other hand, is a different story. The ]{.class-0}["King"]{.class-2}[ of verse 9---Christ---delivers somebody ]{.class-0}["by the blood of thy covenant"]{.class-2}[ (Heb. 10:29; 12:24, 13:20).]{.class-0}

"[I have sent forth thy prisoners out of the pit wherein is no water."]{.class-2}[ That's a funny thing to insert right there. I read back in Genesis 37:24 that Joseph's brothers ]{.class-0}["took him, and cast him into a pit"]{.class-2}[ where ]{.class-0}["there was no water."]{.class-2}[ Well, Joseph is the greatest type of Christ in the entire Old Testament. That is a picture of Christ descending into Hell after shedding His blood on the cross (cf. Acts 2:27--31). The Lord is going down and freeing some prisoners; He's leading a jailbreak. Look at Ephesians 4:8--10.]{.class-0}

"[Wherefore he saith, When he ascended up on high, he led captivity captive, and gave gifts unto men. (Now that he ascended, what is it but that he also descended first into the lower parts of the earth? He that descendeth is the same also that ascended up far above all heavens, that he might fill all things.)"]{.class-2}

[Peter said, ]{.class-0}["For this cause was the gospel preached also to them that are dead."]{.class-2}[ An Old Testament saint didn't go directly to Heaven when he died; he went to a place underground, ]{.class-0}["in the heart of the earth"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 12:40), called ]{.class-0}["paradise"]{.class-2}[ (Luke 23:43) or "Abraham's Bosom" (see Luke 16:22). The reason for that was that although their sins were ]{.class-0}[forgiven]{.class-1}[, they were not completely ]{.class-0}[taken away ]{.class-1}[(Exod. 34:7), because ]{.class-0}["it is not possible that the blood of bulls and of goats should take away sins"]{.class-2}[ (Heb. 10:4). So the Old Testament saints went ]{.class-0}[down]{.class-1}[ until their sins were paid for at Calvary, and then Christ came down at His burial ]{.class-0}["to declare his righteousness for the remission of sins that are past, through the forbearance of God"]{.class-2}[ (Rom. 3:25). ]{.class-0}["The gospel"]{.class-2}[ that Jesus Christ ]{.class-0}["preached"]{.class-2}[ to the ]{.class-0}["dead"]{.class-2}[ was: "Good news, fellas! All those sins I forgave ]{.class-0}[back there]{.class-1}[ are paid in full ]{.class-0}[now]{.class-1}[, and I'm giving you my righteousness so you can finally get to Heaven. ]{.class-0}[Let's get outta here!"]{.class-1}

[When Jesus quoted Isaiah 61 in Luke 4:18, He said He was sent ]{.class-0}["to preach deliverance to the captives."]{.class-2}[ Those ]{.class-0}["captives"]{.class-2}[ were the Old Testament saints in Abraham's Bosom, and when Christ arose from the dead, ]{.class-0}[He took them out of there and led them to Heaven.]{.class-1}[ As a sign that He did that, when He died, ]{.class-0}["the graves were opened; and many bodies of the saints which slept arose, And came out of the graves after his resurrection, and went into the holy city, and appeared unto many"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 27:52--53).]{.class-0}

[Now that's what's going on in Zechariah 9:9--11. Verse 9---Triumphal Entry: First Advent. Verse 10---Second Advent and Millennium. Verse 11---the Death, Burial, and Resurrection: First Advent. They're all right there together. ]{.class-0}[That's what messes up all the scholars.]{.class-1}[ It messes them up the same way it messed up the Jews.]{.class-0}

[When an Old Testament prophet wrote, he often wrote about the victory and defeat of Christ ]{.class-0}[right together,]{.class-1}[ and often in the ]{.class-0}[opposite order]{.class-1}[ of what would take place. When he finished writing and read what he wrote, he couldn't make any sense out of it because the glory and the shame were right together.]{.class-0}

[First Peter 1:11 says, ]{.class-0}["Searching what, or what manner of time the Spirit of Christ which was in them did signify, when it testified beforehand the sufferings of Christ, and the glory that should follow."]{.class-2}

That's why most of the Jews at Christ's First Coming were looking for Him to be a vengeful King who would get rid of the Romans. THAT'S WHAT THE OLD TESTAMENT SAID HE WOULD BE. That thing was so firmly ingrained in them that Christ has to explain it to them after His resurrection.

"[And how the chief priests and our rulers delivered him to be condemned to death, and have crucified him. But we trusted that it had been he which should have redeemed Israel ]{.class-2}[\[that's not a spiritual redemption from sin there; that's the physical redemption of the Jews from their enemies\]]{.class-0}[\...Then he said unto them, O fools, and slow of heart to believe all that the prophets have spoken ]{.class-2}[\[]{.class-0}[that's the problem:]{.class-1}[ the Jews rejected what the prophets said about the First Advent, and the Gentiles reject what the prophets said about the Second Advent\]]{.class-0}[: Ought not Christ to have suffered these things, and to enter into his glory? And beginning at Moses and all the prophets, he expounded unto them in all the scriptures the things concerning himself"]{.class-2}[ (Luke 24:20--21, 25--27).]{.class-0}

[It was all there in the Old Testament---all the details of the First and Second Comings---but it took the Lord Jesus Himself (and the Holy Spirit---John 14:26, 15:26, 16:13--14) to reveal the difference between the two. The only way you are going to get the thing straight is to know the Author of the Scripture (1 Cor. 2:10--14) and ]{.class-0}["study"]{.class-2}[ His Book (2 Tim. 2:15).]{.class-0}

[Now why did God do that? Why did the Lord intermingle the two Advents, ]{.class-0}[sometimes putting them in reverse order?]{.class-1}[ Well, He did that because what you read in the Old Testament about Christ's two Comings depended on what the Jews would do with Him when He came.]{.class-0}

[Of course, He had to be crucified (Psa. 22; Isa. 53); He had to come up from the dead (Psa. 16:10--11). All that's in there, so it had to take place. But when Christ comes up from the dead, you've got a problem: ]{.class-0}[the Jews get a second chance to get right.]{.class-1}

[When Jesus was on the cross, He prayed, ]{.class-0}["Father, forgive them; for they know not what they do"]{.class-2}[ (Luke 23:34). So the Lord forgave them, and when you get to Acts 2, Peter is preaching the Second Coming. ]{.class-0}[Why? Because in the Old Testament, both Advents are together with no Church Age between them.]{.class-1}[ In Acts 2, there's no New Testament written yet, so even though the New Testament is in force because ]{.class-0}["the testator"]{.class-2}[ died (Heb. 9:16--17), Peter and the apostles are still operating in ]{.class-0}[an Old Testament context]{.class-1}[ because that is all they have.]{.class-0}

[Do you want to drive a Fundamentalist mad? Just ask him to show you ]{.class-0}["the gospel of the grace of God"]{.class-2}[ that Paul preached (Acts 20:24) in Acts 2. ]{.class-0}[You can't do it.]{.class-1}

[Paul's Gospel was: ]{.class-0}["Christ died FOR OUR SINS according to the scriptures; And that he was buried, and that he rose again the third day according to the scriptures"]{.class-2}[ (1 Cor. 15:3--4). ]{.class-0}[I'll give you a hundred bucks cash if you can find where Christ died for anybody's sins in Acts 2.]{.class-1}

[You say, "You don't have the money." I could get it before you could find what I asked. In Acts 2, you will find Christ's ]{.class-0}[death]{.class-1}[ (Acts 2:23, 36), His ]{.class-0}[burial]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[resurrection]{.class-1}[ (Acts 2:24--31), and His glorification (Acts 2:33); but none of those things are associate ]{.class-0}[with the blood atonement for sin.]{.class-1}

[When Peter talks about ]{.class-0}["the remission of sins,"]{.class-2}[ it is connected to repentance and water baptism, ]{.class-0}[not Christ dying for anybody]{.class-1}[ (see Acts 2:38). The people to whom Peter preaches don't ask, "What must I do to be]{.class-0}[ saved?"]{.class-1}[ (Acts 16:30 cf. 2:37), and Peter doesn't say anything about ]{.class-0}["Believe on the Lord Jesus Christ, and thou shalt be saved"]{.class-2}[ (Acts 16:31 cf. 2:40). The next time Peter preaches, he talks about Israel's sins being ]{.class-0}["blotted out"]{.class-2}[ at the Second Advent (Acts 3:19), not at Calvary (Col. 2:13--14). The first time Peter mentions salvation through Jesus Christ (Acts 4:12),]{.class-0}[ he still doesn't connect it with the blood atonement. ]{.class-1}[The first time the blood atonement for sins shows up in the book of Acts is in ]{.class-0}[Acts 8]{.class-1}[ where Philip preaches to the Ethiopian eunuch from Isaiah 53 (Acts 8:32--35; Isa. 53:10 talks about Christ bearing our sins and being made ]{.class-0}["an offering for sins"]{.class-2}[).]{.class-0}

[Now what's going on? ]{.class-0}[The Lord puts the two Advents together so that the future depended on man's free will. ]{.class-1}[(Tell that to a Five-point Calvinist and watch him have a conniption fit!)]{.class-0}

[Up to Acts 7, Peter's preaching the Second Advent as if it's going to take place right then. What happens in Acts 7? The nation's leaders are given a ]{.class-0}[second chance]{.class-1}[ to receive their Messiah.]{.class-0}

[Psalm 110:1 says, ]{.class-0}["The LORD ]{.class-2}[\[that's the Father\]]{.class-0}[ said unto my Lord]{.class-2}[ \[the Son---Jesus Christ\]]{.class-0}[, Sit thou at my right hand, until I make thine enemies thy footstool."]{.class-2}[ When the Lord Jesus went back to Glory, He ]{.class-0}["sat on the right hand of God" ]{.class-2}[(Mark 16:19), and that is where He'll be until He returns to take care of His enemies.]{.class-0}

[But in Acts 7:56, Jesus is ]{.class-0}["standing on the right hand of God."]{.class-2}[ Why? ]{.class-0}[He was getting ready to come back.]{.class-1}[ What happened? The Sanhedrin, the official leaders of the nation of Israel, the ones who had Him crucified, rejected Him again and killed Stephen just like they killed the Lord (cf. Acts 7:60).]{.class-0}[ So the Second Coming was taken and put off 2,000 years.]{.class-1}

Up to that point, God had it fixed so that it could go either way. Brethren, the Lord has things worked out so that whether you have it your way or not, what He said would take place is going to take place. That just blows Calvin's switch and puts him "off the main."

[God had things so fixed that if those Jews ]{.class-0}[had]{.class-1}[ accepted their Messiah there in Acts 7, the Second Advent would have taken place ]{.class-0}[right then.]{.class-1}[ Haven't some of you ever wondered why John the Baptist showed up preaching the Kingdom of God and the Kingdom of Heaven were ]{.class-0}["at hand,"]{.class-2}[ and then one of them never shows up? I mean, he said they were ]{.class-0}[right there,]{.class-1}[ right on the ]{.class-0}[doorstep]{.class-1}[. How did the Kingdom of Heaven get delayed for 2,000 years?]{.class-0}

Well, if those Jews had accepted Christ, then BAM!--- the Rapture in Acts 7; BOOM!---the Tribulation starts with the Antichrist being Caesar over in Rome; BLAM!---the Second Advent in less than seven years. But they rejected Him, so it's been 2,000 years now.

12 Turn you to the strong hold, ye prisoners of hope: even to day do I declare that I will render double unto thee;

13 When I have bent Judah for me, filled the bow with Ephraim, and raised up thy sons, O Zion, against thy sons, O Greece, and made thee as the sword of a mighty man.

14 And the LORD shall be seen over them, and his arrow shall go forth as the lightning: and the Lord GOD shall blow the trumpet, and shall go with whirlwinds of the south.

15 The LORD of hosts shall defend them; and they shall devour, and subdue with sling stones; and they shall drink, and make a noise as through wine; and they shall be filled like bowls, and as the corners of the altar.

16 And the LORD their God shall save them in that day as the flock of his people: for they shall be as the stones of a crown, lifted up as an ensign upon his land.

17 For how great is his goodness, and how great is his beauty! corn shall make the young men cheerful, and new wine the maids.

"[Turn you to the strong hold, ye prisoners of hope"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 12). That's aimed at the ]{.class-0}["prisoners"]{.class-2}[ in verse 11---the people in Paradise waiting for their redemption to show up. But suddenly, a new application is placed on the passage: ]{.class-0}["even to day do I declare that I will render double unto thee."]{.class-2}

[Isaiah 61:7 says, ]{.class-0}["For your shame ye shall have double; and for confusion they shall rejoice in their portion: therefore in their land they shall possess the double: everlasting joy shall be unto them."]{.class-2}

[That's a reference to the restoration of the Jew in the Millennium. It's pictured by Job getting ]{.class-0}["twice as much"]{.class-2}[ from God after ]{.class-0}["the LORD turned the captivity of Job"]{.class-2}[ (see the comments on Zeph. 3:20). What does that have to do with someone down in Abraham's Bosom.]{.class-0}

[Well, although when Jesus Christ ]{.class-0}["led captivity captive"]{.class-2}[ (Eph. 4:8) ]{.class-0}["paradise"]{.class-2}[ changed locations from ]{.class-0}["the heart of the earth"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 12:40 cf. Luke 23:43) to the third heaven (Rev. 2:7), ]{.class-0}[during the Tribulation, you go back to an Old Testament situation.]{.class-1}[ When a saint dies during the Tribulation, he doesn't go directly to Heaven. Look at Revelation 6:9--11.]{.class-0}

"[And when he had opened the fifth seal, I saw under the altar the souls of them that were slain for the word of God, and for the testimony which they held: And they cried with a loud voice, saying, How long, O Lord, holy and true, dost thou not judge and avenge our blood on them that dwell on the earth? And white robes were given unto every one of them; and it was said unto them, that they should rest yet for a little season, until their brethren, that should be killed as they were, should be fulfilled."]{.class-2}

[The way that the Tribulation saints are ]{.class-0}["killed"]{.class-2}[ is by ]{.class-0}[decapitation.]{.class-1}[ These souls are taken up to Heaven in a rapture just before the Second Advent at the end of the Tribulation (Rev. 7:9--14, 11:12--15, 14:1--3). During the Millennium, they not only get their tribal inheritance (Ezek. 48:29), they get a Millennial rule.]{.class-0}

"[And I saw thrones, and they sat upon them, and judgment was given unto them: and I saw the souls of them that were beheaded for the witness of Jesus, and for the word of God, and which had not worshipped the beast, neither his image, neither had received his mark upon their foreheads, or in their hands; and they lived and reigned with Christ a thousand years"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 20:4).]{.class-0}

So it looks like Zechariah 9:12 could be a reference to Tribulation saints, while verse 11 is a reference to Old Testament saints.

[Back to Zechariah 9: ]{.class-0}["When I have bent Judah for me, filled the bow with Ephraim, and raised up thy sons, O Zion\...and made thee as the sword of a mighty man"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 13). Now that is obviously figurative. The basic rule of Biblical interpretation is ]{.class-0}[if you can]{.class-1}[ take a passage literally, then ]{.class-0}[you do.]{.class-1}[ The Lord is saying that Judah is His bow, Ephraim is His arrows, and Zion is His sword. Well, He's not going to shoot Ephraimites out of Judeans and swing the inhabitants of Jerusalem around trying to cut off someone's head.]{.class-0}

[The verse is a prophecy about the complete restoration of the Jews as God's ]{.class-0}["armies"]{.class-2}[ (Exod. 6:26). That is what Mohammed tried to ]{.class-0}[imitate]{.class-1}[ when he set himself up as a ]{.class-0}[religious military dictator;]{.class-1}[ the Pope did the same thing in the Dark Ages. But the verse has nothing to do with Arabs or Moslems or Catholics or any other kind of "Christian." It's ]{.class-0}[Ephraim]{.class-1}[ (the northern tribes) and ]{.class-0}[Judah]{.class-1}[ (the southern tribes) back together with ]{.class-0}[Jerusalem]{.class-1}[ as their capital.]{.class-0}

[The ]{.class-0}["sons"]{.class-2}[ of ]{.class-0}["Greece"]{.class-2}[ in the passage is used as another name for the Gentiles as a whole, like Paul uses the term ]{.class-0}["Greek"]{.class-2}[ in the New Testament (see Rom. 1:16, 10:12; 1 Cor. 1:22--24; Gal. 3:28; Col. 3:11). The Lord is saying He's going to arm those Jews and turn them loose on the Gentiles like He did back in Joshua. It's a reference to the remnant of the Jews turning on their attackers at the Second Advent (see 12:2--9, 14:3, 12--14).]{.class-0}

[Why does the Lord single out one nation to represent the Gentile peoples? Well, over in Daniel 8:21, 10:20, 11:2, the King of ]{.class-0}[Greece]{.class-1}[ is associated with the Antichrist (see Dan. 8:5--12, 21--26). That King was Alexander the Great, who claimed to be the son of Jupiter-Amon (the chief god of the Greeks and Egyptians) through a virgin birth. ]{.class-0}[The Antichrist arises out of the territory that comprised Alexander's empire.]{.class-1}[ So when God picks a nation to represent the Gentiles under the leadership of the Antichrist, He picks out Greece.]{.class-0}

[Now, how do you know that's what God's talking about in verse 13? Verse 14: ]{.class-0}["And the LORD shall be seen over them ]{.class-2}[\[literal at the Second Advent---Rev. 1:7, 19:11\]]{.class-0}[, and his arrow shall go forth as the lightning]{.class-2}[ \[Second Advent---see the comments on Hab. 3:9\]]{.class-0}[: and the Lord GOD shall blow the trumpet ]{.class-2}[\[Second Advent---Joel 2:1; Zeph. 1:16\]]{.class-0}[, and shall go with whirlwinds of the south ]{.class-2}[\[Second Advent---see the comments on Hab. 3:14\]]{.class-0}[."]{.class-2}

[Verse 15:]{.class-0}[ "The LORD of hosts shall defend them ]{.class-2}[\[Second Advent---Zech. 12:8, 14:3\]]{.class-0}[; and they shall devour, and subdue with sling stones]{.class-2}[ \[Second Advent---see the comments on David and Goliath under Hab. 3:13\]]{.class-0}[; and they shall drink, and make a noise as through wine ]{.class-2}[\[Second Advent---Psa. 78:65\]]{.class-0}[; and they shall be filled like bowls, and as the corners of the altar ]{.class-2}[\[Second Advent---Deut. 32:42 cf. Lev. 4:18, 25\]]{.class-0}[."]{.class-2}

[It's Second Advent all the way through there. When the Lord settles His controversy with His creation, He does it in the only manner they understand---]{.class-0}[armed warfare.]{.class-1}[ Under his summary of the word ]{.class-0}[Kosmos]{.class-1}[ (see the note on Rev. 13:8), Scofield says, "This world-system is imposing and powerful with armies and fleets; is often outwardly religious, scientific, cultured, and elegant; but, seething with national and commercial rivalries and ambitions, is upheld in any real crisis by ARMED FORCE."]{.class-0}

[The Germans say, ]{.class-0}["In case of rain, the war will be held in the auditorium."]{.class-1}[ That's a cynical way of saying that you're going to have war no matter what you do. So when God settles His accounts with Europe, Asia, Africa, the Americas, and Australia, ]{.class-0}[He uses warfare.]{.class-1}[ Bob Jones Sr. said, "War is God's judgment on sin here; Hell is God's judgment on sin hereafter."]{.class-0}

[When the Lord comes back, He leads an army of ]{.class-0}[indestructible troops]{.class-1}[ who can pass through solid objects and can't be wounded or killed (read Joel 2). ]{.class-0}[That's the theme of the whole Bible]{.class-1}[. There are more verses in the Bible on Christ coming back and ]{.class-0}[killing people]{.class-1}[ at the Battle of Armageddon than there are on ]{.class-0}[salvation]{.class-1}[.]{.class-0}

[Verse 16: ]{.class-0}["And the LORD their God." ]{.class-2}[That's the Lord as the God of a ]{.class-0}[whole nation]{.class-1}[. When Christ instructs His ]{.class-0}[Jewish]{.class-1}[ disciples to pray ]{.class-0}["Our Father which art in heaven"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 6:9), that's not an individual's prayer (notice ]{.class-0}["us"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["our"]{.class-2}[ in Matt. 6:11--13); that's a Jew praying as part of ]{.class-0}[a nation ]{.class-1}[(see David's prayer in 1 Chron. 29:10--16). Bob Jones Sr. used to close his chapel talks with "our Father," but he was addressing the Lord for his congregation. The proper address of the redeemed child of God is ]{.class-0}["Abba, Father"]{.class-2}[ (Rom. 8:15; Gal. 4:6); that's a personal, individual thing, ]{.class-0}[not a corporate one.]{.class-1}

"[The LORD their God shall save THEM in that day]{.class-2}[ \[that's the corporate salvation of a nation, not an individual's personal salvation; see Rom. 11:26--27\]]{.class-0}[ as the flock of his people]{.class-2}[ \[see the comments on Psa. 100:3 in Vol. II of that Commentary\]]{.class-0}[: for they shall be as the stones of a crown, lifted up as an ensign upon his land."]{.class-2}

[That's Israel in the Millennium. Isaiah 62:1--3 says, ]{.class-0}["For Zion's sake will I not hold my peace, and for Jerusalem's sake I will not rest, until the righteousness thereof go forth as brightness, and the salvation thereof as a lamp that burneth. And the Gentiles shall see thy righteousness, and all kings thy glory: and thou shalt be called by a new name, which the mouth of the LORD shall name. Thou shalt also be a crown of glory in the hand of the LORD, and a royal diadem in the hand of thy God."]{.class-2}

[That ]{.class-0}["ensign"]{.class-2}[ is the Lord Jesus Christ (Isa. 11:10, 12), but the Gentiles approach Him in the Millennium through the Jews (see the comments on 8:21--23).]{.class-0}

[Verse 17:]{.class-0}[ "For how great is his goodness]{.class-2}[ \[see Psa. 31:19\]]{.class-0}[, and how great is his beauty."]{.class-2}[ Now that last clause is a Millennial reference. When Christ was here on earth, that wasn't the case. Isaiah 53:2 says, ]{.class-0}["he hath no form nor comeliness; and when we shall see him, there is no beauty that we should desire him."]{.class-2}

[Of course, that's not the case with you, if you're saved. You look at Him through the eyes of faith and love, so to you He's ]{.class-0}["altogether lovely"]{.class-2}[ (Song of Sol. 5:16), but the ]{.class-0}[unsaved world ]{.class-1}[doesn't want to have anything to do with Him at all (John 15:1--8).]{.class-0}

[But that changes in the Millennium. Jesus Christ will once more be here on earth, sitting on the throne of David at Jerusalem, and the Bible says, ]{.class-0}["Thine eyes shall see the king in his beauty"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 33:17). That ]{.class-0}["beauty"]{.class-2}[ is ]{.class-0}["the beauty of holiness"]{.class-2}[ (Psa. 96:9--10).]{.class-0}

"[Corn shall make the young men cheerful, and new wine the maids."]{.class-2}[ There'll be plenty of food in the Millennium (Joel 3:18; Amos 9:14). When Christ is reigning on earth, the Bible says the desert will bloom like a rose (Isa. 35:1--2). All the prophecy boys make that the Jews reclaiming their land through irrigation. That's not about what that passage is talking. ]{.class-0}["The desert"]{.class-2}[ in Isaiah 35 ]{.class-0}[is the desert between the Jordan and Euphrates Rivers. ]{.class-1}[That's the land of the Abrahamic covenant (Gen. 15:18). God takes that wilderness and makes it just like the Garden of Eden ]{.class-0}[before]{.class-1}[ the Fall (Isa. 51:3).]{.class-0}

[Brethren, when the Lord comes back, He will straighten out every problem this world has: there's not one problem on earth that couldn't be straightened out right now by the return of Jesus Christ. I say, "Let 'er rip." As far as I'm concerned, ]{.class-0}[the sooner the better.]{.class-1}

[]{#section-0_1_e499e9f0124ac2ec_b696.xhtml}

CHAPTER TEN

10:1 Ask ye of the LORD rain in the time of the latter rain; so the LORD shall make bright clouds, and give them showers of rain, to every one grass in the field.

2 For the idols have spoken vanity, and the diviners have seen a lie, and have told false dreams; they comfort in vain: therefore they went their way as a flock, they were troubled, because there was no shepherd.

3 Mine anger was kindled against the shepherds, and I punished the goats: for the LORD of hosts hath visited his flock the house of Judah, and hath made them as his goodly horse in the battle.

4 Out of him came forth the corner, out of him the nail, out of him the battle bow, out of him every oppressor together.

5 And they shall be as mighty men, which tread down their enemies in the mire of the streets in the battle: and they shall fight, because the LORD is with them, and the riders on horses shall be confounded.

[We have already discussed the abominable, fouled-up exegesis of the Charismatics on ]{.class-0}["the latter rain"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 1) under the comments on 4:6 (which see). Do you know what ]{.class-0}["the latter rain"]{.class-2}[ is? Well, it sure ain't a bunch of tongues-blabbering fake healers like Oral Roberts, Kathryn Kuhlman, Benny Hinn, Todd Bentley, Azusa Street Mission, and the Bethel Bible College in Topeka, Kansas.]{.class-0}

[Now I know this is really complicated and deep, but ]{.class-0}["the latter rain"]{.class-2}[ is ]{.class-0}[water dumped out of the sky. ]{.class-1}[Verse 1: ]{.class-0}["showers of rain."]{.class-2}[ In Palestine, ]{.class-0}["the latter rain"]{.class-2}[ falls in March/April and the early rain falls in September/October. But in Joel 2:23, the Lord sends both rains in one month.]{.class-0}

"[Be glad then, ye children of Zion, and rejoice in the LORD your God: for he hath given you the former rain moderately, and he will cause to come down for you the rain, the former rain, and the latter rain in the first month."]{.class-2}

[God's sending down a gully washer just like He did in 1 Kings 18:41, 45. ]{.class-0}[The context of Joel 2 is the Second Advent. ]{.class-1}[Elijah shows up in the Tribulation and stops the rain just like he did back in 1 Kings 17:1 (see Rev. 11:6), and he causes the downpour of Joel 2:23 just as he did it in 1 Kings 18. Look at James 5:7.]{.class-0}

"[Be patient therefore, brethren, unto the COMING OF THE LORD ]{.class-2}[\[Second Advent\]]{.class-0}[. Behold, the husbandman waiteth for the precious fruit of the earth, and hath long patience for it, until he receive the EARLY AND LATTER RAIN."]{.class-2}

[And look who shows up right in the same chapter---it's ]{.class-0}[Elijah]{.class-1}[.]{.class-0}

"[Elias was a man subject to like passions as we are, and he prayed earnestly that it might not rain: and it rained not on the earth by the space of three years and six months]{.class-2}[ \[cf. Rev. 11:3\]]{.class-0}[. And he prayed again, and the heaven gave rain, and the earth brought forth her fruit"]{.class-2}[ (James 5:17--18).]{.class-0}

[These rains show up over and over again in the Scriptures in connection to Christ's return; see Psalm 68:8--9, 72:6--8; 2 Samuel 23:4; Proverbs 16:15; Ezekiel 1:28; Song of Solomon 2:10--11; Judges 5:4; Hosea 6:1--3. Jesus said, ]{.class-0}["as it was in the days of Noe, so shall it be also in the days of the Son of man"]{.class-2}[ (Luke 17:26). What took place in Noah's day? It was a ]{.class-0}[deluge.]{.class-1}[ So in Zechariah 10:1, when Zechariah says ]{.class-0}["Ask ye of the LORD rain in the time of the latter rain,"]{.class-2}[ that's like ]{.class-0}["Pray for the peace of Jerusalem"]{.class-2}[: it's a prayer for Jesus Christ to come back.]{.class-0}

[Back to Zechariah 10---]{.class-0}["For the idols have spoken vainly ]{.class-2}[\[see the comments on Hab. 2:18--19\]]{.class-0}[, and the diviners ]{.class-2}[\[those are "seers," people who can see the future; as in John the "Divine"\]]{.class-0}[ have seen a lie, and have told false dreams ]{.class-2}[\[see the remarks on Jer. 23:21--36 under 9:1\]]{.class-0}[; they comfort in vain ]{.class-2}[\[see Jer. 6:14, 8:11\]]{.class-0}[: therefore they went their way as a flock, they were troubled, because there was no shepherd"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 2).]{.class-0}

[That last part is talking about a flock of sheep scattering when the shepherd's not there (Isa. 53:6; 1 Kings 22:17). It refers to the fact that Israel no longer had a King. The last King was ]{.class-0}["Zedekiah the son of Josiah"]{.class-2}[ (Jer. 37:1 cf. 1 Kings 24:17); there would be no more Kings until Jesus Christ showed up (see 9:9; Luke 1:32--33).]{.class-0}

[Jesus said, ]{.class-0}["I am the good shepherd"]{.class-2}[ (John 10:11, 14); He said He was ]{.class-0}["sent\...unto the lost sheep of the house of Israel"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 15:24). He's called ]{.class-0}["that great shepherd of the sheep"]{.class-2}[ (Heb. 13:20), ]{.class-0}["the Shepherd and Bishop of your souls"]{.class-2}[ (1 Pet. 2:25), and ]{.class-0}["the chief Shepherd"]{.class-2}[ (1 Pet. 5:4). Have you ever noticed how many types of Jesus Christ in the Old Testament were shepherds? Abel was a ]{.class-0}[shepherd.]{.class-1}[ Joseph was a ]{.class-0}[shepherd.]{.class-1}[ Judah was a ]{.class-0}[shepherd.]{.class-1}[ Moses was a ]{.class-0}[shepherd.]{.class-1}[ David was a ]{.class-0}[shepherd.]{.class-1}

[David said, ]{.class-0}["The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want"]{.class-2}[ (Psa. 23:1). So the picture in Zechariah 10:2 is ]{.class-0}[Israel]{.class-1}[ out of fellowship with God.]{.class-0}

[Verse 3: ]{.class-0}["Mine anger was kindled against the shepherds."]{.class-2}[ God was mad at the leaders of the nation. Look at Ezekiel 34.]{.class-0}

"[Son of man, prophesy against the shepherds of Israel\...Woe be to the shepherds of Israel that do feed themselves! should not the shepherds feed the flock? Ye eat the fat, and ye clothe you with the wool, ye kill them that are fed: but ye feed not the flock. The diseased have ye not strengthened, neither have ye healed that which was sick, neither have ye bound up that which was broken, neither have ye brought again that which was driven away, neither have ye sought that which was lost; but with force and with cruelty have ye ruled them. And they were scattered, because there is no shepherd\....As I live saith the Lord GOD, surely because my flock became a prey, and my flock became meat to every beast of the field, because there was no shepherd, neither did my shepherds search for my flock, but the shepherds fed themselves, and fed not my flock; Therefore, O ye shepherds, hear the word of the LORD; Thus saith the Lord GOD; Behold, I am against the shepherds; and I will require my flock at their hand, and cause them to cease from feeding the flock; neither shall the shepherds feed themselves any more; for I will deliver my flock from their mouth, that they may not be meat for them"]{.class-2}[ (Ezek. 34:2--4, 8--10).]{.class-0}

Those are the ones God put in charge of His people to care for them and protect them spiritually and physically. Instead they're "fleecing the flock" and making a good living off them, but they're not caring for them as they should. The "flock" is sick, bruised, broken, and wandering all over the place trying to get a decent meal.

[Now, the New Testament application should be clear. A pastor is a ]{.class-0}[shepherd]{.class-1}[ (cf. Jer. 23:1--2). They are a gift of God to the Church ]{.class-0}["for the perfecting of the saints, for the work of the ministry, for the edifying of the body of Christ"]{.class-2}[ (Eph. 4:11--12). They are to ]{.class-0}["feed the flock of God"]{.class-2}[ and be examples to them (1 Pet. 5:2--3). Instead, many pastors today (and I speak as the pastor of a local Baptist church who has started ]{.class-0}[three churches ]{.class-1}["from scratch") are building large works so they can live in big houses, drive fancy cars, and fly around in their own private jets. They are no longer ]{.class-0}[shepherds;]{.class-1}[ they are the CEOs (Chief Executive Officers) of big corporations. That's exactly about what Simon Peter warned you in 1 Peter 5:2--3, ]{.class-0}["Not for filthy lucre\...Neither as being lords over God's heritage." ]{.class-2}[For further study, see the comments on the passage in Volume I of the ]{.class-0}[Bible Believer's Commentary on the General Epistles.]{.class-1}
