[Back to Zechariah 10:3---]{.class-0}["and I punished the goats."]{.class-2}[ That's another picture of the leaders of the Jewish nation. Those ]{.class-0}["goats"]{.class-2}[ are ]{.class-0}["he goats"]{.class-2}[ used to ]{.class-0}[lead flocks ]{.class-1}[(Jer. 50:8). Slaughter houses use a black goat called a "Judas goat" to lead the sheep into the railroad cars and then out and into the slaughter house (cf. Jer. 51:40). The implication of Ezekiel 34:17 and Matthew 25:33 is that they are ]{.class-0}[lost.]{.class-1}

"[For the LORD of hosts hath visited his flock the house of Judah, and hath made them as his goodly horse in battle."]{.class-2}[ Notice once again how a punctuation mark (a colon here) marks over ]{.class-0}[2,000 years of history]{.class-1}[ (see the comments on 9:9--11). The ]{.class-0}[first]{.class-1}[ part of the verse would be God judging the Jews under Nebuchadnezzar (and the Romans as well in A.D. 70 and 135); the ]{.class-0}[second]{.class-1}[ half deals with the reestablishment of the nation as God's ]{.class-0}["armies"]{.class-2}[ (see the comments on 9:13).]{.class-0}

[Verse 4 is a reference to Jesus Christ as the conquering King at the Second Advent. He is ]{.class-0}["the head of the corner"]{.class-2}[ (see the remarks on Matt. 21:42 under 3:9), the ]{.class-0}["nail in a sure place"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 22:23), ]{.class-0}["the battle bow"]{.class-2}[ (see the comments on Hab. 3:9). That being the case, the ]{.class-0}["oppressor"]{.class-2}[ of verse 4 would not be the oppressors of the Jews; it is God using the Jews to oppress the heathen. Look at verse 5.]{.class-0}

"[And they ]{.class-2}[\[the immediate antecedent would be the ]{.class-0}["every oppressor"]{.class-2}[ of vs. 4 who come out of Judah\]]{.class-0}[ shall be as mighty men which tread down their enemies in the mire of the streets."]{.class-2}[ Those Jews are stomping all over their enemies just as Jesus Christ does (Isa. 63:1--3; Rev. 14:19--20).]{.class-0}

[Notice again the reference to ]{.class-0}["Emmanuel"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 1:23)---]{.class-0}["the LORD is with them." "With them" ]{.class-2}[how? Indwelling them in the person of the Spirit of Christ (Col. 1:27; Gal. 2:20; Rom. 8:11)? Nosiree! He's with them ]{.class-0}[in battle]{.class-1}[---]{.class-0}["they shall fight, because the LORD is with them."]{.class-2}[ It's Armageddon (see Zech. 12:4, 14:15; Rev. 14:20, 19:18).]{.class-0}

6 And I will strengthen the house of Judah, and I will save the house of Joseph, and I will bring them again to place them; for I have mercy upon them: and they shall be as though I had not cast them off: for I am the LORD their God, and will hear them.

7 And they of Ephraim shall be like a mighty man, and their heart shall rejoice as through wine: yea, their children shall see it, and be glad; their heart shall rejoice in the LORD.

8 I will hiss for them, and gather them; for I have redeemed them: and they shall increase as they have increased.

9 And I will sow them among the people: and they shall remember me in far countries; and they shall live with their children, and turn again.

10 I will bring them again also out of the land of Egypt, and gather them out of Assyria; and I will bring them into the land of Gilead and Lebanon; and place shall not be found for them.

11 And he shall pass through the sea with affliction, and shall smite the waves in the sea, and all the deeps of the river shall dry up: and the pride of Assyria shall be brought down, and the sceptre of Egypt shall depart away.

12 And I will strengthen them in the LORD; and they shall walk up and down in his name, saith the LORD.

[Verse 6 is another promise of the restoration of Israel as a nation. Notice again the two political kingdoms, ]{.class-0}["the house of Judah"]{.class-2}[ (the southern kingdom) and ]{.class-0}["the house of Joseph"]{.class-2}[ (that would be Ephraim---the northern kingdom of Israel; see the comments on 9:13), "placed" in the land with Jerusalem as their capital. They will be reunited in one nation, ]{.class-0}["and they shall be as though I had not cast them off."]{.class-2}

[The psalmist asks, ]{.class-0}["Will the Lord cast off for ever?\...Is his mercy clean gone for ever?"]{.class-2}[ (Psa. 77:7-8). The answer is, "No, ]{.class-0}[He]{.class-1}[ won't and ]{.class-0}[it's]{.class-1}[ not" (see Lam. 3:31-32). He may have ]{.class-0}["cast off"]{.class-2}[ His people for 1,900 years, but He never did ]{.class-0}["cast"]{.class-2}[ them ]{.class-0}["away"]{.class-2}[ (Rom. 11:1; Jer. 33:25--26). He has brought those Jews back to the land ]{.class-0}["the second time"]{.class-2}[ as He promised (Isa. 11:11), and despite all the efforts of the Clintons, Bush, and Obama, He will completely restore the nation as He said.]{.class-0}

For verse 7, see Habakkuk 3:18; Zephaniah 3:14; and Zechariah 2:10 and the comments on those passages.

For the gathering of verse 8, see the comments on 2:6.

"[And they shall increase as they have increased" ]{.class-2}[(vs. 8). Now we have already commented on this ]{.class-0}["increase"]{.class-2}[ under Zephaniah 3:18--20, but notice that there are ]{.class-0}[two]{.class-1}[ "increases" here: a future one in the Millennium and a past one in the Church Age. This explains a verse changed in ]{.class-0}[all]{.class-1}[ the new "Bibles," including the ]{.class-0}[New King James Version.]{.class-1}[ It's Isaiah 9:3, and it says:]{.class-0}

"[Thou hast multiplied the nation, and not increased the joy: they joy before thee according to the joy in harvest, and as men rejoice when they divide the spoil."]{.class-2}

[Every modern translation put out since 1880 took out the word ]{.class-0}["not"]{.class-2}[ even though it's in ]{.class-0}[every Hebrew manuscript extant.]{.class-1}[ (When these birds talk about "the original Greek and Hebrew," it doesn't mean a cotton-picking thing; they'll change either one whenever they want in order to make it ]{.class-0}[match their own stupidity.]{.class-1}[)]{.class-0}

[Taking the text as it stands, the way the Holy Spirit preserved it for us, what you have is another example of two Advents separated by a ]{.class-0}[punctuation]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-0}[mark;]{.class-1}[ in this case, a ]{.class-0}[colon]{.class-1}[ (see the comments on 9:9--11). The first part of the verse has to do with the increase of the nation in the Church Age and the Tribulation. Look at how the ]{.class-0}[Scripture]{.class-1}[ defines the ]{.class-0}[Scripture]{.class-1}[ in Isaiah 26:15--16 without any reference to ]{.class-0}[any]{.class-1}[ Hebrew manuscript or Hebrew text or "conjectural emendation" (scholarly ]{.class-0}[guesswork]{.class-1}[).]{.class-0}

"[Thou hast increased the nation, O LORD, thou has increased the nation\...."]{.class-2}[ When? ]{.class-0}["thou has REMOVED IT FAR UNTO ALL THE ENDS OF THE EARTH\...."]{.class-2}[ Why, that's James 1:1 in the Church Age---]{.class-0}["to the twelve tribes which are scattered abroad."]{.class-2}[ That's the Dispersion of John 7:35.]{.class-0}

"[\...LORD, in trouble have they visited thee, they poured out a prayer when thy chastening was upon them."]{.class-2}[ That's ]{.class-0}["the time of Jacob's trouble"]{.class-2}[ (Jer. 30:7)---]{.class-0}[the Tribulation]{.class-1}[. So in the Church Age and the Tribulation, God increases the ]{.class-0}[nation]{.class-1}[ without increasing its ]{.class-0}[joy,]{.class-1}[ and then---colon---]{.class-0}["they joy before thee according to the joy in harvest]{.class-2}[ \[the Millennium\]]{.class-0}[, and as men rejoice when they divide the spoil ]{.class-2}[\[Second Advent\]]{.class-0}[."]{.class-2}

[In Zechariah 10:8, it's: ]{.class-0}["they shall increase]{.class-2}[ \[Millennium\]]{.class-0}[ as they have increased ]{.class-2}[\[Church Age and Tribulation\]]{.class-0}[."]{.class-2}[ This time, though, the Lord gathers them from the nations and returns them to the land, and the increase is accompanied by ]{.class-0}[joy]{.class-1}[ and doesn't stop.]{.class-0}

[Isaiah 9:6--7, ]{.class-0}["For unto us a child is born, unto us a son is given: and the government shall be upon his shoulder: and his name shall be called Wonderful, Counsellor, The mighty God, The everlasting Father, The Prince of Peace. Of the INCREASE of his government and peace THERE SHALL BE NO END."]{.class-2}

[Those Jews increase in the Millennium until they not only fill the land of Palestine (including all the "West Bank"), but ]{.class-0}[Lebanon]{.class-1}[ (vs. 10), the nation of ]{.class-0}["Jordan"]{.class-1}[ (]{.class-0}["Gilead,"]{.class-2}[ vs. 10; the inheritance of Reuben, Gad, and the half tribe of Manasseh on the "East Bank" of the Jordan River), the rest of the ]{.class-0}[Abrahamic land grant ]{.class-1}[of Genesis 15:18 (the Nile Delta in Egypt, the Sinaitic Peninsula, northern Saudi Arabia, all of Syria, and half of Iraq), and, eventually (beyond the Great White Throne into Eternity), ]{.class-0}[the whole earth]{.class-1}[ (Rom. 4:13). How's that for "Zionism"?]{.class-0}

[We have already commented on the regathering of the Jews from Assyria and Egypt in 8:7 (which see). Here we will only make two further observations. First, Egypt is a picture of the world, which is why it figures into Israel's history in such a prominent way. Of all the nations mentioned in the Bible, Edom shows up 100 times, Ammon 125 times, Syria 150 times, Moab 175 times, Assyria 200 times, and Babylon 300 times. At the top of the list, of course, is Israel with 2,400 times, but in second place---more than Assyria and Babylon combined---is ]{.class-0}[Egypt]{.class-1}[ with 725 times.]{.class-0}

[In the Bible, Egypt is a picture of ]{.class-0}["this present evil world"]{.class-2}[ (Gal. 1:4), and its King (Pharaoh) is a picture of ]{.class-0}["the prince of this world,"]{.class-2}[ the Devil (John 16:11; also see Ezek. 29:3, 32:2 cf. Rev. 12:9; 1 Pet. 5:8). That Bible says, ]{.class-0}["Out of Egypt have I called my son"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 2:15). That was not only a reference to Jesus Christ (see Matt. 2:14), but to the exodus of the children of Israel (Hos. 11:1). Joseph wouldn't allow his father to be buried in Egypt, and he commanded his own bones to be taken out of Egypt (Gen. 50). That's a lesson for the child of God that he isn't to be ]{.class-0}["conformed"]{.class-2}[ to his "Egypt" (Rom. 12:1) because he has been called out of it ]{.class-0}[spiritually]{.class-1}[ (John 15:19; 2 Cor. 6:17), and one day he will be taken out of it ]{.class-0}[physically]{.class-1}[ (Rev. 4:1; 1 Thess. 4:16--18).]{.class-0}

[God has so much against Egypt that He didn't want those Jews going back down there for anything (Deut. 17:16). In the book of Jeremiah, when that remnant that was left in the land after Nebuchadnezzar destroyed Jerusalem disobeyed God and went back to Egypt, the Lord told them, ]{.class-0}["my name shall no more be named in the mouth of any man of Judah in all the land of Egypt"]{.class-2}[ (Jer. 44:26). ]{.class-0}[God said He wasn't going to fool with any Jew in Egypt.]{.class-1}

[Do you know what comes from those Jews in Egypt? They produce the ]{.class-0}[Apocrypha]{.class-1}[, extra-canonical trash that the Catholics picked up and added to your Bible so they could get Purgatory, indulgences, and prayers for the dead (Ecclesiasticus 3:20; 2 Maccabees 12:43--45; Ecclesiasticus 3:3; Tobit 6:17, 12:9; Wisdom of Solomon 8:19--20; 2 Esdras 14:5--6, 45--48). The ]{.class-0}[Septuagint]{.class-1}[ (the Old Testament in Greek) was supposedly translated in Egypt by 72 Hebrew scholars; ]{.class-0}[Vaticanus, Sinaiticus,]{.class-1}[ and the Alexandrian text come out of Egypt. ]{.class-0}[All those are where the new "Bibles" come from. ]{.class-1}[You can take the whole lot and throw them in the dumpster. The last place you want to go for a ]{.class-0}[Bible manuscript]{.class-1}[ is Egypt, because ]{.class-0}[God said His name wouldn't be mentioned there anymore by any Jew.]{.class-1}

[Now, the second thing you want to notice about Egypt and Assyria is in verse 11: ]{.class-0}["the pride of Assyria shall be brought down, and the sceptre of Egypt shall depart away."]{.class-2}[ That's a reference to the defeat of the Antichrist at the Second Advent. Both the Kings of Assyria and Egypt were types of the Antichrist. That Assyrian is Sennacherib (2 Kings 18), one of the greatest types of Antichrist in the Bible. ]{.class-0}["The Assyrian"]{.class-2}[ is a prophetic reference to the ]{.class-0}[Antichrist]{.class-1}[ in Isaiah 10:5 and Micah 5:2--6; the same is true of ]{.class-0}[Pharaoh]{.class-1}[ in Ezekiel chapters 29--32 (notice especially how Pharaoh is likened to ]{.class-0}["the Assyrian"]{.class-2}[ in Ezek. 31:3).]{.class-0}

[Zechariah 10:11 is a Second Advent verse. That ]{.class-0}["sea"]{.class-2}[ is the Great Deeps of Habakkuk 3:8--15 (see the comments on that passage). ]{.class-0}["All the deeps of the river shall dry up"]{.class-2}[---that's a reference to God drying up the Euphrates River before the Battle of Armageddon. Look at two passages in Revelation---Revelation 9:14--16 and Revelation 16:12, 16.]{.class-0}

"[Saying to the sixth angel which had the trumpet, Loose the four angels which are bound in the great river Euphrates. And the four angels were loosed, which were prepared for an hour, and a day, and a month, and a year, for to slay the third part of men. And the number of the army of the horsemen were two hundred thousand thousand: and I heard the number of them"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 9:14--16).]{.class-0}

"[And the sixth angel poured out his vial upon the great river Euphrates; and the water thereof was dried up, that the way of the kings of the east might be prepared\....And he gathered them together into a place called in the Hebrew tongue Armageddon"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 16:12, 16).]{.class-0}

For the Scriptural commentary on Zechariah 10:12, see Micah 4:5--7.

[]{#section-0_1_1b0e3e528ba6acd0_b697.xhtml}

CHAPTER ELEVEN

11:1 Open thy doors, O Lebanon, that the fire may devour thy cedars.

2 Howl, fir tree; for the cedar is fallen; because the mighty are spoiled: howl, O ye oaks of Bashan; for the forest of the vintage is come down.

[Now, as I have told you on several occasions, there are many things in Zechariah that I do not understand. When I absolutely don't know, my standard answer is the German response ]{.class-0}["Ich weiss nicht"]{.class-1}[ ("I know not"; Americans say, "I don't know"). Sometimes I can give you the cross-references with which to work, but I still haven't got the thing figured out. That is going to be the case here in Zechariah 11. I can give you ]{.class-0}[generally]{.class-1}[ about what the passage is talking, and I can give you the cross-references, but there are a lot of ]{.class-0}[specifics]{.class-1}[ on which I draw a blank.]{.class-0}

[Take that first verse: ]{.class-0}["Open thy doors, O Lebanon."]{.class-2}[ Can any of you preachers get a good sermon on that? I don't get anything out of it. ]{.class-0}["Lebanon"]{.class-2}[ is a country. Does it even have ]{.class-0}["doors"]{.class-2}[? Where are they? How could ]{.class-0}["Lebanon" "open"]{.class-2}[ its ]{.class-0}["doors"]{.class-2}[ if it had any?]{.class-0}

[Now what the commentators and expositors say is this: verses 1--3 are talking about the destruction of Israel and Jerusalem. They get that from Isaiah 2:12--13, 10:32--34, 33:8--14; Jeremiah 22:6--7, 20--23; Ezekiel 17:1--12; and Nahum 1:4. In each of those passages, the nation of Israel is pictured as trees in Lebanon, Bashan (or Gilead---both are on the east side of Jordan), Carmel, and Sharon that are being burned or chopped down. If that is the case, it wouldn't be the destruction of Jerusalem under Nebuchadnezzar because that was over eighty years in the ]{.class-0}[past]{.class-1}[. Zechariah would be prophesying of a ]{.class-0}[future]{.class-1}[ destruction of Jerusalem (see Zech. 14:1--2), and as we shall see from the context, it will be the destruction of the city by the Antichrist (see vs. 17).]{.class-0}

[Verse 2: ]{.class-0}["Howl, FIR TREE; for the CEDAR is fallen; because the mighty are spoiled: howl, O ye OAKS of Bashan; for the FOREST of the vintage is come down."]{.class-2}[ He's preaching to ]{.class-0}[trees.]{.class-1}

[Now I have done that. When I was first learning how to preach, I got out in a woods and preached to the ]{.class-0}[trees]{.class-1}[ to practice my delivery. I have preached to a herd of ]{.class-0}[goats]{.class-1}[. You talk about a congregation, boy, that was one. I would tell them "Repent!" and they would look up at me and go, "Baaa!"]{.class-0}

[In the Scriptures, ]{.class-0}[trees]{.class-1}[ are likened to men. That's all through the Bible. In Judges 9, Gideon's son gets up and preaches to the men that killed his brothers and likened them to trees looking for a King. When Jesus healed the blind man in Mark 8, the first thing that fellow saw was ]{.class-0}["men as trees, walking"]{.class-2}[ (Mark 8:24). In Psalm 37:35, David says ]{.class-0}["the wicked"]{.class-2}[ are ]{.class-0}["like a green bay tree,"]{.class-2}[ and Psalm 92:12 says, ]{.class-0}["the righteous shall flourish like the palm tree: he shall grow like a cedar in Lebanon."]{.class-2}[ The Antichrist is likened to a tree.]{.class-0}

"[Behold, the Assyrian ]{.class-2}[\[see the comments on 10:11\]]{.class-0}[ was a cedar in Lebanon with fair branches, and with a shadowing shroud, and of an high stature; and his top was among the thick boughs\....Therefore his height was exalted above all the trees of the field, and his boughs were multiplied, and his branches became long because of the multitude of waters when he shot forth. All the fowls of heaven made their nests in his boughs, and under his branches did all the beasts of the field bring forth their young, and under his shadow dwelt all great nations"]{.class-2}[ (Ezek. 31:3--6).]{.class-0}

[Do you know what you're reading there? You're reading Daniel 4:10--22; in that passage, ]{.class-0}[Nebuchadnezzar was the tree.]{.class-1}[ In Ezekiel 31, the tree is not only ]{.class-0}["the Assyrian,"]{.class-2}[ it's also ]{.class-0}["Pharaoh"]{.class-1}[ (Ezek. 31:2, 18). All three of those Kings picture the ]{.class-0}["son of perdition."]{.class-2}[ Look back at Ezekiel 31:16--17.]{.class-0}

"[I made the nations to shake at the sound of his fall, when I cast him down to hell with them that descend into the pit: and all the trees of Eden, the choice and best of Lebanon, all that drink water, shall be comforted in the nether parts of the earth ]{.class-2}[\[men likened to trees in the Garden of Eden going to Hell\]]{.class-0}[. They also went down into hell with him unto them that be slain with the sword; and they that were his arm, that dwelt under his shadow in the midst of the heathen."]{.class-2}

[Who's that? Why, ]{.class-0}[that's Satan incarnate]{.class-1}[ (Isa. 14:12--19).]{.class-0}

[So what's going on in Zechariah 11:1--2 is the big shots (]{.class-0}["the mighty,"]{.class-2}[ vs. 2) connected with the Antichrist have taken over the Promised Land during the Tribulation, and when the Lord comes back, they get burned up (see the comments on Hab. 3:5).]{.class-0}

3 There is a voice of the howling of the shepherds; for their glory is spoiled: a voice of the roaring of young lions; for the pride of Jordan is spoiled.

4 Thus saith the LORD my God; Feed the flock of the slaughter;

5 Whose possessors slay them, and hold themselves not guilty: and they that sell them say, Blessed be the LORD; for I am rich: and their own shepherds pity them not.

6 For I will no more pity the inhabitants of the land, saith the LORD: but, lo, I will deliver the men every one into his neighbour's hand, and into the hand of his king: and they shall smite the land, and out of their hand I will not deliver them.

7 And I will feed the flock of slaughter, even you, O poor of the flock. And I took unto me two staves; the one I called Beauty, and the other I called Bands; and I fed the flock.

8 Three shepherds also I cut off in one month; and my soul lothed them, and their soul also abhorred me.

9 Then said I, I will not feed you: that that dieth, let it die; and that that is to be cut off, let it be cut off; and let the rest eat every one the flesh of another.

[Now, ]{.class-0}["the shepherds"]{.class-2}[ of verses 3-5 could be the leaders of Israel who messed up the people (see the comments on 10:2--3), or they could be ]{.class-0}["three kings"]{.class-2}[ subdued by the Antichrist. Look at verse 8 and compare it with Daniel 7:24.]{.class-0}

"[Three shepherds also I cut off in one month; and my soul lothed them, and their soul also abhorred me"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 8). That's talking about three leaders that loathe the Lord, and the Lord loathes them. That's because they are connected with the Antichrist, and the Lord ]{.class-0}[uses]{.class-1}[ the Antichrist to get rid of them.]{.class-0}

[Daniel 7:23--24, ]{.class-0}["Thus he said, The fourth beast shall be the fourth kingdom upon earth, which shall be diverse from all kingdoms, and shall devour the whole earth, and shall tread it down, and break it in pieces ]{.class-2}[\[that's the last kingdom on this earth before Jesus Christ comes back to take over;]{.class-0}[ it's the kingdom of the Antichrist]{.class-1}[---Dan. 7:25--27\]]{.class-0}[. And the ten horns out of this kingdom are ten kings that shall arise: and another shall rise after them ]{.class-2}[\[Rev. 13:1, 17:12\]]{.class-0}[: and he shall be diverse from the first, and he shall subdue three kings."]{.class-2}

[Now why connect those ]{.class-0}["three shepherds"]{.class-2}[ with the Antichrist? Well, in verse 6, the Lord is having no pity on ]{.class-0}["the inhabitants of the land,]{.class-2}[ and He's delivering them to the Antichrist (]{.class-0}["into the hand of his king"]{.class-2}[) to smite them; but in verse 7, He's feeding the ]{.class-0}["poor of the flock."]{.class-2}[ In the Tribulation, the ]{.class-0}["poor"]{.class-2}[ are poor because they won't take the Mark of the Beast, so they can't buy or sell (see the comments on Hab. 2:9--10). The Lord has to feed them in the wilderness with manna just like He did the children of Israel in their wilderness wanderings (see Mic. 7:14--15; Psa. 78:2, 24--25 and the comments on those passages in their respective commentaries).]{.class-0}

[In verse 9, the Lord says, ]{.class-0}["let the rest eat every one the flesh of another"]{.class-2}[: cannibalism (see Deut. 28:50--57; Lam. 4:9--10)! That's what goes on in the Tribulation (Isa. 6:13; Psa. 16:4). And look who shows up in verse 17: it's the Son of Perdition (]{.class-0}["the idol shepherd"]{.class-2}[). So there is good evidence in the context for placing verses 3--9 in the Tribulation.]{.class-0}

10 And I took my staff, even Beauty, and cut it asunder, that I might break my covenant which I had made with all the people.

11 And it was broken in that day: and so the poor of the flock that waited upon me knew that it was the word of the LORD.

12 And I said unto them, If ye think good, give me my price; and if not, forbear. So they weighed for my price thirty pieces of silver.

13 And the LORD said unto me, Cast it unto the potter: a goodly price that I was prised at of them. And I took the thirty pieces of silver, and cast them to the potter in the house of the LORD.

14 Then I cut asunder mine other staff, even Bands, that I might break the brotherhood between Judah and Israel.

Now I have saved comment on the last part of verse 7 till here because the passage before us deals with the subject matter of that verse. So let's go back and pick up verse 7.

"[And I took unto me two staves; the one I called Beauty, and the other I called Bands."]{.class-2}[ The Lord breaks those ]{.class-0}["two staves"]{.class-2}[ in verses 10 and 14, and that symbolizes the breaking of the covenant He made with the Jews (vs. 10---]{.class-0}["Beauty"]{.class-2}[) and the breaking of the ]{.class-0}["brotherhood between Judah and Israel" ]{.class-2}[(vs. 14---]{.class-0}["Bands"]{.class-2}[).]{.class-0}

[Now that describes the condition of Israel right now ]{.class-0}[in the Church Age]{.class-1}[. The ]{.class-0}["covenant"]{.class-2}[ that was broken is the covenant of Exodus 19:5--6; it is "keep the Ten Commandments, and you get to dwell in the land I promised to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob" (Deut. 5). It was a ]{.class-0}[conditional]{.class-1}[ covenant which is called the "Palestinian covenant" by Scofield and the Dispensationalists, and it is ]{.class-0}[conditioned]{.class-1}[ on the keeping of the Mosaic Law. The Lord broke it with Israel because Israel broke it first (Ezek. 16:59; Jer. 31:32). Although the Lord returned the Jews to the land temporarily in Ezra and Nehemiah, He threw them out again in A.D. 70 and A.D. 135 for rejecting "the Prophet like unto Moses" (Deut. 18:18--19). Right now, the Jews have been brought back to the land in unbelief, but the second gathering of the Jews has begun (see Isa. 11:11) and will continue on through the Tribulation and into the Millennium (see the comments under Zeph. 3:18--20). A new, everlasting covenant is made with them at the Second Advent (when they are converted---Zech. 12; Rom. 11) that is ]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ conditional (Jer. 31:31--37; Ezek. 16:60--62).]{.class-0}

[Notice in Jeremiah 31:31 that that new covenant is made with two ]{.class-0}[political]{.class-1}[ entities: ]{.class-0}["the house of Israel"]{.class-2}[ (the ten tribes of the northern kingdom) and ]{.class-0}["the house of Judah"]{.class-2}[ (the two tribes of the southern kingdom). The ]{.class-0}["brotherhood between"]{.class-2}[ them has been busted up since 1 Kings 12, and it hasn't been restored yet. Israel has been restored as a nation to the land (1948) but without]{.class-0}[ its tribal distinctions.]{.class-1}[ When the Lord comes back, He reunites the two "houses," tribes and all, into one single Kingdom again with Him as the King. Look at Ezekiel 37, starting with verse 15, and you're finally going to see the identity of Zechariah's ]{.class-0}["two staves."]{.class-2}

"[The word of the LORD came again unto me, saying, Moreover, thou son of man, take thee one stick, and write upon it. For Judah, and for the children of Israel his companions: then take another stick, and write upon it. For Joseph, the stick of Ephraim, and for all the house of Israel his companions]{.class-2}[ \[there's those ]{.class-0}["two staves"]{.class-2}[ from Zech. 11:7; one's a staff for Judah, and the other's a staff for Israel\]]{.class-0}[: And join them one to another into one stick; and they shall become one in thine hand. And when the children of thy people shall speak unto thee, saying, Wilt thou not shew us what thou meanest by these? Say unto them, Thus saith the Lord GOD; Behold, I will take the stick of Joseph, which is in the hand of Ephraim, and the tribes of Israel his fellows, and will put them with him, even with the stick of Judah, and make them one stick, and they shall be one in mine hand. And the sticks whereon thou writest shall be in thine hand before their eyes. And say unto them, Thus saith the Lord GOD; Behold, I will take the children of Israel from among the heathen, whither they be gone, and will gather them on every side, and bring them into their own land ]{.class-2}[\[see the Scriptures we gave you under the remarks on Zeph. 3:18--20\]]{.class-0}[: And I will make them one nation in the land upon the mountains of Israel; and one king shall be king to them all: and they shall be no more two nations, neither shall they be divided into two kingdoms any more at all"]{.class-2}[ (Ezek. 37:15--22).]{.class-0}

[Notice the reference to ]{.class-0}["the tribes"]{.class-2}[ in Ezekiel 37:19. The average Jew today can't give you his tribe because those records were lost when Titus destroyed the Temple in A.D. 70. The British Israelites teach that the ten northern tribes were "lost" when the Assyrians carried them away into captivity, but they are not lost to God (see Luke 2:36, for example). In Revelation 7:4--8, ]{.class-0}[the Lord identifies all twelve tribes,]{.class-1}[ and in the Millennium, they are given specific tracks of land to inherit (Ezek. 48).]{.class-0}

Now, right in the middle of all this stuff on the Second Advent, look at what shows up in verses 12--13.

"[And I said unto them, If ye think good, give me my price; and if not, forbear. So they weighed for my price thirty pieces of silver. And the LORD said unto me, Cast it unto the potter: a goodly price that I was prised at of them. And I took the thirty pieces of silver, and cast them to the potter in the house of the LORD."]{.class-2}

If you are familiar with the New Testament and the Gospels, you know what you're reading: you are reading about Judas betraying Christ and then going out and hanging himself (Matt. 27:3--10). It's First Advent (see the comments on 9:9--11).

[Now, we have several observations to make on this passage, so we'll start with the New Testament quotation in Matthew 27:1--10. According to the apostate critics of the Holy Bible, Matthew made the unforgiveable ]{.class-0}["faux pas"]{.class-1}[ of ascribing the quote to ]{.class-0}[Jeremiah.]{.class-1}[ The asinine explanation given by E. Schuyler English and the Board of Editors for the ]{.class-0}[New Scofield Reference Bible]{.class-1}[ was that Matthew made the mistake of citing the first name he saw on a scroll of the prophets that began with Jeremiah.]{.class-0}

[The Minor Prophets aren't included on any scroll with the Major Prophets. All twelve books are a single book by themselves in the Hebrew canon (see the Preface to Vol. I of this Commentary); they wouldn't be on the same scroll as Jeremiah. By that logic, Matthew should have cited ]{.class-0}[Hosea]{.class-1}[ as the source of the quote.]{.class-0}

[Do you know what the ]{.class-0}[proof]{.class-1}[ was for the ]{.class-0}[NSRB]{.class-1}[ Board of Editors making such a ridiculous statement as that? It was TALMUDIC TRADITION (footnote 2, pg. 1041, ]{.class-0}[NSRB,]{.class-1}[ 1967). ]{.class-0}[That's]{.class-1}[ what they accepted over the authority of God Almighty, despite the warnings given them by ]{.class-0}[Paul]{.class-1}[ (Col. 2:8) and ]{.class-0}[Jesus Christ]{.class-1}[ (Mark 7:9--13).]{.class-0}

[The Holy Spirit told you in Zechariah 7:7 that the prophecy of Zechariah was connected to ]{.class-0}["the words which the LORD hath cried by the former prophets"]{.class-2}[; that's why ]{.class-0}["The BRANCH"]{.class-2}[ shows up in Zechariah 6:12 (see the comments on that verse). It is nothing unusual in the prophets for ]{.class-0}[God to give the exact same material to two different prophets ]{.class-1}[(Isa. 2:2--4 cf. Mic. 4:1--3). The obvious explanation in Matthew 27:9--10 (which is so simple that only a D.D. \["Dumb Dog"\] could miss it) is that Matthew is quoting something which Jeremiah ]{.class-0}[said]{.class-1}[ but was not allowed by the Holy Spirit to commit to ]{.class-0}[writing]{.class-1}[ because that privilege was reserved for Zechariah. You can see a similar thing in Jude 14 (see the remarks on Deut. 33:2 under Hab. 3:3). As the ORIGINAL ]{.class-0}[Scofield Reference Bible]{.class-1}[ points out, Jeremiah does indeed allude to what Zechariah wrote in Jeremiah 18:1--4, 19:1--3.]{.class-0}

[Now look at what Zechariah wrote in verse 13:]{.class-0}[ "And the LORD said unto me, Cast unto the potter: a goodly price that ]{.class-2}[I]{.class-16}[ was prised at of them. And ]{.class-2}[I]{.class-16}[ took the thirty pieces of silver, and cast them to the potter in the house of the LORD."]{.class-2}[ In that verse, the one who was priced at ]{.class-0}["thirty pieces of silver"]{.class-2}[ is the one doing the casting. Well, Jesus Christ wasn't the one who cast the silver down in the Temple;]{.class-0}[ that was Judas.]{.class-1}[ But what you have here is another example of the Lord working by proxy through Satan (see Job 1--2; also 2 Sam. 24:1 cf. 1 Chron. 21:1).]{.class-0}

[Have you ever stopped to wonder what Judas might have thought if he read his Bible? Did Judas ever read Zechariah 11:13? We don't know one way or the other, but if he did, it should have given him reason to pause and think about what he was doing. After all, he had a free will; ]{.class-0}[he didn't have to betray Christ. ]{.class-1}[I mean, the Scriptures would have been fulfilled anyway, but Judas ]{.class-0}[didn't have to be ]{.class-1}[the one to fulfill them by selling out the Lord (Matt. 18:7).]{.class-0}

[That's the kind of thing that drives a Calvinist up a wall. A Calvinist reasons that if God has foreknowledge about a thing, ]{.class-0}[then the thing can't take place any other way;]{.class-1}[ otherwise, God would be wrong in His foreknowledge. But you see, God's foreknowledge covers not only what ]{.class-0}[will]{.class-1}[ happen, but what ]{.class-0}[could]{.class-1}[ happen.]{.class-0}

[One time David was holed up in a certain town running from Saul, and Saul heard he was there and was coming to get him. David asked the Lord, "Will the men of this city deliver me up to Saul?" God told David, "Yeah, they will." But ]{.class-0}[they didn't.]{.class-1}[ Do you know ]{.class-0}[why?]{.class-1}[ Because David, knowing the foreknowledge of God, ]{.class-0}[got out of there.]{.class-1}[ You can read the whole account in 1 Samuel 23:7--13.]{.class-0}

[Do you know what I would do if I ]{.class-0}[knew]{.class-1}[ I was under the "eternal decree of reprobation"? ]{.class-0}[I'd change the decree.]{.class-1}[ You say, "How?" By coming to Jesus Christ in faith! He said, ]{.class-0}["him that cometh to me I will in no wise cast out"]{.class-2}[ (John 6:37). I would get to Jesus Christ just as quick as I could get, and if I did that, He promised He wouldn't cast me out, and the "eternal decree" would be ]{.class-0}[changed]{.class-1}[.]{.class-0}

When I was getting my Master's Degree at Bob Jones University, the professors examining me asked, "Are you a Calvinist or an Arminian?" I told them, "I'm an Arminian until I get to Calvary, and after that, I'm a Calvinist." They said, "That's not a very good answer," and I said, "That wasn't a very good question."

[The reason I gave the answer I did was because before you get to Calvary, things are flexible. You are a sinner on your way to Hell ]{.class-0}[unless]{.class-1}[ you receive Christ as your Saviour, but once you accept Christ, ]{.class-0}[the thing is fixed.]{.class-1}[ You are predestinated ]{.class-0}["to be conformed to the image of his Son"]{.class-2}[ (Rom. 8:29). Calvin got it all wrong. He had predestination occurring before Genesis 1:1; ]{.class-0}[you aren't predestinated until you get saved.]{.class-1}[ My answer wasn't as dumb as those three Ph.D.'s thought it was. Those were three graduates from Princeton, Harvard, and Edinburgh (one of whom could speak, read, and write eight different languages) ]{.class-0}[who didn't have the sense God gave a brass monkey.]{.class-1}

Judas had the whole thing laid out for him where he could get it, and he went ahead with it and betrayed Christ anyway. But that's the way the Devil is. Brethren, Satan knows that Book better than anyone outside the Trinity itself. He's read what's in store for him, and he goes right on. If I were the Devil and knew what he knows, I think I'd go hide out on the back side of Mars or Jupiter somewhere and not raise my head up. It wouldn't do any good, I don't guess; I mean, the Scriptures are going to be fulfilled. But he's not going to try that; he'll just keep going on till he drives the train off the cliff and straight into the Lake of Fire.

[Judas betrayed Jesus Christ for what was at one time (1950) \$17.00 in American money. I'm sure with the price of gold and silver skyrocketing as it has that amount has gone up, but it still wouldn't be worth a great deal. In the Old Testament, ]{.class-0}["thirty pieces of silver"]{.class-2}[ was the recompense for an ox goring a slave (Exod. 21:32); it was the redemption price of a ]{.class-0}[woman]{.class-1}[ at the Tabernacle or the Temple (Lev. 27:4). That's the value those chief priests and Pharisees placed on ]{.class-0}[Jesus Christ: He was worth the price of a female slave.]{.class-1}

[Now verse 12 is what is known as a "vote of confidence" in a church split. When things get really bad in a church, the pastor may get up and say, "I'm not going to pastor this mess anymore if you're not with me. I want to know how many of you are ]{.class-0}[for]{.class-1}[ me and how many of you are ]{.class-0}[against]{.class-1}[ me. If you're for me, stand up."]{.class-0}

[That's a "vote of confidence." Those things are always a howl. You'll see people politicking ]{.class-0}[while you're taking the vote.]{.class-1}[ A guy will stand up, and his wife will try to pull him down. Another guy will sit there, and his wife is standing, saying, "Stand up! Stand up! Stand up!" (He says, "Yes, ma'am," and he stands up.)]{.class-0}

[After my first wife deserted me, I lived as a single father for ]{.class-0}[twelve years]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[pastored]{.class-1}[ Brent Baptist Church. When I decided to get remarried, I knew there would be some folks in the congregation who wouldn't like it, so I took a vote of confidence to see where I stood. I had about two-thirds of the people with me, which meant I would have to fight (or lose) about ]{.class-0}[a third]{.class-1}[ of the congregation. I resigned even though I didn't have to. I could have stayed and fought, and it wouldn't have bothered me a bit. I have fought all my life. But it's such a waste of time.]{.class-0}

[I'll give a word of advice to anyone in the ministry or going into the ministry. If you can't get 90% of the people to vote with you, if I were you, I ]{.class-0}[wouldn't take or keep the work. ]{.class-1}[The ones who are against you will spend their whole lives---I'm not just talking here or exaggerating; I mean ]{.class-0}[literally]{.class-1}[ 25--40 years---staying just as close to your church as they can so they can ]{.class-0}[work on your members]{.class-1}[ and get them disgruntled so they'll leave ]{.class-0}[with them]{.class-1}[. I haven't seen that just once or twice, or even ten or fifteen times; I've seen it over and over and over again.]{.class-0}

[One of my graduates named Carl Baker took a church one time over near Wing, Alabama. They had just lost a pastor and got Carl in there. He hadn't been there more than a couple of weeks when he saw what he was up against. ]{.class-0}[The song leader was running things. ]{.class-1}[He would have a big gospel concert for three hours on a Sunday afternoon and get 400 people coming out to a church that would only seat about 200 people, and there would only be ]{.class-0}[fifty]{.class-1}[ coming out to hear Bro. Baker teach and preach on a Sunday morning, and only ]{.class-0}[ten]{.class-1}[ at prayer meeting on Wednesday night.]{.class-0}

It got worse and worse until Carl came to me and said, "I don't know what to do. The guy is running the church. He has to have all kinds of junk in there, and he's turned the people against me."

"[Well, maybe you ought to resign," I said.]{.class-0}

He said, "I thought about it, but I didn't know if it was the right thing to do or not."

"[Give them a vote of confidence," I told him. "Just put it straight to them and tell them you can't work with the fellow anymore."]{.class-0}

[So the next Sunday morning, Carl Baker got up and told his people, "This fellow is running the church, and he's not the pastor. If you want]{.class-0}[ him, ]{.class-1}[you can have him, and if you want ]{.class-0}[me]{.class-1}[, you can keep me." Then he went ahead and took the vote.]{.class-0}

[You say, "What happened?" ]{.class-0}[He resigned the church. ]{.class-1}[The ones who were against him did like a lot of Southern Baptists do in a situation like that: they brought in about a hundred members who were on the roll but who hadn't attended church in twenty years. That song leader had about 150 for him, and poor, old Carl ended up with twenty, I think.]{.class-0}

Carl Baker ended up leaving that church and taking one near Camp Lejeune, the Marine base in South Carolina. Today, he's got a fine work going there with a bunch of Bible-believing street preachers he's trained. He has been there now for nearly thirty years. So it isn't a calamity if you lose a church.

15 And the LORD said unto me, Take unto thee yet the instruments of a foolish shepherd.

16 For, lo, I will raise up a shepherd in the land, which shall not visit those that be cut off, neither shall seek the young one, nor heal that that is broken, nor feed that that standeth still: but he shall eat the flesh of the fat, and tear their claws in pieces.

17 Woe to the idol shepherd that leaveth the flock! the sword shall be upon his arm, and upon his right eye: his arm shall be clean dried up, and his right eye shall be utterly darkened.

[The ]{.class-0}[paragraph mark]{.class-1}[ at verse 15 marks a 1,900 year break from the previous verses (see the comments on 9:9--11). You go from the First Advent and the Church Age in verses 10--14 to the Tribulation in verses 15--17. In verses 12--13, the Jews sold out the Good Shepherd, so the Lord sends them ]{.class-0}["a foolish shepherd.]{.class-2}[ Why does God do that? That's what they said they wanted.]{.class-0}

[John 19:15, ]{.class-0}["But they cried out, Away with him, away with him, crucify him. Pilate saith unto them, Shall I crucify your King? The chief priests answered, WE HAVE NO KING BUT CAESAR."]{.class-2}

[So for 1,900 years the Lord gave them Roman shepherds who exiled them, confiscated their property, tortured them, and tried to exterminate them (Adolf Hitler and the commandants of all the concentration camps were all Roman Catholics). The difference between all those "shepherds" and the one that's going to show up in the near future is that the ]{.class-0}["shepherd"]{.class-2}[ in the passage before us will be ]{.class-0}["in the land"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 6).]{.class-0}

[The Antichrist will be partly Semitic: a ]{.class-0}[half Jew]{.class-1}[ or a ]{.class-0}[third Jew.]{.class-1}[ That Beast over in Revelation 13:2 is ]{.class-0}["a leopard"]{.class-2}[! A leopard is a ]{.class-0}[yellow]{.class-1}[ cat (Shem), with ]{.class-0}[black]{.class-1}[ spots (Ham), and a ]{.class-0}[white]{.class-1}[ belly (Japheth). He's the perfect integrated mulatto (like Obama). In Ezekiel 21:25, he's called the ]{.class-0}["profane wicked prince of Israel."]{.class-2}[ Daniel says he won't ]{.class-0}["regard the God of his fathers"]{.class-2}[ and that he would honor ]{.class-0}["a god whom his fathers knew not"]{.class-2}[ (Dan. 11:37--38). Those ]{.class-0}["fathers"]{.class-2}[ are the Jewish fathers: Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob (cf. Rom. 11:28). Isaiah says he will destroy ]{.class-0}[his own land]{.class-1}[ (that's Palestine) and slay ]{.class-0}[his own people]{.class-1}[ (those are the Jews) \[Isa. 14:20 cf. Dan. 11:30--41\]. So the Antichrist is in some way connected to ]{.class-0}[Israel]{.class-1}[.]{.class-0}

[Now, the general idea of verse 16 is that the ]{.class-0}["foolish shepherd"]{.class-2}[ doesn't care for the sheep at all; in fact, he takes the lives of the sheep. That is set in contrast to ]{.class-0}["the good shepherd"]{.class-2}[ who ]{.class-0}["giveth his life for the sheep"]{.class-2}[ (John 10:11). But I'm not too sure about that last phrase: ]{.class-0}["and tear their claws in pieces."]{.class-2}

[Most of the commentators will tell you that that means the cattle are being overdriven and cracking their hooves, but the verses says nothing about hooves. It says ]{.class-0}["claws"]{.class-2}[; sheep don't have claws. Now like I pointed out above, the Antichrist is pictured as a leopard, and leopards have ]{.class-0}[claws]{.class-1}[. But how you put all of that together here in verse 16, I have no idea, so I'll just have to say ]{.class-0}["Ich weiss nicht."]{.class-1}

[Now we come to one of the salient verses in the book of Zechariah: verse 17. ]{.class-0}["Woe to the IDOL SHEPHERD."]{.class-2}[ All the modern translations change the word ]{.class-0}["idol"]{.class-2}[---meaning an image that is "venerated" or worshipped---to ]{.class-0}[idle,]{.class-1}[ meaning "lazy" or "worthless." That's the new "Bibles" covering up for Satan (again!) just like the ]{.class-0}[NIV]{.class-1}[ did in Isaiah 14:12.]{.class-0}

[The verse is not talking about some lazy "good-for-naught." It's talking about a fellow parading around with a shepherd's crook talking about ]{.class-0}["one fold, and one shepherd"]{.class-2}[ (John 10:16) with HIM as the shepherd. He likes to be called "Holy Father" (see John 17:11) and the "substitute Son of God" (]{.class-0}[Vicarius Filii Dei]{.class-1}[) and speak "from the seat" (]{.class-0}[ex cathedra]{.class-1}[) like the Holy Spirit. He likes to have men bow down to him and kiss his ring (cf. Acts 10:25--26 and comments in that Commentary). His "flock" bows down and prays to ]{.class-0}[images]{.class-1}[---IDOLS. In fact, he gets rid of the third commandment (]{.class-0}["Thou shalt not make unto thee any graven image\...Thou shalt not bow down thyself to them, nor serve them,"]{.class-2}[ Exod. 20:4--5) and makes two commandments of the tenth one to justify his flock's dolly worship. ]{.class-0}[That's]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-0}["the idol shepherd."]{.class-2}

[The one coming up ]{.class-0}["opposeth and exalteth himself above all that is called God, or that is worshipped; so that he as God sitteth in the temple of God, shewing himself that he is God"]{.class-2}[ (2 Thess. 2:4). He has an image of himself made and kills anyone who won't worship ]{.class-0}[it]{.class-1}[ or ]{.class-0}[him]{.class-1}[ (Rev. 13:15, 8). That's the ]{.class-0}["wicked prince"]{.class-2}[ who is ]{.class-0}["the idol shepherd.]{.class-2}[ The ]{.class-0}[AV]{.class-1}[ reading is the right one, and any other opinion is not even worthy of consideration.]{.class-0}

"[The sword shall be upon his arm, and upon his right eye: his arm shall be clean dried up, and his right eye shall be utterly darkened." ]{.class-2}[This ]{.class-0}["idol shepherd"]{.class-2}[ is the infamous ]{.class-0}["man of sin"]{.class-2}[ who becomes ]{.class-0}["the son of perdition"]{.class-2}[ (2 Thess. 2:3). He has a bad ]{.class-0}["right eye"]{.class-2}[ and a ]{.class-0}[bad arm]{.class-1}[, and although the Scriptures do not outright say, the implication is that it is his right arm that is bad, making him ]{.class-0}[left-handed]{.class-1}[. The Mark of the Beast is placed in the ]{.class-0}["right hand"]{.class-2}[ ]{.class-16}[or in the forehead where the Tibetan monks locate the mystic "third eye" (Rev. 13:16).Now the Beast of Revelation 13:2 is ]{.class-0}["a leopard."]{.class-2}[ The foot of a leopard is called a "paw." Do you know what a person who is left-handed is called? ]{.class-0}[He's a "SOUTH PAW."]{.class-1}

[This ]{.class-0}["shepherd"]{.class-2}[ is pictured in Scripture by Jeroboam---the King who led the northern ten tribes into idolatry and who had his arm shriveled up for defying ]{.class-0}["the man of God"]{.class-2}[ in 1 Kings 13:4--5---and ]{.class-0}["the horn of Moab"]{.class-2}[ in Jeremiah 48:25. That ]{.class-0}["horn of Moab"]{.class-2}[ is associated with ]{.class-0}["Kerioth"]{.class-2}[ in Jeremiah 48:24, 41. Do you know what ]{.class-0}[Iscariot]{.class-1}[ (as in "Judas Iscariot") means? It means "man of Kerioth." ]{.class-0}[Judas was the only apostle who was not from Galilee; he was from Kerioth in Moab, on the east side of Jordan.]{.class-1}

[Why, Judas Iscariot was the one Jesus Christ called ]{.class-0}["the son of perdition"]{.class-2}[ in John 17:12. His name has ]{.class-0}[thirteen]{.class-1}[ letters in it (watch out for that thirteen!). Judas Iscariot is the apostle in John ]{.class-0}[13:13]{.class-1}[ who consistently called Jesus ]{.class-0}["Master"]{.class-2}[ (see Matt. 26:25, 49; Mark 14:45). If you don't count the italicized word added by the ]{.class-0}[AV]{.class-1}[ translators in John 13:13, there are ]{.class-0}[thirteen]{.class-1}[ words in the verse with ]{.class-0}[39]{.class-1}[ letters in them (13x3). Jesus spoke John 13:13 in the upper room at the Last Supper, where there were ]{.class-0}[thirteen]{.class-1}[ men in the room (Judas being the odd man out, John 13:26--30). By the way, did you notice that Jesus identified Judas as the traitor in John 13:26, and 26 is ]{.class-0}[13x2]{.class-1}[ (a hillbilly in Bro. Lince's church up in North Carolina pointed out that the ]{.class-0}["SOP"]{.class-2}[ in that verse was ]{.class-0}[the initials]{.class-1}[ of ]{.class-0}[S]{.class-1}[on ]{.class-0}[O]{.class-1}[f ]{.class-0}[P]{.class-1}[erdition).]{.class-0}

In psychiatry, the fear of the number thirteen is called "triskaidekaphobia"; most people count it as a superstition. Of course, that doesn't stop them from not putting a thirteenth floor on a building like a hotel or hospital. For years Eastern Airlines had no Gate 13 in the Atlanta airport. But as we have seen, there is more to the belief than just mere superstition.

[The Satanic trinity is given in Revelation 16:]{.class-0}[13.]{.class-1}[ The Antichrist and his False Prophet show up in Revelation ]{.class-0}[13.]{.class-1}[ They are pictured in Scripture by Jeroboam in 1 Kings ]{.class-0}[13]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}["a false prophet"]{.class-2}[ who opposes Paul on his first missionary journey in Acts ]{.class-0}[13]{.class-1}[:6. That last reference is interesting because the number of the Beast is 666. That's found in Revelation 13:18, and 6+6+6 is 18. No wonder people are worried about Friday the Thirteenth: Friday is the ]{.class-0}[sixth]{.class-1}[ day of the week.]{.class-0}

[You say, "That's just a coincidence, Ruckman." It is? Well, what about this: the first time the word ]{.class-0}["sinners"]{.class-2}[ shows up in your Bible is in Genesis ]{.class-0}[13:13.]{.class-1}[ Quite a coincidence, wouldn't you say?]{.class-0}

[The first Gentile kingdom on this earth was set up by a man who was the ]{.class-0}[thirteenth]{.class-1}[ from Adam (Gen. 10:8--10 cf. 1 Chron. 1:1--10). That was ]{.class-0}[Nimrod,]{.class-1}[ a type of the Antichrist, just like Jeroboam and Judas Iscariot. The Hebrew root of his name means "panther." In the Middle East, ]{.class-0}[a panther]{.class-1}[ is a ]{.class-0}[black leopard.]{.class-1}[ A leopard is a ]{.class-0}[black cat,]{.class-1}[ so you don't want a ]{.class-0}[black cat]{.class-1}[ to cross your path on ]{.class-0}[Friday the Thirteenth.]{.class-1}

[In Jeremiah ]{.class-0}[13]{.class-1}[ \[got that!\]:23, that leopard is connected to Cush, Nimrod's father. ]{.class-0}["Can the Ethiopian ]{.class-2}[\[the Hebrew word for Ethiopia is ]{.class-0}[Cush]{.class-1}[\] ]{.class-0}[change his skin, or the leopard his spots?"]{.class-2}[ By the way, did you know that Baibars, the Moslem commander who polished off the Pope's forces in the last crusade (A.D. 1257), was called the "ONE-EYED Panther." Just a coincidence, of course, of course.]{.class-0}

[Nimrod's name also means "rebel." Nimrod set up the first rebellion against God after the Flood: it was Babel (Gen. 10:10, 11:1--9), later called ]{.class-0}[Babylon.]{.class-1}[ The final destruction of Babylon occurs in Revelation ]{.class-0}[18]{.class-1}[ (6+6+6). So when America enters its War of Rebellion against Britain, she has ]{.class-0}[thirteen ]{.class-1}[colonies.]{.class-0}

[On the "Great Seal of the United States" on the back of your dollar bill, there are ]{.class-0}[thirteen]{.class-1}[ arrows in the left claw of the eagle, ]{.class-0}[thirteen]{.class-1}[ olive leaves in his right claw, ]{.class-0}[thirteen]{.class-1}[ stars above him, and ]{.class-0}[thirteen]{.class-1}[ letters in the phrase ]{.class-0}["E Pluribus Unum."]{.class-1}[ If you look over to the side with the pyramid on it, you will find ]{.class-0}[thirteen]{.class-1}[ letters above the pyramid in the phrase ]{.class-0}["Annuit Coeptis."]{.class-1}

[One of the first American flags had a ]{.class-0}[snake]{.class-1}[ dismembered into ]{.class-0}[thirteen]{.class-1}[ pieces with the words "Don't Tread on Me" beneath it. There are ]{.class-0}[thirteen]{.class-1}[ letters in "Don't Tread on Me." When the South rebelled against the North in the Civil War, the flag had ]{.class-0}[thirteen]{.class-1}[ stars on an ]{.class-0}[X]{.class-1}[ (see chap. 2 of the work ]{.class-0}[The Mark of the Beast]{.class-1}[).]{.class-0}

[Ever since Alaska and Hawaii became States, I have taught that you can expect two more, making a full deck (52). I don't know whether it will be Puerto Rico and Guam, or whether California will be divided into three separate States, or whether you can count Canada and Mexico under NAFTA, but you are going to end up with 52, which is ]{.class-0}[13x4.]{.class-1}[ That is represented in Daniel 7:6 by the third beast; a leopard with ]{.class-0}["four heads"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["four wings of a fowl."]{.class-2}[ Those aren't eagle's wings like the British lion of Daniel 7:4; those are ]{.class-0}[chicken]{.class-1}[ wings. When it comes to foreign policy and doing right in its domestic policy, America has lost its resolve. It has been emasculated; ]{.class-0}[it is run by women and children ]{.class-1}[(see Isa. 3:12). I tell people America is not a ]{.class-0}[bald eagle;]{.class-1}[ it's a ]{.class-0}[plucked chicken.]{.class-1}[ That's why you've lost your last ]{.class-0}[three wars]{.class-1}[ (Korea, Vietnam, and Iraq), and you are losing the ones you are in right now (]{.class-0}[Afghanistan]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[Libya]{.class-1}[).]{.class-0}

Now, the Son of Perdition has a bad right arm. Look at the two passages in the Psalms.

"[Break thou the ARM of the wicked and the evil man]{.class-2}[ \[in the Psalms, ]{.class-0}["the wicked"]{.class-2}[ and the ]{.class-0}["wicked man"]{.class-2}[ are prophetic references to the Antichrist\]]{.class-0}[: seek out his wickedness till thou find none"]{.class-2}[ (Psa. 10:15). The context in verse 16 of Psalm 10 is the Second Advent. Look at the same kind of thing over in Psalm 37:17.]{.class-0}

"[For the ARMS of the wicked shall be broken: but the LORD upholdeth the righteous."]{.class-2}

[You see that thing not only in the Bible, but in ]{.class-0}[history]{.class-1}[. There are types of the Antichrist outside of the Bible throughout history. I have a photograph of Adolf Hitler when he was in the Nazi Party in Austria. He is holding his ID card, and the number is 555. Hitler kills that Jew just like the one who follows him---Mr. 666 himself, the Beast of Revelation 13:18. Well, the Antichrist is overthrown by Jesus Christ, and His number would be 777. That means if you traced the great world leaders back in the Church Age, 111 would be ]{.class-0}[Nero]{.class-1}[ (the Roman Emperor who killed Paul and started the first worldwide persecution of Christians); 222 would be ]{.class-0}[Constantine]{.class-1}[, the Emperor who combined Church and State under the Roman Catholic Church; 333 would be ]{.class-0}[Charlemagne]{.class-1}[, the first Emperor of the "Holy Roman Empire" in Europe; and 444 would be ]{.class-0}[Napoleon]{.class-1}[, the next ruler to conquer nearly all of Europe. Those are all world rulers (along with ]{.class-0}[Hitler]{.class-1}[) who picture the Antichrist in history.]{.class-0}

[Napoleon started a peculiar custom with important and famous men of the Nineteenth Century when they were photographed. You probably have seen pictures of Napoleon with his ]{.class-0}[right arm]{.class-1}[ in his waistcoat. He didn't have a bad arm; that was just a pose he struck for a painting. But because he was an historical type of the Antichrist, there has always been speculation that something was wrong. ]{.class-0}[All kinds of people copied it,]{.class-1}[ though. ]{.class-0}[Karl Marx]{.class-1}[ had his picture taken like that, and so did Lincoln's ]{.class-0}[Yankee Generals.]{.class-1}

[Kaiser Wilhelm had a withered ]{.class-0}[left arm]{.class-1}[ which he always kept behind him in pictures. Adolf Hitler had a tremor in his left arm, and he would always hold it to hide the tremors in public. Those are all pictures and types of Zechariah 11:17.]{.class-0}

[But not only does the Antichrist have a bad arm, he also has a bad ]{.class-0}["right eye."]{.class-2}

[Job 38:15, ]{.class-0}["And from the wicked]{.class-2}[ \[a prophetic reference to the Son of Perdition in the Wisdom books\]]{.class-0}[ their light is withholden]{.class-2}[ \[there's that ]{.class-0}["darkened"]{.class-2}[ eye from Zech. 11:17; cf. Luke 11:34)]{.class-0}[, and the high arm shall be broken ]{.class-2}[\[and there's the bad arm\]]{.class-0}[."]{.class-2}

So in Greek mythology, you have a one-eyed giant (see Gen. 6:4) called a cyclops who ate his own children (1 Pet. 5:8; Isa. 6:13). Do you know what Proverbs 23:6 says? It says this:

"[Eat thou not the bread of him that hath an EVIL EYE, neither desire thou his dainty meats."]{.class-2}

[Of course, I understand the practical application of that verse, but isn't it interesting that ]{.class-0}["the bread"]{.class-2}[ of ]{.class-0}["the idol shepherd"]{.class-2}[ is his own flock (vs. 16) and that flock is people (vs. 9)? Didn't that giant (see Gen. 6:11) in "Jack and the Beanstalk" say, "Fe Fi Fo Fum! I smell the blood of an Englishman; be he alive or be he dead, I'll GRIND HIS BONES TO MAKE MY BREAD"? (As the Germans say, "Very interesting!")]{.class-0}

[It gets even better. Proverbs 30:17 says, ]{.class-0}["The EYE that mocketh at his father, and despiseth to obey his MOTHER]{.class-2}[ \[a reference to the Son of Perdition in verses 11--14 of the same chapter\]]{.class-0}[, the ravens of the valley shall pick it out, and the young eagles shall eat it."]{.class-2}[ There's the great feast of Revelation 19:17--21 (see the remarks under Zeph. 1:7--8).]{.class-0}

[Now, how does ]{.class-0}["the idol shepherd"]{.class-2}[ get that bad ]{.class-0}["arm"]{.class-2}[ and bad ]{.class-0}["right eye"]{.class-2}[? ]{.class-0}["The sword shall be upon his arm, and upon his right eye."]{.class-2}

[The Bible says, ]{.class-0}["And I saw one of his heads]{.class-2}[ \[talking about the Beast of Rev. 13\]]{.class-0}[ as it were wounded to death; and his deadly wound was healed: and all the world wondered after the beast"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 13:3).]{.class-0}

[The Antichrist is not just ]{.class-0}[against]{.class-1}[ Christ (]{.class-0}[anti]{.class-1}[---"against); he is a]{.class-0}[ counterfeit, imitation]{.class-1}[ Christ (see the comparison under 2 Thess. 2:2 in that Commentary) who deceives the whole world with a counterfeit, imitation ]{.class-0}[death, burial,]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-2}[and ]{.class-0}[resurrection]{.class-1}[ (cf. 1 Cor. 15:3--4).]{.class-0}

[The Bible says, ]{.class-0}["The beast that thou sawest was]{.class-2}[ \[he was alive\]]{.class-0}[, and is not ]{.class-2}[\[he is dead and buried\]]{.class-0}[; and shall ascend out of the bottomless pit]{.class-2}[ \[he comes up\]]{.class-0}[, and go into perdition: and they that dwell on the earth shall wonder\...when they behold the beast that was, and is not, and yet is"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 17:8).]{.class-0}

[The Beast starts out as the ]{.class-0}["man of sin"]{.class-2}[ but is somehow killed (probably by assassination) and comes back to life as ]{.class-0}["the son of perdition."]{.class-2}[ When I illustrated that in ]{.class-0}[Ruckman's Apocalypse]{.class-1}[ (see pp. 204--205), I had his ]{.class-0}[arm]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[eye]{.class-1}[ wounded by a "raghead" shooting the Pope (]{.class-0}["the idol shepherd,"]{.class-2}[ remember?) like that Turk shot John Paul II several years back.]{.class-0}

For further study, we refer you to the works The Mark of the Beast, the Bible Believer's Commentary on Revelation (chap. 13), and the Bible Believer's Commentary on 1--2 Thessalonians and Philemon (2 Thess. 2).

[]{#section-0_1_a5d1907423d52bff_b698.xhtml}

CHAPTER TWELVE

12:1 The burden of the word of the LORD for Israel, saith the LORD, which stretcheth forth the heavens, and layeth the foundation of the earth, and formeth the spirit of man within him.

2 Behold, I will make Jerusalem a cup of trembling unto all the people round about, when they shall be in the siege both against Judah and against Jerusalem.

3 And in that day will I make Jerusalem a burdensome stone for all people: all that burden themselves with it shall be cut in pieces, though all the people of the earth be gathered together against it.

4 In that day, saith the LORD, I will smite every horse with astonishment, and his rider with madness: and I will open mine eyes upon the house of Judah, and will smite every horse of the people with blindness.

[Verse one is the complete and utter repudiation of Darwin's "fairy tale for grown-ups": evolution. The Scriptures recognize no "Puddle-to-Paradise" modern "Pilgrim's Progress," whether randomly by natural forces or by design ("theistic" evolution). The only "theory of origins" in the Bible is the direct creation of the universe and life by God Almighty (Isa. 44:24, 45:7, 12, 18, 48:13): ]{.class-0}[creationism.]{.class-1}[ That is the view taken by Moses (Exod. 20:11), David (Psa. 146:5--6), Paul (Col. 1:16--17; Heb. 11:3), and JESUS CHRIST (Mark 13:19). Your modern creationists talk about "Biblical creationism" and "scientific creationism," but the fact of the matter is that the Bible is the only really scientific textbook on the subject.]{.class-0}

There are only four theories about the existence of the universe. They are:

[1) ]{.class-0}[The universe has always been here;]{.class-1}[ that is the teaching of ]{.class-0}[the eternity of matter.]{.class-1}[ It is a violation of the scientific, demonstrable ]{.class-0}[Second Law of Thermodynamics,]{.class-1}[ which states that available energy continually ]{.class-0}[lessens]{.class-1}[ in a closed system. That is known as ]{.class-0}[entropy:]{.class-1}[ things wear out and break down unless they are repaired. The longer the universe has been here, the more it would get ]{.class-0}[worse,]{.class-1}[ not better. Scientific "outlaws" (]{.class-0}[any]{.class-1}[ evolutionist) try to claim that the law doesn't apply to an "open system," but if there is no God, the whole universe is a ]{.class-0}[closed system]{.class-1}[.]{.class-0}

[2) ]{.class-0}[The universe came about naturally from nothing.]{.class-1}[ That violates another demonstrable, provable law of real science: the ]{.class-0}[First Law of Thermodynamics.]{.class-1}[ It is also known as the ]{.class-0}[Law of Conservation of Matter and Energy,]{.class-1}[ and it states that matter and energy cannot be created or destroyed naturally.]{.class-0}

[3) ]{.class-0}[The universe is not really here;]{.class-1}[ it is just an ]{.class-0}[illusion.]{.class-1}[ And there you can throw ]{.class-0}[science]{.class-1}[ out the window. If the whole thing is just a dream, there are no scientific laws because dreams and illusions are subject to the one dreaming and hallucinating; there is no objective truth whatsoever. All you are left with is Einstein's "black hole": ]{.class-0}[absolute anarchy.]{.class-1}

[The only possible solution you are left with is number 4: ]{.class-0}[The universe was created out of nothing supernaturally and miraculously by a Supreme Intelligence that is self-existing.]{.class-1}[ That Supreme Intelligence has revealed Himself in a Book, and He has proven it by ]{.class-0}[fulfilled prophecy]{.class-1}[ (Isa. 41:21--23, 42:8--9, 44:6--8, 46:9--10, 48:3--7).]{.class-0}

[A mathematician named Dr. Peter Stoner once wrote a book entitled ]{.class-0}[Science Speaks.]{.class-1}[ In that work, he calculated the odds of Jesus Christ fulfilling 48 Old Testament prophecies on His First Advent. He figured out that the odds of one man fulfilling 48 prophecies made anywhere from 400--1,500 years before he showed up was ]{.class-0}[one out of ten with 157 zeroes after it. ]{.class-1}[That is more than the total number of electrons in ]{.class-0}[three universes]{.class-1}[ the size of this one (the total number of electrons in the known universe is estimated at ten with 52 zeroes after it). Moreover, according to a mathematician named Lecomte du Nouy, if the chances of something are one out of ten with ]{.class-0}[fifty zeroes]{.class-1}[ after it, that thing will ]{.class-0}[never]{.class-1}[ take place. In other words, the chances of Jesus Christ fulfilling all 48 prophecies ]{.class-0}[lie completely outside of science and nature.]{.class-1}[ The Bible is proven to be the only ]{.class-0}[completely accurate account of creation ]{.class-1}[by the ]{.class-0}[laws of statistical probability.]{.class-1}

[Evolution is ]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ science. According to the following men, evolution is a ]{.class-0}[religion]{.class-1}[ accepted by ]{.class-0}[faith,]{.class-1}[ not observable evidence.]{.class-0}

[1) Irving W. Knobloch, Professor of Natural Sciences at Michigan State University, said evolution is "an act of ]{.class-0}[faith]{.class-1}[" (J. C. Monsma, ]{.class-0}[Evidence of God in an Expanding Universe,]{.class-1}[ Putnam and Sons, 1958, pp. 87--88).]{.class-0}

[2) Dr. Hubert Yockey of the University of California, Berkeley, said that the "basis" of evolution is "]{.class-0}[faith]{.class-1}[," not "fact" (]{.class-0}[Journal of Theoretical]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-0}[Biology]{.class-1}[, Vol. 67, 1977, pg. 398).]{.class-0}

[3) Biologist Stephen Jay Gould wrote "These ]{.class-0}[tales]{.class-1}[\...of evolutionary natural history, ]{.class-0}[do not prove anything"]{.class-1}[ ("The Return of the Hopeful Monsters," ]{.class-0}[Natural History,]{.class-1}[ June/July 1977).]{.class-0}

[4) I. L. Cohen, a member of the New York Academy of Sciences, wrote that evolutionists "accept these concepts on ']{.class-0}[faith]{.class-1}['" (]{.class-0}[Darwin Was Wrong: A Study in Probabilities,]{.class-1}[ New Research Publications, 1984, pg. 129).]{.class-0}

[Cohen, citing Gould, wrote: "Truth as preached by scientists often turns out to be no more than ]{.class-0}[prejudice]{.class-1}[ inspired by prevailing social and political beliefs" (Ibid., pg. 215).]{.class-0}

[5) H. S. Lipson, Professor of Physics at the University of Manchester, wrote: "In fact, evolution became in a sense a scientific ]{.class-0}[religion;]{.class-1}[ almost all scientists have accepted it, and many are prepared to ]{.class-0}[bend their observations to fit in with it"]{.class-1}[ (]{.class-0}[Physics Bulletin,]{.class-1}[ Vol. 31, No. 4, May 1980, pg. 138). (I will speak a little plainer than the good professor and tell you that scientists will outright ]{.class-0}[lie]{.class-1}[ to prove evolution \[see archaeopteryx, brontosaurus, "ontogeny recapitulates phylogeny," ]{.class-0}[Bathybius Haeckelii,]{.class-1}[ and Piltdown Man for some good examples\].)]{.class-0}

[6) Geologist David Raup calls evolution in public school textbooks "]{.class-0}[pure fantasy]{.class-1}[" ("Evolution and the Fossil Record," ]{.class-0}[Science]{.class-1}[ magazine, cited by Henry Morris, ]{.class-0}[Scientific Creationism,]{.class-1}[ pp. xi--xii).]{.class-0}

[7) Evolutionist Loren Eisley admitted: "After having chided the theologian for his reliance on myth and miracles, science found itself in the unenviable position of having to ]{.class-0}[create a mythology of its own]{.class-1}[" (]{.class-0}[The Immense Journey,]{.class-1}[ Random House, New York, 1957, pg. 199).]{.class-0}

[Those are the facts that will never be given to one child in the Federal school system. ]{.class-1}[After forbidding the teaching of creationism on the basis that it is a violation of the separation of Church and State, the District Court judges and the Supreme Court judges have decreed that ]{.class-0}[only one]{.class-1}[ theory of origins can be taught, and that theory is a ]{.class-0}[religious faith]{.class-1}[ composed of ]{.class-0}[mythological fantasies.]{.class-1}[ For the most complete, definitive work on the subject, get the book by Vance Ferrell entitled ]{.class-0}[The Evolution Handbook.]{.class-1}

Now, all the rest of chapter 12 is Second Advent. Verses 2--3 should be compared to 14:1--3, 12--15. At the Second Advent, there are three "fronts" on which the Antichrist is fighting against those Jews: there is Armageddon (Rev. 19), Selah-Petra in Edom (see the comments on Hab. 3:3), and Jerusalem (Zech. 14:1--3). The testimony in the prophets is that God Almighty gathers the 191 Gentile nations in the UN to Israel and Jerusalem to wipe them out (Isa. 29:8 cf. Mic. 4:11--12; see the comments on Zeph. 3:8).

[Notice that the Lord makes the city of Jerusalem two things to the UN: ]{.class-0}["a cup of trembling"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 2) and ]{.class-0}["a burdensome stone"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 3). That ]{.class-0}["cup of trembling"]{.class-2}[ is defined in Isaiah 51:17 as ]{.class-0}["the cup of his ]{.class-2}[\[i.e., God's\]]{.class-0}[ fury."]{.class-2}[ Look at Jeremiah 25:15--18.]{.class-0}

"[For thus saith the LORD God of Israel unto me; Take the wine cup of this fury at my hand, and cause all the nations, to whom I will send thee to drink it. And they shall drink, and be moved, and be mad, because of the sword that I will send among them. Then took I the cup at the LORD'S hand, and made all the nations to drink, unto whom the LORD had sent me."]{.class-2}

[And in verses 18--26 of Jeremiah 25, those ]{.class-0}["nations"]{.class-2}[ are listed: Israel (Jer. 25:18), Egypt (Jer. 25:19), the "Palestinians" in the "Gaza Strip" (Jer. 25:20), Jordan (Jer. 25:21), Lebanon (Jer. 25:22), Saudi Arabia (Jer. 25:23--24), and Iraq and Iran (Jer. 25:25). But that's not all. Look at Jeremiah 25:26.]{.class-0}

"[And ALL the kings of the north, far \[]{.class-2}[Russia and the European Union\] ]{.class-0}[and near]{.class-2}[ \[Turkey and Syria\]]{.class-0}[, and ALL THE KINGDOMS OF THE WORLD, which are upon the face of the earth ]{.class-2}[\[America, Canada, Mexico, Central and South America, Africa, China, Australia, India, etc.\]]{.class-0}[."]{.class-2}

[Psalm 75.8 says, ]{.class-0}["For in the hand of the LORD there is a cup and the wine red; it is full of mixture; and he poureth out the same: but the dregs thereof, ALL THE WICKED OF THE EARTH shall wring them out, and drink them."]{.class-2}

[This is the cup of God's wrath on sin that Christ drank Himself at the First Advent]{.class-1}[ (see the comments on John 18:11 in that Commentary). You will either accept what He did for you ]{.class-0}[then]{.class-1}[, or you will drink of that cup yourself in Hell or at His ]{.class-0}[return]{.class-1}[ (2 Thess. 1:7--10).]{.class-0}

[That ]{.class-0}["burdensome stone"]{.class-2}[ of verse 3 is the smiting stone of Daniel 2:44--45 that grinds whomever it falls on ]{.class-0}["to powder" ]{.class-2}[(Matt. 21:44). Verses 2--3 are a reference to Jesus Christ using Jerusalem to judge the United Nations at His return.]{.class-0}

[Verse 4: ]{.class-0}["In that day, saith the LORD, I will smite every horse with astonishment, and his rider with madness: and I will open mine eyes upon the house of Judah, and will smite every horse of the people with blindness."]{.class-2}[ That is pictured two places in your Bible. The ]{.class-0}[first]{.class-1}[ is in Judges 7:22, and the ]{.class-0}[second]{.class-1}[ is in 2 Kings 6:18.]{.class-0}

[In Judges 7, Gideon goes to fight the Amalekites with the 300 men God left him. They surround the Amalekite encampment, and all they have are pitchers, lamps, and trumpets. They blow the trumpets, brake the pitchers, and "let their lights shine." Those Amalekites wake up out of a dead sleep and think they are surrounded by 300 divisions of 800 men each---240,000 men; almost twice the size of the Amalekite host (135,000---Judg. 8:10). Then Gideon shouts, ]{.class-0}["The sword of the LORD ]{.class-2}[\[Second Advent---Rev. 19:13--15\]]{.class-0}[, and of Gideon" ]{.class-2}[(Judg. 7:20). When he does that, the Bible says, ]{.class-0}["the LORD set every man's sword against his fellow, even throughout all the hosts" ]{.class-2}[(Judg. 8:22). The Amalekites think they are under attack, and every one of them starts killing the nearest guy to him, which turns out to be his own buddy. That's what's going on in Zechariah 12:4.]{.class-0}

[The next place is in 2 Kings 6 where the Syrians have surrounded the city where Elisha is staying. He's been telling the King of Israel how to escape the Syrian ambushes, and the Syrians want to put a stop to that. Elisha's servant walks out of the house in the morning and sees the Syrian cavalry around the town and says to the prophet, "Master! what are we going to do?!" Elisha says, "Don't worry]{.class-0}[; 'they that be with us are more than they that be with them']{.class-2}[" (2 Kings 6:16). Then Elisha prays for God to open his servant's eyes, and the kid sees ]{.class-0}["the mountain\...full of horses and chariots of fire"]{.class-2}[(2 Kings 6:17; see the comments on Hab. 3:8). Then Elisha prays, ]{.class-0}["Smite this people ]{.class-2}[\[i.e., the Syrians\]]{.class-0}[, I pray thee, with BLINDNESS. And he smote them with blindness according to the word of Elisha"]{.class-2}[(2 Kings 6:18)---just like the Lord said He would do at the Second Advent in Zechariah 12:4.]{.class-0}

5 And the governors of Judah shall say in their heart, The inhabitants of Jerusalem shall be my strength in the LORD of hosts their God.

6 In that day will I make the governors of Judah like an hearth of fire among the wood, and like a torch of fire in a sheaf; and they shall devour all the people round about, on the right hand and on the left: and Jerusalem shall be inhabited again in her own place, even in Jerusalem.

7 The LORD also shall save the tents of Judah first, that the glory of the house of David and the glory of the inhabitants of Jerusalem do not magnify themselves against Judah.

"[And the governors of Judah shall say in their heart, The inhabitants of Jerusalem shall be my strength in the LORD of hosts their God"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 5). The leaders of the Jews are getting optimistic about whipping their enemies, but it's because the Lord has shown up to fight for them (vss. 7--8 cf. 14:3). They have no strength on their own; the Antichrist is about to wipe them all out.]{.class-0}

[That Bible says, ]{.class-0}["The joy OF THE LORD is your strength"]{.class-2}[ (Neh. 8:10), and ]{.class-0}["I can do all things THROUGH CHRIST which strengtheneth me"]{.class-2}[ (Phil. 4:13). You are commanded to ]{.class-0}["be strong IN THE LORD, and in the power OF HIS MIGHT"]{.class-2}[ (Eph. 6:10), because there is no strength or power of your own that will get the job done.]{.class-0}

[One of the saddest things to see is that Jew trying to take credit for his own survival. The Lord told them in Malachi 3:6, ]{.class-0}["I am the LORD, I change not; therefore ye sons of Jacob are not consumed."]{.class-2}[ ]{.class-0}[If it weren't for Jehovah God, there wouldn't be a Jew left alive on this planet.]{.class-1}

[Verse 6: ]{.class-0}["In that day will I make the governors of Judah like an hearth of fire among the wood, and like a torch of fire in a sheaf; and they shall devour all the people round about]{.class-2}[ \[see Obad. 18 and the comments in Vol. I of this Commentary\]]{.class-0}[\...and Jerusalem shall be inhabited again in her own place, even in Jerusalem."]{.class-2}

[Now that's a prophecy just like Jesus' promise in John 14:2---]{.class-0}["I go to prepare a place for you."]{.class-2}[ The prophecy is that Jerusalem is going to be inhabited only by Jews after the Lord helps them burn all the Moslems and Catholics out of it; ]{.class-0}[there won't be a Moslem left within 200 miles of that city.]{.class-1}

[As this commentary is being written, Barack Obama is putting the pressure on the Prime Minister of Israel, Benjamin Netanyahu, to return Israel to its pre-1967 borders before the Jews were ]{.class-0}[attacked]{.class-1}[ by Syrian, Jordanian, and Egyptian Moslems (Six Day War). That would put Jerusalem back under the control of the Mohammedans as the capital of a "Palestinian State" (which NEVER existed at any time all the way back to Josh. 13)---AFTER the Congress of the United States (under Clinton) voted to move the American embassy from Tel Aviv to Jerusalem to recognize Jerusalem as the capital of the Jewish State of Israel. That's your "first black President" who told you ]{.class-0}[before]{.class-1}[ he was elected that in any dispute between the Moslems and Israel, HE WOULD TAKE THE ]{.class-0}[MOSLEM SIDE]{.class-1}[. According to God Almighty (]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ "Allah"; ]{.class-0}[never]{.class-1}[ Allah. Allah couldn't prophesy anything beyond a 50-50 guess), Obama and his Mohammedan buddies are going through a blast furnace right before God turns the whole cotton-picking city over to the descendants of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob (]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ Abraham, Ishmael, and Esau).]{.class-0}

[Verse 7: ]{.class-0}["The LORD also shall save the tents of Judah first ]{.class-2}[\[see the order given in Jer. 23:6, 33:16; Hos. 1:7\]]{.class-0}[, that the glory of the house of David and the glory of the inhabitants of Jerusalem do not magnify themselves against Judah."]{.class-2}[ Whoever is in Jerusalem doesn't take precedence over the other inhabitants of Judah. This is one of those places that seems to indicate that at the Second Advent, Christ goes to Armageddon and Edom ]{.class-0}[before]{.class-1}[ heading to Jerusalem (see the closing remarks in the comment on Hab. 3:3).]{.class-0}

8 In that day shall the LORD defend the inhabitants of Jerusalem; and he that is feeble among them at that day shall be as David; and the house of David shall be as God, as the angel of the LORD before them.

9 And it shall come to pass in that day, that I will seek to destroy all the nations that come against Jerusalem.

10 And I will pour upon the house of David, and upon the inhabitants of Jerusalem, the spirit of grace and of supplications: and they shall look upon me whom they have pierced, and they shall mourn for him, as one mourneth for his only son, and shall be in bitterness for him, as one that is in bitterness for his firstborn.

"[In that day]{.class-2}[ \[Second Advent\]]{.class-0}[ shall the LORD defend the inhabitants of Jerusalem ]{.class-2}[\[see Psa. 20:1--2; Joel 3:16--17; Zech. 14:3\]]{.class-0}[, and he that is feeble among them at that day shall be as David ]{.class-2}[\[i.e., like David in his prime---a giant killer and in good health\]]{.class-0}[, and the house of David ]{.class-2}[\[his descendants; specifically Jesus Christ\]]{.class-0}[ shall be as God, as the angel of the LORD before them ]{.class-2}[\[see the remarks on the Angel of the Lord under the comments on Zech. 8:1\]]{.class-0}["]{.class-2}[ (vs. 8).]{.class-0}

For verse 9, see the comments on Zephaniah 3:8 and Habakkuk 2:22.

Verse 10 is one of those key places in the Scriptures that destroys the Jehovah's Witness doctrine that Jesus Christ is a "created God" who is not Jehovah. Look at the wording.

"[And I\...." ]{.class-2}[Who's that? Well, the speaker in verses 1 and 4 was ]{.class-0}["the LORD"]{.class-2}[: ]{.class-0}["The burden of the word of the LORD for Israel, saith the LORD"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 1); ]{.class-0}["In that day, saith the LORD"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 4).
