The Jehovah's Witness will tell you that L-O-R-D is Jehovah and L-o-r-d is Jesus, a separate God. Two gods, just like you will find it in John 1:18 in an ]{.class-0}[NASV:]{.class-1}[ a ]{.class-0}["begotten God"]{.class-1}[ and an "unbegotten God." The reading of John 1:18 in the ]{.class-0}[New American Standard Version]{.class-1}[ is a ]{.class-0}[Jehovah's Witness reading.]{.class-1}

[So the ]{.class-0}["I"]{.class-2}[ of verse 10 is ]{.class-0}["the LORD"]{.class-2}[ (see vss. 8, 7, 5, 4, 1). The Jehovah's Witness ]{.class-0}[New World Translation]{.class-1}[ will translate that as ]{.class-0}["Jehovah"]{.class-1}[ every time it shows up. But look on down to the end of the verse and see what "Jehovah" says: ]{.class-0}["and they shall look upon ME WHOM THEY HAVE PIERCED."]{.class-2}[ Why, that's Jesus Christ!]{.class-0}

[Revelation 1:5--7, ]{.class-0}["And from JESUS CHRIST, who is the faithful witness, and the first begotten of the dead, and the prince of the kings of the earth. Unto him that loved us, and washed us from our sins in his own blood, And hath made us kings and priests unto God and his Father; to him be glory and dominion for ever and ever. Amen. Behold, he cometh with clouds; AND EVERY EYE SHALL SEE HIM, AND THEY ALSO WHICH PIERCED HIM."]{.class-2}

[Zechariah 12:10 says that Jehovah's got ]{.class-0}[holes]{.class-1}[ in Him from being pierced. There's no doubt about ]{.class-0}[who's]{.class-1}[ talking and ]{.class-0}[to whom]{.class-1}[ the verse is referring. It clearly identifies the ]{.class-0}[Jehovah]{.class-1}[ of the Old Testament as the ]{.class-0}[Jesus Christ ]{.class-1}[of the New Testament. When the J.W.s saw Zechariah 12:10, they went into a panic and changed it to read ]{.class-0}["they shall look upon HIM whom they have pierced"]{.class-1}[ so you wouldn't make the connection.]{.class-0}

[T]{.class-0}[he Apostle Thomas made it.]{.class-1}[ When he saw where Christ had been pierced by the nails and the spear, he said,]{.class-0}[ "My Lord and my God"]{.class-2}[ (John 20:28).]{.class-0}

[The Apostle John made the connection.]{.class-1}[ After recording the words of Jesus as ]{.class-0}["that they might know thee]{.class-2}[ \[i.e., the Father---Jehovah, John 17:1\]]{.class-0}[ THE ONLY TRUE GOD"]{.class-2}[ (John 17:3), he wrote, ]{.class-0}["that we may know him that is true, and we are in him that is true, even in his Son Jesus Christ, THIS IS THE TRUE GOD"]{.class-2}[ (1 John 5:20).]{.class-0}

[If L-O-R-D is Jehovah, then what do you make of this: ]{.class-0}["And he hath on his venture and on his thigh a name written, KING OF KINGS, AND LORD OF LORDS"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 19:16)? According to the J.W.s, that verse is saying that Jesus Christ is JEHOVAH OF JEHOVAHS!]{.class-0}

[Do you know what versions match the Jehovah's Witness ]{.class-0}[New World Translation]{.class-1}[ in Zechariah 12:10? The main ones would be the ]{.class-0}[Revised Standard Version,]{.class-1}[ the ]{.class-0}[New Revised Standard Version,]{.class-1}[ Kenneth Taylor's ]{.class-0}["Living" Bible,]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[Good News for Modern Man.]{.class-1}[ And while the ]{.class-0}[ASV,]{.class-1}[ the ]{.class-0}[NASV,]{.class-1}[ the ]{.class-0}[NIV,]{.class-1}[ and the ]{.class-0}[ESV]{.class-1}[ may have the correct reading here in Zechariah, all four of those corrupt translations will give you the J.W. reading in John 1:18.]{.class-0}

[Back to Zechariah 12:10---]{.class-0}["And I will pour upon the house of David, and upon the inhabitants of Jerusalem, the spirit of grace and of supplications."]{.class-2}[ The cross-reference is Joel 2:28--29, the passage Peter preached at Pentecost (see Acts 2:16--18)---]{.class-0}["And it shall come to pass afterward, that I will pour out my spirit upon all flesh\...."]{.class-2}[ What ]{.class-0}["flesh"]{.class-2}[? Gentile "flesh"? The Body of Christ? No---]{.class-0}["YOUR sons and YOUR daughters shall prophecy, YOUR old men shall dream dreams, YOUR young men shall see visions."]{.class-2}

[To whom is Joel writing? He's writing to JEWS, not Gentile Christians in the Body of Christ. To whom is Peter preaching in Acts 2? ]{.class-0}["Men of Judea"]{.class-2}[ (Acts 2:14), ]{.class-0}["all ye that dwell at Jerusalem"]{.class-2}[ (Acts 2:14), ]{.class-0}["Ye men of Israel"]{.class-2}[ (Acts 2:22), ]{.class-0}["the house of Israel"]{.class-2}[ (Acts 2:36). ]{.class-0}[There's not a Gentile Christian in the lot.]{.class-1}[ All three passages---Zechariah 12:10; Joel 2:28--29; and Acts 2:16--18---are addressed to ]{.class-0}[Jews at the Second Advent.]{.class-1}

"[\...and they shall look upon me whom they have pierced\...."]{.class-2}[ Well, the Romans were the ones who actually did the piercing: ]{.class-0}[they]{.class-1}[ were the ones who whipped Him, put the crown of thorns on His head, nailed Him to the cross, and shoved the spear in His side. Outside of slapping Him before Annas and Caiaphas, ]{.class-0}[the Jews never touched Him. ]{.class-1}[But you see, the Jews were guilty by proxy.]{.class-0}

[When Mel Gibson made his Roman Catholic movie about "The Passion of the Christ," he was going to put in the verse where the Jews said ]{.class-0}["His blood be on us, and on our children"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 27:25), but Jewish groups like the Antidefamation League raised such a stink that he left it out. The reason for the objection was that throughout history, the Roman Catholics have used that verse to label the Jews as "Christ-killers" and to persecute them. You talk about the pot calling the kettle black! ]{.class-0}[It was Rome that carried out the execution.]{.class-1}

[But there is a sense in which the Jews were guilty of the death of Christ. Do you know what Sarah told Abraham when she couldn't have children? ]{.class-0}["I pray thee, go in unto my maid; it may be that I may obtain children BY HER"]{.class-2}[ (Gen. 16:2). That's a child ]{.class-0}[by proxy]{.class-1}[: the slave is bearing the child for the mistress. Rachel and Leah did the same thing with Jacob.]{.class-0}

[There's a place in the Old Testament where it says: ]{.class-0}["So Joab and Abishai his brother slew Abner"]{.class-2}[ (2 Sam. 3:30). Well, if you read the account of the murder in 2 Samuel 3:26--27, ]{.class-0}[Abishai wasn't anywhere around when Joab killed Abner,]{.class-1}[ but Abishai knew about it and consented to it. That's called an "accessory to the fact" in modern law. That's why Saul was guilty of the death of Stephen even though ]{.class-0}[he never picked up a rock]{.class-1}[ to throw at him (see Acts 9:1).]{.class-0}

So when it comes to the death of Christ, those Jews are guilty because they were the ones who turned Him over to the Romans to have Him killed, they were the ones shouting "Crucify Him! Crucify Him!" when Pilate wanted to let Him go, they were the ones who chose Barabbas over Jesus to be released. The Romans wouldn't have done anything to Jesus if it hadn't been for the Jews.

[Peter says the Jews crucified Him]{.class-1}[ (Acts 2:36); he says they ]{.class-0}["killed the Prince of life"]{.class-2}[ (Acts 3:15). Stephen said they were ]{.class-0}["the betrayers and murderers"]{.class-2}[ of Christ (Acts 7:52). ]{.class-0}[Paul says they killed Him]{.class-1}[ (1 Thess. 2:15). How? By being the ones to set up the whole thing.]{.class-0}

[Now, if that should lead you to think ill of that Jew and try to persecute him for the blood-guilt of Jesus Christ like a Roman Catholic, just remember this: ]{.class-0}[it was your sins that put Christ on the cross.]{.class-1}

"[Christ died for OUR SINS according to the scriptures"]{.class-2}[ (1 Cor. 15:3).]{.class-0}

"[Who his own self bare OUR SINS in his own body on the tree"]{.class-2}[ (1 Pet. 2:24).]{.class-0}

"[For Christ also hath once suffered FOR SINS, the just for THE UNJUST"]{.class-2}[ (1 Pet. 1:18).]{.class-0}

You and I are just as guilty of the death of Christ as any Jew or Roman.

[Listen, people, no Jew or Roman could take Christ's life from Him if He hadn't let him. Jesus said, ]{.class-0}["Therefore doth my Father love, me, because I lay down my life\...NO MAN TAKETH IT FROM ME, but I lay it down OF MYSELF"]{.class-2}[ (John 10:17--18). So there's no need to persecute that Jew and call him names. You are commanded to treat them as ]{.class-0}["beloved for the fathers' sakes"]{.class-2}[ (Rom. 11:28).]{.class-0}

[Back to Zechariah 12:10---]{.class-0}["\...and they shall mourn for him, as one mourneth for his only son\...." ]{.class-2}[There's the two natures of Christ right in the same verse. There's Jehovah God saying ]{.class-0}["they shall look upon ME,"]{.class-2}[ and there's that Jew looking at Christ returning as ]{.class-0}["the Son of man"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 26:64).]{.class-0}

"[\...and shall be in bitterness for him, as one that is in bitterness for his firstborn."]{.class-2}[ Christ is called ]{.class-0}["the firstborn among many brethren"]{.class-2}[ (Rom. 8:29), ]{.class-0}["the firstborn of every creature"]{.class-2}[ (Col. 1:15), and ]{.class-0}["the firstborn from the dead"]{.class-2}[ (Col. 1:18).]{.class-0}

11 In that day shall there be a great mourning in Jerusalem, as the mourning of Hadadrimmon in the valley of Megiddon.

12 And the land shall mourn, every family apart; the family of the house of David apart, and their wives apart; the family of the house of Nathan apart, and their wives apart;

13 The family of the house of Levi apart, and their wives apart; the family of Shimei apart, and their wives apart;

14 All the families that remain, every family apart, and their wives apart.

[The ]{.class-0}["mourning"]{.class-2}[ of verses 11--12 is obviously connected to the mourning of verse 10. There are two types of mourning that go on when Christ comes back. Revelation 1:7 says, ]{.class-0}["all kindreds of the earth shall wail because of him."]{.class-2}[ Those are the Gentiles following the Antichrist. They see the Lord come back right along with the Jews (see the first part of Rev. 1:7), but they are mourning because it means their end.]{.class-0}

"[And the kings of the earth, and the great men, and the rich men, and the chief captains, and the mighty men, and every bondman, and every free man, hid themselves in the dens and in the rocks of the mountains; And said to the mountains and rocks, Fall on us, and hide us from the face of him that sitteth on the throne, and from the wrath of the Lamb: For the great day of his wrath is come; and who shall be able to stand?"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 6:15--17).]{.class-0}

The Jews are repenting of rejecting their Messiah; the Gentiles are mourning over their own destruction.

[The historic reference in verse 11 to ]{.class-0}["the mourning of Hadadrimmon in the valley of Megiddon"]{.class-2}[ is to the death of Josiah in 2 Kings 23:29 and 2 Chronicles 35:22--24 (which see). Hadadrimmon is a place in ]{.class-0}[Megiddo]{.class-1}[ named after two Syrian gods (cf. 2 Kings 5:18).]{.class-0}

[But the context obviously puts the application beyond anything in Kings or Chronicles. ]{.class-0}["The valley of Megiddon]{.class-2}[ \[Megiddo\]]{.class-0}["]{.class-2}[ is where Armageddon is located (Rev. 16:16). Armageddon is the high ground in the ]{.class-0}[valley of Megiddo]{.class-1}[. The name ]{.class-0}[Armageddon]{.class-1}[ means "hill of the crowded" because in the near future, 200 million UN troops will be gathered there to fight against their Creator (Rev. 9:14--16, 16:12--16, 19:11--19). Napoleon said, "All the armies of the world could maneuver their forces on this vast plain."]{.class-0}

[The valley of Megiddo is called ]{.class-0}["the valley of Jehoshaphat"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["the valley of decision"]{.class-2}[ in Joel 3:11--14 (see the comments on Joel 3:13 in Vol. I of this Commentary); you will also see it called "the plain of Esdraelon," which is the Greek form of ]{.class-0}["the valley of Jezreel"]{.class-2}[ (see Hos. 1:5). It runs from the north side of Mt. Carmel down south and east to the Jordan River, with the Kishon River running through it.]{.class-0}

[That's where ]{.class-0}[Elijah]{.class-1}[ cut off the heads of the prophets of Baal in 1 Kings 18 (see the comments on Hab. 3:13). That's where ]{.class-0}[Gideon]{.class-1}[ fought with the Midianites and the Amalekites (Judg. 6--7; see the remarks on Gideon under vs. 4). That's where ]{.class-0}[Deborah and Barak]{.class-1}[ fought with Sisera (Judg. 5:13--14). Those are all pictures of the Second Advent. In fact, look at Judges 5:19--20.]{.class-0}

"[The kings came and fought, then fought the kings of Canaan in Taanach by the waters of Megiddo\...."]{.class-2}[ ]{.class-0}[No, they didn't]{.class-1}[. The ]{.class-0}["king]{.class-2}[ \[singular\]]{.class-0}[ of Canaan"]{.class-2}[ was Jabin (Judg. 4:2); Sisera was only the captain of the host. There were no ]{.class-0}["kings"]{.class-2}[ fighting in Judges 4, but there will be at the Battle of Armageddon (Rev. 16:12--14, 17:12--14, 19:19). ]{.class-0}["\...They fought from heaven; the stars in the courses fought against Sisera."]{.class-2}[ Well, not in Judges 4, no; but at the ]{.class-0}[Second Advent]{.class-1}[ there will be (2 Thess. 1:7--8 cf. Rev. 1:20; Job 38:7).]{.class-0}

[Do you see what's going on here? I took you from Zechariah in 487 B.C. to Revelation in A.D. 96 and then back to Judges in 1296 B.C. ]{.class-0}[That's three separate authors stretching over a period of almost 1,400 years.]{.class-1}[ There's no way they could have been in collusion with each other. The author of Judges had no way of knowing what Zechariah and John would write, and there is no making sense of Deborah's song without Paul, John, Zechariah, and Job. ]{.class-0}[Whoever the real author of that Book is, He's sitting up in the future past the year 3000 writing His material back into A.D. 96, 487 B.C., and 1296 B.C.]{.class-1}

[Now, the mourning of the Jews in verses 12--14 is broken down into separate groups. There is the family of ]{.class-0}[David]{.class-1}[ (cf. 2 Sam. 4:2--5, 5:13--16), the family of ]{.class-0}[Nathan]{.class-1}[ (this could refer to the prophet Nathan \[2 Sam. 7; 1 Kings 1\] or one of David's sons \[Luke 3:31\]), the ]{.class-0}[Levites,]{.class-1}[ the family of ]{.class-0}[Shimei]{.class-1}[ (2 Sam. 16:5--13; 2 Kings 2:36--46; Esth. 2:5), and the ]{.class-0}[rest of the people.]{.class-1}[ You say, "What's that all about?" All I can tell you is that I haven't got any idea in this world; I couldn't apply that practically or doctrinally if my life depended on it. I've gone through the Bible over 150 times, and I've never found any answer to it. There it is---if you find out what it means, let me know, will ya?]{.class-0}

[]{#section-0_1_c17fe3340cd3e5be_b699.xhtml}

CHAPTER THIRTEEN

13:1 In that day there shall be a fountain opened to the house of David and to the inhabitants of Jerusalem for sin and for uncleanness.

[Chapter 13 is a continuation of ]{.class-0}["the burden"]{.class-2}[ of chapter 12. The previous chapter ended with the remnant of the Jews in Jerusalem in mourning and repenting for rejecting their Messiah. The setting is the ]{.class-0}[Second Advent,]{.class-1}[ not Jews being saved in the Church Age (]{.class-0}["in that day"]{.class-2}[---12:3, 4, 6, 8, 9, 11; 13:1, 2, 4 cf. 14:1, 4).]{.class-0}

[The Lord's response to that mourning is found in verse 1---]{.class-0}["there shall be a fountain opened to the house of David and to the inhabitants of Jerusalem for sin and for uncleanness."]{.class-2}

We sing: "There is a fountain filled with blood Drawn from Immanuel's veins, And sinners plunged beneath that flood Lose all their guilty stains"; and "There is a Fountain opened for my cleansing, where sin's atonement by my Lord was made; He was the Lamb that was led to the slaughter, His blood the fountain where my debt was paid"; and "O now I see the cleansing wave, The fountain deep and wide: Jesus, my Lord, mighty to save, Points to His wounded side." In the little children's chorus "Deep and Wide," one of the verses goes: "For you, for me, at Calvary There's a fountain flowing deep and wide."

[All of that is true ]{.class-0}[spiritually.]{.class-1}[ First John 1:7 says, ]{.class-0}["the blood of Jesus Christ his Son cleanseth us from all sin."]{.class-2}[ But here in verse 1 of Zechariah 13, the ]{.class-0}["fountain"]{.class-2}[ is not a spiritual fountain of Christ's blood; it is a ]{.class-0}[physical]{.class-1}[ fountain of living water.]{.class-0}

[Revelation 7:17, ]{.class-0}["For the Lamb which is in the midst of the throne shall feed them, and shall lead them unto living fountains of waters."]{.class-2}

[Over in Ezekiel 36 where those Jews are given ]{.class-0}["a new heart"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["a new spirit"]{.class-2}[ at the Second Advent of Christ (Ezek. 36:26), the verse right before it says, ]{.class-0}["Then will I sprinkle clean water upon you, and ye shall be clean: from all your filthiness, and from all your idols, will I cleanse you"]{.class-2}[ (Ezek. 36:25).]{.class-0}

[That's where the Roman Catholic Church got its baloney about Roman Catholic water baptism---]{.class-0}[sprinkling---]{.class-1}[removing "original sin." It's an Old Testament thing where a person is ]{.class-0}[purified with water]{.class-1}[ (see John 3:25, 2:6). Under the Old Testament system, that was a ritualistic cleansing from sin. That's why Ananias told Saul, ]{.class-0}["arise and be baptized, and wash away thy sins"]{.class-2}[ (Acts 22:16); as a Jew, ]{.class-0}[Ananias]{.class-1}[ thought water had something to do with getting rid of sin.]{.class-0}

[Now that's not your case. Your sins are taken away the moment you receive the New Birth (Tit. 3:5---]{.class-0}["the washing of REGENERATION,"]{.class-2}[ ]{.class-0}[not water]{.class-1}[). Water is connected with the ]{.class-0}[first birth,]{.class-1}[ not the second (John 3:5--6).]{.class-0}

[For the New Testament Christian after the Pauline revelation (Gal. 1:11--12), ]{.class-0}[water baptism]{.class-1}[ has nothing to do with your salvation (Acts 15:11) or your New Birth (Acts 10:47); it is only a ]{.class-0}["figure."]{.class-2}

[Second Peter 3:21---]{.class-0}["The like FIGURE\...."]{.class-2}[ Well, if it's a figure, then it's NOT the actual thing. ]{.class-0}["\...whereunto even baptism doth also now save us\...."]{.class-2}[ How? By washing away the moral filth of sin (see 2 Cor. 7:1; James 1:21; 2 Pet. 2:7; Rev. 22:11)? Not on your life, brother! ]{.class-0}["\...NOT the putting away of the filth of the flesh, but the answer of a good conscience toward God."]{.class-2}

[Every Campbellite in this world will quote ]{.class-0}[one quarter]{.class-1}[ of the verse to get you into a waterdog bath tub: they will quote the verse as ]{.class-0}["baptism doth also now save us."]{.class-2}[ That's not what the verse said. It's a ]{.class-0}["figure"]{.class-2}[ of your salvation.]{.class-0}

[What's it a figure of? It's a figure of Christ's ]{.class-0}[death, burial,]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[resurrection]{.class-1}[ (see Rom. 6:3--4). Of course, water baptism isn't anywhere in Romans 6, but the ]{.class-0}[figure]{.class-1}[ is still there. You get baptized in water as a testimony that you have already been baptized by the Holy Spirit into the Body of Christ at salvation (Acts 10:47 cf. 1 Cor. 12:13; Gal. 3:27); you are showing that you have already been ]{.class-0}["buried with him in baptism, wherein also ye are risen with him through the faith of the operation of God, who hath raised Him from the dead"]{.class-2}[ (Col. 2:12).]{.class-0}

[But that Old Testament purification by water was a ]{.class-0}[picture]{.class-1}[ of what will happen in Ezekiel 36:25. That fountain is a river that flows out of the Millennial Temple down to the Dead Sea and returns life to it (see Ezek. 47:1--12). ]{.class-0}[That river is a precursor to the River of Life in the New Jerusalem]{.class-1}[ (see Rev. 21:6--7, 22:1--2).]{.class-0}

2 And it shall come to pass in that day, saith the LORD of hosts, that I will cut off the names of the idols out of the land, and they shall no more be remembered: and also I will cause the prophets and the unclean spirit to pass out of the land.

3 And it shall come to pass, that when any shall yet prophesy, then his father and his mother that begat him shall say unto him, Thou shalt not live; for thou speakest lies in the name of the LORD: and his father and his mother that begat him shall thrust him through when he prophesieth.

4 And it shall come to pass in that day, that the prophets shall be ashamed every one of his vision, when he hath prophesied; neither shall they wear a rough garment to deceive:

5 But he shall say, I am no prophet, I am an husbandman; for man taught me to keep cattle from my youth.

[When Jesus returns, He gets rid of three things out of the land of Palestine: ]{.class-0}["I will cut off the names of the idols out of the land ]{.class-2}[\[]{.class-0}[there go "Mary" and the Pope]{.class-1}[\]]{.class-0}[\...and also I will cause the prophets and the unclean spirit to pass out of the land]{.class-2}[ \[]{.class-0}[and there go Mohammed and "Allah"]{.class-1}[\]]{.class-0}["]{.class-2}[ (vs. 2).]{.class-0}

[During the Millennium, the ministry of the prophets is over. If someone claims to be prophesying, he is to be ]{.class-0}[killed]{.class-1}[ by his own parents (which is where Mohammed got his "honor killings"; ]{.class-0}[he stole that from the Old Testament too]{.class-1}[---see the remarks under Hag. 1:8). The reason is that Jesus Christ is right there in the Temple on the throne.]{.class-0}

[In reference to the ]{.class-0}["new covenant"]{.class-2}[ the Lord makes with that Jew during the Millennium, Jeremiah 31:34 says, ]{.class-0}["And they shall teach no more every man his neighbour, and every man his brother, saying, Know the LORD: for they shall all know me, from the least of them unto the greatest of them, saith the LORD: for I will forgive their iniquity, and I will remember their sin no more."]{.class-2}

[In the Millennium, no one lives by faith; everybody lives by works. The famous definition of faith in Hebrews 11:1 says, ]{.class-0}["Now faith is the substance of things HOPED FOR, the evidence of things NOT SEEN."]{.class-2}[ Nobody in the Millennium is "hoping" for anything. Christ has come back, and they can ]{.class-0}[see]{.class-1}[ Him. If anybody wants to know what "the word of the Lord" is, all they have to do is ]{.class-0}[go ask Him]{.class-1}[ (Isa. 2:3). As a matter of fact, the Gentile nations are ]{.class-0}[required]{.class-1}[ to go see Him every year at the Feast of Tabernacles (Zech. 14:16).]{.class-0}

Now that explains a passage over in 1 Corinthians 13 that has been obscured by John R. Rice and the Fundamentalists.

"[Charity never faileth: but whether there be prophecies, they shall fail ]{.class-2}[\[just as you read it in Zech. 13\]]{.class-0}[\...For we know in part, and we prophesy in part. But when that which is perfect is come\..."]{.class-2}[ (1 Cor. 13:8--10).]{.class-0}

[And that's where the Fundamentalists lose it. They define ]{.class-0}["that which is perfect"]{.class-2}[ as the completion of the New Testament. But look down at verse 12 in 1 Corinthians 12: ]{.class-0}["For now we see through a glass darkly; but then ]{.class-2}[\[when ]{.class-0}["that which is perfect is come"]{.class-2}[\] ]{.class-0}[FACE TO FACE."]{.class-2}

Then it couldn't possibly have anything to do with the Scriptures. Look at 2 John 12.

"[Having many things to write unto you, I would not write with paper and ink ]{.class-2}[\[there's the Scriptures---]{.class-0}["paper and ink"]{.class-2}[\]]{.class-0}[: but I trust to come unto you, and speak FACE TO FACE."]{.class-2}

[So ]{.class-0}["face to face"]{.class-2}[ is someone showing up ]{.class-0}[in person,]{.class-1}[ not through the Scriptures. ]{.class-0}["That which is perfect"]{.class-2}[ would then have to be Jesus Christ coming back and showing up ]{.class-0}["face to face"]{.class-2}[; that's when ]{.class-0}["that which is in part ]{.class-2}[\[prophecy in 1 Cor. 13:9\]]{.class-0}[ shall be done away"]{.class-2}[ (1 Cor. 13:10). Right now, any Christian can prophesy because ]{.class-0}["the testimony of Jesus is the spirit of prophecy"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 19:10). Of course, our prophesying is not extra-Scriptural, but if you know where you're going when you die (1 John 5:13), then you are certainly prophesying.]{.class-0}

"[Neither shall they wear a rough garment to deceive"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 4). The idea is that the false prophets are dressing like the real prophets in order ]{.class-0}["to deceive"]{.class-2}[ folks. Both John the Baptist and Elijah dressed with a leather girdle around their waists (Mark 1:6; 1 Kings 1:8). The old advertisement for Camel cigarettes was: "I'd walk a mile for a Camel." Well, John the Baptist would ]{.class-0}[walk a mile for a camel]{.class-1}[ because that's where he got his ]{.class-0}[clothes]{.class-1}[. Jesus said John didn't dress in ]{.class-0}["soft clothing"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 11:8); that means that he dressed in ]{.class-0}["a rough garment."]{.class-2}

"[Neither shall they wear a rough garment to deceive."]{.class-2}[ One of those fellows back in the Old Testament had a guy sock him, and then he smeared ashes all over his face to disguise himself so he could trap Ahab in his own words (1 Kings 20:35--43).]{.class-0}

[The irony of verse 5 should not be lost on the reader. Here's a fellow saying, "I'm not a prophet; I'm a ]{.class-0}['husbandman']{.class-2}[ (that's another name for a farmer) and a cattle herder." That's the exact opposite of what Amos said.]{.class-0}

"[Then answered Amos, and said to Amaziah, I was no prophet, neither was I a prophet's son; but I was an herdman, and a gatherer of sycamore fruit: And the LORD took me as I followed the flock, and the LORD said unto me, Go prophesy unto my people Israel"]{.class-2}[ (Amos 7:14--15).]{.class-0}

[A bunch of people in the Bible are like that. Moses was a shepherd before God called him; David was a shepherd before God called him; Peter, James, and John were fishermen. Well, in the Millennium, ]{.class-0}[God doesn't call anyone to be a prophet;]{.class-1}[ everyone's back at the old occupations.]{.class-0}

6 And one shall say unto him, What are these wounds in thine hands? Then he shall answer, Those with which I was wounded in the house of my friends.

7 Awake, O sword, against my shepherd, and against the man that is my fellow, saith the LORD of hosts: smite the shepherd, and the sheep shall be scattered: and I will turn mine hand upon the little ones.

Here's another situation like the one we saw in 9:9--11 (see the comments on that passage). The First Advent shows up right in the middle of a chapter on the Second Coming. God does that to mess up all the scholars who won't rightly divide that Book.

[Verse 6 hearkens back to 12:10. The one who was ]{.class-0}["pierced"]{.class-2}[ was wounded in the ]{.class-0}["hands."]{.class-2}[ The Rabbis will do everything they can to prevent a Jew from reading passages like this because there's absolutely no doubt about whom is being referred to: it's Jesus Christ. A few years back, the United Bible Societies made a deal with the chief Rabbis in Israel: the Rabbis would allow the national Bible society to import and distribute the Old Testament Scriptures in Hebrew ]{.class-0}[as long as Psalm 22 and Isaiah 53 were REMOVED from the text.]{.class-1}[ Both those chapters are on the blood atonement of Jesus Christ for sinners, and anyone reading the passages is able to make the connection. The Rabbis didn't want their congregations reading those chapters because "they would confuse them" (don't you know, honey!).]{.class-0}

[The ]{.class-0}[RSV]{.class-1}[ of the Communist National Council of Churches reads: ]{.class-0}["What are those wounds on your BACK?"]{.class-1}

[How about the ]{.class-0}[English Standard Version,]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-16}[the "Conservative" revision of the ]{.class-0}[RSV?]{.class-1}[ Surely the Conservatives won't be like those "Liberals" in the NCC, will they? Wanna bet? ]{.class-0}[ESV]{.class-1}[---]{.class-0}["What are these wounds on your back?"]{.class-1}

[Kenneth Taylor's ]{.class-0}["Living" Bible]{.class-1}[ reads: ]{.class-0}["What are these scars on your chest and back?"]{.class-1}[ Well, that's just a "paraphrase," so let's look at what Tyndale House (the publishers of ]{.class-0}[TLB]{.class-1}[) replaced it with---the ]{.class-0}[New Living Translation. NLT---"What about those wounds on your chest?]{.class-1}

NIV---"What are those wounds on your BODY?"

[NASV---"What are these wounds between your arms?"]{.class-1}[ (Even an edition of the ]{.class-0}["New" King James Version]{.class-1}[ reads with the ]{.class-0}[NASV,]{.class-1}[ showing you that the ]{.class-0}[NKJV]{.class-1}[ is NOT a revision of the ]{.class-0}[KJV,]{.class-1}[ but the readings of the ]{.class-0}[RSV]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[ASV]{.class-1}[ smuggled into the ]{.class-0}[King James]{.class-1}[ text.)]{.class-0}

[My, my, how much "clearer" that is; how much more "light" we now have on the passage! It's not like that old, archaic ]{.class-0}[King James Version]{.class-1}[ that obscures things with its Elizabethan English, is it? Just think of the horrible error we could have fallen into if we had believed our ]{.class-0}[King James]{.class-1}[ Bibles as they stood. Why, we might have actually thought that Zechariah was prophesying about the NAIL PRINTS IN CHRIST'S HANDS! Thank you, ]{.class-0}[ESV]{.class-1}[, ]{.class-0}[RSV, NASV, NLT, NIV, ]{.class-1}[etc., for saving us from such an "erroneous interpretation" (see App. XII of the ]{.class-0}[Bible Believer's Commentary on the Pastoral Epistles]{.class-1}[).]{.class-0}

"[Then he shall answer, Those with which I was wounded in the house of my friends"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 6). John 1:11 says, ]{.class-0}["He came unto his own]{.class-2}[ \[the Jews\]]{.class-0}[, and his own received him not." ]{.class-2}[Do you know what Jesus called Judas when Judas betrayed the Lord with a kiss? He said, ]{.class-0}["Friend, wherefore art thou come?"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 26:59). The prophecy on that is found in Psalm 41:9---]{.class-0}["Yea, mine own familiar friend, in whom I trusted, which did eat of my bread, hath lifted up his heel against me."]{.class-2}

Verse 7 was quoted by Christ in reference to His betrayal and arrest in the Garden of Gethsemane.

"[Then saith Jesus unto them, All ye shall be offended because of me this night: for it is written, I will smite the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock shall be scattered abroad"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 26:31).]{.class-0}

[But notice what Christ left out: ]{.class-0}["and I will turn mine hand upon the little ones."]{.class-2}[ That wasn't fulfilled in Gethsemane. But when Jesus stood before Pilate, those Jews said, ]{.class-0}["His blood be on us, and on our CHILDREN"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 27:25). There are the ]{.class-0}["little ones"]{.class-2}[ on whom the Lord turned His hand.]{.class-0}

I've got pictures of little boys, four, five, six, and seven years old, starving to death in the streets of Warsaw; they are lying down in the gutter to die with people just walking by and looking at them as those poor, emaciated children fall over. I have accounts where those German guards in the concentration camps would take a Jewish baby and pitch it in a fire and then tell the mother, "If you were a good mother, you'd jump in the fire too, if you loved your baby."

[I have case after case, documented by eyewitnesses with their names given, where when the Germans would clean out a ghetto, they would take babies from those Jewish mothers and throw them up in the air and catch them on bayonets; they would pull babies apart by the legs right in front of the mothers. In Deuteronomy 28:53--57, God told those Jews they would end up ]{.class-0}[eating their own children.]{.class-1}[ That took place (2 Kings 6:28--29; Lam. 22:20), and it will again when the Antichrist shuts off the food to those who don't take the mark (Rev. 13:16--17).]{.class-0}

8 And it shall come to pass, that in all the land, saith the LORD, two parts therein shall be cut off and die; but the third shall be left therein.

9 And I will bring the third part through the fire, and will refine them as silver is refined, and will try them as gold is tried: they shall call on my name, and I will hear them: I will say, It is my people: and they shall say, The LORD is my God.

"[Two parts therein shall be cut off and die"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 8). See the comments on Zephaniah 1:2--3 and note especially our remarks on Isaiah 6:13.]{.class-0}

"[And I will bring the third part through the fire"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 9). As I stated in my comments on Habakkuk 2:18--19 (which see), Daniel 3 is going to be repeated, except this time, it will be played out by the Antichrist and the Jews in the Tribulation who won't worship his image.]{.class-0}

"[And will refine them as silver is refined, and will try them as gold is tried."]{.class-2}[ The cross-references are Malachi 3:2--3 and Job 23:10, and you can see the comments on those two passages in their respective Commentaries.]{.class-0}

"[They shall call on my name, and I will hear them."]{.class-2}[ See the comments on Joel 2:32 in Volume I of this Commentary.]{.class-0}

"[I will say, It is my people: and they shall say, The LORD is my God."]{.class-2}[ See the comments on Hosea 2:23, 1:10 in Volume I of this Commentary, and in Romans 9:25--26 of that Commentary.]{.class-0}

The chapter ends with the total, complete, permanent restoration of the Jewish nation to the supreme position among the nations; the next chapter opens with the destruction of every Gentile nation in the UN and the relegation of those nations to an inferior, subordinate position to one nation and one city---the nation of Israel with Jerusalem as the capital of a Jewish State. And as we shall see in Zechariah 14:21, God's going to get rid of every Moslem, Arab, Jordanian, and "Palestinian" out of the land. Allah and Mohammed will be OUT; that's O-U-T; OUT!

[]{#section-0_1_649da4fa9811fbc5_b69a.xhtml}

CHAPTER FOURTEEN

[The theme of this chapter is the main theme of the entire Bible: ]{.class-1}[it is the Second Advent of Jesus Christ. The]{.class-0}[ main theme]{.class-1}[ of the Bible is ]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ salvation. Of course, we thank God that it is in there; but when it comes to the space committed to a particular subject, God is more interested in the government of the world than He is your individual salvation.]{.class-0}

[Now don't get me wrong. I am thankful Jesus Christ came and died for a sinner like me. I appreciate it, and I wouldn't have missed it for nothin', man. Nobody had to coax me to get saved. The ]{.class-0}[first time ]{.class-1}[I heard the Gospel preached, I got saved. Nobody had to sing two stanzas for me; it was "Just as I am without one plea," and I was down the aisle and to the altar.]{.class-0}

[So don't get me wrong;]{.class-0}[ I'm not putting down salvation]{.class-1}[. That's the important thing for ]{.class-0}[us]{.class-1}[, but it's not the most important thing to ]{.class-0}[God]{.class-1}[. The most important thing to the Father was ]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ the day when wicked men took His Son and nailed Him to a cross; the most important thing to God the Father is the day ]{.class-0}[when His Son gets what's coming to Him.]{.class-1}[ That day is ]{.class-0}["the day of the LORD,"]{.class-2}[ so without further ado, let's get into one of the most definitive chapters in the Bible on the Second Coming of Jesus Christ. You will find more details in ]{.class-0}[this chapter ]{.class-1}[on that subject than you will find in Revelation 19--20 on the same material.]{.class-0}

14:1 Behold, the day of the LORD cometh, and thy spoil shall be divided in the midst of thee.

2 For I will gather all nations against Jerusalem to battle; and the city shall be taken, and the houses rifled, and the women ravished; and half of the city shall go forth into captivity, and the residue of the people shall not be cut off from the city.

3 Then shall the LORD go forth, and fight against those nations, as when he fought in the day of battle.

Now we have already commented about the gathering of the nations against Jerusalem under Zephaniah 3:8 (which see). Jerusalem will be taken and spoiled by the Antichrist just as it was under Nebuchadnezzar (586 B.C.) and Titus (A.D. 70), which is why the destruction of Jerusalem is recorded three times in your Bible (2 Kings 25; 2 Chron. 36; Jer. 52).

"[The houses rifled, and the women ravished" ]{.class-2}[(vs. 2). The word ]{.class-0}[rifle]{.class-1}[ means "to gouge a thing out." As it is used here, the armies of the Antichrist are going into the houses and taking what they want (like the IRS, FBI, DEA, and BATF do in the USSA---the "United Search and Seizure of Assets"); ]{.class-0}[it's another word for "plundered."]{.class-1}

[In the passage, the houses are being plundered, and the women are being raped. ]{.class-0}[Those are two marks of Moslem "jihad." ]{.class-1}[A Moslem is not only allowed to go to war against Jews and Christians to convert them to Islam at sword point (Suras 2:193, 9:5, 29), but also to take whatever ]{.class-0}[spoil]{.class-1}[ he can as a reward from Allah (Suras 48:19--2, 59:7). The women taken in such campaigns can be made into ]{.class-0}[sex slaves]{.class-1}[ (Suras 33:50, 70:30). Mohammed would tell all of his followers before a military campaign, "You see, God (]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ the God of the Bible; he's talking about "the god" \[Allah\] about whom you read in 2 Cor. 4:4 and Dan 11:38) will soon make you inherit their land, their treasure, ]{.class-0}[and make you sleep with their women." ]{.class-1}[That wasn't written by some Christian or Jew; it was recorded by a faithful Arab ]{.class-0}[Mohammedan]{.class-1}[ (Al-Rawd Al-Anf, ]{.class-0}[Biography of Muhammad,]{.class-1}[ Vol. 2, pg. 182).]{.class-0}

[After killing her father and brother, Mohammed offered to make one Jewish woman his wife because of her beauty. She declined and told him, "I'd rather you keep me as a slave in your possession, which would be easier for you and me."]{.class-0}[ So she spent the rest of her life being raped at will when Mohammed's other fourteen wives and ten concubines couldn't satiate his lusts]{.class-1}[ (see ]{.class-0}[Will the Real Allah Please Stand Up?]{.class-1}[, Ruckman, 2010, pp. 83--84).]{.class-0}

[That's the religion that the Federal government is currently teaching in the public schools after throwing the Bible and prayer out because it was a violation of the "separation of Church and State."]{.class-1}[ The Defense Department has special rules for how to handle the ]{.class-0}[Koran]{.class-1}[ for Moslem prisoners ]{.class-0}[taken in combat]{.class-1}[, but they will ]{.class-0}[burn New Testaments]{.class-1}[ so as not to "offend" a bunch of murdering fornicators. To appease such thin-skinned reprobates, your President (]{.class-0}[yours,]{.class-1}[ not mine) went overseas and ]{.class-0}[bowed]{.class-1}[ before a Moslem King as an act of submission. Then he told those people that America was no longer a "Christian nation," but it was the largest ]{.class-0}[Islamic nation]{.class-1}[ in the world. That is, even though the number of Moslems in the United States ranks somewhere around ]{.class-0}[23rd ]{.class-1}[in the entire world, the Mohammedans have claimed the ]{.class-0}[entire country ]{.class-1}[as their own, even though 85% of Americans are professing Christians. Folks, this country is headed for hell on wheels if the Lord tarries, I kid you not. (Psa. 9:17).]{.class-0}

"[And half of the city shall go forth into captivity, and the residue of the people shall not be cut off from the city." ]{.class-2}[So half the population of Jerusalem will be taken, and half will be left. At this writing (2011), Obama is pressuring Israel to return to the boundaries it had before the Six Day War. That would make Jerusalem a ]{.class-0}[split city]{.class-1}[ between the Jews and the Moslems with both sides claiming it as their capital, but with the UN only recognizing ]{.class-0}[one side]{.class-1}[---the Mohammedans.]{.class-0}

[Of course, no Moslem is going to be content with only half of Jerusalem. There is one goal of the PLO (now called the "PA" \[Palestinian Authority\], but still controlled by a ]{.class-0}[terrorist organization]{.class-1}[---Hamas) that has never been abrogated or changed. The goal of every Moslem in "Palestine" is to drive the Jews into the Mediterranean Sea and make Palestine ]{.class-0}["Judenrein"]{.class-1}[ ("free of all Jews").]{.class-0}

[There is no "two-State solution" to the Middle East.]{.class-1}[ The Arab Palestinians will ]{.class-0}[never]{.class-1}[ recognize a Jewish State, and if perchance they do, it will only be long enough to re-arm (cf. Dan. 11:27). As anyone who has actually read the ]{.class-0}["Holy Koran"]{.class-1}[ knows, it is perfectly all right for a Moslem to ]{.class-0}[lie]{.class-1}[ under oath or to ]{.class-0}[break]{.class-1}[ an oath if it is to his advantage in order to advance the cause of Allah (Suras 24:11, 5:89, 2:225).]{.class-0}

[Verse 3: ]{.class-0}["Then shall the LORD go forth, and fight against those nations."]{.class-2}[ Exodus 15:3 says, ]{.class-0}["The LORD is a man of war," ]{.class-2}[and lookee what shows up right in the context of that statement.]{.class-0}

[Exodus 15:14, ]{.class-0}["The people shall hear, and be afraid: sorrow shall take hold on the inhabitants of PALESTINA."]{.class-2}

There are the "Palestinians" right in the passage. Exodus 15 says that God is going to war with every "Palestinian" in "Palestine." Are you a "Palestinian"? Well, if you are, you had better get out of Palestine before your Creator runs you out.

"[As when he fought in the day of battle." ]{.class-2}[Do you know what the Lord did the last time He fought against the "Palestinians"? Why, that's Joshua 10:13--14.]{.class-0}

"[And the sun stood still, and the moon stayed, until the people had avenged themselves upon their enemies\....So the sun stood still in the midst of heaven, and hasted not to go down about a whole day. And there was no day like that before it of after it, that the LORD hearkened unto the voice of a man: for the LORD fought for Israel."]{.class-2}

[When the Lord ]{.class-0}["fought in the day of battle," ]{.class-2}[the sun and the moon ]{.class-0}["stood still"]{.class-2}[ exactly as it will at the Second Advent (see the comments on Hab 3:11).]{.class-0}

[Now when the Bible says ]{.class-0}["The LORD is a MAN of war,"]{.class-2}[ that turns out to be ]{.class-0}[literally true]{.class-1}[. When God decides to settle the controversy with His creation by armed conflict, He does it as ]{.class-0}["God\...manifest in the flesh"]{.class-2}[ (1 Tim. 3:16)---the Lord Jesus Christ.]{.class-0}

"[And I saw heaven opened, and behold a white horse; and he that sat upon him was called Faithful and True, and in righteousness he doth judge and make war\....And he was clothed with a vesture dipped in blood: and his name is called The Word of God" ]{.class-2}[(Rev. 19:11,13).]{.class-0}

[John 1:14 says, ]{.class-0}["And the Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us, (and we beheld his glory, the glory as of the only begotten of the Father,) full of grace and truth."]{.class-2}

[That's Jesus Christ, the God-man. That's the One about whom the little children sing, "Jesus loves me. This I know, for the Bible tells me so." Not then, boy. ]{.class-0}[When He comes back, He's going to stomp on 200 million UN troops (Rev. 9:16) until the blood flows something like five feet deep for 200 miles]{.class-1}[ (Rev. 14:20).]{.class-0}

[Isaiah 63:1--6 says, ]{.class-0}["Who is this that cometh from Edom, with dyed garments from Bozrah ]{.class-2}[\[see the comments on Hab. 3:3\]]{.class-0}[?\...Wherefore art thou red in thine apparel, and thy garments like him that treadeth in the winefat? I have trodden the winepress alone; and of the people there was none with me: for I will tread them in mine anger, and trample them in my fury; and their blood shall be sprinkled upon my garments, and I will stain all my raiment. For the day of vengeance is in mine heart, and the year of my redeemed is come\....And I will tread down the people in mine anger, and make them drunk in my fury, and I will bring down their strength to the earth."]{.class-2}

[That's the One who ]{.class-0}[loved them]{.class-1}[ enough to ]{.class-0}[die]{.class-1}[ for them and ]{.class-0}[shed His blood]{.class-1}[ for them. They turned Him down (2 Thess. 1:8), so now He's going to shed theirs. "Even Steven"; "tit for tat."]{.class-0}

[God tells the nations, ]{.class-0}["Proclaim ye this among the Gentiles; Prepare war, wake up the mighty men, let all the men of war draw near; let them come up: Beat your plowshares into swords, and your pruning hooks into spears ]{.class-2}[\[that's what the UN should have put on their "Isaiah Wall"; it should have been a "Joel Wall." Since its founding in 1945, ]{.class-0}[the UN has sponsored 177 wars around the globe]{.class-1}[\]\....]{.class-0}[Assemble yourselves ]{.class-2}[\[see the comments on Zeph. 3:8\], ]{.class-0}[and come, all ye heathen, and gather yourselves together round about: thither cause thy mighty ones to come down, O LORD" ]{.class-2}[(Joel 3:9--11).]{.class-0}

[Joel 2:11 says, ]{.class-0}["And the LORD shall utter his voice before his ARMY: for his camp is very great: for he is strong that executeth his word: for the day of the LORD is great and very terrible; and who can abide it?"]{.class-2}

[Isaiah 34 says, ]{.class-0}["For the indignation of the LORD is upon all nations, and his fury upon all their armies: he hath utterly destroyed them ]{.class-2}[\[ a prophecy in the past tense just like you see over in Isa. 53\], ]{.class-0}[he hath delivered them to the slaughter\....For my sword shall be bathed in heaven: behold, it shall come down upon Idumea ]{.class-2}[\[that's Edom---Isa. 63:1\]\...]{.class-0}[The sword of the LORD is filled with blood\...for the LORD hath a sacrifice in Bozrah ]{.class-2}[\[Isa. 63:1\]]{.class-0}[, and a great slaughter in the land of Idumea\....For it is the day of the LORD'S vengeance ]{.class-2}[\[that's what you just read over in Isa. 63:4\]]{.class-0}[, and the year of recompenses for the controversy of Zion ]{.class-2}[\[see the comments on 12:2--3\]]{.class-0}[" ]{.class-2}[(Isa. 34:2, 5--6, 8).]{.class-0}

[The question is: "Who gets Jerusalem?" (see the remarks on the last part of vs. 2). The Bushs, the Clintons, and Obama say "Half Allah, half Jehovah; half Moses, half Mohammed." The Lord God Almighty says, "It's MY city (Matt. 5:35), and I'm giving the whole cotton pickin' thing to MY chosen people" (and they sure ain't any Moslems or Arabs like ]{.class-0}[Mohammed]{.class-1}[ taught; see ]{.class-0}[Will The Real Allah Please Stand Up?, ]{.class-1}[2011, pg. 81). Look at Jeremiah 33:14--16.]{.class-0}

"[Behold, the days come, saith the LORD, that I will perform that good thing which I have promised unto the HOUSE OF ISRAEL and to the HOUSE OF JUDAH ]{.class-2}[\[Mohammed and the Pope are OUT!\]]{.class-0}[. In those days, and at that time will I cause the Branch of righteousness to grow up unto David ]{.class-2}[\[Jesus Christ---see the comments on Zech. 3:8\]]{.class-0}[;]{.class-2}[ ]{.class-0}[and he shall execute judgment and righteousness in the land. In those days shall Judah be saved, and Jerusalem shall dwell safely: and this is the name wherewith she shall be called, The LORD ]{.class-2}[\[]{.class-0}[Jehovah]{.class-1}[, NOT Allah\] ]{.class-0}[our righteousness."]{.class-2}

[That thing is all through the Old Testament. Like I said, there is more in the ]{.class-0}[Old Testament ]{.class-1}[about the ]{.class-0}[Second Coming of Christ]{.class-1}[ than you will find in the book of Revelation.]{.class-0}

[Psalm 118:10--11 says, ]{.class-0}["All nations compassed me about ]{.class-2}[\[just like you read in Zech. 14:2\]]{.class-0}[: but in the name of the LORD will I destroy them. They compassed me about; yea, they compassed me about: but in the name of the LORD I will destroy them."]{.class-2}

[Isaiah 29:7--9, ]{.class-0}["And the multitude of all the nations that fight against Ariel]{.class-2}[ \[the name means "lion of God"; it's another name for Jerusalem\]]{.class-0}[, even all that fight against her and her munition, and that distress her, shall be as a dream of a night vision. It shall even be as when an hungry man dreameth, and, behold, he drinketh; but he awaketh, and behold, he is faint, and his soul hath appetite: so shall the multitude of all the nations be, that fight against mount Zion."]{.class-2}

That's only a few of the references; there are 500 altogether in the Old Testament.

4 And his feet shall stand in that day upon the mount of Olives, which is before Jerusalem on the east, and the mount of Olives shall cleave in the midst thereof toward the east and toward the west, and there shall be a very great valley; and half of the mountain shall remove toward the north, and half of it toward the south.

5 And ye shall flee to the valley of the mountains; for the valley of the mountains shall reach unto Azal: yea, ye shall flee, like as ye fled from before the earthquake in the days of Uzziah king of Judah: and the LORD my God shall come, and all the saints with thee.

6 And it shall come to pass in that day, that the light shall not be clear, nor dark:

7 But it shall be one day which shall be known to the LORD, not day, nor night: but it shall come to pass, that at evening time it shall be light.

"[And his feet shall stand in that day upon the mount of Olives" ]{.class-2}[(vs. 4). Job said, ]{.class-0}["For I know that my redeemer liveth, and that he shall stand at the latter day upon the earth" ]{.class-2}[(Job 19:25).]{.class-0}

[That's not ]{.class-0}[figurative;]{.class-1}[ it's not "anthropomorphic." That's literal---literal feet standing on literal earth on a literal mountain. In Acts 1:9, the Lord went up ]{.class-0}[bodily]{.class-1}[ into Heaven from the Mount of Olives, and the promise is that that is the way He's coming back (Acts 1:11). We gave you all the details of that ]{.class-0}[physical]{.class-1}[ return under Habakkuk 3:3 (which see).]{.class-0}

[While this book was being written, some radio fellow out in California (Harold Camping) predicted the end of the world on May 21, 2011 (assumably the Rapture, but the whole thing was a confused mess from the start). He put up billboards all over the country about it and took out ads on radio, television, and in newspapers nationwide. Of course, nothing happened, so he blamed the whole fiasco on God saying, ]{.class-0}["I was just going by what's in the Bible." ]{.class-1}[The dumb, stupid reporter interviewing him never even thought to ask him for chapter and verse. Brother, if I were asked that, do you think I couldn't run some references on the subject?]{.class-0}

[Anyway, when May 22 rolled around, he said, "Well, Judgment Day began ]{.class-0}[spiritually]{.class-1}[ on May 21st." That's exactly how the Jehovah's Witnesses and the Millerites (Seventh-day Adventists) handled it. There was a young man in our church once who was predicting the Rapture in September (sometime back in the 90s); he handled it the same way. In the Bible, the Second Coming of Jesus Christ---whether you are talking about the Rapture or the Advent---is a ]{.class-0}[physical]{.class-1}[ return that can be seen: the Rapture by the born-again child of God (1 Cor. 15:51--54 cf. 1 Thess. 4:13--18) and the Advent by everybody (Rev. 1:7). There is no "spiritual" return found in the Scriptures.]{.class-0}

[Now when Christ lands on Olivet, the mountain is split to the north and to the south, creating a level road that runs east to west leading to the Eastern Gate of Jerusalem (see the remarks on the path of the Second Advent under Hab. 3:3). When that happens, ]{.class-0}[every]{.class-1}[ mountain around Jerusalem is flattened; thus the phrase ]{.class-0}["valley of the mountains"]{.class-2}[ in verse 5. Isaiah 40:5 says, ]{.class-0}["Every valley shall be exalted, and every mountain and hill shall be made low."]{.class-2}

[This not only creates a level road for Christ to take from the Mount of Olives to the Eastern Gate, it also allows a way of escape to the Jews under siege in Jerusalem. Although no one can locate it precisely, ]{.class-0}["Azal"]{.class-2}[ is obviously a place of refuge to which the Jews fled during the earthquake of Uzziah's day (see Amos 1:1 and the remarks on that verse in Vol. I of this Commentary). The prophetic reference is to the events of Revelation 6:12--17, 11:13, 16:18--20.]{.class-0}

[We have already given you the material on ]{.class-0}["the saints"]{.class-2}[ of verse 5 in the remarks on Deuteronomy 33:2 and Jude 14 under the comments on Habakkuk 3:3. Further cross-references would be Daniel 7:21--22, 27; Song of Solomon 6:13 (and the comments in that Commentary); Joel 2:2--11; and Revelation 14:1--4, 15:1--3, 17:14, 19:14.]{.class-0}

[Now, I must admit that I don't have all the answers on verses 6--7. There is no doubt that the Second Advent is likened to the sun coming up in the morning (Mal. 4:2; 2 Pet. 1:19), but at the same time, it is described as ]{.class-0}["a day of darkness and of gloominess, a day of clouds and of thick darkness"]{.class-2}[ (Joel 2:2 cf. Amos 5:18, 20; Zeph. 1:15). Here, verses 6--7 seem to describe a perpetual twilight, more than likely brought on by the fourth and fifth vials of Revelation 16:8--10. Since I am unclear as to how to bring all of that together, I will simply leave it right there the way it is: ]{.class-0}[there it is.]{.class-1}

[For the ]{.class-0}["one day which shall be known to the LORD"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 7), we refer you to the comments on Matthew 24:36 and 1 Thessalonians 5:1--2 in those respective Commentaries. Here, we will only say this: while no man may know the precise ]{.class-0}["day"]{.class-2}[ or ]{.class-0}["hour"]{.class-2}[ of the Second Advent (Mark 13:32), there is no excuse for being ignorant of the ]{.class-0}["times"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["seasons"]{.class-2}[ (1 Thess. 5:1--2) and not reading the ]{.class-0}[signs]{.class-1}[ (Matt. 24:1--7). The signs may not be for you as a Christians (1 Cor. 1:22), but that doesn't mean you can't read them if they're in plain view.]{.class-0}

8 And it shall be in that day, that living waters shall go out from Jerusalem; half of them toward the former sea, and half of them toward the hinder sea: in summer and in winter shall it be.

9 And the LORD shall be king over all the earth: in that day shall there be one LORD, and his name one.

10 All the land shall be turned as a plain from Geba to Rimmon south of Jerusalem: and it shall be lifted up, and inhabited in her place, from Benjamin's gate unto the place of the first gate, unto the corner gate, and from the tower of Hananeel unto the king's winepresses.

11 And men shall dwell in it, and there shall be no more utter destruction; but Jerusalem shall be safely inhabited.

[We have already commented on the ]{.class-0}["living waters"]{.class-2}[ in the remarks on 13:1 (which see).]{.class-0}

[And then a great verse: ]{.class-0}["And the LORD]{.class-2}[ \[that's Jehovah---see the comments on 12:10\]]{.class-0}[ shall be king over all the earth"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 9). That's ]{.class-0}[not now]{.class-1}[ as the Amillennial expositors would have you believe. There's nothing "spiritual" in the verse; everything is 100% ]{.class-0}[literal.]{.class-1}[ The verse is talking about a ]{.class-0}[literal]{.class-1}[ King (Psa. 2:6) in a ]{.class-0}[literal]{.class-1}[ city (Matt. 5:35) on a ]{.class-0}[literal]{.class-1}[ earth (Rev. 5:10), reigning on a ]{.class-0}[literal]{.class-1}[ throne (Luke 1:32--33), which has been converted from ]{.class-0}["the throne of David"]{.class-2}[ (Jer. 22:2, 30) to ]{.class-0}["the throne of the LORD"]{.class-2}[ (1 Chron. 29:23; Jer. 3:17), because He is not just ]{.class-0}["the Son of David"]{.class-2}[ but the ]{.class-0}["Lord"]{.class-2}[ as well (Matt. 22:42-45).]{.class-0}

[Psalm 72:8 says, ]{.class-0}["He shall have dominion also from sea to sea, and from the river unto the ends of the earth."]{.class-2}[ ]{.class-0}

[Revelation 11:15 says, ]{.class-0}["The kingdoms of this world are become the kingdoms of our Lord, and of his Christ."]{.class-2}[ They're not right now; they have been given to the Devil to run until the smiting Stone of Daniel 2 shows up (2 Cor. 4:4; Luke 4:5--6; John 16:11).]{.class-0}

[Psalm 67:4, 7---]{.class-0}["O let the nations be glad and sing for joy]{.class-2}[ \[every Christmas they sing, "Joy to the world; the Lord is come. Let earth receive her King." Kinda got ahead of themselves on that one, didn't they?\]]{.class-0}[: for thou shalt judge the people righteously, and govern the nations upon earth. Selah\....God shall bless us ]{.class-2}[\[i.e., the Jews\]]{.class-0}[; and all the ends of the earth shall fear him."]{.class-2}[ Not now they don't. Right now, they outlaw His Book, break His laws, and try to steal His land.]{.class-0}

[The psalmist wrote, ]{.class-0}["For the LORD most high is terrible; he is a great King over all the earth. He shall subdue the people under us ]{.class-2}[\[see the comments on 12:6\]]{.class-0}[, and the nations under our feet\....Sing praises to God, sing praises: sing praises unto our King, sing praises. For God is the King of all the earth: sing ye praises with understanding. God reigneth over the heathen: God sitteth upon the throne of his holiness ]{.class-2}[\[called ]{.class-0}["the throne of his glory"]{.class-2}[ in Matt. 25:31\]]{.class-0}[" ]{.class-2}[(Psa. 47:2--3, 6--8).]{.class-0}

[Like I said, God's "big deal" is ]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ your redemption; it's the day He sets His Son up as the absolute, supreme military Dictator over all 192 nations in the UN. Your salvation is just the Lord letting you in on the "goodies."]{.class-0}

[Back to Zechariah 14:9---]{.class-0}["in that day shall there be one LORD." "One LORD"]{.class-2}[---that's ]{.class-0}[Jehovah.]{.class-1}[ Allah is OUT; Mohammed is OUT; Mary is OUT; the Pope is OUT: O-U-T, OUT!]{.class-0}

[Now it's Jehovah, but 12:10 and 13:6 identified exactly who "Jehovah" is. It's ]{.class-0}[Jesus Christ.]{.class-1}[ When Paul says, ]{.class-0}["One Lord, one faith, one baptism"]{.class-2}[ (Eph. 4:5), Jesus Christ is that ]{.class-0}["Lord."]{.class-2}[ God the Father's ]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ one Lord and Jesus Christ another Lord and the Holy Ghost a third Lord like the Mohammedans, Jehovah's Witnesses, and Mormons teach; they're all ]{.class-0}["one Lord"]{.class-2}[ (1 John 5:7).]{.class-0}

"[Three in One, One in Three; and the One in the middle died for me."]{.class-1}[ Jesus said, ]{.class-0}["I and my Father are ONE"]{.class-2}[ (John 10:30). He told Philip, ]{.class-0}["He that hath seen me hath seen the Father"]{.class-2}[ (John 14:9). John 1:18 says, ]{.class-0}["No man hath seen God at any time; the only begotten SON]{.class-2}[ \[not "]{.class-0}[begotten God]{.class-1}[" like all the apostate translations read to give you two Gods; a begotten one and an unbegotten one\]]{.class-0}[, which is in the bosom of the Father, he hath declared him."]{.class-2}

[John identified Jesus Christ with the ]{.class-0}["only true God"]{.class-2}[ (John 17:3 cf. 1 John 5:20). Thomas called Him: ]{.class-0}["my Lord and my God"]{.class-2}[ (John 20:28). Even the Father called Him God.]{.class-0}

"[But unto the Son he saith, Thy throne]{.class-2}[ \[it's ]{.class-0}["the throne of his glory"]{.class-2}[ at His return---Matt. 25:31\]]{.class-0}[, O God, is for ever and ever: a sceptre of righteousness is the sceptre of thy kingdom]{.class-2}[ \[the passage being quoted is about the Millennial Kingdom at the Second Advent; see Psa. 45:6 and context\]]{.class-0}["]{.class-2}[ (Heb. 1:8).]{.class-0}

[Verse 9: ]{.class-0}["and his name one."]{.class-2}[ That's the name the Father exalted ABOVE the name of Jehovah.]{.class-0}

"[Wherefore God also hath highly exalted him, and given him a name which is above EVERY NAME]{.class-2}[ \[including the name by which He revealed Himself in the Old Testament; see Exod. 6:3\]]{.class-0}[: That at the name of JESUS every knee should bow\...And that every tongue should confess that Jesus Christ is Lord to the glory of God the Father"]{.class-2}[ (Phil. 2:9--11).]{.class-0}

[Do you know what Paul is quoting there? He's quoting Isaiah 45:23.]{.class-0}[ Read it!]{.class-1}[ Look it up and read it and note the context (Isa. 45:21--25). That's the Father identifying His ]{.class-0}[Son]{.class-1}[ (see the word ]{.class-0}["Saviour"]{.class-2}[ in Isa. 45:21?) as the Jehovah ("]{.class-0}[LORD"]{.class-2}[ in Isa. 45:24--25) of the Old Testament and the One who will shame His enemies (Isa. 45:24 cf. Rom. 10:11) and justify Israel (Isa. 45:25 cf. Rom. 11:26--27).]{.class-0}

P.S. That's the only name that's going to save you.

"[Neither is there salvation in any other: for there is none other name under heaven given among men, whereby we must be saved"]{.class-2}[ (Acts 4:12).]{.class-0}

[P.P.S. There is only one thing higher than that name, and it is ]{.class-0}[God's word.]{.class-1}

"[I will worship toward thy holy temple, and praise thy name for thy lovingkindness and for thy truth: for thou hast MAGNIFIED THY WORD ABOVE ALL THY NAME"]{.class-2}[ (Psa. 138:2).]{.class-0}

("Ahh, there you go again, Ruckman: Bibliolatry; King James Onlyism; double inspiration. Blah, blah, blah, blah, blah." Shut your mouth! You don't know about what you're talking. Your problem is that you don't esteem that Book highly enough. That Book is so high that it takes precedence over God Almighty's spoken words \[see 2 Pet. 1:17--19\].)

[Verse 10: ]{.class-0}["All the land shall be turned as a plain ]{.class-2}[\[see the comments on vs. 5\]]{.class-0}[ from Geba ]{.class-2}[\[that's a Benjamite city---Josh. 18:21--24, 21:17\]]{.class-0}[ to Rimmon ]{.class-2}[\[a Simeonite city in Judah---Josh 19:7\]]{.class-0}[ south of Jerusalem: and it shall be lifted up, and inhabited in her place ]{.class-2}[\[see the comments on the last clause in 12:6\]]{.class-0}[, from Benjamin's gate ]{.class-2}[\[see Jer. 20:2, 37:13, 38:7. Some commentators identify it with the ]{.class-0}[sheep gate]{.class-1}[ on the north side of the city\]]{.class-0}[, unto the place of the first gate, unto the corner gate ]{.class-2}[\[see 2 Kings 14:13; 2 Chron. 25:23, 26:6; also called the ]{.class-0}["prison gate"]{.class-2}[ (Neh. 12:39) and the gate of ]{.class-0}["Miphkad"]{.class-2}[ (Neh. 3:31)\]]{.class-0}[, and from the tower of Hananeel ]{.class-2}[\[see Jer. 31:38\]]{.class-0}[ unto the king's winepresses."]{.class-2}[ You can see the locations of the gates and tower laid out for you in Appendix II of the ]{.class-0}[Bible Believer's Commentary on Ezra, Nehemiah, and]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-0}[Esther.]{.class-1}

[Verse 11: ]{.class-0}["And men shall dwell in it, and there shall be no more utter destruction; but Jerusalem shall be safely inhabited." ]{.class-2}[From A.D. 70--1917, Jerusalem experienced nothing but ]{.class-0}["utter destruction"]{.class-2}[; it was invaded twenty times. Look at Amos 9:8--10.]{.class-0}

"[Behold, the eyes of the Lord GOD are upon the sinful kingdom]{.class-2}[ \[i.e., the northern kingdom of Israel\]]{.class-0}[, and I will destroy it from off the face of the earth ]{.class-2}[\[He did in 722 B.C. with the Assyrians\]]{.class-0}[; saving that I will not utterly destroy the house of Jacob, saith the LORD]{.class-2}[ \[He won't destroy the people as a race\]]{.class-0}[. For, lo, I will command, and I will sift the house of Israel among all nations, like as corn is sifted in a sieve, yet shall not the least grain fall upon the earth. All the sinners of my people shall die by the sword."]{.class-2}

[Just because the Jews are God's chosen people doesn't mean any of them are going to get away with their sins.]{.class-0}[ A Jew can still go to Hell individually ]{.class-1}[(Matt. 8:12). Paul said ]{.class-0}["wrath is come upon them to the uttermost"]{.class-2}[ for rejecting their Messiah and rejecting the Gospel (1 Thess. 2:14--16), but that does not mean that ]{.class-0}["God's wrath has come upon them\...COMPLETELY AND FOREVER"]{.class-1}[ as the grossly corrupt ]{.class-0}[Amplified Version]{.class-1}[ says.]{.class-0}

[Look at what follows the passage we just read in Amos 9: ]{.class-0}["the tabernacle of David"]{.class-2}[ is raised back up (Amos 9:11), the Jews get the entire Abrahamic land grant (Amos 9:12), the land is ]{.class-0}[restored]{.class-1}[ to its original productiveness (Amos 9:13), and the Jews ]{.class-0}[return]{.class-1}[ to the land in peace (Amos 9:14). The chapter ends with this promise:]{.class-0}

"[And I will plant them upon their land, and they shall no more be pulled up out of their land which I have given them, saith the LORD thy God"]{.class-2}[ (Amos 9:15).]{.class-0}

[The Lord told them in Ezekiel 34:25 and 28, ]{.class-0}["And I will make with them a covenant of peace, and will cause the evil beasts to cease out of the land: and they shall dwell safely in the wilderness, and sleep in the woods\....And they shall no more be a prey to the heathen, neither shall the beast of the land devour them; but they shall dwell safely, and none shall make them afraid."]{.class-2}

[Well, it hasn't happened yet, so unless He lied, ]{.class-0}[it's still future]{.class-1}[. It will take place when Jesus Christ reigns from Jerusalem in the Millennium.]{.class-0}

"[In those days, and at that time, will I cause the Branch of righteousness to grow up unto David]{.class-2}[ \[see the comments on Zech. 3:8\]]{.class-0}[; and he shall execute judgment and righteousness in the land. In those days shall Judah be saved, and Jerusalem shall dwell safely: and this is the name wherewith she shall be called, The LORD our righteousness]{.class-2}[ \[see Rom. 10:3--4\]]{.class-0}[" ]{.class-2}[(Jer. 33:15--16; see also Jer. 23:5--6).]{.class-0}

12 And this shall be the plague wherewith the LORD will smite all the people that have fought against Jerusalem; Their flesh shall consume away while they stand upon their feet, and their eyes shall consume away in their holes, and their tongue shall consume away in their mouth.

13 And it shall come to pass in that day, that a great tumult from the LORD shall be among them; and they shall lay hold every one on the hand of his neighbour, and his hand shall rise up against the hand of his neighbour.

14 And Judah also shall fight at Jerusalem; and the wealth of all the heathen round about shall be gathered together, gold, and silver, and apparel, in great abundance.

15 And so shall be the plague of the horse, of the mule, of the camel, and of the ass, and of all the beasts that shall be in these tents, as this plague.

[The best description you can give for ]{.class-0}["the plague"]{.class-2}[ of verse 12 is an ]{.class-0}[atomic blast]{.class-1}[. You are not dealing with something here like the bubonic plague that slowly kills you; this is a plague that burns the flesh away in a matter of seconds.]{.class-0}

[Verse 12 is a description of how Jesus Christ destroys the armies of the Antichrist in Revelation 19:15. The plague consumes the ]{.class-0}["flesh"]{.class-2}[ because ]{.class-0}["they allure through the lusts of the flesh, through much wantonness, those that were clean escaped from them who live in error" ]{.class-2}[(2 Pet. 2:18). It consumes their ]{.class-0}["eyes"]{.class-2}[ because they have ]{.class-0}["eyes full of adultery, and that cannot cease from sin"]{.class-2}[ (2 Pet. 2:14). It consumes their ]{.class-0}["tongues"]{.class-2}[ because they ]{.class-0}["blasphemed the name of God, which hath power over these plagues: and they repented not to give him glory"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 16:9--10).]{.class-0}

[We have already commented on verses 13--15 under 12:4--6 (which see). Note in verse 14 that ]{.class-0}["the wealth of all the heathen round about shall be gathered together, gold, and silver, and apparel, in great abundance."]{.class-2}[ That's not just the Jews spoiling their attackers (Ezek. 39:10 cf. Judg. 8:24--26; 2 Kings 7:16); that's the Gentiles bringing tribute to Jerusalem (Isa. 60--61).]{.class-0}

[Psalm 72:10--11 says, ]{.class-0}["The kings of Tarshish and of the isles shall bring presents: the kings of Sheba and Seba shall offer gifts. Yea, all kings shall fall down before him: all nations shall serve him."]{.class-2}[ So at Christmas time, you sing: "We three Kings of Orient are; Bearing gifts we traverse afar." Well, the Bible doesn't say "Kings"; it says ]{.class-0}["wise men"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 2). But what the wise men did at Christ's First Coming (see Matt. 2:11) was a picture of what the Gentile monarchs will do at His Second Coming.]{.class-0}

16 And it shall come to pass, that every one that is left of all the nations which came against Jerusalem shall even go up from year to year to worship the King, the LORD of hosts, and to keep the feast of tabernacles.

17 And it shall be, that whoso will not come up of all the families of the earth unto Jerusalem to worship the King, the LORD of hosts, even upon them shall be no rain.

18 And if the family of Egypt go not up, and come not, that have no rain; there shall be the plague, wherewith the LORD will smite the heathen that come not up to keep the feast of tabernacles.

19 This shall be the punishment of Egypt, and the punishment of all nations that come not up to keep the feast of tabernacles.

20 In that day shall there be upon the bells of the horses, HOLINESS UNTO THE LORD; and the pots in the LORD'S house shall be like the bowls before the altar.

21 Yea, every pot in Jerusalem and in Judah shall be holiness unto the LORD of hosts: and all they that sacrifice shall come and take of them, and seethe therein: and in that day there shall be no more the Canaanite in the house of the LORD of hosts.

"[And it shall come to pass, that every one that if LEFT of all the nations which came against Jerusalem"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 6). Just as only a remnant of ]{.class-0}[Jews]{.class-1}[ will be saved out of the Tribulation (Rom. 11), only a small number of ]{.class-0}[Gentiles]{.class-1}[ will be saved at the Judgment of the Nations (Matt. 25:31--46).]{.class-0}

[We have already commented on the desolation of the Tribulation in the comments on Zephaniah 1:2--3 (which see). Jesus said, ]{.class-0}["For then shall be great tribulation, such as was not since the beginning of the world to this time, no, nor ever shall be"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 24:21). Well, the Noahic Flood took place ]{.class-0}["since the beginning of the world,"]{.class-2}[ so the number killed in the Tribulation has to be ]{.class-0}[greater than those killed in the Flood.]{.class-1}

[Estimates put the population of Noah's day between 200 and 300 million. During the Tribulation, over ]{.class-0}[three billion]{.class-1}[ are slain by the Antichrist's army alone (Rev. 9:17--18). That number doesn't even include the number that starve to death or who die by other means (Rev. 6:5--8), which would be a minimum of ]{.class-0}[another billion.]{.class-1}[ During the Tribulation, at least ]{.class-0}[half]{.class-1}[ of the earth's population is wiped out, and it's probably greater than that.]{.class-0}

"[Every one that is left of all the nations which came against Jerusalem shall even go up from year to year to worship the King, the LORD of hosts."]{.class-2}[ The Scriptural commentary is found in Isaiah 2:2--3 and Psalm 66:4, 86:9, 72:11. This has nothing to do with the Church Age; it is the second gathering of the Gentiles during the Millennium which we have discussed under Zephaniah 3:9 (which see).]{.class-0}

"[To keep the feast of tabernacles."]{.class-2}[ Now why is that? That wasn't the main Jewish feast; it was Passover. But in the Old Testament, those Jews were to show up at Jerusalem three times in the year: Passover, Pentecost, and Tabernacles (Exod. 23:14--17; Deut. 16:16). Passover represented Christ's death on the cross (1 Cor. 5:7), Pentecost was the birth of the Church (Acts 2 cf. Acts 1:5; 1 Cor. 12:13), and Tabernacles is connected with what?]{.class-0}

[Well, the Feast of Tabernacles dates creation and both the First and Second Advents. ]{.class-1}[That feast falls in the seventh month. The seventh month is so important that ]{.class-0}[three]{.class-1}[ of the seven feasts of Leviticus 23 fall in it: the Feast of Trumpets, the Day of Atonement, and the Feast of Tabernacles. In Leviticus 23, you have something special on the seventh day (the Sabbath), seven weeks from Passover and then something special (Pentecost), seven months and then something special (Trumpets, Atonement, Tabernacles), and in Leviticus 25 there is something special in the seventh year (Jubilee).]{.class-0}

[Second Peter 3:8 says, ]{.class-0}["one day is with the Lord as a thousand years, and a thousand years as one day."]{.class-2}[ So you number a thousand years from creation, and what do you get? ]{.class-0}[You have a Millennial "sabbath" of the earth.]{.class-1}

[All of this gets into calendars and dates, and it is obvious that Christ did ]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ return in the year 2000. Nevertheless, I stick to my guns on the subject because I know the Lord has a way of counting (or not counting) years that we don't have (see Job 3:6 and Matt. 1, for example). When the Lord starts the clock ticking again at the Rapture, it will be 2001 on ]{.class-0}[His]{.class-1}[ calendar, no matter what ]{.class-0}[ours]{.class-1}[ reads.]{.class-0}

Now, what about the Feast of Tabernacles in regards to both of Christ's Advents? Well, Christ's coming is likened to the sun coming up in the morning (Mal. 4:2; 2 Pet. 1:16--19). The sun was created on the fourth day (Gen. 1:14--19), and Jesus Christ showed up the first time 4,000 years after creation.

[Did you know that at a certain time of the year, the earth is ]{.class-0}[four days off center]{.class-1}[ in its orbit around the sun? The earth's orbit is not a perfect circle; it is an oval shape: an ellipse. At certain times of the year, it is closer to the sun than at others. One of those times is ]{.class-0}[September 20--23,]{.class-1}[ in the seventh month at the Feast of Tabernacles.]{.class-0}

[At the Feast of Tabernacles, the children of Israel were to set up booths, so in September--October (the Jewish seventh month), you have fairs with ]{.class-0}[booths.]{.class-1}[ In our calendar, ]{.class-0}[September]{.class-1}[ is the ninth month, but that's ]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ what the word means. That prefix ]{.class-0}[sept]{.class-1}[ means "seven." ]{.class-0}[Oct]{.class-1}[ means "eight (as in ]{.class-0}[oct]{.class-1}[opus or ]{.class-0}[oct]{.class-1}[ogenerian), ]{.class-0}[nov]{.class-1}[ is "nine," and ]{.class-0}[dec]{.class-1}[ is "ten" (as in ]{.class-0}[decimal]{.class-1}[). So ]{.class-0}[September is the seventh month,]{.class-1}[ October is the eighth month, November is the ninth month, and December is the tenth month, matching the Jewish calendar.]{.class-0}

[John 1:14 says, ]{.class-0}["And the Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us."]{.class-2}[ That's a verse on the Incarnation. All the Greek boys will tell you that the Greek word for ]{.class-0}["dwelt"]{.class-2}[ (]{.class-0}[eskanosen]{.class-1}[) means "tabernacled," as in the Feast of TABERNACLES. Christ, ]{.class-0}["the Sun of righteousness,"]{.class-2}[ was born on the same day the sun was created---September 23. If you really wanted to celebrate Christ's birth, you would observe it on September 23, not December 25 when Baal the sun god is resurrected from the dead (see Hislop's ]{.class-0}[The Two Babylons]{.class-1}[).]{.class-0}

[Now turn over to Matthew 17. In that chapter, Christ is transfigured before Peter, James, and John. The Scriptures tell you that's a picture of ]{.class-0}["the Son of man coming in his kingdom"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 16:28 cf. 2 Pet. 1:16--18)---the Second Advent. Look at what Peter says about it in Matthew 17:4.]{.class-0}

"[Then answered Peter, and said unto Jesus, Lord, it is good for us to be here: if thou wilt, let us make here three TABERNACLES"]{.class-2}[---as in the Feast of TABERNACLES. And lo and behold, there's Moses and Elijah (Matt. 17:3--4), just as you find them in Malachi 4 and Revelation 11.]{.class-0}

[Christ returns in power at the Second Advent, so during the Millennium, the important feast is not Passover;]{.class-0}[ it's Tabernacles.]{.class-1}

"[Therefore, behold, the days come, saith the LORD, that they shall no more say, The LORD liveth, which brought up the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt; But, The LORD liveth, which brought up and which led the seed of the house of Israel out of the north country, and from all countries whither I have driven them; and they shall dwell in their own land"]{.class-2}[ (Jer. 23:7--8).]{.class-0}

[Verse 17: ]{.class-0}["And it shall be, that whoso will not come up of all the families of the earth unto Jerusalem to worship the King."]{.class-2}[ The old hymn goes: "O worship the King, all glorious ABOVE." That's not the case here. This is not ]{.class-0}["the heavenly Jerusalem"]{.class-2}[ where ]{.class-0}["Jesus"]{.class-2}[ is ]{.class-0}["the mediator of the new covenant"]{.class-2}[ (Heb. 12:22--24). This is the Jerusalem ]{.class-0}[on earth]{.class-1}[ (vss. 2, 4, 9, 11, 16) where ]{.class-0}["the families of the EARTH"]{.class-2}[ can come and celebrate a Jewish feast that is observed on EARTH. It is the city where ]{.class-0}[King]{.class-1}[ Jesus sits on ]{.class-0}["the throne of his father David"]{.class-2}[ reigning ]{.class-0}["over the house of Jacob"]{.class-2}[ (Luke 1:32--33), NOT ]{.class-0}["on the right hand of the Majesty on high"]{.class-2}[ (Heb. 1:3).]{.class-0}

[In Psalm 110:1, the Father told the Son: ]{.class-0}["Sit thou at my right hand, UNTIL\...."]{.class-2}[ Did you catch that? Jesus Christ is seated at the Father's right hand ]{.class-0}["UNTIL"]{.class-2}[ something happens; it's not a permanent session.]{.class-0}

"[Sit thou at my right hand, until\...."]{.class-2}[ Until what? ]{.class-0}["\...until I make thine enemies thy footstool"]{.class-2}[---Second Advent. Jesus said, ]{.class-0}["the EARTH\...is his footstool"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 5:35), not Heaven.]{.class-0}

[What happens after He makes His ]{.class-0}["enemies"]{.class-2}[ His ]{.class-0}["footstool"]{.class-2}[? Psalm 110:2, ]{.class-0}["The Lord shall send the rod of thy strength out of Zion\...."]{.class-2}[ That's the earthly ]{.class-0}["Zion."]{.class-2}[ How do you know? ]{.class-0}[Look at the rest of the verse:]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-0}["rule thou in the MIDST of thine enemies."]{.class-2}[ Why, Heaven's not ]{.class-0}["in the midst"]{.class-2}[ of God's enemies! The Bible says ]{.class-0}["WITHOUT are dogs\...sorcerers\...whoremongers,"]{.class-2}[ etc. (Rev. 22:15). When He returns, He reigns in a place that was surrounded on all sides by Moslems who were trying to kill those Jews and drive them into the sea.]{.class-0}
