[Psalm 2 says, ]{.class-0}["Yet have I set my king upon my holy hill of Zion\....Ask of me, and I shall give thee the heathen for thine inheritance, and the uttermost parts of the EARTH for thy possession. Thou shalt break them with a rod of iron\..."]{.class-2}[ (Psa. 2:6, 8--9). And there you are back at ]{.class-0}["the rod of thy strength"]{.class-2}[ in Psalm 110:2.]{.class-0}

[When Jesus Christ comes back, He doesn't reign at "God the Father's own right hand" or as "King of my life I crown thee now" or just about anything else you sing out of the hymn book. There's nothing wrong with any of that; it's wonderful sentiment. I mean you love the Lord, and you want to sing about Him because He saved you and gives you joy and all that kind of stuff (see the work on ]{.class-0}[God Is Love]{.class-1}[); I do too. But you have to make a distinction between your spiritual relationship to the Lord and the doctrinal realities of His return.]{.class-0}

"[And it shall be, that whoso will not come up of all the families of the earth unto Jerusalem to worship the King, the LORD of hosts, even upon them shall be no rain."]{.class-2}[ Isaiah 60:12 says, ]{.class-0}["For the nation and kingdom that will not serve thee shall perish; yea, those nations shall be utterly wasted." "The plague"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 18) that God uses to "waste" them is ]{.class-0}[drought.]{.class-1}

[Now the one nation that is singled out in verses 18--19 is ]{.class-0}["Egypt"]{.class-2}[ (see the comments on Zech. 10:10). How you reconcile this passage with Isaiah 19:25 (see the remarks on the verse under the comments on Zech. 2:11), I am not completely sure, so for right now my only comment will be ]{.class-0}["Ich weiss nicht."]{.class-1}

[Verse 20: ]{.class-0}["In that day shall there be upon the bells of the horses, HOLINESS UNTO THE LORD." ]{.class-2}[The horses have two things that were on the garments of the high priest in the Old Testament. The high priest had a gold plate tied to the front of his miter that read ]{.class-0}["HOLINESS TO THE LORD"]{.class-2}[ (Exod. 28:36, 39:30), and on the bottom of his robe were sewn bells and pomegranates (Exod. 28:34, 39:26). That plate on the miter signified that the priest dealt with things that had been "hallowed" to the Lord (Exod. 28:38), and the ]{.class-0}[bells]{.class-1}[ were worn by the high priest so that he could be heard by the congregation as he went about his priestly duties (see the remarks on the bells under the comments on the linen breeches in the ]{.class-0}[Bible Believer's Commentary on Exodus]{.class-1}[---chap. 28).]{.class-0}

[The horses in verse 20 have been dedicated to the service of Jehovah just as the horses of the Canaanites and the Syrians had been dedicated to the service of the sun god. In Greek mythology, the chariot of Apollo the sun god was pulled by horses of fire (cf. 2 Kings 2:11, 6:17). The horses dedicated to the heathen gods were used in worship services that incorporated ]{.class-0}[bestiality]{.class-1}[ as part of the worship of the gods (Lev. 18:23, 20:15--16); this was why Joshua and David cut the hamstrings of the horses they took in battle (see Josh. 11:6, 2 Sam. 8:4). The horses of verse 20 are being used in the Temple worship because when the King came into the Temple to remove the image of the Antichrist, He and his armies rode in on ]{.class-0}[white horses ]{.class-1}[(Rev. 19:11, 14). For further study on the nature of animals during the Millennium, we refer you to the comments on Habakkuk 2:14.]{.class-0}

"[And the pots in the LORD'S house shall be like the bowls before the altar. Yea, every pot in Jerusalem and in Judah shall be holiness unto the LORD of hosts"]{.class-2}[ (vss. 20--21). ]{.class-0}["The bowls"]{.class-2}[ are the vessels used to catch the blood of the sacrifices, and ]{.class-0}["the pots"]{.class-2}[ are the vessels used to cook the meat of the sacrifices which the priests were allowed to eat (Lev. 6:28; 1 Sam. 2:14; 2 Chron. 35:13). In the Millennial Temple, there will be special chambers to cook the priests' portion of the sacrifices that are apart from the altar (Ezek. 46:20--24); nevertheless, the vessels will be just as holy to the Lord as the bowls of the Brazen Altar.]{.class-0}

But it's not just the cooking pots in the Temple that are dedicated to the Lord; it's every pot in the city of Jerusalem and the land of Judah. These vessels come from the destruction of Babylon the Great in Revelation 18 and are to be used specifically for feeding and clothing the Levitical priesthood of the Millennial Temple.

[Isaiah 23:18---]{.class-0}["And her merchandise ]{.class-2}[\[talking about Tyre as a type of Babylon the Great---Isa. 23:17\]]{.class-0}[ and her hire shall be holiness to the LORD; it shall not be treasured nor laid up; for her merchandise shall be for them that dwell before the LORD, to east sufficiently, and for durable clothing."]{.class-2}

We have already commented on the sacrifices of verse 21 in the remarks on Isaiah 56:7 under Zephaniah 3:9 (which see).

"[And in that day there shall be no more the Canaanite in the house of the LORD of hosts."]{.class-2}[ Zephaniah ends his prophecy with a vicious ]{.class-0}[discrimination]{.class-1}[ against ]{.class-0}[one race]{.class-1}[ of people, exactly as you find the Apostle Paul "stereotyping" them in Titus 1:12--13 (see the comments on the passage in the ]{.class-0}[Bible Believer's Commentary on the Pastoral Epistles]{.class-1}[). When all the nations of the earth come to Jerusalem to worship Christ, ]{.class-0}[one group]{.class-1}[ is forbidden to enter the Temple---the ]{.class-0}[Canaanites.]{.class-1}[ The curse placed on ]{.class-0}[Canaan]{.class-1}[ (see Gen. 9:25 and the comments on the verse in that Commentary) creeps all the way into the Millennium.]{.class-0}

[The Canaanites are connected to two religions: Catholicism]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[Islam]{.class-1}[---]{.class-0}[Mary]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[Mohammed]{.class-1}[. In southern Ireland---a good Roman Catholic country---there is a rock with this inscription: "We are ]{.class-0}[Canaanites]{.class-1}[ who have fled from Joshua the son of Nun, the robber." Those are some of the original inhabitants of Palestine who got kicked out by Joshua and wound up in Ireland. Sixteen verses of the national song of Ireland end with: "I am Paddy]{.class-0}[ the Canaanite."]{.class-1}[ That's your Roman Catholic Church, and you can find its roots in Judges 17--18.]{.class-0}

[Today, the Canaanites are "Palestinians" who are, for the most part, ]{.class-0}[Moslems]{.class-1}[. They are the ones trying to run Israel out of the land God gave them. When the ]{.class-0}["King of the Jews"]{.class-2}[ takes over as the ]{.class-0}["KING OF KINGS,"]{.class-2}[ He's going to ]{.class-0}[throw them out of His house.]{.class-1}[ The only way they can escape the curse God put on them back in Genesis is to do what the Syrophenician woman (Matt. 15) and the Ethiopian eunuch (Acts 8) did: take their place as ]{.class-0}["a servant of servants"]{.class-2}[ now (this side of the Rapture) and in humble faith trust a ]{.class-0}[Jew]{.class-1}[ to take away their sins. Of course, to do that, they will have ]{.class-0}[to go against their religion]{.class-1}[ (Mohammedanism), which teaches them ]{.class-0}[that God never had a Son and that Jesus Christ was only a prophet who was inferior to a demon-possessed, epileptic, fornicating, child-molesting, murdering, thieving, slave-owning religious dictator (Mohammed).]{.class-1}[ Now it's been done. We have the testimony of one of them who did exactly that (]{.class-0}[Why I Left Jihad]{.class-1}[ by Walid Shoebat), but when they do, they usually have to leave Palestine and get away from their families, or they'll be ]{.class-0}[killed.]{.class-1}[ That's Mohammed---that's the dirty lying, two-faced hypocrite who said, ]{.class-0}["There is no compulsion in religion" ]{.class-1}[(Sura 2:256), and then turned right around and said, "I have been ordered to fight with the people till they say, none has the right to be worshipped but Allah" (]{.class-0}["Holy" Hadith,]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-16}[Bukhari, vol. 4, no. 196).]{.class-0}

[Listen, when God picked the standard to ]{.class-0}["judge the poor" "with righteousness"]{.class-2}[ and]{.class-0}[ "reprove with equity for the meek of the earth"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 11:4), He didn't pick some blood-thirsty ]{.class-0}[sex maniac who couldn't control his gonads. ]{.class-1}[He chose a Man who was ]{.class-0}["holy, harmless, undefiled, separate from sinners"]{.class-2}[ (Heb. 7:26), and He proved it by raising Him from the dead.]{.class-0}

"[Because he hath appointed a day, in the which he will judge the world in righteousness by that man whom he hath ordained; whereof he hath given assurance unto all men, in that he hath raised him from the dead"]{.class-2}[ (Acts 17:31).]{.class-0}

[That's the Man whose place Mohammed tried to usurp (see Sura 4:80 in the ]{.class-0}["Holy" Koran]{.class-1}[).]{.class-0}[ He failed miserably.]{.class-1}[ You say, "How do you know?" Because Mohammed is ]{.class-0}[stone-cold dead]{.class-1}[ on the market, and you can go to Medina and see ]{.class-0}[his remains.]{.class-1}[ You can't find one bone of Jesus Christ because ]{.class-0}["He is not here: for he is risen, as he saith"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 26:6). That's ]{.class-0}["the King"]{.class-2}[ who's going to punish Mohammed's followers (vs. 19) and pitch them out on their ear (vs. 21).]{.class-0}

[]{#section-0_1_fdd41b9e1676b136_b69b.xhtml}

INTRODUCTION TO

MALACHI

[Malachi is the last prophet to write in the Old Testament. Scofield has Malachi making his prophecy in Nehemiah 13 (Mal. 2:14--17 cf. Neh. 13:15--27). Bullinger dates the book around 374 B.C., but we have seen problems with Bullinger's dating system before (see App. I on the ]{.class-0}[Bible Believer's Commentary on Ezra, Nehemiah, and Esther]{.class-1}[). Archbishop Ussher gives a date of somewhere between 434--397 B.C., with Scofield giving the 397 B.C. date.]{.class-0}

[Nearly all the expositors talk about "400 Silent Years" between the end of the Old Testament and the beginning of the New; that is, there were 400 years between God's last written revelation and the First Advent of Jesus Christ.]{.class-0}[ If we apply this to Christ's Second Coming,]{.class-1}[ we would have 400 years from God's last written revelation in the universal language of the last days (the ]{.class-0}[AV]{.class-1}[ 1611), giving a date of 2011 for the Rapture. Of course, there are other things "at play" here (see the remarks on the Feast of Tabernacles under Zech. 14), but that is something to consider.]{.class-0}

[The name ]{.class-0}[Malachi]{.class-1}[ means "my messenger." That is a double application to both John the Baptist as the forerunner of Christ (Mal. 3:1; Isa. 40:1--4) and to Jesus Christ as the Messenger of Jehovah (see ]{.class-0}["the Lord\...even the messenger of the covenant"]{.class-2}[ in Mal. 3:1). Malachi uses this double application constantly to mess up the scholars. ]{.class-0}[In Malachi 3, both advents are spoken up as right together]{.class-1}[ (3:1--5), and you could prove that by going to Mark 1:1--3. If you did, you would be ]{.class-0}[dead wrong]{.class-1}[ because the advents are separated by 2,000 years (see the comments on Zech. 9:9--11).]{.class-0}

[A peculiar expression keeps popping up in Malachi: ]{.class-0}["ye say."]{.class-2}[ It (and its variations) show up in 1:2, 5, 6, 7 (twice), 12, 2:14, 17 (twice), and 3:8, 13. ]{.class-0}[That's God quoting what the Jews say;]{.class-1}[ as a consequence, some of the things recorded are not true. They are statements of fact as far as what the Jews said, but the ]{.class-0}[statements themselves]{.class-1}[ are false.]{.class-0}

[For example, take 3:14---]{.class-0}["It is vain to serve God."]{.class-2}[ Well, that's not so.]{.class-0}

"[Therefore, my beloved brethren, be ye stedfast, unmoveable, always abounding in the work of the Lord, forasmuch as ye know that your labour is NOT IN VAIN in the Lord"]{.class-2}[ (1 Cor. 15:50).]{.class-0}

[Paul told you that you'll reap your reward when the time is right (Gal. 6:9). But you see, the Lord's telling you what a bunch ]{.class-0}[of backslidden Jews said,]{.class-1}[ not what is doctrinally so.]{.class-0}

[Do you see the trouble into which you get when you mess with the Book? You can make the Bible prove ]{.class-0}[anything,]{.class-1}[ but to do that, you have to: 1) ]{.class-0}[add]{.class-1}[ something to the Book that isn't there, 2) ]{.class-0}[subtract]{.class-1}[ something from the Book that is there, 3) take what the Book says ]{.class-0}[out of context.]{.class-1}[ The last one is a "goody" for teaching outright lies from the Scriptures. That's why I don't mess with it. If my "buddies" in the ministry want to mess with it, they can ]{.class-0}[count me out.]{.class-1}

[Malachi has 4 chapters, 55 verses, and 1,781 words. It ends the Old Testament with ]{.class-0}["a curse"]{.class-2}[ (4:6); whereas the New Testament ends with ]{.class-0}["Amen"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 22:21). ]{.class-0}[The Old Testament curses you; the New Testament blesses you.]{.class-1}[ Negative and positive: you need ]{.class-0}[both]{.class-1}[ to make a battery operate. You need ]{.class-0}["repentance toward God"]{.class-2}[ ]{.class-0}[first]{.class-1}[ (negative) and then ]{.class-0}["faith toward our Lord Jesus Christ"]{.class-2}[ ]{.class-0}[second]{.class-1}[ (Acts 20:21). The modern positive approach to preaching (see Robert Schuller, Rick Warren, and Joel Osteen, for example) is straight out of Hell (read Jer. 23).]{.class-0}

[]{#section-0_1_75eca83416d88728_b69c.xhtml}

CHAPTER ONE

1:1 The burden of the word of the LORD to Israel by Malachi.

2 I have loved you, saith the LORD. Yet ye say, Wherein hast thou loved us? Was not Esau Jacob's brother? saith the LORD: yet I loved Jacob,

3 And I hated Esau, and laid his mountains and his heritage waste for the dragons of the wilderness.

4 Whereas Edom saith, We are impoverished, but we will return and build the desolate places; thus saith the LORD of hosts, They shall build, but I will throw down; and they shall call them, The border of wickedness, and, The people against whom the LORD hath indignation for ever.

5 And your eyes shall see, and ye shall say, The LORD will be magnified from the border of Israel.

[For ]{.class-0}["the burden of the word of the LORD"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 1), see the comments on Habakkuk 1:1 and Zechariah 9:1. This time ]{.class-0}["the burden"]{.class-2}[ is laid on ]{.class-0}["Israel,"]{.class-2}[ and it comes to them ]{.class-0}["by Malachi."]{.class-2}

"[I have loved you, saith the LORD"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 2). Think about that! ]{.class-0}[What a thing to say after telling them He was going]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-0}["to rejoice"]{.class-2}[ ]{.class-0}[over destroying them]{.class-1}[ (Deut. 28:63). That's some ]{.class-0}[love]{.class-1}[, ain't it?]{.class-0}

[But look here, ]{.class-0}["God so loved the world,"]{.class-2}[ didn't He (John 3:16)? Well, if you're talking about the physical creation, what is He going to do to it? He's going to ]{.class-0}[burn it up]{.class-1}[ (2 Pet. 3:10--12). If you're talking about the masses of unsaved people for whom He died, what's He going to do to them? ]{.class-0}[They're going to burn]{.class-1}[ (Matt. 25:11; Rev. 20:15).]{.class-0}

[That's the ]{.class-0}["God"]{.class-2}[ who ]{.class-0}["is love"]{.class-2}[ (1 John 4:8). Americans have been brainwashed by Hollywood to the point where they can't see how a God of love could burn ]{.class-0}[anyone]{.class-1}[. Recently, one of these Neo-Liberals wrote a book entitled "Love Wins" in which no one goes to Hell because God's nature is love. ]{.class-0}[That's not God's full nature.]{.class-1}

Do you want the part of God's nature the fellow left out of his book? All right, here it is.

"[For our God is a CONSUMING FIRE"]{.class-2}[ (Heb. 12:29).]{.class-0}

[You can't discuss the love of God without discussing ]{.class-0}[fire]{.class-1}[ (see the comments on Song of Sol. 8:6 in that Commentary), because His nature is ]{.class-0}[both.]{.class-1}[ God's nature is not only love, God's nature is HOLY (1 Pet. 1:15--16). He can't tolerate sin because He's ]{.class-0}[sinless]{.class-1}[. His nature is that He ]{.class-0}[has to punish sin ]{.class-1}[in the fire of His wrath. He wouldn't sin to keep you out of Hell. If He had to sin to save you, ]{.class-0}[He'd let you burn.]{.class-1}[ God satisfied both His love and His holiness by coming down here as a man and taking the just punishment of your sin on Himself.]{.class-0}

[When you say ]{.class-0}["God is love,"]{.class-2}[ what does that mean? John 3:16 says, ]{.class-0}["For God so loved the world that he gave his only begotten Son."]{.class-2}[ The first time love shows up in your Bible is in connection with ]{.class-0}[a father loving his son and offering him up to God]{.class-1}[ as ]{.class-0}["a BURNT offering"]{.class-2}[ (Gen. 22:2).]{.class-0}

What everyone keeps forgetting is that the love of God is far superior to any other love which you know. For example:

"[Greater love hath no man than this, that a man lay down his life for HIS FRIENDS"]{.class-2}[ (John 15:13).]{.class-0}

[Compare that with Romans 5:8---]{.class-0}["But God commendeth his love toward us, in that, WHILE WE WERE YET SINNERS, Christ died for us."]{.class-2}

[Christ's love went beyond the greatest love men know. When ]{.class-0}["Christ died for the ungodly"]{.class-2}[ (Rom. 5:6), we weren't ]{.class-0}["his friends"]{.class-2}[ (John 15:13); we were His ]{.class-0}["enemies"]{.class-2}[ (Rom. 5:10). Well, if God did ]{.class-0}[that]{.class-1}[ for you, and you ]{.class-0}[turn down]{.class-1}[ His love, what do you think is going to happen? I mean if you are His enemy, and He makes the ultimate sacrifice to patch things up between the both of you, and you turn down His offer in order ]{.class-0}[to remain His enemy]{.class-1}[, do you think everything's going to be "hunky-dory" between you two?]{.class-0}

[You know what the problem is? It's ]{.class-0}[jealousy.]{.class-1}[ No man who loves his wife is going to let her mess around with another guy; no woman is going to let her husband mess around with another woman every other night if she loves him. If ]{.class-0}["God is love,"]{.class-2}[ He's not going to let you spurn that love and get away with it.]{.class-0}

"[For thou shalt worship no other god: for the LORD, whose name is Jealous, is a jealous God"]{.class-2}[ (Exod. 34:14).]{.class-0}

[If you turn down a love that is ]{.class-0}[superior]{.class-1}[ to anything you know, then you'll experience a ]{.class-0}[hatred]{.class-1}[ and a ]{.class-0}[wrath]{.class-1}[ against your sin that is just as superior. You'll not only burn, you'll burn ]{.class-0}[without mercy. ]{.class-1}[The rich man in Hell said, ]{.class-0}["Father Abraham, have mercy on me"]{.class-2}[ (Luke 16:24). God didn't even have enough mercy to let Abraham send someone with one drop of water; He just let the guy burn. If you ]{.class-0}[turn down]{.class-1}[ the love, you get the]{.class-0}[ "consuming fire." ]{.class-2}[If that Liberal had had any sense, he wouldn't have called his book ]{.class-0}["Love Wins"]{.class-1}[; He would have called it "God Wins." God's going to act ]{.class-0}[according to His nature]{.class-1}[ whether or not you get the love side of it or the fire side of it.]{.class-0}

[You are going to be in God's presence one way or another (Psa. 138:7--8). If you receive God's free gift that was purchased with His love (Rom. 6:23), then you'll be given a body that can ]{.class-0}[withstand]{.class-1}[ the ]{.class-0}["consuming fire"]{.class-2}[ of God's presence. But if you reject Christ, you'll be out in eternity with the blood of Christ no longer available to you and the opportunity to receive Him past, with a body that can only ]{.class-0}[endure]{.class-1}[ that ]{.class-0}["consuming fire"]{.class-2}[ forever (Mark 9:44, 46, 48).]{.class-0}

[The reason I'll be able to take that presence is that I'll have a ]{.class-0}[body]{.class-1}[ just like His Son's, and God gets along really well with His Son because they're both part of ]{.class-0}[the Trinity.]{.class-1}[ When I got saved, the Holy Spirit made me one with God's Son (Eph. 2:12--22), and God loves]{.class-0}[ His Son.]{.class-1}[ He said, ]{.class-0}["This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased; hear ye him"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 17:5). So when I trusted Christ, I became ]{.class-0}["accepted in the beloved"]{.class-2}[ (Eph. 1:6). I won't burn because ]{.class-0}[He burned in my place ]{.class-1}[(Psa. 18:5). The wrath of God on my sins already fell on ]{.class-0}[Him]{.class-1}[.]{.class-0}

"[I have loved you, saith the LORD."]{.class-2}[ In the context, that's a ]{.class-0}[national]{.class-1}[ love; the Lord loved those Jews as a ]{.class-0}[nation.]{.class-1}[ He says over in Deuteronomy 4:37, ]{.class-0}["And because he loved thy fathers, therefore he chose their seed after them."]{.class-2}[ Deuteronomy 7:7--8 says, ]{.class-0}["The LORD did not set his love upon you, nor choose you, because ye were more in number than any people; for ye were the fewest of all people: But because the LORD loved you, and because he would keep the oath which he had sworn unto your fathers."]{.class-2}

[The Lord loves that nation because of the promises He made to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. That's why Paul says, ]{.class-0}["As concerning the gospel, they are enemies for your sakes: but as touching the election, they are beloved for the fathers' sakes"]{.class-2}[ (Rom. 11:28). The Lord still loves ]{.class-0}[that nation ]{.class-1}[despite them being Christ-rejectors and self-righteous and a lot of them atheists. That doesn't mean they're all going to Heaven, but it does mean that He will fulfill every promise He made to them, even though He's judged them for their sins.]{.class-0}

[Jeremiah 31:2--4, ]{.class-0}["Thus saith the LORD, The people which were left of the sword found grace in the wilderness; even Israel, when I went to cause him to rest. The LORD hath appeared of old unto me, saying, Yea, I have loved thee with an everlasting love: therefore with lovingkindness have I drawn thee. Again I will build thee, and thou shalt be built\...."]{.class-2}[ And the rest of the chapter is about the restoration of Israel at the Second Advent.]{.class-0}

[Back to Malachi 1: ]{.class-0}["Yet ye say, Wherein hast thou loved us? Was not Esau Jacob's brother? saith the LORD: yet I loved Jacob, and I hated Esau"]{.class-2}[ (vss. 2--3). Lord, you can't do that; you can't ]{.class-0}[hate]{.class-1}[ anybody! That's "hate speech"; you should be arrested, fined, and imprisoned for violating Obama's "Hate Crimes Bill"! After all, it's wrong to "hate" people. "God loves the sinner; He just hates the ]{.class-0}[sin,"]{.class-1}[ right?]{.class-0}

[That's American Christianity nowadays: it's ]{.class-0}[cuckoo.]{.class-1}[ Instead of aping a bunch of hackneyed clichés, have you actually ever looked up in the Bible what (or ]{.class-0}[who]{.class-1}[) God hates? Look at Proverbs 6:16--19.]{.class-0}

"[These six things doth the LORD HATE: yea, seven are an abomination unto him: A proud look, a lying tongue, and hands that shed innocent blood, An heart that deviseth wicked imaginations, feet that be swift in running to mischief\...."]{.class-2}

[Now up to that point, you might have reason to say that God hates only the sin, although the ]{.class-0}["heart"]{.class-2}[ comes awfully close to the man himself (cf. Prov. 23:7), but look at the]{.class-0}[ last two items]{.class-1}[ in the list.]{.class-0}

"[\...A false witness that speaketh lies\...."]{.class-2}[ That's not just the ]{.class-0}[sin]{.class-1}[ of lying, it's the ]{.class-0}[liar himself.]{.class-1}[ "\...and he ]{.class-2}[\[not "it" or "that"; it's the ]{.class-0}[man]{.class-1}[\]]{.class-0}[ that soweth discord among brethren."]{.class-2}[ That's God hating (that's what Prov. 6:16 said) the ]{.class-0}[sinner]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-0}[himself]{.class-1}[, not just his ]{.class-0}[sin]{.class-1}[.]{.class-0}

[Do you know what the Lord said He was going to do to those who hate Him? Deuteronomy 7:10---]{.class-0}["And REPAYETH them that hate him to their face to destroy them: he will not be slack to him that hateth him, HE WILL REPAY HIM TO HIS FACE."]{.class-2}

[That's the Lord telling a sinner He's going to give him the ]{.class-0}[same hatred]{.class-1}[ with which the fellow hated God. The problem with modern Christians is that they don't have a balanced view of God. The One who loved sinners enough to send His Son to die for them said about a lost man: ]{.class-0}["the WRATH OF GOD abideth on him"]{.class-2}[ (John 3:36). He never preached "the Fatherhood of God and the brotherhood of man"; He said lost men are ]{.class-0}["by nature the children of WRATH"]{.class-2}[ (Eph. 2:3).]{.class-0}

[Now, Paul quotes verses 2--3 in regards to the doctrine of Election: ]{.class-0}["As it is written, Jacob have I loved, but Esau have I hated"]{.class-2}[ (Rom. 9:13). Because he links Malachi 1:2--3 with Genesis 25:23 (see Rom. 9:12), the Calvinists latch onto the passage to teach Unconditional Election and the eternal decree of Reprobation. Not one passage cited (Mal. 1; Rom. 9; Gen. 25) had anything to do with any "eternal decrees" or, in the case of Esau and Jacob, individual salvation. Look at Genesis 25:23. Don't just accept what somebody says about it. Look it up and READ IT!]{.class-0}

"[And the LORD said unto her, Two NATIONS are in thy womb, and two manner of PEOPLE shall be separated from thy bowels; and the one PEOPLE shall be stronger than the other PEOPLE; and the elder shall serve the younger."]{.class-2}

[He's ]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ talking about saving Jacob and damning Esau before Genesis 1:1. It is a prophecy (not a decree) made after Rebekah conceived (not ]{.class-0}["before the foundation of the world,"]{.class-2}[ Eph. 1:4) in regard to the ]{.class-0}["NATIONS"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["PEOPLE"]{.class-2}[ who came from the two boys. As in every case of election, it was based on ]{.class-0}[foreknowledge]{.class-1}[ (see 1 Pet. 1:2), not on the arbitrary whims of a capricious Deity. Malachi 1:2--3 is a ]{.class-0}[racial]{.class-1}[ statement about two ]{.class-0}[nations]{.class-1}[: ]{.class-0}["Israel"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 1) and ]{.class-0}["Edom"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 4).]{.class-0}

[Verse 3:]{.class-0}[ "And I hated Esau, and laid his mountains and his heritage waste for the dragons of the wilderness."]{.class-2}[ The new "Bibles" will change the word ]{.class-0}["dragons"]{.class-2}[ to "]{.class-0}[jackals]{.class-1}[" and then lead you to believe that the verse is a description of the present condition of Edom, after the Romans got through with it. ]{.class-0}[The verse hasn't come anywhere close to being fulfilled.]{.class-1}

[The reason for the apostates' change is that things like dragons, unicorns, and satyrs obviously are mythological creatures that don't exist. To them, the ]{.class-0}[King James]{.class-1}[ translators were too stupid and superstitious to know that, so "we" (i.e., the revisers and translators of the modern versions) have to correct their mistakes for an "enlightened," scientific age.]{.class-0}

[Ahh, blow it out your nose! Any time these apostates come across something in the Scriptures they don't understand, they go running to the Greek or Hebrew instead of letting Scripture interpret Scripture (1 Cor. 2:13). The Bible critics changed ]{.class-0}["dragons"]{.class-2}[ to "]{.class-0}[jackals]{.class-1}[" because the Hebrew word (]{.class-0}[tanniyn]{.class-1}[) means "howlers" or "criers." But look at Psalm 74:13.]{.class-0}

"[Thou didst divide the sea by thy strength: thou brakest the heads of the dragons in the waters."]{.class-2}

[You've run into those "sea jackals" dogpaddling around in the ]{.class-0}[Mediterranean]{.class-1}[, have you? You see the kind of nonsense into which you get when you change what God says? In the Bible, ]{.class-0}["dragons"]{.class-2}[ are ]{.class-0}[serpents]{.class-1}[ (Deut. 32:33) associated with a ]{.class-0}[seven-headed dragon]{.class-1}[ called Leviathan (Psa. 74:13--14 cf. Rev. 12:3, 9). Any of you seen a seven-headed jackal lately? I thought not.]{.class-0}

"[Dragons" ]{.class-2}[are ]{.class-0}[devils]{.class-1}[ under the authority of ]{.class-0}[THE Devil]{.class-1}[ (Mark 4:4, 15 cf. Rev. 18:2). The same is true of ]{.class-0}[satyrs]{.class-1}[ (Isa. 34:14 cf. 2 Chron. 11:15; Lev. 17:7) and, in some instances, possibly ]{.class-0}[unicorns]{.class-1}[ (Isa. 34:7 cf. Psa. 22:12; Dan. 8:5).]{.class-0}

The reference in Malachi 1:3 is to a time in the future when the Lord will turn everything south of the Dead Sea (where He burned Sodom) and north of Arabia into a lake of fire. Look at Isaiah 34.

"[Come near, ye nations ]{.class-2}[\[Second Advent---Zeph. 3:8\]]{.class-0}[, to hear; and hearken, ye people: let the earth hear, and all that is therein; the world, and all things that come forth of it. For the indignation of the LORD is upon all nations, and his fury upon all their armies ]{.class-2}[\[Second Advent---Joel 3; Zech. 14; Rev. 19\]]{.class-0}[: he hath utterly destroyed them, he hath delivered them to the slaughter\....For my sword shall be bathed in heaven: behold, it shall come down upon Idumea ]{.class-2}[\[another name for Edom\]]{.class-0}[, and upon the people of my curse]{.class-2}[ \[like you read in Mal. 1:3\]]{.class-0}[, to judgment.]{.class-2}

"[The sword of the LORD is filled with blood]{.class-2}[ \[Second Advent---see the remarks on Gideon under the comments on Zech. 12:4\]]{.class-0}[, it is made fat with fatness, and with the blood of lambs and goats, with the fat of the kidneys of rams: for the LORD hath a sacrifice in Bozrah]{.class-2}[ \[it's in Edom; see Isa. 63:1\]]{.class-0}[, and a great slaughter in the land of Idumea. And the unicorns shall come down with them, and the bullocks with the bulls; and their land shall be soaked with blood, and their dust made fat with fatness.]{.class-2}

"[For it is the day of the LORD'S vengeance, and the year of recompenses for the controversy of Zion ]{.class-2}[\[Second Advent---Isa. 63:4)]{.class-0}[. And the streams thereof shall be turned into pitch, and the dust thereof into brimstone, and the land thereof shall become burning pitch. It shall not be quenched night or day; the smoke thereof shall go up for ever: from generation to generation it shall lie waste ]{.class-2}[\[like you read in Mal. 1:3\]]{.class-0}[; none shall pass through it for ever and ever. But the cormorant and the bittern shall possess it; the owl also and the raven shall dwell in it]{.class-2}[ \[pictures of unclean spirits---Rev. 18:2\]]{.class-0}[.]{.class-2}

"[And thorns shall come up in her palaces, nettles and brambles in the fortresses thereof: and it shall be an habitation of dragons ]{.class-2}[\[that's what you just read in Mal. 1:3\]]{.class-0}[, and a court for owls. The wild beasts of the desert shall also meet with the wild beasts of the island, and the satyr shall cry to his fellow; the screech owl also shall nest there, and find for herself a place of rest"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 34:1--14).]{.class-0}

[Back to Malachi 1: ]{.class-0}["Whereas Edom saith, We are impoverished, but we will return and build the desolate places; thus saith the LORD of hosts, They shall build, but I will thrown down; and they shall call them, The border of wickedness, and, The people against whom the LORD hath indignation for ever"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 4). You just read in Isaiah 34:5 where God called the Edomites ]{.class-0}["the people of my curse."]{.class-2}

[To understand what the Lord had against the Edomites, go back and read the book of Obadiah. When the Lord sent Nebuchadnezzar in to wipe out Jerusalem, ]{.class-0}[the Edomites helped out and prevented the Jews from escaping]{.class-1}[ (Obad. 11--14). That will be repeated in the future by the Moslem Jordanians, the new inhabitants of Edom (Obad. 15).]{.class-0}

"[And your eyes shall see, and ye shall say, The LORD will be magnified from the border of Israel"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 5). Edom is on the south border of Israel. That's the Lord coming down with His armies and kicking the Edomites out of their land and giving it to the Jews. He didn't do that when Israel came out of Egypt (Deut. 2:4--5), but He will when Christ returns.]{.class-0}

[Obadiah 17--21: ]{.class-0}["But upon mount Zion shall be deliverance, and there shall be holiness; and the house of Jacob shall possess their possessions. And the house of Jacob shall be a fire, and the house of Joseph a flame, and the house of Esau for stubble ]{.class-2}[\[the Lord's going to burn them up just like you read it in Isa. 34\]]{.class-0}[, and devour them; and there shall not be any remaining of the house of Esau; for the LORD hath spoken it\....And saviours ]{.class-2}[\[]{.class-0}[plural.]{.class-1}[ Who's that? It's ]{.class-0}[you,]{.class-1}[ if you're saved---Rev. 19:14; 2 Thess. 1:7--10; Joel 2:1--11\]]{.class-0}[ shall come up on mount Zion to judge the mount of Esau; and the kingdom shall be the LORD'S]{.class-2}[ (Dan. 2:44\]]{.class-0}[."]{.class-2}

6 A son honoureth his father, and a servant his master: if then I be a father, where is mine honour? and if I be a master, where is my fear? saith the LORD of hosts unto you, O priests, that despise my name. And ye say, Wherein have we despised thy name?

7 Ye offer polluted bread upon mine altar; and ye say, Wherein have we polluted thee? In that ye say, The table of the LORD is contemptible.

8 And if ye offer the blind for sacrifice, is it not evil? and if ye offer the lame and sick, is it not evil? offer it now unto thy governor; will he be pleased with thee, or accept thy person? saith the LORD of hosts.

9 And now, I pray you, beseech God that he will be gracious unto us: this hath been by your means: will he regard your persons? saith the LORD of hosts.

10 Who is there even among you that would shut the doors for nought? neither do ye kindle fire on mine altar for nought. I have no pleasure in you, saith the LORD of hosts, neither will I accept an offering at your hand.

[Now the Lord's back to addressing Israel: ]{.class-0}["A son honoureth his father, and a servant his master"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 6). Both the ]{.class-0}["son"]{.class-2}[ and the ]{.class-0}["servant"]{.class-2}[ are pictures of ]{.class-0}[Israel]{.class-1}[ (see Exod. 4:22; Isa. 41:8). The verse describes the SOP (Standard Operating Procedure) of the two relationships. A ]{.class-0}["father"]{.class-2}[ is different from a ]{.class-0}["master."]{.class-2}[ A father demands ]{.class-0}[honor]{.class-1}[ (Exod. 20:12); a master demands ]{.class-0}[fear]{.class-1}[ (1 Pet. 2:18)---and I don't mean "reverential trust." I mean F-E-A-R, as in ]{.class-0}[trembling]{.class-1}[ (Eph. 6:5) and ]{.class-0}[quaking]{.class-1}[ (Heb. 12:21) in ]{.class-0}[terror]{.class-1}[ (2 Cor. 5:11), with your ]{.class-0}[knees knocking together]{.class-1}[ (Dan. 5:6). All this "positive" stuff! Listen, if you aren't afraid of a Being who can make a nuclear furnace like the sun and who can drown out 300 million people without blinking, you are a ]{.class-0}[fool.]{.class-1}

"[Saith the LORD of hosts unto you, O priests."]{.class-2}[ He now addresses the priesthood specifically. They are despising the name of the Lord by offering up ]{.class-0}["polluted bread"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 7) and animals with imperfections (vs. 8). The ]{.class-0}["polluted bread"]{.class-2}[ could be a reference either to the Table of Showbread (Lev. 24:5--9) or to the ]{.class-0}["meat offering"]{.class-2}[ (Lev. 2:11). It is probably the latter, since the ]{.class-0}["bread"]{.class-2}[ of verse 7 is being offered at the ]{.class-0}["altar."]{.class-2}[ The ]{.class-0}["meat offering"]{.class-2}[ was ]{.class-0}["polluted"]{.class-2}[ when it was made with ]{.class-0}[leaven]{.class-1}[ (Amos 4:4--5; 1 Cor. 5:7--8).]{.class-0}

[The law of the burnt offering was: ]{.class-0}["Blind, or broken, or maimed, or having a wen, or scurvy, or scabbed, ye shall not offer these unto the LORD, nor make an offering of them upon the altar unto the LORD"]{.class-2}[ (Lev. 22:22). The sacrifice was to be ]{.class-0}["without blemish"]{.class-2}[ (Lev. 22:18--21) because it was a type of Christ's blood atonement (1 Pet. 1:18--19). The Jews were taking the worst they had and offering it to the Lord.]{.class-0}

"[Offer it now unto thy governor; will he be pleased with thee, or accept thy person?"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 8). That's God being sarcastic. He says, "Go give that stuff to your Governor and see if he'll take it." The idea is that if the Governor won't take that mess, what makes them think ]{.class-0}[God]{.class-1}[ will? The answer is He ]{.class-0}[won't;]{.class-1}[ He considers it to be an insult to be brought a piece of junk like that.]{.class-0}

"[And now, I pray you, beseech God that he will be gracious unto us: this hath been by your means"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 9). ]{.class-0}[More sarcasm.]{.class-1}[ The Lord is quoting what ]{.class-0}[they're]{.class-1}[ saying. The people are bringing their junk to the Lord and asking the priests to pray the Lord will be ]{.class-0}["gracious"]{.class-2}[ to them for making the offering. ]{.class-0}["Will he regard your persons?"]{.class-2}[ No, He won't.]{.class-0}

"[Who is there even among you that would shut the doors]{.class-2}[ \[i.e., the doors of the Temple\]]{.class-0}[ for nought]{.class-2}[ \[for nothing\]]{.class-0}[? neither do ye kindle fire on mine altar for nought"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 10). There are two ways of taking this passage. The first is that God is saying, "I would rather you just shut the doors of the Temple than offer the polluted and blemished offerings you're giving me." To get that interpretation, though, you have to change the verse like Scofield does (see his center-column note on the verse).]{.class-0}

[The other way you can look at the passage is that the priests were serving in the Temple for ]{.class-0}[what they could get out of it. ]{.class-1}[The job of opening and shutting the doors of the Temple was one of the menial jobs performed every morning and evening (cf. 1 Sam. 3:15). David said, ]{.class-0}["I had rather be a doorkeeper in the house of my God, than to dwell in the tents of wickedness"]{.class-2}[ (Psa. 84:10). In other words, the privilege of being near to God and serving Him, even in the most menial of tasks, is better than enjoying ]{.class-0}["the pleasures of sin for a season"]{.class-2}[ (Heb. 11:25).]{.class-0}

The priests of verse 10 are like those of Micah 3:11.

"[The heads thereof judge for reward, and the priests thereof teach for hire, and the prophets thereof divine for money."]{.class-2}

That's probably the meaning of Malachi 1:10 based on what the Lord tells them over in 3:14.

"[Ye have said, It is vain to serve God: and what PROFIT is it that we have kept his ordinance\...?"]{.class-2}

[Both Paul and Peter warn the elders of the New Testament local church not to serve for ]{.class-0}["filthy lucre"]{.class-2}[ (money---see Tit. 1:7 and 1 Pet. 5:2 and the comments on those passages in their respective commentaries).]{.class-0}

[Back to Malachi 1:10---]{.class-0}["I have no pleasure in you, said the LORD of hosts, neither will I accept an offering at your hand."]{.class-2}[ He told them He wouldn't take their offerings anymore because their ]{.class-0}[motives]{.class-1}[ (their attitudes) and their ]{.class-0}[methods]{.class-1}[ (their ways of doing it) were both wrong. That's what He told them about 360 years earlier back in Isaiah.]{.class-0}

"[To what purpose is the multitude of your sacrifices unto me? saith the LORD\..."]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 1:11). Well, it was supposed to be for the ]{.class-0}["atonement"]{.class-2}[ of their ]{.class-0}["souls"]{.class-2}[ (Lev. 17:11), but something has obviously changed.]{.class-0}

"[\...I am full of the burnt offerings of rams, and the fat of fed beasts; and I delight not in the blood of bullocks, or of lambs, or of he goats"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 1:11).]{.class-0}

What was the problem? The problem was that the sacrifices were being made in the wrong spirit.

[Micah 6:6--8 says, ]{.class-0}["Wherewith shall I come before the LORD, and bow myself before the high God?]{.class-2}[ \[Micah is speaking for an Old Testament Jew under the Law; there is no doctrinal application for the New Testament Christian in the passage. See the comments in Vol. I of this Commentary\]]{.class-0}[ shall I come before him with burnt offerings, with calves of a year old?]{.class-2}[ \[the implied answer is "no"\]]{.class-0}[ Will the LORD be pleased with thousands of rams ]{.class-2}[\[no\]]{.class-0}[\...He hath shewed thee, O man, what is good; and what doth the LORD require of thee]{.class-2}[ \[an Old Testament Jew under the Law; not anybody in the Body of Christ\]]{.class-0}[, but to do justly, and to love mercy, and to walk humbly with thy God?"]{.class-2}

[Samuel told Saul, ]{.class-0}["Hath the LORD as great delight in burnt offerings and sacrifices, as in obeying the voice of the LORD? Behold, to obey is better than sacrifice, and to hearken than the fat of rams"]{.class-2}[ (1 Sam. 15:22).]{.class-0}

[Solomon wrote, ]{.class-0}["The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination to the LORD"]{.class-2}[ (Prov. 15:8).]{.class-0}

[Back to Isaiah 1: ]{.class-0}["When ye come to appear before me, who hath required this at your hand, to tread in my courts?"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 1:12) Well, He did, all through Exodus, Leviticus, Numbers, and Deuteronomy; but He didn't want them coming in an unrepentant state, flaunting their sin (note "gay pride" in Isa. 1:10). When a fellow did that in Number 25, ]{.class-0}[the Lord imputed His righteousness to the guy who killed that fellow]{.class-1}[ just like He did to Abraham for believing God (Num. 25:6--13; Psa. 106:28--31 cf. Gen. 15:6; Rom. 4:3--9).]{.class-0}

"[Bring no more vain oblations; incense is an abomination unto me ]{.class-2}[\[it wasn't in Exodus and Leviticus; it was commanded. In fact, it was so holy that ]{.class-0}[the Lord killed Aaron's two oldest sons]{.class-1}[ for lighting the incense altar with strange fire\]]{.class-0}[; the new moons and sabbaths, I cannot away; it is iniquity, even the solemn assembly]{.class-2}[ \[it wasn't in 1 Chron. 23:31; 2 Chron. 2:4, 8:13\]]{.class-0}[. Your new moons and your appointed feasts ]{.class-2}[\[He was the one who ]{.class-0}["appointed"]{.class-2}[ them in Lev. 23\]]{.class-0}[ my soul hateth: they are a trouble unto me; I am weary to bear them. And when ye spread forth your hands ]{.class-2}[\[like the Moslems do when they recite the Shahada\]]{.class-0}[, I will hide mine eyes from you: yea, when ye make many prayers ]{.class-2}[\[like the Moslems do five times a day\]]{.class-0}[, I will not hear: your hands are full of blood"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 1:13--15). And then in Isaiah 1:16--18, He tells them what to do to make things right just like He did in Micah 6:8.]{.class-0}

[Now, that was God telling those Jews what He thought of their offerings: once before the captivity (Isa. 1) and once after the captivity (Mal. 1). He does it one more time in the New Testament, and this time, instead of replacing the sacrifices with ]{.class-0}[works]{.class-1}[ (Isa. 1:16--18; Mic. 6:8), He replaces them with the ]{.class-0}[one, effectual offering of His Son.]{.class-1}

"[Wherefore when he cometh into the world, he saith, Sacrifice and offering thou wouldest not, but a body hast thou prepared me: In burnt offerings and sacrifices for sin thou hast had no pleasure. Then said I, Lo, I come (in the volume of the book it is written of me,) to do thy will, O God. Above when he said, Sacrifice and offering and burnt offerings and offering for sin thou wouldest not, neither hadst pleasure therein; which are offered by the law; Then said he, Lo, I come to do thy will, O God. He taketh away the first, that he may establish the second. By the which will we are sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all"]{.class-2}[ (Heb. 10:5--10).]{.class-0}

[That means that, ]{.class-0}[right now]{.class-1}[, in the Church Age, every sacrifice made by man is OUT! That would include any "bloody sacrifice offered up in an unbloody manner" every ]{.class-0}[Sunday morning at 11:00 a.m. ]{.class-1}[When the sacrifices, holy days, new moons, and Sabbaths are ]{.class-0}[reinstituted]{.class-1}[ (see Col. 2:16--17 and comments in that Commentary), they won't be offered by any fat wino in Rome with a half a grapefruit on his head or by any bachelor, child-molesting "father." They will be made by Jewish priests from the tribe of Levi (Ezek. 40:46).]{.class-0}

11 For from the rising of the sun even unto the going down of the same my name shall be great among the Gentiles; and in every place incense shall be offered unto my name, and a pure offering: for my name shall be great among the heathen, saith the LORD of hosts.

12 But ye have profaned it, in that ye say, The table of the LORD is polluted; and the fruit thereof, even his meat, is contemptible.

13 Ye said also, Behold, what a weariness is it! and ye have snuffed at it, saith the LORD of hosts; and ye brought that which was torn, and the lame, and the sick; thus ye brought an offering: should I accept this of your hand? saith the LORD.

14 But cursed be the deceiver, which hath in his flock a male, and voweth, and sacrificeth unto the Lord a corrupt thing: for I am a great King, saith the LORD of hosts, and my name is dreadful among the heathen.

[Verse 11 was the verse Father Sullivan (the priest who gave me Catholic convert courses down at St. Michael's before I was saved) used to prove that ]{.class-0}[incense]{.class-1}[ should be offered at ]{.class-0}[Sunday morning Mass; ]{.class-1}[that's the Catholic alibi for waving a censer around and filling the church building with smoke. He was saying that verse 11 is fulfilled ]{.class-0}[now]{.class-1}[ in the Church Age.]{.class-0}

[The famous hymn writer Isaac Watts wrote: "Jesus shall reign where e'er the sun Does his successive journeys run, His kingdom spread from shore to shore Till moons shall wax and wane no more." He got that from verse 11. The third verse of that hymn goes: "To Him shall endless prayer be made And endless praises crown His head; His name like ]{.class-0}[sweet perfume]{.class-1}[ shall rise With every ]{.class-0}[morning sacrifice."]{.class-1}

[The idea that Watts was trying to get across was that all these missionaries are going to go out and win these heathen to Christ, and they're all going to pray to Him (incense was connected with prayer; see Rev. 8:3--4; Luke 1:10). Watts thought like that because a lot of those Christians back in the Eighteenth and Nineteenth Centuries were ]{.class-0}[Postmillennial:]{.class-1}[ they believed Christ's kingdom was being spread ]{.class-0}[by the preaching of the Gospel.]{.class-1}[ That's that old Southern Baptist prayer for the Sunday morning offering: "Lord, we thank Thee for this beautiful Sabbath morning, and we pray that Thou wouldest bless these tithes and offerings ]{.class-0}[for the ongoing of Thy kingdom."]{.class-1}

[The reason those old-time Christians thought that was because during the Philadelphian Church Period, God gave Christians ]{.class-0}["an open door"]{.class-2}[ to preach the Gospel all around the world (Rev. 3:7--8). He raised up America and Great Britain to take that ]{.class-0}[King James]{.class-1}[ Bible to the ends of the earth (Rev. 3:10), and enabled them to do it. He blessed them so much politically, materially, and economically that Darwin actually thought ]{.class-0}[mankind was evolving and getting better and better.]{.class-1}[ Queen Victoria said it was that Book (the ]{.class-0}[AV]{.class-1}[ 1611) that built the British Empire.]{.class-0}

[When the British and Foreign Bible Society ]{.class-0}[dumped that Book]{.class-1}[ in 1904 for the Alexandrian text, ]{.class-0}[God dumped Britain]{.class-1}[ and got them into World War I. When England ]{.class-0}[dumped that Jew]{.class-1}[ in 1921 (Churchill's White Papers), God got them in World War II and tore the whole empire apart. It took America to save that nation from losing ]{.class-0}[two world wars;]{.class-1}[ and when America dumped that Book for an ]{.class-0}[RSV ]{.class-1}[(1952)]{.class-0}[, ]{.class-1}[an ]{.class-0}[NASV]{.class-1}[ (1963), and an ]{.class-0}[NIV]{.class-1}[ (1973), ]{.class-0}[the Lord let us lose our next three wars]{.class-1}[ (Korea, Vietnam, and Iraq). You aren't spreading Christ's kingdom today. The doors are slamming shut all around the world because this country got rid of God's Book.]{.class-0}

[Verse 11 is a reference to the second gathering of the Gentiles (see the comments under Zeph. 3:9). The Gentiles are coming to the Temple at Jerusalem (Isa. 2:2--3; Zech. 14:16--21) and offering up incense and sacrifices (Ezek. 40--46) to the King on the throne at Mount Zion (Zech. 14:9; Psa. 2:6). It has ]{.class-0}[nothing]{.class-1}[ to do with a bunch of heathen in the South Sea islands, Africa, Pantagonea, Borneo, etc., hearing a missionary preach the Gospel and getting saved and praying to Jesus Christ as their Lord and Saviour.]{.class-0}

[Verse 12: ]{.class-0}["But ye have profaned it." "Profaned"]{.class-2}[ what? They profaned God's ]{.class-0}[name]{.class-1}[ (vss. 6, 11). The word ]{.class-0}[profane]{.class-1}[ is a Latin word that means "before (]{.class-0}[pro]{.class-1}[) the temple (]{.class-0}[fane]{.class-1}[)." The idea is that you're standing in front of the temple half in and half out: you've got one foot in the church and one foot in the world. Our word would be ]{.class-0}[worldling.]{.class-1}[ The English meaning of the word is "to treat the sacred with contempt." A good example would be cussing; you're taking God's name in vain and showing ]{.class-0}[no respect]{.class-1}[ for it. That's why it's called "PROFANity."]{.class-0}

[How did the priests profane God's ]{.class-0}[name?]{.class-1}[ Well, they were treating the Lord's table as "polluted" and showing contempt for the bread and meat of the sacrifices that the Lord gave them for their food. That's what the two sons of Eli, Hophni and Phinehas, were doing back in 1 Samuel 2:12--17. The Lord had prescribed a certain manner for the priests to get their portion of the sacrifice after it was offered to God, and Hophni and Phinehas were coming in there and taking the best part for themselves before it was sacrificed. Because of that, the people ]{.class-0}["abhorred the offering of the LORD"]{.class-2}[ (1 Sam. 2:17). Those priests showed contempt for what the Lord said about making the sacrifice, and the people knew it and couldn't stand to make the sacrifices anymore because of it. That's the same type of thing going on here in Malachi; look at verse 13.]{.class-0}

"[Ye said also, Behold, what a weariness is it! and ye snuffed at it, saith the LORD of hosts."]{.class-2}[ They were growing tired of serving in the Temple and turning up their noses at it. They were getting tired of making sure the candlestick stayed lit and setting the showbread out fresh every morning and burning the incense for prayer at the set time day in and day out. They were growing weary of cleaning the offerings and burning the bad stuff outside the camp and having their food fixed the same way every time a sacrifice was made. It reminds you of what the children of Israel said about the manna after they had been eating it for forty years: ]{.class-0}["our soul loatheth this light bread"]{.class-2}[ (Num. 21:5).]{.class-0}

They were upset because, as Levites, they served in the Temple with nothing to show for it. Those Levites received no inheritance in the land because the Lord was supposed to be their inheritance (Deut. 10:9). That's why the Lord gave them the tithe that people brought to the Temple (Num. 18:20--24).

[Now the New Testament applications is this: ]{.class-0}["And let us not be weary in well doing: for in due season we shall reap, if we faint not"]{.class-2}[ (Gal. 6:9). Your reward is not down here right now, Christian. It's up in Heaven and with Christ at His return.]{.class-0}

[Jesus said, ]{.class-0}["Lay not up for yourselves treasures upon earth, where moth and rust doth corrupt, and where thieves break through and steal: But lay up for yourselves treasures in heaven, where neither moth and rust doth corrupt, and where thieves do not break through nor steal: For where your treasure is, there will your heart be also"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 6:19--21).]{.class-0}

[The Apostle Paul wrote, ]{.class-0}["If we suffer, we shall also reign with him"]{.class-2}[ (2 Tim. 2:12). It's the cross now and the crown later; you have to eat your vegetables before you get the dessert.]{.class-0}

[One time Peter came to the Lord and said, "We've left everything to follow you. What are we going to get for it?" Jesus told him, ]{.class-0}["Verily I say unto you, That ye which have followed me, in the regeneration ]{.class-2}[\[that's the Millennium---see Isa. 11\]]{.class-0}[ when the Son of man shall sit in the throne of his glory, ye also shall sit upon twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 19:27--28).]{.class-0}

[It's not all here and now.]{.class-1}[ The Christian has to have what I call "the long look." He has to serve the Lord faithfully with the Judgment Seat of Christ and Eternity in mind.]{.class-0}

[Back to Malachi 1: ]{.class-0}["Ye brought that which was torn, and the lame, and the sick"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 13). That Jew was to bring the ]{.class-0}[best]{.class-1}[ to the Lord, and he was to bring the ]{.class-0}[first]{.class-1}[ (Num. 18:12--13). As we shall see, the Jews to whom Malachi was preaching violated both (see the comments on 3:8).]{.class-0}

"[Thus ye brought an offering: should I accept this of your hand? saith the LORD."]{.class-2}[ No (see the comments on vs. 8).]{.class-0}

"[But cursed be the deceiver, which hath in his flock a male, and voweth, and sacrificeth unto the Lord a corrupt thing"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 14). There's a fellow who has a proper beast to sacrifice, and he promises it to God, but when the time comes to sacrifice it, he substitutes a bad one for the good one. God calls a fellow like that a liar, and He curses him. That's what happened to Ananias and Sapphira in Acts 5. The problem there wasn't that they substituted a bad for a good, but ]{.class-0}[they lied about what they offered in order to impress everybody. ]{.class-1}[Peter says they lied to the Holy Ghost, and the Holy Ghost killed them because of it (Acts 5:3--10).]{.class-0}

[Solomon wrote, ]{.class-0}["When thou vowest a vow unto God, defer not to pay it; for he hath no pleasure in fools: pay that which thou hast vowed. Better is it that thou shouldest not vow, than that thou shouldest vow and not pay. Suffer not thy mouth to cause thy flesh to sin; neither say thou before the angel]{.class-2}[ \[that's the Angel of the Lord\]]{.class-0}[, that it was an error: wherefore should God be angry at thy voice, and destroy the work of thine hands?"]{.class-2}[ (Eccl. 5:4--6)]{.class-0}

"[For I am a great King, saith the LORD of hosts."]{.class-2}[ He certainly is. In theology, this is called "the sovereignty of God." He oversees the kingdoms of this world as ]{.class-0}["the King of heaven"]{.class-2}[ and sets up ]{.class-0}["the basest of men"]{.class-2}[ as its rulers (Dan. 4:17, 34--37): ]{.class-0}["the powers that be are ordained of God"]{.class-2}[ (Rom. 13:1). In the Old Testament, He was spiritually ]{.class-0}["the King of Israel"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 44:6). But all of that does not detract from the fact that in the future He will be a ]{.class-0}[literal]{.class-1}[ King of a ]{.class-0}[literal]{.class-1}[ nation on the ]{.class-0}[literal ]{.class-1}[throne of a ]{.class-0}[literal]{.class-1}[ kingdom (Luke 1:32--33; Isa. 9:6--7; Psa. 2:6). He will return as ]{.class-0}["KING OF KINGS, AND LORD OF LORDS"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 19:16) to be ]{.class-0}["king over all the earth"]{.class-2}[ (Zech. 14:9; see also Jer. 23:5).]{.class-0}

"[And my name is dreadful among the heathen."]{.class-2}[ Not right now it's not (Psa. 2:1--3). Right now, they use it as a cuss word. The Popes steal it for themselves ("Vicar of Christ" and "Holy Father"); the Hindus and Buddhists treat it as just another "avatar," like Krishna and Buddha; the Moslems ]{.class-0}[change]{.class-1}[ it to Esau ("Isa," which is not a translation or even a ]{.class-0}[transliteration]{.class-1}[ of "Jesus") and make it inferior to Mohammed and Allah. They ban His Book (or burn it); they violate His commandments; they torture, imprison, and kill His followers; and when He shows up the second time, they will try to kill Him again (Rev. 19:19). But when He returns, they won't be spitting on Him and slapping Him around like they did the first time. They won't be calling Him ]{.class-0}[Beelzebub]{.class-1}[ (Matt. 12:24) or a ]{.class-0}[bastard]{.class-1}[ (John 8:41). They'll be flat on their faces before Him, ]{.class-0}[licking the dust off His feet ]{.class-1}[(Psa. 72:9, 2:12); and if they don't, they'll get a ]{.class-0}[two-inch, galvanized iron pipe ]{.class-1}[in the skull right between the eyes (Psa. 2:9; Rev. 2:27, 12:5, 19:15).]{.class-0}

[You talk about a dreadful name! When Jesus Christ sits on His throne in Jerusalem, He will be an ]{.class-0}[absolute]{.class-1}[ monarch with ]{.class-0}[absolute]{.class-1}[ power---literally. He will be a ruler with the literal power to do ]{.class-0}[anything He wants:]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-0}["Alleluia: for the Lord God OMNIPOTENT reigneth"]{.class-2}[ (Rev. 19:6)---that's ALL POWER. He can turn visible or invisible at will (Luke 24:31); He can pass through solid objects (John 20:19); He can travel faster than the speed of light through airless space (Eph. 4:10); His word holds the universe together (Col. 1:17); and He can't be assassinated because He already conquered death (1 Cor. 15).]{.class-0}

Well GLORY! That's the King I'm waiting for. "Which in his times he shall shew, who is the blessed and only Potentate, the King of kings, and Lord of lords" (1 Tim. 6:15).

[]{#section-0_1_dde206cd04218a9d_b69d.xhtml}

CHAPTER TWO

2:1 And now, O ye priests, this commandment is for you.

2 If ye will not hear, and if ye will not lay it to heart, to give glory unto my name, saith the LORD of hosts, I will even send a curse upon you, and I will curse your blessings: yea, I have cursed them already, because ye do not lay it to heart.

3 Behold, I will corrupt your seed, and spread dung upon your faces, even the dung of your solemn feasts; and one shall take you away with it.

[Verses 1--9 are addressed to the ]{.class-0}["priests"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 1) and are a continuation of what the Lord was telling them back in 1:6--14. Instead of glorifying God's name by being faithful priests according to what was laid down in the Law, they were despising God's name by offering the ]{.class-0}["polluted"]{.class-2}[ and the imperfect (1:6--8).]{.class-0}

"[I will even send a curse upon you, and I will curse your blessings: yea, I have cursed them already, because ye do not lay it to heart"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 2). How's ]{.class-0}[that]{.class-1}[ for anti-Semitism. The most anti-Semitic literature in the world is not by Hitler, the KKK, or the Roman Catholic Church; it is the ]{.class-0}[Old Testament.]{.class-1}

[God told that Jew, ]{.class-0}["But it shall come to pass, if thou wilt not hearken unto the voice of the LORD thy God, to observe to do all his commandments and his statutes which I command thee this day; that all these curses shall come upon thee, and overtake thee"]{.class-2}[ (Deut. 28:15). The rest of Deuteronomy 28 is devoted to the curses God would put on the Jews if they didn't obey Him (Deut. 28:16--68).]{.class-0}

[Here in Malachi 2, the Lord tells those priests what He's going to do with them: ]{.class-0}["Behold, I will corrupt your seed, and spread DUNG upon your faces"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 3). You won't find ]{.class-0}[that]{.class-1}[ "updated" in the modern versions. ]{.class-0}[All]{.class-1}[ these Bible-corrupting apostates warn you about falling into the extreme of not accepting new versions because the Bible couldn't be read by the common man in an "ancient language" (that's the way one of them put it). That's to make you think the ]{.class-0}[King James Version]{.class-1}[ can't be understood by the average reader because it's in "ancient English." That's the ]{.class-0}[excuse]{.class-1}[ they give you for removing 64,000 words from the Bible (]{.class-0}[NIV]{.class-1}[).]{.class-0}

[Listen! not one of these new "Bibles" is in the common, "Koine" street language of the average American or "Brit"; Malachi 2:3 is a good example. Do you know what the common expression is for ]{.class-0}["dung"]{.class-2}[ in current usage? ]{.class-0}[Of course you do]{.class-1}[. Well, if these revisers and publishers are trying to make the Bible easier to understand, ]{.class-0}[why didn't they put it in?]{.class-1}

[You say, "It's crude; it's vulgar." Aww, come off it! You people reading this are watching stuff in your living rooms that would have taken an act of Congress back when I was growing up just to put in print. You can hear any dirty, four-letter word ever invented through television, movies, and the radio. You can get the belly dancers, burlesque shows, sex, faggots, and Lesbians pumped right into your living room through cable television. This past week some of you have seen as many as eight murders, cars driving off cliffs and bridges, helicopters blowing up, some masked madman chasing down girls and stabbing them repeatedly with a butcher knife, vampires sucking someone's blood, some fellow turning into an animal and attacking someone, etc. ]{.class-0}[Don't try to kid me.]{.class-1}[ Are you so refined and holy that you couldn't take the modern terminology for ]{.class-0}["dung"]{.class-2}[?]{.class-0}

[Now if anybody could put out a "Bible" in modern language,]{.class-0}[ I could do it.]{.class-1}[ A few years ago, I put out a study Bible with my notes in it; I didn't change (or even ]{.class-0}[recommend]{.class-1}[ a change in) one word in the ]{.class-0}[King James]{.class-1}[ text. But if you wanted an "updated" Bible, I could flat give you one, boy. All these "hothouse" scholars, translators, and revisers don't have any experience talking with the average man to know what his speech is like; they spend all their time in classrooms, libraries, and air-conditioned offices. I have been in the locker rooms, the barracks, the beaches, the bars, the construction sights, the fishing boats, the ball fields, etc.; ]{.class-0}[I know how men talk.]{.class-1}

Imagine "Ruckman" putting out a Bible in modern language! Moses goes in to Pharaoh and says, "You'd better shape up or ship out, boy!" Pharaoh says, "Aww, don't bug me; hit the road!" "You'll be sorry, man," says Moses, "Heavy, heavy hangs over your head."

[See, that's a "Bible" you can understand. Of course, it's not much of a ]{.class-0}[Bible;]{.class-1}[ but then again, neither are the ]{.class-0}[RSV, ASV, NASV, NRSV, NIV, ESV, NKJV,]{.class-1}[ and the other 226 pieces of corrupt garbage that have come off the printing presses since 1880.]{.class-0}

[Verse 3 is ]{.class-0}[literal]{.class-1}[. It was literally fulfilled in the Nazi concentration camps in Germany and Poland (see our remarks under Hab. 2:16). The ]{.class-0}["solemn feasts"]{.class-2}[ would be the feasts of the ]{.class-0}[seventh month: ]{.class-1}[Trumpets, Atonement, and Tabernacles (see the comments in the Introduction to Haggai).]{.class-0}

4 And ye shall know that I have sent this commandment unto you, that my covenant might be with Levi, saith the LORD of hosts.

5 My covenant was with him of life and peace; and I gave them to him for the fear wherewith he feared me, and was afraid before my name.

6 The law of truth was in his mouth, and iniquity was not found in his lips: he walked with me in peace and equity, and did turn many away from iniquity.

7 For the priest's lips should keep knowledge, and they should seek the law at his mouth: for he is the messenger of the LORD of hosts.

8 But ye are departed out of the way; ye have caused many to stumble at the law; ye have corrupted the covenant of Levi, saith the LORD of hosts.

9 Therefore have I also made you contemptible and base before all the people, according as ye have not kept my ways, but have been partial in the law.

The passage is set in contrast to the New Testament priesthood. The Old Testament priesthood was exclusive to one tribe separated from the other twelve. They were a special people who wore special clothes to perform a special job.

[But when you get to the New Testament, the priesthood of the Messiah transcends the Levitical priesthood (Heb. 7 cf. Psa. 110:4), so in the Body of Christ, there is ]{.class-0}[no special priest class. Every believer is a priest]{.class-1}[ (1 Pet. 2:5, 9). That was the keystone of the ]{.class-0}[Reformation;]{.class-1}[ it was called "the Priesthood of the Believer."]{.class-0}

[The problem is over 900 million professing Christians on earth who believe that when God got rid of the Levitical priesthood, ]{.class-0}[He replaced it with a bachelor priesthood headquartered in Rome.]{.class-1}[ The Nicolaitan hierarchy of the Roman Catholic Church has ]{.class-0}[usurped]{.class-1}[ the Old Testament priesthood. That's why they have holy water, holy garments, incense, censers, miters, "arks," etc.]{.class-0}

[The New Testament Christian has a high priest, and it]{.class-0}[ isn't ]{.class-1}[any monkey over in Rome with a half a grapefruit on the top of his head. Peter was ]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ the "chief of apostles"; Jesus Christ was.]{.class-0}

"[Wherefore, holy brethren, partakers of the heavenly calling, consider the APOSTLE and HIGH PRIEST of our profession, CHRIST JESUS"]{.class-2}[ (Heb. 3:1).]{.class-0}

[Simon Peter was just ]{.class-0}["an elder"]{.class-2}[ among ]{.class-0}["elders"]{.class-2}[ (1 Pet. 5:1). He didn't rule over any Council of Bishops or College of Cardinals. In fact, he said Jesus Christ was ]{.class-0}["the chief Shepherd"]{.class-2}[ (1 Pet. 5:4). When someone from the priest tribe of Levi actually shows up in the congregation of a local church (Barnabas---Acts 4:36), he enters as a ]{.class-0}[common believer]{.class-1}[ among many; his lineage gives him no special treatment or position; he exercises no special power or authority over ]{.class-0}[any]{.class-1}[ other believer. That is ]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ the case in the Old Testament.]{.class-0}

"[That my covenant might be with Levi, saith the LORD of hosts. My covenant was with him of life and peace"]{.class-2}[ (vss. 4--5). That covenant was made with Levi in Numbers 3:5--10 (cf. Deut. 10:8--9) and Numbers 25:12--13. Notice in that last reference that it is called a ]{.class-0}["covenant of an EVERLASTING priesthood"]{.class-2}[ (Num. 25:13). That means that even though the Levites have been temporarily set aside as priests and are now just one of the twelve tribes (see Rev. 7:7), they will one day be ]{.class-0}[restored to the priesthood]{.class-1}[ in the Temple during the Millennium (Ezek. 44:10--16).]{.class-0}

"[And I gave them to him for the FEAR wherewith he FEARED, and was AFRAID before my name"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 5). Notice ]{.class-0}[again]{.class-1}[ (see the original comments under 1:6), that the]{.class-0}[ fear of God]{.class-1}[ has nothing to do with "reverential trust." It is being ]{.class-0}["afraid."]{.class-2}[ Is that clear? But what the Devil does is quote Scripture right back at you to take away any fear of God out of your heart.]{.class-0}

"[There is NO FEAR in love; but perfect love casteth out fear: because fear hath torment. He that feareth is not made perfect in love"]{.class-2}[ (1 John 4:18).]{.class-0}

[In other words, if you love God perfectly, you won't be afraid of Him. ]{.class-0}[That is your positive, lovey-dovey Charismatic approach.]{.class-1}[ It completely ignores the ]{.class-0}[context]{.class-1}[. The context is God loving us ]{.class-0}[first]{.class-1}[ (1 John 4:19) and us dwelling in the love of God (1 John 4:16) which Paul told you was ]{.class-0}["in Christ Jesus our Lord"]{.class-2}[ (Rom. 8:39). Your love for God is perfected when you believe what the love of God accomplished for you at Calvary (1 John 4:14--16). When you do that, you do not have to ]{.class-0}[fear]{.class-1}[ what He will do to you ]{.class-0}["in the day of judgment"]{.class-2}[ (1 John 4:17); in other words, you don't have to worry ]{.class-0}[about going to Hell]{.class-1}[ (Rom. 8:38--39 cf. 1 Cor. 1:7--8; Eph. 4:30; Phil. 1:6).]{.class-0}

[You can know right ]{.class-0}[now]{.class-1}[ that you have eternal life as a present possession (1 John 5:13): ]{.class-0}["because as he is]{.class-2}[ \[i.e., ]{.class-0}["the Son of God"]{.class-2}[---1 John 4:15\]]{.class-0}[, so are we in this world"]{.class-2}[ (1 John 4:17). Oddly enough, it is the ]{.class-0}[Charismatics]{.class-1}[ who are afraid all the time of losing their salvation, so I guess they don't have ]{.class-0}["perfect love."]{.class-2}

[Their problem is that they are afraid of the ]{.class-0}[wrong thing.]{.class-1}[ If you want something of which to be afraid, be afraid God will kick you in the pants for not living right (Gal. 6:7--8; Rom. 8:12--13), be afraid of losing rewards at the Judgment Seat of Christ (1 Cor. 3:11--15), but don't be afraid of losing your salvation (2 Tim. 1:12; Heb 13:5).]{.class-0}

[Back to Malachi 2: ]{.class-0}["The law of truth was in his mouth\...For the priest's lips should keep knowledge, and they should seek the law of his mouth"]{.class-2}[ (vss. 6--7). This brings us to the second group of usurpers to the Levitical priesthood. The Levites were ]{.class-0}[custodians]{.class-1}[ of the Old Testament Scriptures; they were the ]{.class-0}[Bible teachers]{.class-1}[ of the nation of Israel (see Deut. 17:9; 2 Chron. 17:8--9, 30:22, 31:4, 35:3). The Levites who copied the Scriptures were called "scribes." Although by the time of Christ they were corrupt like most of the priesthood (see Matt. 23), they didn't start out that way. Ezra was an example of a ]{.class-0}[good scribe]{.class-1}[ (Ezra 7:1--6, 10).]{.class-0}

[But in the New Testament, it is the Body of Christ as a whole that takes care of the Scriptures (cf. Acts 8:4, 11:19--21). There is no "church council" or "Congress of Fundamentalism" that sets itself up as the final authority in matters of faith and practice to define sound doctrine or heresy for the Body of Christ; ]{.class-0}[it is the universal witness of the Holy Spirit in the body of believers as a whole]{.class-1}[ that decides what ]{.class-0}[is]{.class-1}[ Scripture and what ]{.class-0}[isn't.]{.class-1}

[When the Catholics tried to do that at the Council of Carthage (A. D. 397), they ]{.class-0}[added fourteen books]{.class-1}[ to your Old Testament that Jesus Christ ]{.class-0}[rejected]{.class-1}[ as Scripture (see Luke 24:44; Matt. 23:35). The fact that they happened to "hit it right" when it came to the 27 books of the New Testament is neither here nor there; the Holy Spirit had testified to that fact for over 300 years to Christians throughout the Roman Empire.]{.class-0}

[The Protestants don't fare much better. They replaced the Popes, Bishops, and Cardinals with Fundamental, Conservative, and Evangelical ]{.class-0}[scholarship]{.class-1}[. The apostates who worship ]{.class-0}[unknown, unread, ]{.class-1}[non-existent "originals" have taken it upon themselves to tell Christians what God said and what He didn't. They do that despite the fact that the Holy Spirit bore witness for sixteen centuries as to exactly what was His word. The term ]{.class-0}["Textus Receptus" ]{.class-1}[(Received Text) was not some publisher's "blurb" invented by the Elzevir brothers (1633) to sell books (like the modern versions do); it was put on the God-honored text in recognition of the fact that ]{.class-0}[that]{.class-1}[ was the text that had been accepted by evangelical believers from the times of the apostles until German rationalists and English deists cast doubt on it.]{.class-0}

[The reason the ]{.class-0}[King James]{.class-1}[ Bible has lasted for 400 years is ]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ because any ]{.class-0}[committee, congress, ]{.class-1}[or ]{.class-0}[council]{.class-1}[ decreed that Christians should read it or use it. ]{.class-0}[In fact, just the opposite is true.]{.class-1}[ Every translation committee since 1880 has tried to get rid of that Book; every teacher and professor of every major College, University, and Seminary in America and Europe has ]{.class-0}[criticized]{.class-1}[ that Book and tried to ]{.class-0}[replace]{.class-1}[ it with the text of Westcott and Hort, Aland and Metzger, the ]{.class-0}[ASV]{.class-1}[, and the ]{.class-0}[RSV]{.class-1}[ (same text). Yet after 400 years, 85% of the Christians in America who read their Bibles on a regular basis still read the ]{.class-0}[King James Version]{.class-1}[ instead of all the other junk. Why is that?]{.class-0}

[The reason is the same reason that the ]{.class-0}[KJV]{.class-1}[ replaced the ]{.class-0}[Great Bible]{.class-1}[, the ]{.class-0}[Geneva Bible]{.class-1}[, the ]{.class-0}[Bishops' Bible]{.class-1}[, etc., within fifty years of its publication. It is why the ]{.class-0}[King James Version]{.class-1}[ became the ]{.class-0}[Authorized Version]{.class-1}[, even though it did not bear that title when it was first published in 1611, and no King or church officially authorized its use. It is the reason that the ]{.class-0}[AV ]{.class-1}[became THE STANDARD to which all other "Bibles" ]{.class-0}[have to compare themselves in order to sell. ]{.class-1}[The Lord's hand was on that Book, and the ]{.class-0}[Holy Spirit]{.class-1}[ bore witness that it was ]{.class-0}["the holy scriptures"]{.class-2}[ in the universal language of the last days---English.]{.class-0}

[The Holy Spirit bears witness to ]{.class-0}[that Book]{.class-1}[, and He ]{.class-0}[doesn't ]{.class-1}[to all the other 233 pieces of junk on the market. I don't know how many times I have asked a congregation "How many of you believed the ]{.class-0}[King James]{.class-1}[ Bible was the word of God ]{.class-0}[before]{.class-1}[ you ever heard of me" and had over three-quarters of them raise their hands (or stand). It's not "Ruckman" that teaches you that the ]{.class-0}[King James]{.class-1}[ is God's word; ]{.class-0}[it's the Third Person of the Trinity.]{.class-1}[ Don't you blame that on me!]{.class-0}

[We have had a young man come to PBI and testify that he got saved in jail fooling around with some new version. Then some guy came through there and gave him a ]{.class-0}[King James]{.class-1}[ Bible, and the prisoner dumped his new version for the ]{.class-0}[KJV]{.class-1}[, because when he compared the two, he said the modern translation didn't ]{.class-0}[read]{.class-1}[ like "the Bible" (]{.class-0}[KJV]{.class-1}[!).]{.class-0}

[One fellow said, after he got saved, he went into a bookstore to get a Bible. The sales clerk showed him everything ]{.class-0}[but]{.class-1}[ a ]{.class-0}[King James]{.class-1}[ (]{.class-0}["Living" Bible, NASV, RSV, NIV, "Good News," ]{.class-1}[and all the other crap). Without knowing what he was doing, he reached down for the ]{.class-0}[only one]{.class-1}[ the clerk hadn't shown him and picked up a ]{.class-0}[King James]{.class-1}[ Bible. He said, "Well, I don't know much about Bibles, but what about ]{.class-0}[this one?"]{.class-1}[ The clerk said, "Oh, that's an old-fashioned Bible. It's not like the others; you won't understand it." That new Christian said, ]{.class-0}["I think I'll take this one anyway," ]{.class-1}[and bought himself a ]{.class-0}[King James]{.class-1}[ Bible. Now what was that? That was the Holy Spirit in that fellow saying, "That's it right there." ]{.class-0}["Ruckman" had nothing to do with it.]{.class-1}

[One of our young men was out on the street preaching one time, and a former member of Brent Baptist Church approached him. That fellow had gone off to Bob Jones University and been talked out of his faith in the Book, so he just had to "straighten out" our student. He said, "Do you believe that ]{.class-0}[King James]{.class-1}[ Bible is perfect?"]{.class-0}

The young man replied, "Yeah; I don't believe it has any mistakes in it.

[That apostate from BJU said, "]{.class-0}[Ruckman]{.class-1}[ taught you that; ]{.class-0}[you're following a man."]{.class-1}

"[Well, do you believe it ]{.class-0}[has]{.class-1}[ mistakes in it?" asked our student.]{.class-0}

"[Yes," said the apostate.]{.class-0}

[Then the young street preacher asked that fellow, ]{.class-0}["Well who taught you that?"]{.class-1}

[Good question.]{.class-1}[ Brethren, sooner or later, you will have to face the questions of ]{.class-0}[who]{.class-1}[ teaches you that Book ]{.class-0}[is]{.class-1}[ the perfect word of God without proven error, and ]{.class-0}[who ]{.class-1}[teaches you that ]{.class-0}[it's not.]{.class-1}[ You say, "You're responsible for 'King James Onlyism,' Ruckman." Well, then, who's responsible for teaching that ]{.class-0}[all those "Bibles" that contradict each other]{.class-1}[ are all the "Word of God"? What ]{.class-0}[heretic]{.class-1}[ taught you that?]{.class-0}

[I teach that it's the Holy Ghost of God that teaches you that Book is the pure word of God and it is the ]{.class-0}[Devil]{.class-1}[ who teaches you that it ]{.class-0}[isn't]{.class-1}[.]{.class-0}

Now some of the brethren are really "touchy" about that. There's a nut out in California---and I say that with apologies to every peanut, cashew, almond, pecan, walnut, and macadamia in the State---named R. L. Hymers. (I call him "Hotdog" because he likes to show off for the camera; he's always having his picture taken with some "big shot" or causing some kind of stir to get on television. In sports, when a guy is doing a lot of unnecessary stuff just to look good, the term is "hotdogging," so that's why I call him "Hotdog Hymers.")

[Anyway, old "Hotdog" had gotten ahold of some of my stuff about the ]{.class-0}[King James]{.class-1}[ and read it. Being a typical apostate Fundamentalist, he thought the ]{.class-0}[AV]{.class-1}[ was just the work of men and had errors in it. He wrote a thing that got published by D. A. Waite ("Bible for Today") on "Ruckman" being "demonized."]{.class-0}

One day, over 20 years ago, he called me up and said, "Ruckman, I sure appreciate the great work you're doing. You could be another Martin Luther."

(I said to myself, "Uh oh; here it comes." I mean when these guys start bowing down and kissing your feet, that usually means they're about to pull the rug our from underneath you.)

[He went on, "I'll tell you what I'll do for you. If you come out here and let me cast the demons ]{.class-0}[out of you, I'll let you preach in my church]{.class-1}[ while you're here." (He had a store-front church in Los Angeles with something like 120 members, and I had preached for some of the "big boys" in the BBF, like John Rawlings and Beauchamp Vick, who had a thousand or more in their congregations.)]{.class-0}
