[Now the reason he talked about casting the demons out of me was because he had read my testimony and figured I had gotten demon-possessed (he called it "demonized") ]{.class-0}[when I was studying Buddhism before I was saved. ]{.class-1}[He believed that the reason I believed the ]{.class-0}[AV]{.class-1}[ was because the ]{.class-0}[demons]{.class-1}[ were still in me.]{.class-0}

[Of course, I was laughing so hard that I could hardly talk to him over the phone. He said, "I've been praying for you." I told him,]{.class-0}[ "Good, you need the practice, and I need the prayer."]{.class-1}

[At one point he asked me where I got what I taught about the ]{.class-0}[King James]{.class-1}[ Bible being inerrant and infallible (I say "without proven error" because none of these birds have been able yet to ]{.class-0}[prove ]{.class-1}[any of the "errors" of which they accuse the Book of making). I asked him, "Where do ]{.class-0}[you]{.class-1}[ think I got it from?"]{.class-0}

He said, "Well, you just got off on the wrong track there."

[I told him, "I couldn't have been taught that about the ]{.class-0}[King James]{.class-1}[ Bible by my momma and daddy; they were ]{.class-0}[unsaved Episcopalians]{.class-1}[. And I certainly wasn't taught it in ]{.class-0}[any school."]{.class-1}

[Listen! my daddy had an IQ of 150; He was a graduate of MIT in Boston. He was a Deist and an evolutionist. He never believed ]{.class-0}[any]{.class-1}[ Bible was the word of God. My mother could work any crossword puzzle in this country in thirty minutes and was a chronic alcoholic. The only time she ever came out to hear me preach, she made fun of the Pennsylvania Dutch thinking the Devil could get in their barns. I didn't learn anything about the ]{.class-0}[King James]{.class-1}[ Bible at home.]{.class-0}

[Well, if I didn't learn it at home,]{.class-1}[ where did I learn it? From the ]{.class-0}[Episcopalian Church?]{.class-1}[ Why, the rector of the Episcopalian church my family attended in Topeka, Kansas would use ]{.class-0}[anything]{.class-1}[ before he would use a ]{.class-0}[King James]{.class-1}[ Bible, even though a bunch of Anglicans and Puritans put it out. That rector didn't preach the English Bible; he preached ]{.class-0}[gun control]{.class-1}[. From 1933--1939, when Hitler and Mussolini were getting ready to start World War II, the fellow was preaching ]{.class-0}[unilateral disarmament.]{.class-1}[ At that time, my dad was a Major (he was promoted to Colonel just before the war hit), and he would come out of that church every Sunday morning cussing because of the message. I don't recall that preacher giving the plan of salvation one time, under any condition, the entire time I went to that church.]{.class-0}

[So where did I learn what I teach about the King James]{.class-1}[ Bible? Did I get it in ]{.class-0}[school?]{.class-1}[ I never heard any sermons in ]{.class-0}[high school]{.class-1}[. The only one I heard while attending ]{.class-0}[Kansas State Agricultural College]{.class-1}[ was some Pentecostal preacher over a radio screaming "You're going to Hell! You're going to Hell!" I shut the radio off quick, so I didn't learn it there. When I went to the ]{.class-0}[University of Alabama]{.class-1}[, it was known as "the nightclub of the South." I played in a ]{.class-0}[dance band]{.class-1}[ called the "Star Dusters" there in Alabama. Do you think I learned it there? Are you kidding me, man? My friends were lost, cussing, drunken]{.class-0}[ Italian Roman Catholics.]{.class-1}[ I didn't learn it at ]{.class-0}[Bob Jones University]{.class-1}[ after I got saved. They were the ones who taught me that only the ]{.class-0}[original autographs]{.class-1}[ were inspired; therefore, the ]{.class-0}[King James]{.class-1}[ Bible couldn't be Scripture since ]{.class-0}["All scripture is given by inspiration of God"]{.class-2}[ (2 Tim. 3:16). ]{.class-0}[No, I didn't learn it at any school.]{.class-1}

[I didn't learn it in ]{.class-0}[the military]{.class-1}[. The only two encounters I had with the Bible didn't amount to anything. I didn't read it or even look at it. My mother-in-law sent me a Bible. My buddies saw it and were making fun of me for it. I said, "This thing won't stop bullets," and ]{.class-0}[I threw it in the trash can.]{.class-1}[ The other time, my C.O. over in the Philippines, Lieutenant Self, tried showing me something out of the Bible, and I told him, ]{.class-0}["Put that thing away; that's a lot of baloney."]{.class-1}[ He put it away, and that was that. I didn't learn it in the military.]{.class-0}

[I didn't learn it from Hugh Pyle;]{.class-1}[ he never even brought up the subject with me. When he led me to Christ in the radio station of WEAR, he pulled out a ]{.class-0}[King James]{.class-1}[ Bible and asked, "Do you believe this is the word of God?" I said, ]{.class-0}["Yep, that's it." ]{.class-1}[But that wasn't because of anything ]{.class-0}[he]{.class-1}[ taught me. That was because a few weeks before, I had stolen a ]{.class-0}[King James]{.class-1}[ Bible out of a flop house where some drunk was passed out, and ]{.class-0}[I had been reading it with the Holy Spirit dealing with me the whole time.]{.class-1}

[Brethren, there are only two people who could have taught me that that ]{.class-0}[King James]{.class-1}[ Bible was God's holy word in English. It was either the ]{.class-0}[Holy Spirit, ]{.class-1}[or it was an ]{.class-0}[unclean spirit:]{.class-1}[ either God taught me that, or ]{.class-0}[the Devil ]{.class-1}[taught me that.]{.class-0}

[I'll say this for old "Hotdog": he finally admitted I was demon-possessed, and that ]{.class-0}[the Devil taught it to me!]{.class-1}[ All the rest of the brethren were afraid to take that step because they were scared of a lot of Christians in their congregations ]{.class-0}[who believed the Book]{.class-1}[ (cf. Matt. 21:26--27). According to "Hotdog" Hymers, I am responsible for getting a bunch of people demon-possessed because I taught them to ]{.class-0}[believe the Bible]{.class-1}[ over a bunch of College-educated, Bible-correcting, ]{.class-0}[Nicolaitan jackrabbits.]{.class-1}

[Now if you believe the Book, whether you got the teaching from me or not, I know who taught you to believe it. It wasn't the Devil and it wasn't me. ]{.class-0}[It was the Holy Ghost.]{.class-1}

"[The Spirit itself beareth witness with our spirit, that we are the children of God"]{.class-2}[ (Rom. 8:6).]{.class-0}

"[Which things also we speak, not in words which man's wisdom teacheth, but which the Holy Ghost teacheth; comparing spiritual things with spiritual"]{.class-2}[ (1 Cor. 2:13).]{.class-0}

"[Howbeit when he, the Spirit of truth, is come, he will guide you into all truth"]{.class-2}[ (John 16:13).]{.class-0}

[Now, because the Levites ]{.class-0}["departed out of the way"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["caused many to stumble at the law"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["corrupted the covenant of Levi"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 8), God made them ]{.class-0}["contemptible and base before all the people"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 9). If God did that with His own chosen priesthood, don't you know what He's going to do with this bunch that's trying to take over their job? The Roman Catholic "fathers" turned out to be ]{.class-0}[pederast sex perverts.]{.class-1}[ I don't know how many ]{.class-0}[millions]{.class-1}[ of dollars the Roman Catholic Church in America had to pay out in order to keep their Priests from going to prison for child molestation, but it "liked to have gone broke" doing it. The "Church" had to have five Roman Catholic Supreme Court judges help "restock" its failing membership in the United States by making "decisions" allowing illegal immigrants into this country from Roman Catholic Mexico, but the fact still remains that in the eyes of most Americans, the Roman Catholic Church's name is ]{.class-0}[Mud.]{.class-1}

[The Fundamentalists and Conservatives shot themselves in the foot as well. The schools taught the preachers that no Bible was the final authority, and the preachers, in turn, talked their congregations out of faith in ]{.class-0}[the Book.]{.class-1}[ With no absolute truth on which to rely, the "Christians" turned to the ]{.class-0}[news media]{.class-1}[ who told them what to believe. Since the Christians themselves didn't believe the Book, there was no need for the rest of the country to have to put up with it, so ]{.class-0}[they threw it out of the public schools, the Federal buildings, and the military.]{.class-1}[ They replaced the Book with money, sex, and education (in that order); so now instead of ]{.class-0}[prayer]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[Bible reading]{.class-1}[ in the schools, you have promiscuity, sodomy, condoms, abortions, drugs, and ]{.class-0}[demoniac rock music,]{.class-1}[ along with a bunch of children and teenagers living like animals because they were taught ]{.class-0}[they came from animals]{.class-1}[ (]{.class-0}[evolution]{.class-1}[). If any Christian opposes such a mess, he is "backwards," "superstitious," a "demagogue," and "hate filled"; in other words, he's considered ]{.class-0}["contemptible and base before all people."]{.class-2}[ Why? Because the ]{.class-0}[Christians]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-0}[dumped the Book!]{.class-1}

10 Have we not all one father? hath not one God created us? why do we deal treacherously every man against his brother, by profaning the covenant of our fathers?

11 Judah hath dealt treacherously, and an abomination is committed in Israel and in Jerusalem; for Judah hath profaned the holiness of the LORD which he loved, and hath married the daughter of a strange god.

12 The LORD will cut off the man that doeth this, the master and the scholar, out of the tabernacles of Jacob, and him that offereth an offering unto the LORD of hosts.

"[Have we not all one father? hath not one God created us?"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 10). That's God ]{.class-0}[mocking]{.class-1}[ what the Jews were saying like He did back in 1:9. Jesus told the Pharisees, ]{.class-0}["Ye are of your father the devil, and the lusts of your father ye will do"]{.class-2}[ (John 8:44). He told them, ]{.class-0}["ye compass sea and land to make one proselyte, and when he is made, ye make him twofold more the child of hell than yourself"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 23:16).]{.class-0}

[The Bible knows nothing of "the Fatherhood of God and the brotherhood of Man"; that's a Liberal pipe dream. Everyone might be ]{.class-0}["the offspring of God"]{.class-2}[ by creation (Acts 17:28--29), but to become a ]{.class-0}[son]{.class-1}[ of God, you have ]{.class-0}["to repent"]{.class-2}[ (Acts 17:30) and ]{.class-0}[receive]{.class-1}[ Jesus Christ as your Saviour by faith (John 1:12). If you don't, the One who made you (John 1:1--3) will tell you: ]{.class-0}["Depart from me, ye cursed, into everlasting fire, prepared for the devil and his angels"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 25:41). You will end up where your ]{.class-0}[real]{.class-1}[ "daddy" ends up.]{.class-0}

"[Why do we deal treacherously every man against his brother, by profaning the covenant of our fathers?"]{.class-2}[ In other words, if God was their father, why would they lie about His word to their "brothers in Christ"?]{.class-0}

[Jesus told the Pharisees, ]{.class-0}["If ye were Abraham's children, ye would do the works of Abraham. But now ye seek to kill me, a man that hath told you the truth, which I have heard of God: this did not Abraham\....If God were your Father, ye would love me: for I proceeded forth and came from God; neither came I of myself, but he sent me. Why do ye not understand my speech? even because ye cannot hear my word\....He that is of God heareth God's words: ye therefore hear them not, because ye are not of God" ]{.class-2}[(John 8:39--40, 42--43, 47).]{.class-0}

[Verse 11 switches from the priests to the people as a whole. The priests ]{.class-0}["dealt treacherously"]{.class-2}[ and taught the people in Judah, Israel, and Jerusalem to do the same.]{.class-0}

[Now, what was the ]{.class-0}["abomination"]{.class-2}[ that ]{.class-0}["profaned the holiness of the LORD"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 11)? The people did what they did in Ezra 9:1--2. They ]{.class-0}["married the daughter of a strange god"]{.class-2}[---]{.class-0}[racial integration]{.class-1}[ that led to ]{.class-0}[spiritual integration.]{.class-1}[ The Jews were intermarrying with the heathen people of the land and ended up worshipping their false gods just like Solomon did (Neh. 13:23--26).]{.class-0}

[In the New Testament, the problem is ]{.class-0}[spiritual adultery.]{.class-1}[ James 4:4, ]{.class-0}["Ye adulterers and adulteresses, know ye not that the friendship of the world is enmity with God? whosoever therefore will be a friend of the world is the enemy of God."]{.class-2}

"[The world" ]{.class-2}[is ]{.class-0}["this present evil world"]{.class-2}[ from which Christ delivered you at salvation (Gal. 1:4). It is comprised of ]{.class-0}["the lust of the flesh, and the lust of the eyes, and the pride of life"]{.class-2}[ (1 John 2:16). You are commanded ]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ to conform to it (Rom. 12:2) and ]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ to love it (1 John 2:15), because if you do, you will forsake serving God (2 Tim. 4:10) and die spiritually as a Christian (Mark 4:19). You are warned that you can't love God and love this world at the same time (1 John 2:15). Why? Because you are committing ]{.class-0}[spiritual adultery: ]{.class-1}[you are "stepping out" on the Lord.]{.class-0}

[Notice how often in the Scriptures ]{.class-0}[physical adultery ]{.class-1}[is connected to ]{.class-0}[spiritual adultery.]{.class-1}[ Here is Christ talking to the Pharisees in Luke 16:14. Notice the ]{.class-0}["covetous"]{.class-2}[ in the verse: that's ]{.class-0}["the lust"]{.class-2}[ you read about in 1 John 2:16. It shows up in the context of James 4:4 (see James 4:1--3). Here in Luke 16, Jesus tells them, ]{.class-0}["Ye are they which justify yourselves before men; but God knoweth your hearts: for that which is highly esteemed among men is abomination in the sight of God"]{.class-2}[ (Luke 16:15). There's the ]{.class-0}[spiritual adultery]{.class-1}[, and look on down at verse 18 in the same chapter. There's the ]{.class-0}[physical adultery.]{.class-1}

"[Whosoever putteth away his wife, and marrieth another, committeth adultery: and whosoever marrieth her that is put away from her husband committeth adultery."]{.class-2}[ Paul told the Corinthians that they shouldn't join their bodies to a harlot (]{.class-0}[physical adultery]{.class-1}[) because they would be defiling the temple of the Holy Spirit (]{.class-0}[spiritual adultery]{.class-1}[) \[1 Cor. 6:15--20 cf. 2 Cor. 6:14--18\]. He said, ]{.class-0}["If any man defile the temple of God, him shall God destroy; for the temple of God is holy, which temple ye are"]{.class-2}[ (1 Cor. 3:17). Now you can't lose your salvation (Rom. 8:38--39; 1 Cor. 1:8; Eph. 4:30; Phil. 1:6); but you can lose your ]{.class-0}[joy]{.class-1}[ (Psa. 51:12), your ]{.class-0}[testimony]{.class-1}[ (2 Tim. 3:4), your ]{.class-0}[health]{.class-1}[ (1 Cor. 5:5), your ]{.class-0}[life]{.class-1}[ (1 Cor. 11:30), your ]{.class-0}[rewards]{.class-1}[ at the Judgment Seat of Christ (1 Cor. 3:14--15), and your Millennial ]{.class-0}[inheritance]{.class-1}[ (1 Cor. 6:9--11; Gal. 5:16--21).]{.class-0}

[That's a pretty big loss, wouldn't you say? You can't lose your ]{.class-0}[soul]{.class-1}[. You are going home to Heaven, but boy, you can make a mess along the way! You can either go to Heaven in the first-class section aboard the "QE II" (a cruise liner), or you can come floating in on a plank like the Apostle Paul at Malta (Acts 27:44), but you will still make it. The question is how do you ]{.class-0}[want]{.class-1}[ to make the trip.]{.class-0}

One time I saw a big, fat colored woman leading a little white boy down the street by the hand. She looked like Aunt Jemima, man! Her mistress must have told her to take that kid somewhere, so she was taking him. But that boy didn't want to go wherever he was going, so he was hollering and screaming and squalling. That colored woman didn't let it phase her a bit. She had a hold of that kid, and she was walking along singing "La de da de da!" That kid was going boink, boink, boink! hitting the ground about every four feet. He didn't want to go where he was going---wherever that was---but he was going anyway.

[I said to myself, "That's a picture of the Holy Spirit leading a ]{.class-0}[disobedient]{.class-1}[ Christian home to Heaven." If you are saved, the Holy Spirit will get you to Heaven. It may be a rocky trip for you if you want to live for the world and the flesh, but you're going whether you enjoy the ride or not.]{.class-0}

[Back to Malachi 2: ]{.class-0}["The LORD will cut off the man that doeth this, the master and the scholar, out of the tabernacles of Jacob"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 12). Notice how the word ]{.class-0}["scholar"]{.class-2}[ is used here. Today it is used of a highly educated man, but that is the exact ]{.class-0}[opposite]{.class-1}[ of its original meaning.]{.class-0}

[Remember this nursery rhyme: "A dillar, a dollar, a ten o'clock ]{.class-0}[scholar,]{.class-1}[ what makes you come so soon? You used to come at ten o'clock, but now you come at noon"? That's a little poem about a boy showing up late for school. The "scholar" in that poem is the ]{.class-0}[student,]{.class-1}[ not the ]{.class-0}[teacher]{.class-1}[. In Malachi 2:12, ]{.class-0}["the master"]{.class-2}[ is the teacher, which is why a post-graduate degree is called a ]{.class-0}[Master's]{.class-1}[ degree: you are supposed to have mastered your subject well enough to teach it. But today, the term ]{.class-0}[scholar]{.class-1}[ is applied to the teacher; it's not that way in an English dictionary.]{.class-0}

[I have never claimed to be a Bible scholar in that latter sense;]{.class-1}[ that is, I have never claimed to have ]{.class-0}[mastered]{.class-1}[ the Book. I have five earned degrees and have written over 120 books, but you have never seen or heard where I talked about ]{.class-0}[being a scholar ]{.class-1}[because I know what the educated crowd means when ]{.class-0}[they]{.class-1}[ use the term. They mean a "top dog" or a "big shot" scholastically when they talk about the "great scholarship" or the "profound scholarship" of Dr. So-and-so.]{.class-0}

[I wouldn't classify myself as that kind of a scholar; I would be taking a ]{.class-0}[step down.]{.class-1}[ I claim to be a ]{.class-0}[student]{.class-1}[ of the Bible (see 2 Tim. 2:15); I'm still learning many things from the Book. This bunch of stuffed-shirts make you think that just because they've been through four years of undergraduate work, three years of post-graduate work, can read and write Hebrew and Greek, and have a Ph.D., D.D., or Th.D. after their name, that that qualifies them to ]{.class-0}[sit in judgment of what God said.]{.class-1}[ I'm not that smart. I'll take ]{.class-0}[my place]{.class-1}[ with the old Southern grandma who believed the Book and had no education past the]{.class-0}[ sixth grade.]{.class-1}

[Now, what God is telling those Jews in verse 12 is that it didn't matter if they were a teacher or a student---a big shot or a little shot---if they were profaning God's name (1:12), profaning God's covenant (2:10), or profaning God's holiness (2:11), the Lord was going to ]{.class-0}[kill them.]{.class-1}[ That went for the common man (vs. 11) as well as the priest (]{.class-0}["him that offereth an offering unto the LORD of hosts,"]{.class-2}[ vs. 12).]{.class-0}

[Over in Ezekiel 9, the Lord had all the people in Jerusalem marked ]{.class-0}["that cry for all the abominations that be done in the midst thereof"]{.class-2}[ (Ezek. 9:4). Then six men went throughout the city killing everyone that didn't have that mark on their foreheads. The Lord tells those six fellows, ]{.class-0}["slay utterly old and young, both maids, and little children, and women\...AND BEGIN AT MY SANCTUARY" ]{.class-2}[(Ezek. 9:6). The priests serving in the Temple were the first to get it.]{.class-0}

[The New Testament application is this: ]{.class-0}["For the time is come that judgment must begin at the house of God: and if it first begin at us, what shall the end be of them that obey not the gospel of God?"]{.class-2}[ (1 Pet. 4:17). When God gets ready to judge this nation, He's going to start with the ]{.class-0}[preachers]{.class-1}[ and work His way out.]{.class-0}

13 And this have ye done again, covering the altar of the LORD with tears, with weeping, and with crying out, insomuch that he regardeth not the offering any more, or receiveth it with good will at your hand.

14 Yet ye say, Wherefore? Because the LORD hath been witness between thee and the wife of thy youth, against whom thou hast dealt treacherously: yet is she thy companion, and the wife of thy covenant.

15 And did not he make one? Yet had he the residue of the spirit. And wherefore one? That he might seek a godly seed. Therefore take heed to your spirit, and let none deal treacherously against the wife of his youth.

16 For the LORD, the God of Israel, saith that he hateth putting away: for one covereth violence with his garment, saith the LORD of hosts: therefore take heed to your spirit, that ye deal not treacherously.

[Verse 13 is those Jews weeping and crying in repentance, ]{.class-0}[and God's not paying any more attention to them than He did to Esau]{.class-1}[ (Heb. 12:16--17). Pharaoh repented (Exod. 10:16--17); Balaam repented (Num. 22:34); Saul repented (1 Sam. 15:24); Judas repented (Matt. 27:3). They all ]{.class-0}[repented]{.class-1}[, and it didn't do any of them any good. There is a difference between ]{.class-0}[sacramental repentance]{.class-1}[ for what you've ]{.class-0}[done]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[evangelical repentance]{.class-1}[ for what you ]{.class-0}[are.]{.class-1}[ For a thorough discussion of the two, see the comments on 2 Corinthians 7:10; Matthew 27: 3--4; and Exodus 9:27, 10:16--17 in their respective Commentaries.]{.class-0}

[What was happening was that the priests and the people were not only marrying heathen women and worshipping their gods as they did in Ezra 9 and Nehemiah 13, this time they were divorcing their Jewish wives to do it (vs. 14 cf. vs. 16). All of this gets into questions of marriage, divorce, and remarriage: What constitutes a marriage? What constitutes a divorce? When is divorce allowed? When is it not? Is it a sin to remarry after you've been divorced? What I'm about to give you I have taught since ]{.class-0}[1950;]{.class-1}[ I haven't changed any through the years to make the Scriptures match my circumstances.]{.class-0}

[First of all, you have to distinguish the difference between the ]{.class-0}[essence]{.class-1}[ of a marriage and an ]{.class-0}[ideal]{.class-1}[ marriage in the sight of God. An ]{.class-0}[ideal]{.class-1}[ marriage consists of two ]{.class-0}[saved]{.class-1}[ people. When Jesus said, ]{.class-0}["What therefore God hath joined together, let not man put asunder"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 19:6), He was referring to Adam and Eve in the Garden of Eden ]{.class-0}[before the Fall]{.class-1}[ (Matt. 19:4--5 cf. Gen. 2:22--24). He was talking to Jews under the Mosaic covenant, not Gentiles who were ]{.class-0}["strangers from the covenants of promise"]{.class-2}[ (Eph. 2:12). It is a picture of Christ's union with the Church (Eph. 5:30--32). Technically speaking, marriage between two unsaved people is nothing but a couple of animals mating. They are ]{.class-0}["dead in trespasses and sins"]{.class-2}[ (Eph. 2:1), and the Lord doesn't even know them (Matt. 7:23), so He's not the one joining them together.]{.class-0}

[An ideal marriage also consists of a ]{.class-0}["covenant"]{.class-2}[ between two saved people (vs. 14). So at a wedding, the preacher asks the man and woman, "Do you PROMISE to love, honor, obey, etc.?" Those are marriage ]{.class-0}[vows]{.class-1}[. That's a covenant made between the two parties, and it's sealed with a symbol of commitment. That's a ring nowadays. In the Middle Ages, the couple would wear robes with long, loose sleeves, and to seal their vows, the ends of the sleeves would be tied together. That's where we get the expression "tying the knot" to describe marriage.]{.class-0}

[Now that's an ]{.class-0}[ideal]{.class-1}[ marriage. It's not only a private consummation; it's a public commitment. It manifests itself different ways in different cultures. Back in Genesis, the father of the bride would hold a big party, and afterwards bring his daughter to the bridegroom's tent (Gen. 29:22--23). There's nothing wrong with any of that, but that's not the ]{.class-0}[essence]{.class-1}[ of a marriage. In the Bible, a marriage is flesh joining flesh.]{.class-0}

[Genesis 2:24---]{.class-0}["Therefore shall a man leave his father and his mother, and shall cleave unto his wife: and they shall be one flesh."]{.class-2}

[Jesus confirmed that in Matthew 19:5, and Paul confirmed it again in Ephesians 5:31. That's what the Lord tells Malachi right in the context of the passage: ]{.class-0}["And did not he make one?"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 15).]{.class-0}

[Now we'll discuss divorce when we hit verse 16. Right now it is important for you to get that marriage is ]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ just a ceremony. Marriage is a physical affair where ]{.class-0}[flesh joins flesh.]{.class-1}[ That thing is so strong that Paul says if you shack up with a whore, you've married her. Don't believe me? Look at 1 Corinthians 6:16.]{.class-0}

"[What? Know ye not that he which is joined to an harlot is one body? for two, saith he, shall be one flesh."]{.class-2}

[Why, that's exactly what he said about ]{.class-0}[the husband and wife]{.class-1}[ in Ephesians 5:31. So what actually makes the marriage a ]{.class-0}[marriage]{.class-1}[ is the physical act of coming together as man and wife. When I "marry" a couple, I'm not the one joining them together; for all I know, maybe they're ]{.class-0}[already joined]{.class-1}[. I dare say there is many a preacher in America who only has one ring, one ceremony, and one marriage certificate, but who has ]{.class-0}[more than one wife]{.class-1}[ if you go by the Biblical definition of marriage. But more on that when we get to verse 16.]{.class-0}

[Back to verse 15: ]{.class-0}["Yet had he the residue of the spirit. And wherefore one? That he might seek a godly seed. Therefore take heed to your spirit, and let none deal treacherously against the wife of his youth."]{.class-2}[ Now there are all kinds of things in the verse, and none of the 22 commentaries I've read have got it figured out. Twenty of them duck the verse, and two of them don't even make an attempt to expound it. I have never found an actual explanation for it, but I will give you what I can. I know one thing: the verse has something to do with the ]{.class-0}[right seed.]{.class-1}

[First off, there are two spirits in the passage. There is ]{.class-0}["the spirit" ]{.class-2}[and]{.class-0}[ "your spirit,"]{.class-2}[ referring to the personal spirit of the Jews to whom he's writing. The first ]{.class-0}["spirit"]{.class-2}[ is the Holy Spirit, even though it is not capitalized. There are two reasons for that. First, in the Old Testament, the Holy Spirit wasn't revealed as a ]{.class-0}[person,]{.class-1}[ but just as an ]{.class-0}[influence]{.class-1}[ of God. So in Genesis 6:3 and Psalm 51:11, the ]{.class-0}[King James]{.class-1}[ doesn't capitalize ]{.class-0}["spirit,"]{.class-2}[ even though there is no doubt whatsoever that the reference is to the Third Person of the Trinity.]{.class-0}

[The second reason is because the ]{.class-0}[work]{.class-1}[ of the Holy Spirit is what is under discussion and not His ]{.class-0}[personality.]{.class-1}[ That's why Romans 8:16 says, ]{.class-0}["The Spirit ITSELF beareth witness with our spirit, that we are the children of God."]{.class-2}[ That's not a mistake in the ]{.class-0}[King James]{.class-1}[; that's a correct translation of the Greek word ]{.class-0}[pneuma]{.class-1}[ based on the fact that the verse is talking about the Spirit's ]{.class-0}[work]{.class-1}[ (for a complete explanation, see the comments on the verse in the ]{.class-0}[Bible Believer's Commentary on Romans]{.class-1}[).]{.class-0}

[The ]{.class-0}["he"]{.class-2}[ of Malachi 2:15 is ]{.class-0}["the LORD"]{.class-2}[ who was the ]{.class-0}["witness" ]{.class-2}[between that Jewish man and that Jewish woman (vs. 14) and made them ]{.class-0}["one"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 15). ]{.class-0}["The residue"]{.class-2}[ is something leftover. The Spirit of the Lord is choosing a ]{.class-0}[remnant]{.class-1}[ of the people who haven't intermarried with the heathen women. Why is He doing that? ]{.class-0}["That he might seek a godly seed."]{.class-2}

[That's where Scofield got his ]{.class-0}["godly line of Seth" ]{.class-1}[in Genesis 6:4 (see the ]{.class-0}[Scofield]{.class-1}[ note on Genesis 6:2). Of course, to get that interpretation, he had to ]{.class-0}[invent]{.class-1}[ sexless angels (see the comments on Matt. 22:30 in that Commentary), even though there is no such thing in the entire Bible (see the remarks under Zech. 5:9). Outside of Adam (Luke 3:38), there were no individual ]{.class-0}[human]{.class-1}[ sons of God in the Old Testament. Israel was a ]{.class-0}[national, corporate]{.class-1}[ son of God (Exod. 4:22), but the only other ]{.class-0}["sons of God"]{.class-2}[ you will find in the Old Testament were the ones that were around before Adam or any "godly line of Seth" showed up (Job 38:4--7).]{.class-0}

[Besides that, there was no "godly line of Seth" or ]{.class-0}[anyone else ]{.class-1}[in the Old Testament. You say, "It was the line of Christ." That's a ]{.class-0}[Messianic]{.class-1}[ line, but it's no "godly line." Have you ever read Matthew 1 and checked out how "godly" Christ's line was? One of His ancestors was a professional ]{.class-0}[whore]{.class-1}[ (Matt. 1:5), one played the part of a whore to commit ]{.class-0}[incest]{.class-1}[ with her father-in-law (Matt. 1:3), there was an ]{.class-0}[adulterer]{.class-1}[ and an ]{.class-0}[adulteress]{.class-1}[ (Matt. 1:6), there was a ]{.class-0}[murderer]{.class-1}[ (Matt. 1:6) and an ]{.class-0}[idolater]{.class-1}[ (Matt. 1:7), and one of His line was so "godly" that the Lord had to cut off the man's descendants from ever being King (Matt. 1:11). ]{.class-0}[What do you mean a "godly line"?]{.class-1}

[The ]{.class-0}["seed"]{.class-2}[ comes from the man, and that being the case, the ]{.class-0}["godly seed"]{.class-2}[ wouldn't have anything to do with Jesus Christ at all. Christ didn't come from the seed of a man; He came from the seed of a ]{.class-0}[woman]{.class-1}[ (Gen. 3:15) who was never ]{.class-0}["one"]{.class-2}[ with any man (Isa. 7:14; Matt. 1:18--20).]{.class-0}

[All through the Old Testament, the Devil is trying to mess up ]{.class-0}[the seed]{.class-1}[ so that Genesis 3:15 will never take place. When Eve had her first boy (there's Adam's seed), he turned out to be ]{.class-0}["of that wicked one, and slew his brother"]{.class-2}[ (1 John 3:12)---]{.class-0}[wrong seed.]{.class-1}[ Noah curses the seed of his son Ham (Canaan) because Ham committed sodomy with his daddy; that was no ]{.class-0}["godly seed."]{.class-2}[ Sarah gets Abraham to bear a child for her by her Egyptian maid Hagar, and that's where you get Ishmael, the Arabs, and Mohammed (Mohammed claimed to be the seventieth from ]{.class-0}[Ishmael]{.class-1}[)---WRONG SEED! Isaac bears Esau and Jacob; Esau sells his birthright, threatens to kill his brother, and marries a couple of Hamites---]{.class-0}[wrong seed.]{.class-1}

[Sex]{.class-1}[ keeps rearing its ugly head all through that Bible from cover to cover. Jacob has twelve sons by four wives, and his oldest (Reuben) shacks up with one of his ]{.class-0}[daddy's wives]{.class-1}[ and loses the birthright. What's going on? The Devil is attacking ]{.class-0}[the seed. ]{.class-1}[He tries to get Joseph messed up with Potiphar's wife. He moves Pharaoh to kill all the baby boys of the Hebrews.]{.class-0}

[Now if the ]{.class-0}["godly seed"]{.class-2}[ has no reference to the line of Christ, what is it? Go back and pick up Nehemiah 9:2.]{.class-0}

"[For they have taken of their daughters for themselves, and for their sons: so that the HOLY SEED have mingled themselves with the people of those lands: yea, the hand of the princes and rulers hath been chief in this trespass."]{.class-2}

[The ]{.class-0}["holy seed"]{.class-2}[ is ]{.class-0}[the nation of Israel.]{.class-1}[ They are not ]{.class-0}["holy"]{.class-2}[ in the sense of being righteous, because they ]{.class-0}[weren't ]{.class-1}[(Ezek. 16:1--6). They were ]{.class-0}["holy"]{.class-2}[ because God ]{.class-0}[chose them]{.class-1}[ and made a covenant with them to be ]{.class-0}[His people]{.class-1}[ above all the other nations and peoples of the earth (Ezek. 16:8 cf. Exod. 19:5--6).]{.class-0}

[When the Lord says He's seeking ]{.class-0}["a godly seed,"]{.class-2}[ the Holy Spirit is going through ]{.class-0}[that nation ]{.class-1}[looking for a remnant (]{.class-0}["the residue"]{.class-2}[) that haven't corrupted themselves by integrating with the Hamite nations around them and worshipping their false gods. What was involved in the idol worship of the heathen? It was ]{.class-0}[fornication]{.class-1}[ (Num. 25:1--3; Rev. 2:14). That's why those Jews were to watch their own ]{.class-0}["spirit"]{.class-2}[ and not ]{.class-0}["deal treacherously"]{.class-2}[ with their wives by running off to fornicate with heathen idolaters.]{.class-0}

[The prophetic application is to the Jewish remnant in the Tribulation who don't bow down and kiss the image of the Antichrist (Rom. 11:4; 1 Kings 19:18 cf. Rev. 14:1--4, 15:1--3). The Bible says of that remnant: ]{.class-0}["Except the Lord of Sabaoth had left us a SEED, we had been as Sodom, and been made like unto Gomorrah"]{.class-2}[ (Rom. 9:29). What was the sin of Sodom and Gomorrah? It was ]{.class-0}[sex perversion]{.class-1}[ (Jude 7). In the Tribulation, Jerusalem is likened to Sodom (Rev. 11:8) ]{.class-0}[because there's a queer in charge of it ]{.class-1}[(Dan. 11:37). The ]{.class-0}["godly seed"]{.class-2}[ are the Jews who won't go along with him or his agenda.]{.class-0}

"[For the LORD, the God of Israel, saith that he hateth putting away"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 16). Now we come to the verse where the self-righteous Pharisaism of the Fundamentalist brethren shines through. They believe that since God hates divorce (]{.class-0}["putting away"]{.class-2}[) that He would never make ]{.class-0}[any]{.class-1}[ provision for it or have mercy on some brother (or sister) less fortunate in his martial relationship than they are. The Pharisees have their "standards," and the violation of ]{.class-0}[those]{.class-1}[ standards is almost an unforgiveable sin.]{.class-0}

[Now let me say from the outset that I have never recommended divorce to ]{.class-0}[anyone.]{.class-1}[ I don't believe in divorce. I think a couple should forgive and forget, and compromise to get along and live together. I thank God for every Christian man and wife who have managed to keep the marriage ties together and who have been faithful to their spouses after going through only one marriage ceremony. More power to them.]{.class-0}

[Moreover, I have never "put a woman away" for any reason.]{.class-1}[ Some of you don't believe that, but it's true. You say, "You've been divorced twice." That's true, but I didn't dump either woman. ]{.class-0}[They dumped me.]{.class-1}[ My first wife deserted me, and under Alabama law at that time (where we lived), when a spouse was gone for a year, the court ]{.class-0}[automatically]{.class-1}[ granted a divorce to the deserted party (myself). When my second wife decided she didn't want to be married to me anymore, a lawyer told me that there was no way I could fight the divorce because Florida was a "no fault" State. In other words, if one party wanted to get out of the marriage, there was nothing ]{.class-0}[the other party]{.class-1}[ could do to stop it, because the party suing for divorce didn't have to have any ]{.class-0}[reason]{.class-1}[ for it at all. Some of the brethren, evidently, are not too well informed.]{.class-0}

[Now here is what the New Testament says about divorce and remarriage, and when the New Testament speaks about divorce, ]{.class-0}[remarriage is always in the context.]{.class-1}[ As I said earlier under my comments on verse 14, what I am about to give you, I have taught since ]{.class-0}[1950]{.class-1}[. I taught this years before my first wife left me. I lived as a single man for ]{.class-0}[twelve years]{.class-1}[ before I remarried (the brethren won't tell you that), and when I did, all the youth directors and camp directors of the Baptist Bible Fellowship in Indiana had a meeting to decide whether or not to have me back in to preach to their young people. I had been preaching at their summer camps and youth rallies for a couple of years. The questions was: "Since Bro. Ruckman has been remarried, how do you feel about having him back?"]{.class-0}

[Well, they took the vote, and I think it was something like 30% for and 70% against. After that vote, the moderator stood up and said, "I'm going to honor and respect the majority; we'll not have Brother Ruckman back. However, I want to have you fellows know that I think some of you are a bunch of hypocrites. Some of you have fellows in your pulpits and you ]{.class-0}[trade pulpits]{.class-1}[ with preachers who are in the same situation, and I never ]{.class-0}[heard you open your mouths about it."]{.class-1}

[You see, the problem the brethren have with me is not my "marital status." That's an ]{.class-0}[excuse.]{.class-1}[ The problem the brethren have with me is ]{.class-0}[that Book. ]{.class-1}[They will tolerate in other preachers what they won't tolerate in me (e.g., C. I. Scofield who had a Roman Catholic wife run out on him. Before he remarried, a hundred well-known preachers from around the nation said he had Biblical grounds to remarry and remain in the ministry) because of the stand I take on that Book.]{.class-0}

[That moderator went on to say: "The worst thing is this: you had Bro. Ruckman's book (the ]{.class-0}[Bible Believer's Commentary on Matthew]{.class-1}[) that told you what he believed about marriage, divorce, and remarriage. You read it, and you had him come in to preach to our kids for two years before he got remarried ]{.class-0}[knowing]{.class-1}[ what he taught, believed, and preached. He hasn't changed; ]{.class-0}[you fellows have."]{.class-1}

[So here is what I have taught for over sixty years about what the Bible says about divorce and remarriage. Since I have already given a thorough discussion of the matter in the booklet ]{.class-0}[Marriage, Divorce, and Remarriage]{.class-1}[ (1980) and the ]{.class-0}[Bible Believer's Commentaries on Matthew]{.class-1}[ (chap. 19) and ]{.class-0}[1 & 2 Corinthians]{.class-1}[ (1 Cor. 7), I will only give a brief synopsis here. You can get those works for more study.]{.class-0}

[A divorce is the dissolving of the marriage bonds (1 Cor. 7:27). The marriage bond is broken when a spouse ]{.class-0}[dies,]{.class-1}[ and the surviving spouse is allowed to remarry (Rom. 7:3). ]{.class-0}[Fornication]{.class-1}[ is grounds for divorce, and the offended party is allowed to ]{.class-0}[remarry]{.class-1}[ (Matt. 19:9). Why? Because the marriage bond was broken when the sinning party left to join his or her body to someone else (1 Cor. 6:15--16). A ]{.class-0}[legal]{.class-1}[ divorce is allowed because an ]{.class-0}[actual]{.class-1}[ divorce has already occurred. These dumb preachers think a divorce is a ]{.class-0}[piece of paper]{.class-1}[ you get from a judge; ]{.class-0}[it's not. ]{.class-1}[A divorce occurs when the marriage bond is broken. You might be divorced four or five times (or more) and still be living with the one with whom you had your ]{.class-0}[one ceremony]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[marriage certificate.]{.class-1}

[Finally, a divorce is allowed in the case of ]{.class-0}[desertion.]{.class-1}[ Paul said, ]{.class-0}["But if the unbelieving depart, let him depart ]{.class-2}[\[John Wesley had a "helpmeet" who used to drag him around the house by his hair and stand up in the middle of his sermon and say, "You hypocrite, I saw you drunk last night." When she left him, Wesley said, "I didn't send her away, and I don't recall her\]]{.class-0}[. A brother or sister is not under bondage in such cases"]{.class-2}[ (1 Cor. 7:15). And while no allowance is made for a believing spouse deserting (because Paul takes it for granted that a believer is not going to act like an ]{.class-0}[unbeliever]{.class-1}[ in such a case), the reality is that ]{.class-0}[they do]{.class-1}[, and when that happens, Paul says the deserted party is ]{.class-0}[not sinning]{.class-1}[ if he or she ]{.class-0}[remarries]{.class-1}[.]{.class-0}

"[Art thou loosed from a wife ]{.class-2}[\[divorced\]]{.class-0}[? seek not a wife. But and if thou marry, THOU HAST NOT SINNED" ]{.class-2}[(1 Cor. 7:27--28).]{.class-0}

[Is that clear? If your spouse runs out on you and won't come back, ]{.class-0}[you are free to remarry without it being a sin.]{.class-1}

[Now what about this matter about ]{.class-0}["a bishop"]{.class-2}[ being ]{.class-0}["the husband of one wife"]{.class-2}[ (1 Tim. 3:2)? My Fundamentalist preacher brethren will say, "Well, that means a preacher (or a pastor) can't be ]{.class-0}[divorced]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[remarry]{.class-1}[ because then he will have more than one living wife."]{.class-0}

[When I was pastoring the Brent Baptist Church after my first spouse deserted me, I got a letter from Bob Jones Jr. telling me that I didn't qualify to be a pastor because I was ]{.class-0}[single]{.class-1}[ at the time.]{.class-0}

[(He and Arlin Horton were good buddies at the time, before Horton started his College and gave BJU some competition. Bob Jones Jr. was "going to bats" for his buddy because Horton wanted the property at Brent Baptist for his school. As long as I pastored the church, we paid off all our debts---including a big church bond program where the former pastor \[a Tennessee Temple graduate\] had absconded with thousands of dollars of bearer bonds. If I were to leave, the church wouldn't be able to pay its bills, and Horton could take the property, which is what ended up happening when I finally ]{.class-0}[did resign]{.class-1}[ as pastor for other reasons.)]{.class-0}

[I needed to resign as pastor because I wasn't ]{.class-0}["the husband of one wife."]{.class-2}[ I wrote him back and said, "Okay, I'll get ]{.class-0}[married]{.class-1}[ and have a wife; then I'll qualify." He wrote me back and said, "Oh no, then you'd have ]{.class-0}[two wives."]{.class-1}[ I told him, ]{.class-0}["You're the only College president I ever saw who thinks zero and one equals two."]{.class-1}

[That's ]{.class-0}[exactly]{.class-1}[ how these Pharisees think. There was a case one time where a young man married when he was sixteen and got divorced when he was eighteen. At 27, he was convicted of murder and sentenced to prison. Well, just before his conviction, he got saved. While in prison, he got in the Book and got to preaching and witnessing to his fellow inmates. He led some of them to Christ and was having regular Bible studies with them teaching them the Book. He hadn't been in for two years when some prison evangelist came in there from a well-known, international, Fundamental prison ministry and told that young Christian ]{.class-0}[that he wasn't qualified to teach and preach to those prisoners]{.class-1}[ because he had been divorced. Mind you, the fact that he was a ]{.class-0}[murderer]{.class-1}[ was no problem at all, just that horrible ]{.class-0}[divorce!]{.class-1}[ That's your "well-meaning" brethren. They'll stop people from getting saved and studying the Book just to enforce their Pharisaical standards.]{.class-0}

[That happened to me one time. I was at a meeting in the Carolinas somewhere, and the pastor of the church where I was holding the meeting got me into a juvenile prison. Well, a bunch of those teenage boys got saved and got excited about the Book and wanted to have me back in for a whole week, sort of a prison revival. At the prison, there was one of the workers who attended a big Fundamental church in town pastored by a bigwig in the Southwide Baptist Fellowship---Clarence Sexton. That prison worker told his pastor what was going on, and the pastor said, ]{.class-0}["We can't have that;]{.class-1}[ Ruckman's been divorced." That pastor went to the chaplain of that facility and got that revival shut down before it even got started.]{.class-0}

[You say, "What did that pastor do for those teenage boys in that prison?" ]{.class-0}[Nothing]{.class-1}[. Things went right back to the way they were. That Fundamental Baptist pastor booted a bunch of those teenagers straight into Hell just as sure as you're reading this page, all because he didn't want a divorced man preaching to them. ]{.class-0}[That's the "brethren."]{.class-1}

[Now when Paul said ]{.class-0}["a bishop"]{.class-2}[ is to be ]{.class-0}["the husband of one wife,"]{.class-2}[ what he ]{.class-0}[didn't]{.class-1}[ say was ]{.class-0}["only married once."]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-16}[The Pharisaical position of the Fundamentalist is that a man qualifies for the ministry no matter how many women he may have fornicated with before his wedding ceremony or how many women he committed adultery with after his wedding ceremony as long as he doesn't get ]{.class-0}[divorce papers]{.class-1}[ and a ]{.class-0}[second marriage certificate. ]{.class-1}[You see the hypocrisy involved.]{.class-0}

[Moreover, Paul couldn't have possibly meant "only married once" because that would disqualify ]{.class-0}[thousands of ministers serving as pastors]{.class-1}[. After my second marriage, I got into a discussion with a Tennessee Temple graduate connected to a Fundamental work here in Pensacola. The fellow told me, "You're not qualified to be a pastor because you're the husband of more than ]{.class-0}[one wife."]{.class-1}

"[Well, what about it?" I asked.]{.class-0}

He said, "The Bible forbids it."

[I said, ]{.class-0}["No, it doesn't."]{.class-1}

"[Well, it says ]{.class-0}['the husband of one wife']{.class-2}[; that ]{.class-0}[means]{.class-1}[ 'only married once,'" he replied.]{.class-0}

"[It doesn't say 'only married once,'" I said, "It says ]{.class-0}['the husband of one wife.']{.class-2}[ You're to be the husband of the wife you've got ]{.class-0}[right now]{.class-1}[. That's to prevent ]{.class-0}[polygamy."]{.class-1}

[He said, "No, it ]{.class-0}[means]{.class-1}[ if you've been married more than once, you don't qualify for the ministry."]{.class-0}

I asked him, "Do you really believe that?"

He said, "Yes, I do."

[I said, ]{.class-0}["No you don't."]{.class-1}

"[Are you calling me a liar?" he asked indignantly."]{.class-0}

"[Yes, as a matter of fact, I am,"]{.class-1}[ I said, "and I'll prove it to you in about ]{.class-0}[five seconds]{.class-1}[ if you're man enough to take it."]{.class-0}

"[All right, what is it?" he asked.]{.class-0}

"[Do you honestly believe that the verse says a man cannot be a minister ]{.class-0}[if he has been married more than once?]{.class-1}[ Yes or no?"]{.class-0}

"[No, he can't," he replied.]{.class-0}

"[You're a liar,"]{.class-1}[ I said. ]{.class-0}["Bob Jones Sr.]{.class-1}[ had two wives." And he did. ]{.class-0}[William Carey]{.class-1}[ had three wives, as did ]{.class-0}[Adoniram Judson. DeWitt Talmage]{.class-1}[ had more than one wife.]{.class-0}

"[Oh, yeah," he said, "but their first wives all ]{.class-0}[died."]{.class-1}

[I told that bird, "Don't back out now. ]{.class-0}[You told me that if a preacher's been married more than once he didn't qualify."]{.class-1}

"[But, but, but\...," he stuttered.]{.class-0}

[I said, ]{.class-0}["But nothing, buddy.]{.class-1}[ You don't believe that."]{.class-0}

[You see, these ]{.class-0}[hypocrites]{.class-1}[ make an exception for death (Rom. 7). Well, if you make an ]{.class-0}[exception]{.class-1}[ for one thing for which the Bible makes an exception, ]{.class-0}[you have to make an exception for the other two as well.]{.class-1}

[What's the conclusion of this whole thing? It's this: if you're married, ]{.class-0}[stay married]{.class-1}[. If you have Scriptural grounds for divorce, my advice is ]{.class-0}[don't take them ]{.class-1}[if you don't have to; stay together and try to work things out. If a divorce is unavoidable in such circumstances, you are no longer married, legally or morally, to the other person; ]{.class-0}[you are free to remarry. ]{.class-1}[If you can stay single, ]{.class-0}[stay single;]{.class-1}[ but if you can't, you haven't committed adultery if you remarry, and you only have one spouse when you do. You are to remain faithful to that spouse, and if you are a man God has called to the ministry, answer the call and don't worry about what any of the brethren think. ]{.class-0}[To your own Master you'll stand or fall ]{.class-1}[(Rom. 14:4).]{.class-0}

17 Ye have wearied the LORD with your words. Yet ye say, Wherein have we wearied him? When ye say, Every one that doeth evil is good in the sight of the LORD, and he delighteth in them; or, Where is the God of judgment?

[The Scriptural commentary on the verse can be found in Isaiah 5:20, 1:16--17, which see.]{.class-0}[ "Every one that doeth evil is good\...Where is the God of judgment?" ]{.class-2}[That's the present philosophy of the United States. If you're a left-wing, Socialistic Communist (like Obama, Hillary, and the Democratic Party), then you love the down-trodden masses, but if you believe in a capitalistic, Constitutional republic based on the freedoms, responsibilities, and Biblical principles on which this country was founded and which made it great, you are a ]{.class-0}[demagogue]{.class-1}[ trying to establish a theocratic Church-State, and take away everyone's "rights." You give special privileges and rights that nobody else has to a bunch of ]{.class-0}[fornicating, disease-ridden sex perverts trying to seduce your children;]{.class-1}[ and if you don't promote their abominable "lifestyle," you are filled with "hate." You kick the Bible and prayers to God and Jesus Christ out of the public schools and teach the kiddies how to dress like Moslems, recite the Shahada, memorize verses out of the ]{.class-0}[Koran,]{.class-1}[ and set aside special rooms and times for the Moslems to pray; you give the ]{.class-0}[Koran ]{.class-1}[special treatment in military prisons while your military leaders ]{.class-0}[burn Bibles.]{.class-1}[ ]{.class-0}["Every one that doeth evil is good." ]{.class-2}

[Now when those Jews asked ]{.class-0}["Where is the God of judgment,"]{.class-2}[ they weren't trying to appeal to God's sense of justice like Abraham did when he asked the Lord ]{.class-0}["Shall not the Judge of all the earth do right?"]{.class-2}[ (Gen. 18:25). The question is asked defiantly like: "What's God going to do to me for my treachery and violence? He's not going to judge me. I mean, ]{.class-0}['God is love,']{.class-2}[ man; don't you know that?" That's the attitude of the question.]{.class-0}

[Americans nowadays---]{.class-0}[saved or lost]{.class-1}[---don't believe God's going to judge "nobody for nothin'." ]{.class-0}[Boy, do they have a shock coming]{.class-1}[ (see Psa. 9:17; Jer. 30:11; Isa. 34:2)!!]{.class-0}

[]{#section-0_1_932c541d4f4ad6e4_b69e.xhtml}

CHAPTER THREE

3:1 Behold, I will send my messenger, and he shall prepare the way before me: and the Lord, whom ye seek, shall suddenly come to his temple, even the messenger of the covenant, whom ye delight in: behold, he shall come, saith the LORD of hosts.

[Verse 1 is one of those passages that must be read carefully, which is why we have chosen to set it apart by itself for comment. The speaker is ]{.class-0}["the LORD," ]{.class-2}[so the ]{.class-0}["my"]{.class-2}[ and the ]{.class-0}["me"]{.class-2}[ are referring to Jehovah. But notice that there are two "Lords" in the verse. There is ]{.class-0}["LORD"]{.class-2}[ and ]{.class-0}["Lord,"]{.class-2}[ so the Jehovah's Witnesses try to use the passage to prove that Jesus Christ is a lesser deity (see the comments on Zech. 12:10).]{.class-0}

[Now the verse contains both Advents of Christ (see the comments on Zech. 9:9--11). When Mark quotes the verse in Mark 1:2, he divides it as neatly as Jesus did when He read Isaiah 61:1--2 (see Luke 4:17--21). Note the two "messengers" in the passage. The first ]{.class-0}["messenger" ]{.class-2}[is John the Baptist at the First Advent (Mark 1:1--4), and the second ]{.class-0}["messenger"]{.class-2}[ is Jesus Christ at the Second Advent (cf. Hag. 2:9). In regard to these two messengers, there are two interesting occurrences we have already come across in the studies on this second half of the Minor Prophets. Look at Malachi 2:7 and Haggai 1:13.]{.class-0}

"[For the priest's lips should keep knowledge, and they should seek the law at his mouth: for he is the MESSENGER of the LORD of hosts"]{.class-2}[ (Mal. 2:7).]{.class-0}

[There's a Levite who ]{.class-0}["is the messenger of the LORD of hosts,"]{.class-2}[ just like the ]{.class-0}["my messenger"]{.class-2}[ of Malachi 3:1. But ]{.class-0}["the messenger of the LORD"]{.class-2}[ in Malachi 3:1 was a reference to John the Baptist. Do you know what John the Baptist was? He was the son of a priest from the tribe of Levi (Luke 1).]{.class-0}

"[Then spake Haggai the LORD'S MESSENGER in the LORD'S message unto the people, saying, I am with you, saith the LORD"]{.class-2}[ (Hag. 1:13).]{.class-0}

[There Haggai is called ]{.class-0}["the LORD'S messenger,"]{.class-2}[ but look at what shows up in the context: ]{.class-0}["I am with you, saith the LORD."]{.class-2}[ That's ]{.class-0}["Emmanuel," "God with us"]{.class-2}[ (Matt. 1:23); it's a reference to the Lord Jesus Christ. Remarkable "coinky-dinky," wouldn't you say?]{.class-0}

[Now back to Malachi 3:1---]{.class-0}["I will send MY messenger]{.class-2}[ \[that is, the ]{.class-0}["messenger"]{.class-2}[ of ]{.class-0}["the LORD"]{.class-2}[---Jehovah\]]{.class-0}[, and he shall prepare the way before ME ]{.class-2}[\[]{.class-0}["the LORD"]{.class-2}[---Jehovah\]]{.class-0}[."]{.class-2}[ So when John the Baptist showed up, he was to be the forerunner of Jehovah. But look at how Mark changes the wording when he quotes the verse.]{.class-0}

"[As it is written in the prophets, Behold, I will send my messenger]{.class-2}[ \[the messenger of Jehovah in Mal. 3:1\]]{.class-0}[ before THY face, which shall prepare thy way before THEE"]{.class-2}[ (Mark 1:2). Mark changes the ]{.class-0}["me"]{.class-2}[ (Jehovah) of Malachi 3:1 to ]{.class-0}["thee."]{.class-2}[ Well, who's the ]{.class-0}["thee"]{.class-2}[? It's ]{.class-0}["Jesus Christ, the Son of God"]{.class-2}[ (Mark 1:1). Malachi 3:1 is a Messianic prophecy, showing that Jew that when his Messiah showed up, He would be ]{.class-0}[God in the flesh]{.class-1}[ (you find the same kind of thing in Mic. 5:2; see the remarks on that verse under the comments on Hab. 1:12).]{.class-0}

[Every corrupt modern translation on the market changes Mark 1:2, attacking the integrity of Scripture as well as the Deity of Christ. The new "Bibles" change ]{.class-0}["prophets"]{.class-2}[ (plural---]{.class-0}[KJV]{.class-1}[) to ]{.class-0}["Isaiah,"]{.class-1}[ but Isaiah is only the source of Mark 1:3 (Isa. 40:3). Being much more accurate and honest than the ]{.class-0}[ASV, RSV, NIV, ESV,]{.class-1}[ etc., the ]{.class-0}[King James]{.class-1}[ lets you know that two authors are being cited in Mark 1:2--3. Next, the modern versions help out the Jehovah's Witnesses in Mark 1:2 by getting rid of ]{.class-0}["before thee"]{.class-2}[ so you won't connect Jesus Christ to the Jehovah of the Old Testament. This is the kind of depraved scholarship recommended by Bob Jones University, John R. Rice, Harold Rawlings, Rick Warren, et al.]{.class-0}

2 But who may abide the day of his coming? and who shall stand when he appeareth? for he is like a refiner's fire, and like fullers' soap:

3 And he shall sit as a refiner and purifier of silver: and he shall purify the sons of Levi, and purge them as gold and silver, that they may offer unto the LORD an offering in righteousness.

4 Then shall the offering of Judah and Jerusalem be pleasant unto the LORD, as in the days of old, and as in former years.

5 And I will come near to you to judgment; and I will be a swift witness against the sorcerers, and against the adulterers, and against false swearers, and against those that oppress the hireling in his wages, the widow, and the fatherless, and that turn aside the stranger from his right, and fear not me, saith the LORD of hosts.

6 For I am the LORD, I change not; therefore ye sons of Jacob are not consumed.

"[But who may abide the day of his coming? and who shall stand when he appeareth?"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 2) That's ]{.class-0}[not]{.class-1}[ the First Coming of Christ (Rev. 6:17; Joel 2:11; Nah. 1:6). Of course, you certainly can make spiritual application to the ]{.class-0}["refiner's fire"]{.class-2}[ (see Job 23:10) and the ]{.class-0}["fullers' soap"]{.class-2}[ (see Tit. 3:5; 1 John 1:7; Eph. 5:26). It is evident that Jesus was doing some refining and purifying of the Levites (vs. 3) in Matthew 21--23, but no priest made ]{.class-0}["an offering in righteousness"]{.class-2}[ to the Lord (vs. 3) during Christ's earthly ministry. They were a bunch of thieving (Matt. 21:13), murdering (John 7:25), lying (Matt. 28:11--14) hypocrites (Matt. 23:13--14). But in the Millennium, the Old Testament Temple, priesthood, and sacrifices are coming back (vs. 4 cf. Ezek. 40--46; see the comments on Zeph. 3:9).]{.class-0}

[In some ]{.class-0}[King James]{.class-1}[ Bibles in verse 3, you will still find ]{.class-0}["soap"]{.class-2}[ with the archaic spelling ]{.class-0}["sope."]{.class-2}[ The apostates use instances such as this as an excuse to make 36,000 (]{.class-0}[NASV]{.class-1}[) to 64,000 (]{.class-0}[NIV]{.class-1}[) changes in your Bible that attack every major fundamental of the faith and cause you to lose hundreds of revelations that the Holy Spirit could have given you by comparing Scripture with Scripture (1 Cor. 2:13).]{.class-0}

[The truth is, there are only about fifty archaic words in the ]{.class-0}[King James Version.]{.class-1}[ For years I have given these words in class to our students at the Pensacola Bible Institute. Of those words, the students could figure out what twenty of them meant without looking up anything. Well, thirty words out of 788,258 is no need to change what God said. There's no reason why thirty or fifty words can't be defined in the margin.]{.class-0}

[Again, verse 5 could be said to be fulfilled ]{.class-0}[spiritually]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[devotionally]{.class-1}[ at the First Advent (John 8:7; Matt. 23:14--22; Acts 8:9--23, 13:6--11), but the ]{.class-0}[literal]{.class-1}[ fulfillment awaits the Second Coming (Zech. 5:4; James 5:4; Rev. 9, 18).]{.class-0}

[Verse 6 is a great passage. It defines the very first symbol ever given of Israel in the Scriptures. The national symbol of Israel is not a six-pointed star; it is the burning bush that was not consumed (Exod. 3:2). The Jews have literally been burned in furnaces in the Nazi Holocaust and yet have not been ]{.class-0}["consumed."]{.class-2}[ After losing 42% of their population between 1939--1945, the Jews numbered nine million in 1980 and fourteen-and-a-half million in 2005.]{.class-0}

[Malachi 3:6 gives the only explanation for the existence of the Jewish nation today. The only reason there is a Jewish State in Palestine today is because God swore by Himself to one man to give him that land (Gen. 22:15--18; Heb. 6:13), and He won't change what He promised. Paul said, ]{.class-0}["For the gifts and calling of God are without repentance"]{.class-2}[ (Rom. 11:29). The context of that is God restoring the nation of Israel at Christ's return. God won't go back and undo what He swore He'd do.]{.class-0}

If you want to see a genuine miracle, it's the Jews. The Devil has been trying to wipe them out since 1500 B.C., and they're still here. The only reason the Holocaust didn't wipe them out completely is because God took care of them. I mean, those Germans are thorough, boy; in six years they managed to get rid of almost half of the Jewish population of the world. Just think if they had had twenty years to do it. But after all that, the Jews not only survived, they grew in numbers and became a nation again after almost nineteen centuries. God did that; it's a miracle.

That's where I have a bone to pick with those Jews. I have read books by Jews who survived the Holocaust, and they take credit for their own preservation. They don't even acknowledge that God had anything to do with it. It's enough to make me want to cuss. I know more about their nation than some of them do. It's a genuine miracle that one of them is left alive.

You crazy nut! Do you know why you're not dead right now? It's because God keeps you breathing and keeps your heart beating. If the Lord wanted you dead, do you think He'd have to exert Himself to do it? Why, it'd be one little blood clot and down you'd go. Well, then, why don't you give Him the credit? Why don't you give Him the glory?

7 Even from the days of your fathers ye are gone away from mine ordinances, and have not kept them. Return unto me, and I will return unto you, saith the LORD of hosts. But ye said, Wherein shall we return?

8 Will a man rob God? Yet ye have robbed me. But ye say, Wherein have we robbed thee? In tithes and offerings.

9 Ye are cursed with a curse: for ye have robbed me, even this whole nation.

10 Bring ye all the tithes into the storehouse, that there may be meat in mine house, and prove me now herewith, saith the LORD of hosts, if I will not open you the windows of heaven, and pour you out a blessing, that there shall not be room enough to receive it.

11 And I will rebuke the devourer for your sakes, and he shall not destroy the fruits of your ground; neither shall your vine cast her fruit before the time in the field, saith the LORD of hosts.

12 And all nations shall call you blessed: for ye shall be a delightsome land, saith the LORD of hosts.

"[Ye are gone away from mine ordinances\...Return unto me, and I will return unto you, saith the LORD of hosts"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 7). Basically, all sin is departing from God, and all repenting is returning to God. (For further commentary on returning to the Lord, see the remarks on Zech. 1:3.) The way they ]{.class-0}["return"]{.class-2}[ in the passage before us is by ]{.class-0}[giving]{.class-1}[.]{.class-0}

[Now the question of tithing arises in verses 8--10. The Old Testament method of giving under the Mosaic Law was ]{.class-0}["tithes and offerings"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 8). The tithe was the ]{.class-0}[tenth]{.class-1}[ that that Jew owed the Lord (Lev. 27:30), so a man robbed God when he didn't give the Lord what He had coming to Him, and when he didn't give beyond that amount in appreciation for the Lord taking care of him.]{.class-0}

[God addresses the nation as a whole: ]{.class-0}["Ye are cursed with a curse: for ye robbed me, even this whole nation"]{.class-2}[ (vs. 9). You reckon Americans give a tenth of their income to God? The statistics are that only 4--7% of Americans give a tenth of their income to a church or religious organization. I guess we live in a nation of ]{.class-0}[thieves.]{.class-1}

[Tithing is a big thing all around with Christians. The preachers will lay verse 10 on their congregations: ]{.class-0}["Bring ye all the tithes into the storehouse,"]{.class-2}[ meaning the ]{.class-0}[local church.]{.class-1}[ But look at what follows in verse 10: ]{.class-0}["that there may be MEAT in mine house."]{.class-2}

[Do you tithe ]{.class-0}[meat]{.class-1}[ when the offering place is passed? The Jews were commanded to tithe ]{.class-0}[cattle, sheep,]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[goats]{.class-1}[ (Lev. 27:32); that was meat "on the hoof." They were to pass those beasts under a rod and count out one, two, three, four, five, etc.; and the tenth one belonged to God (Lev. 27:32--33). They were to tithe ]{.class-0}[what they grew in their fields and gardens]{.class-1}[ (Lev. 27:30; Deut. 14:22); they even tithed their ]{.class-0}[herbs and spices]{.class-1}[ (Matt. 23:23). The purpose of that was to ]{.class-0}[feed the Levites]{.class-1}[ who served in the priesthood (Num. 18:26--31).]{.class-0}

[The tithes were brought to the Temple and stored in chambers called ]{.class-0}["the treasure house"]{.class-2}[ (Neh. 10:38) or ]{.class-0}["the treasuries"]{.class-2}[ (Neh. 13:12). The chambers stored the excess until the priests needed it (2 Chron. 31:5--11). So ]{.class-0}["the storehouse"]{.class-2}[ was a big building like a warehouse or a barn.]{.class-0}

[That's not the case in the New Testament. ]{.class-0}["The collection"]{.class-2}[ is taken up at the local assemblies ]{.class-0}["for the saints"]{.class-2}[ (1 Cor. 16:1), not the priests. You are not under Law; you are under grace. There is no hard and fast rule for giving. The principles laid down by the Apostle Paul are:]{.class-0}

"[But this I say, He which soweth sparingly shall reap also sparingly; and he which soweth bountifully shall reap also bountifully. Every man according as he purposeth in his heart, so let him give; not grudgingly, or of necessity: for God loveth a cheerful giver"]{.class-2}[ (2 Cor. 8:6--7).]{.class-0}

[So in the New Testament, the amount you give is left up to you; it's voluntary. A tight-fisted Christian will use that as an excuse not to tithe, and there are all kinds of things wrong with that. The first is the fact that you ]{.class-0}[are]{.class-1}[ under grace and not Law. Brethren, there's no reason a saved, born-again Christian under grace can't give ]{.class-0}[as much]{.class-1}[, or ]{.class-0}[more than,]{.class-1}[ a Jew under the Mosaic Law. The tithe should be the ]{.class-0}[starting point]{.class-1}[ in Christian giving, not something to be avoided or some legalistic thing laid on you where if you don't tithe God's going to take your baby, etc. My goodness, people, you are free in Christ! Why wouldn't you want to start at the tithe to show your appreciation for God saving you?]{.class-0}

[Second, you have to consider your local church. Could your church survive if all its members gave like ]{.class-0}[you]{.class-1}[ give? The truth of the matter is if every member of a church tithed, that church would be able to meet all its bills, pay its pastor, and have plenty left over to get the Gospel out overseas. The statistics are that 15% of a congregation does 85% of the giving. When a Christian says "Well, I don't give 10%, but what I do give, I give cheerfully," it usually means that he's too much of a cheap tightwad to give that much, so he'll be content with "tipping" God.]{.class-0}

You also have to consider God's blessings on your giving. That is a principle common to both Testaments.

[Verse 8: ]{.class-0}["Prove me now herewith, saith the LORD of hosts, if I will not open you the windows of heaven, and pour you out a blessing, that there shall not be room enough to receive it."]{.class-2}

"[Give, and it shall be given unto you; good measure, pressed down, and shaken together, and running over, shall men give into your bosom. For with the same measure that ye mete withal it shall be measured to you again"]{.class-2}[ (Luke 6:38).]{.class-0}

"[And God is able to make all grace abound toward you: that ye, always having all sufficiency in all things, may abound to every good work"]{.class-2}[ (2 Cor. 9:8).]{.class-0}

[The promise is that God will give you anything you ]{.class-0}[need]{.class-1}[ to do anything He's ]{.class-0}[called you to do]{.class-1}[, but the condition is that you give ]{.class-0}[abundantly]{.class-1}[ and give ]{.class-0}[cheerfully.]{.class-1}[ You aren't giving abundantly if you are not even meeting the level of the tithe (10%).]{.class-0}

[I will give you my experience with tithing, and I hope it will be an encouragement to you. I was tithing ]{.class-0}[before]{.class-1}[ I was saved. I was taking Catholic convert courses down at St. Michael's on Palafox Street in downtown Pensacola. I was trying to get to Heaven by works, you know, so I would put my tithe in the box when I came into the Catholic church.]{.class-0}

When I got saved, I stopped tithing because I was "saved by grace through faith" and I wasn't "under Law" anymore, and all that kind of stuff. I really didn't know anything about tithing from a Bible standpoint. I would hear preachers talk about it, but no demand was laid on me about it, so I didn't do it.

Well, I went off to Bob Jones University, and I was there for three or four months, and I ran out of money. I finally had to send my wife and kids back to the farm in Alabama because there was no food in the trailer and no money to buy any food.

[When our students come down here to go to school and live in a trailer eating crackers and peanut butter, I know what they're going through because ]{.class-0}[I sat where they're sitting.]{.class-1}[ I got thirty dollars a week, with a wife and two babies. We lived in a 24-foot-long used trailer with no bathroom in it; we had to use a community bathroom down at the end of the trailer court. We didn't have a refrigerator; we had an old "icebox" where you had to haul a 25-pound block of ice in with a pair of tongs. There was no hot water; if you wanted hot water, you had to heat it up on the stove in a pot. So I know exactly what my students are going through because ]{.class-0}[I've been through it myself.]{.class-1}

I lived that way the first year at Bob Jones, and I wasn't tithing. I remember one time when I was in that trailer by myself after sending the wife and kids back to Alabama. I was out of food and I was hungry. I began banging around through the cupboards looking for something to eat. I finally came up with an old box of pancake flour that a previous occupant had left; that was all I had. I mixed it with some water and got kind of a blue paste. I was so hungry that it tasted good.

Well, I got to thinking about that thing, and I was about ready to quit. The next day, though, some of my buddies in the trailer court, who had been watching me go through all that, left a big box of groceries on my porch, so I phoned to have the wife and kids come back. I decided then that I was going to start tithing and test God out. I had a cup there on the icebox, and when some money came in, before I paid any of my debts or bills, I would put my tithe money in that cup and give it to the Lord the next Sunday.

Things began to pick up and get better and were going along pretty well. I got a car and was going out preaching in meetings on the weekends. One time I had a meeting to which to go, and I didn't have enough gas to get there. We had a new baby then, and I had no milk for the baby. There was about five dollars in the tithe cup, and I was tempted to use that money to buy gas to get to the meeting. I figured I would pick up an offering there and have money to buy the milk. But I said, "No, the tithe is the Lord's, so I'm not going to do that." I decided to start out for the meeting, and if I ran out of gas, I just wouldn't make it there.

Well, I got about thirty miles down the road, with about a hundred miles to go. The gauge was already hitting empty. About that time I saw a hitchhiker along the side of the road, so I stopped to pick him up (it was safe to do that back then; it isn't now). We got to shooting the breeze, and it turned out he was a Christian. When we got to where he was going, I was going to let him out, and he said, "Wait a minute; pull over into this gas station." I pulled in there, and he bought me a tank of gas. It was enough to get me the rest of the way to the meeting and back. When I got home, I found out that somebody had left some milk there for my wife, and we got a letter from the farm where someone had sent us fifty bucks.

[From that time on, I gave the Lord the tithe first, no matter what. ]{.class-0}["Honour the LORD with thy substance, and with the firstfruits of all thine increase,"]{.class-2}[ the Bible says in Proverbs 3:9.]{.class-0}

[My experience with that in the last sixty years has been ]{.class-0}[seventeen cars]{.class-1}[ all paid for, ]{.class-0}[three houses ]{.class-1}[all paid for, ]{.class-0}[a church]{.class-1}[ with all its bills paid, ]{.class-0}[a gym ]{.class-1}[fully paid for, ]{.class-0}[a bookstore]{.class-1}[ completely paid for. I increased my giving to 15%, 20%, 30%, and then 40%. A couple of times when someone gave me a big check, I just gave the whole thing to the Lord. I'm not telling this to you to brag. I'm telling you this to let you know that you can tithe---and give beyond the tithe---and ]{.class-0}[the Lord will take care of you.]{.class-1}[ I've made it with no "red ink" on my bank account. When it comes to how to handle your money, the Lord wants you to give ]{.class-0}[cheerfully]{.class-1}[ and ]{.class-0}[abundantly]{.class-1}[. You'll never do that if you don't learn to tithe first.]{.class-0}

[Now why the tithe? Why 10%? The first time the tithe shows up, it is not under the Law. The tithe doesn't begin with the nation of Israel. The first time the tithe shows up is ]{.class-0}[under grace]{.class-1}[, so there goes the excuse of a tight-fisted Christian for not tithing.]{.class-0}

[The first mention of tithing in the Bible is in Genesis 14:18--20, where Abraham gave Melchizedek ]{.class-0}["tithes of all"]{.class-2}[ he had taken in battle with the Kings from Babylon. Melchizedek was a type of Christ at both His First and Second Advents (Heb. 7:1--3): ]{.class-0}["King of righteousness"]{.class-2}[ first (First Advent), and ]{.class-0}["King of peace"]{.class-2}[ next (Second Advent).]{.class-0}

[The next mention of the tithe is in Genesis 28:20--22, where Jacob vows to ]{.class-0}["give the tenth"]{.class-2}[ to God if the Lord will bring Jacob back home again safely. Jacob is ]{.class-0}[under grace]{.class-1}[ just like his granddaddy Abraham was. Tithing is a ]{.class-0}[grace]{.class-1}[ thing as well as a Law thing.]{.class-0}

[In fact, the next occurrence of tithing is still ]{.class-0}[under grace,]{.class-1}[ even though it is the nation of Israel doing it. Israel has come out of Egypt by the "blood of the Lamb" and has been "baptized" (1 Cor. 10:1--2). They are in the wilderness, and the Lord is going to give them something to ]{.class-0}[eat.]{.class-1}[ It is manna, and the Lord tells Moses to take ]{.class-0}["an omer"]{.class-2}[ of manna and put it in a pot to ]{.class-0}["lay it up before the LORD"]{.class-2}[ (Exod. 16:33). Guess how much ]{.class-0}["an omer"]{.class-2}[ is? It's ]{.class-0}["the TENTH part ]{.class-2}[\[a tithe\]]{.class-0}[ of an ephah"]{.class-2}[ (Exod. 16:36).]{.class-0}

[So far, you have been given ]{.class-0}[no reason]{.class-1}[ for the tithe. There is no reason given why Abraham or Jacob gave the tenth. In fact, there is ]{.class-0}[no explanation ]{.class-1}[given in Leviticus, Numbers, or Deuteronomy---where the tithe is commanded in the Law---as to why God required 10%. But when you get to Exodus 16, you have an interesting association that hints at the reason.]{.class-0}

[In Exodus 16, the ]{.class-0}["tenth"]{.class-2}[ is connected to something that is ]{.class-0}[eaten]{.class-1}[ (see our remarks on ]{.class-0}["the storehouse"]{.class-2}[ above). Well, I know a tithe that is going to be ]{.class-0}["eaten"]{.class-2}[ in the Tribulation: it's ]{.class-0}["the holy seed"]{.class-2}[ (Isa. 6:13); it's ]{.class-0}[people]{.class-1}[ (see the remarks on the ]{.class-0}["godly seed"]{.class-2}[ under 2:15). That's why Nehemiah had the Jews ]{.class-0}["bring one out of ten to dwell in Jerusalem"]{.class-2}[ (Neh. 11:1). That was a tithe of ]{.class-0}[people]{.class-1}[.]{.class-0}

[As I told you under Zephaniah 3:12 (see the comments on the passage), the Antichrist is going to take a tenth of the Jewish populace, which will be located in Jerusalem, and behead them at an altar (Rev. 6:9, 20:4), and eat their flesh and drink their blood in a Roman Catholic Mass (Isa. 6:13; Psa. 16:4, 106:37; Jer. 51:34). While that is going on, another group of Jews flees to Sela-Petra where they are fed by God with manna (Mic. 7:14--15; Rev. 12:6, 14). That goes on until ]{.class-0}["Melchisedec"]{.class-2}[ (Heb. 7:1--2) comes and puts a stop to it (Psa. 44:22--26). That manna Moses set aside in that pot and all those sacrifices decapitated at the Brazen Altar (read Lev. 1) are prophetic pictures of what will happen to the Jews in the Tribulation (cf. Psa. 78:2--3).]{.class-0}

[Now you won't pick that up in 35 reference Bibles, 45 commentaries, three Bible dictionaries, six lexicons, and 21 concordances. Why is that? Because the authors are more concerned with "original autographs" and "grammatico-historical interpretations" than they are with the Holy Spirit's prescribed method of obtaining truth: ]{.class-0}[comparing Scripture with Scripture]{.class-1}[ (1 Cor. 2:13). We believe in ]{.class-0}[Sola Scriptura]{.class-1}[---]{.class-0}["the Scriptures alone."]{.class-1}[ The other guys want to replace the Book with ]{.class-0}[their]{.class-1}[ authority so ]{.class-0}[they]{.class-1}[ will have the supreme place: they want you to look to ]{.class-0}[them]{.class-1}[ for your authority instead of the Book. ]{.class-0}[We'll stick with the Book;]{.class-1}[ it is so far ahead of them that they couldn't catch up with an X-15.]{.class-0}

Now practically speaking, God is telling the Jews in verse 11 that if they will give the Lord what belongs to Him, He won't send in the locusts and caterpillars to eat up all their crops (see Amos 4:9). But prophetically, the verse is a reference to God getting rid of a plague of demoniac insects from the Bottomless Pit (see the comments on Joel 1:4 in Vol. I of this Commentary).

[Verse 12 is obviously a Second Advent reference because it sure ain't true ]{.class-0}[right now]{.class-1}[ (see the comments on Zeph. 3:19--20). ]{.class-0}["All nations"]{.class-2}[ call Israel ]{.class-0}["blessed"]{.class-2}[ when King Jesus reigns in Jerusalem (Psa. 72:1, 17; Jer. 33:7--9, 15--16).]{.class-0}

13 Your words have been stout against me, saith the LORD. Yet ye say, What have we spoken so much against thee?

14 Ye have said, It is vain to serve God: and what profit is it that we have kept his ordinance, and that we have walked mournfully before the LORD of hosts?

15 And now we call the proud happy; yea, they that work wickedness are set up; yea, they that tempt God are even delivered.

[The word ]{.class-0}["stout"]{.class-2}[ in verse 13 means "hard" or "strong" in a bad sense, "obstinate." The people are murmuring against the Lord like they did back in Numbers.
